Lugol's 5% Solution 82-100mg daily
Selenium (200mg/2x's daily)

Boulouke (just started)
Phosphodelserine PS (new)
ATP Cofactors
Cytozyme AD (adrenal fatigue)
Magnesium (citrate-- theronate just started)
SAM-e 800mg
Prescript Assist Probiotic
Rhodiola

### Shijin13 — 2013-10-29T01:20:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2013-lab-results-and-link-to-2011-2012-labs.8588/post-99307>

Hey MsYo. I took a quick look - but I'll go back and look indepth tomorrow but here's what I saw

Vit D3 - is low
LDL - is low
HDL is good
TRIGs are good
your pregnenlone is low
looks like you're showing some positive signs of Hashis b/c you're showing positve antibodies- I was confused b/c you had two TSH's drawn - one was above 1 and one was below 1. This is where I need to go back and re-look

your PG & E2 looks good. Unfortunately I don't know how to calculate a Pg/E2 ratio from saliva... off to figure that out after this

your DHEA-S is decent... and your ASI is a bit off so you need to mind the light.

I think you've got an altered circadian cycle, that's impacting your Vitamin A production, add that to your low Pregnenolone and LDL - I think you've got some pregnenonlone steal going on - but you are making some sex hormones - Unless that's all BHRT. I'm drawing a blank as to if you were taking BHRT -

More to follow but there's first crack!

I use a digital scale that provides a decently accurate reading based on your age/gender/activity level. I weigh 217lbs with a large/muscular bone structure. If I'm dehydrated my weight/bf% goes up and the water % (scale measures that too) goes down.

### MsYo — 2013-10-29T17:39:43+0200

My vitamin d3 was 72 before the summer. I slacked off supplementation to see how it would respond. Likely my office job and minimal sunlight in addition to EMF exposure caused it to drop. I'm now supplementing 10000iu daily. I'm going for a blood draw for my hormones this week with my primary care. I do use progesterone/BHRT creams dosing day 12-25 of my cycle. My cycle is about 28-32 days normally. I also was dosing K2 twice a day but need to reorder. My HsCRP was .4 earlier in the year and this last measure was 1.1
My ASI has been challenged for some time now. Looking into the magnetico and other areas that can make an immediate impact.

### JanSz — 2013-12-12T02:03:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2013-lab-results-and-link-to-2011-2012-labs.8588/post-103176>

MsYo

I am trying to figure out your

preg
prog
cortisol

It looks like (not sure) that you are supplementing with progesterone and are still low in morning cortisol.
So you are looking for methods to increase cortisol.
Meditation or whatever would work.

Over lat three years I (73yo man) have had good results supplementing with pregnenolone Micronize Lipid Matrix (from Nutricology).
I use huge doses of pregnenolone. Much larger than what dr Tim Jackson mentioned recently in his booth-camp.
I increase preg doses until my morning cortisol(7:30AM) is at the top level.
I also observe free,serum Cortisol and aim at the top range.
That way I do not have a problem of having plenty of cortisol.

Doing that my progesterone is very high.
If I was a woman, that should please dr Kruse, per him, the more prog the better.

My current prog=2.9 that is over 2x of max laboratory range of 1.4 (for men of for woman in first half of cycle).
But I had it at 4.4 without any negative observable sides.
Again, I am doing it close to 3 years now.

If I read correctly, you are supplementing with progesterone in second half of cycle.
Likely not enough, and totally neglecting first half of cycle.
And you are supplementing with progesterone while pregnenolone may possibly do better job, plus, it may take care of a whole cycle.

If you are doing CT (like you should), there is a good possibility that you are washing of and wasting lot or most progesterone cream.

### JanSz — 2013-12-13T02:20:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2013-lab-results-and-link-to-2011-2012-labs.8588/post-103272>

> [MsYo said:](/goto/post?id=103222)
>
> JanZ---
>
> My morning cortisol is still low. I was using progesterone cream (50ml) on days 12-25 of my cycle and pregnenolone all month. It is likely my initial dose wasn't high enough 150-300mg (2tabs of nutricology). I am taking 600 mg daily. **My CT has been lacking.** I slacked off on the preg dosing prior to my labs in Aug/Sept. Maybe my pregnenolone is being directed to my dhea. (wild guess)

You should not stop using pregnenolone or DHEA pills for the sake of laboratory testing.

There is a minimal chance that you will increase DHEAs by supplementing with pregnenolone (of any type),
but by having low DHEAs you can waste lots of benefits that you would otherwise have from pregnenolone supplementation.
.
You should make sure that you have a high DHEAs (Women 275-400 μg/dL), close to 400 better.
.
.
You are menstruating woman.
Theoretically, in first half of cycle (say 15 days) you should have progesterone at least 1.5ng/mL
Going by my (I am man) personal experience, nothing bad is going to happen if you put high limit on progesterone:
in first 15 days do not exceed progesterone,serum~5ng/mL
keep increasing pregnenolone Micronized Lipid Matrix from Nutricology until
1\. your cortisol,serum(7:30AM)=(~20)ug/dL
or
2.progesterone level reaches ~5ng/mL
At this time I would put maximum daily pregnenolone at 1200mg=8tabs, each 150mg

If that dose is still not giving you good cortisol, you may want to add
prometrium (micronized progesterone) 100mg per gelcap.
Prometrium may induce sleepiness, use at bed time

Again, establishing good DHEAs is first step.

I think that you should first work on first half of cycle.

\-------------------

Have you ever done 24hr urine cortisol?

\-------------------
Note that I am using pills, nothing in any creams.

Because our group of people spends lots of time in the water (CT), transdermal applications of anything have less chance to work.

Besides, lots of people, specially those who still have thyroid problems, have difficulty in benefiting from transdermal applications even if they do not wash as frequently as we are. Their skin is harder to penetrate.

///

### JanSz — 2014-03-18T22:03:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/2013-lab-results-and-link-to-2011-2012-labs.8588/post-116269>

> [MsYo said:](/goto/post?id=116231)
>
> JanZ
> No new cortisol results yet. In process results coming.
> Labs drawn on Day 21-22 of cycle. (Day 21 was Sunday/labs drawn on Monday am)
> Pregnenolone supplementation reduced after lab results to 150mg daily. Will go back on UNILIVER not sure why the drop in iron other than longer periods (5-6 days average). Currently on gluten free diet but that did not happen until January 2014.
> Can I buy prometrium online without a script?

You can get progesterone over internet.
Your very low iron is old story at least since 2011 as you posted on previous thread.
It is about time to take care of it.
Look at ingredients in
Thorne Methyl-Guard Plus, and possibly replace this with the other similar pills you are already taking.
Consider eating coconut oils by tablespoons rather than pills.
In my local Asian stores I have 16oz cans with fresh pre-shucked oysters.
They are only $9.50/can, To fill up that can I would need 3-3.5 dozens of live oysters.
I am eating them raw, no problems.
\-----------------------
day 21,22, desirable levels=
estradiol 140pg/mL
prog 14ng/mL

day 2-4, desirable levels=
estradiol 40 pg/mL
prog 1.5ng/mL
\-----------------------------
blood taken on day 21-22
desirable E2=140 you got 101
desirable progesterone=14 you got 10.4

When you will reduce pregnenoloneMLM by one pill, your progesterone will also drop.
But you are taking NatureGest progesterone cream applied once daily mornings
Consider doubling the dose.
Overall, now your p/e situation should be improved but is far from critical at this time of your menstrual cycle.
Consider checking where you are on day 2-4.

If you get Prometrium or similar, they come in 100mg and 200mg gelcaps.
You may try:
pregMLM(wakeup)+prometrium(bed time) as your basic two
and possibly additional prometrium on days 18-24, or NatureGest progesterone.
Overall you are attempting to aproximate progesterone curve on this chart:
[http://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/c/c8/Hormones\_estradiol,\_progesterone,\_LH\_and\_FSH\_during\_menstrual\_cycle.svg](http://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/c/c8/Hormones_estradiol%2C_progesterone%2C_LH_and_FSH_during_menstrual_cycle.svg)

Please clarify your DHEAs situation.

.

I have personal experience with two types of DHEA pills.
Looking at my blood test results, there is small difference between them.
So it may be important to keep the same brand.

I have a problem in deciding about your DHEAs status.

There is
DHEAs
and DHEA
you tested for DHEA,serum (and you are low on it, low within range).

Think of DHEAs ar analogus to TotalTestosterone
Think of DHEA as analogus to FreeTestosterone
Similar but different.

You are taking 100mg/day DHEA pills, that is sizeable dose (for a girl)
I am 165# man, I need to use 200mg/day now, but was using 400mg/day
\------------
Your zinc is low, if you implement my suggestion above, it may affect your testosterone and DHEA/DHEAs
Your
testosterone 34 (2-45)
FreeT 2.3 (0.1-6.4)

You could improve there.

My first choice would be to have DHEAs,serum test.
But with all above, you likely may increase your daily dose from 100 to (125-150)mg/day
and do tests latter.
\-------------------
I currently use
DHEA 50mg Micronized Lipid Matrix from Allergy research
previously I was using
100mg or 50mg DHEA from lef.org

//

MsYo, can you educate me about the value of using hair analysis for sodium / potassium balance issues instead of - for example - relying on a 24 hour urine collection to see how much you excrete and a serum blood measurement while fasting to see your current serum levels?

Would hair analysis also help with other electrolytes like magnesium and calcium?

I happen to be looking at iodine use now, and I noticed your comment earlier in this thread about having fibroids. I am NOT recommending iodine at all, but you should listen to this audio interview and what is interesting is that one of the groups most-helped by iodine supplementation is women with fibroids. It apparently also reduces cancer risk in such women substantially. It is however a pretty serious therapy and can disregulate thyroid in about 10% of patients. So it needs supervision. Here is interview:

[http://www.xpeditionstv.com/V2/](http://www.xpeditionstv.com/V2/)

P.S., Try to ignore the very unfortunate advertising used on this show, which looks outright strange.

My B12 has been historically high. I am an energy supplement addict (recovering). I switched from SF Red Bull to 5 Hour Energy. My doctor also gave me a methylated B12 that seemed to work wonderfully for a couple of weeks then I tanked. I suspect that was the time my iron dropped. I am stopping all B12 and more effort to STOP 5hour now that I know that my fatigue is from the low iron. My cortisol seems to have risen as I sleep throughout the night and awake at sunrise every morning. The exercise has helped my sleep and stress levels. I suspect a touch of overtraining as I was using a coach to help me with body recomposition. I was adapting his calorie recommendation to (EPI PALEO) but 2 weeks ago he added fasting cardio and more cardio type strength training. I know this wasn't the right thing for me but rather than whine I attempted. I have since modified my workouts.

I stopped DHEA and pregnenolone about 6 weeks ago. I have ordered both again and also the Thorne. Should I use the Thorne with my B12 being so high already? I suspect my RNY/gastric bypass has created a problem for me. Any susggestions for where I can research how to fix this?

Thanks for the reference to iodine. I will take a look at that after work. I used iodine for a year 100mg/daily and my fibroids have shrunk tremendously. I am going to see my GYN next week for an ultrasound. I have recently started back using iodine (lugol's 5%) to ensure they don't come back.
Re: Hair Analysis -- I've only done a hair analysis once. The results showed an issue with my sodium/potassium balance. I've scaled back my exercising and dosing with liquid iron (SSS Tonic). Feeling much better. I agree I need a good doctor. My functional MD is good (overall) but I just got back from my OB/Gyn visit and my fibroids are bigger. I thought they were shrinking. She suggested surgery ASAP (as she always does) or LUPRON. I'm temporarily feeling a little down but I won't linger in that.

### Liveea — 2013-10-29T04:46:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/treating-chronic-lymes.8590/post-99319>

I got my labs back from Igenx. Positive for Lyme's. I will have had it for 28 years.

I read Dr. Kruse's blog on Lyme's. My doc is going to make sure that the CD57 test for Lyme's was run or get it ordered. (I guess the full Lyme's blood work panel may not have included it.)

So, he said we would be treating with 3 different antibiotics. It would take a minimum of a year. I asked him about the neurotoxins...(I have been in debilitating pain 24/7 for well over 26 years). He didn't really dismiss my comment but implied that the antibiotics would be it.

I am going to print the blog for him. It is pretty straight forward for new Lyme's. How would I educate or help myself get the best care? (Mind you, Mayo Clinic didn't know what to do with me. So, he is the most literate doctor I have run into in 28 years. He is my one hope.)

Optimal??? IV or oral antibiotics? I don't care. I just want to get well.

Thank you for any input. Treatment may be starting as soon as this week.

(More background health information on my profile)

# Thread: Now Everything Hurts - Whatever This Is, It Is Progressing
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/now-everything-hurts-whatever-this-is-it-is-progressing.8593/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mafixit — 2013-10-29T15:42:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/now-everything-hurts-whatever-this-is-it-is-progressing.8593/post-99343>

I have been posting for about a year about my headaches and menopause issues, and now everything seems to have taken a turn in another direction and I am not sure where to go next. Mayo Clinic diagnosis is Fibromyalgia.

I am in menopause (very long, erratic cycles - am on around Day 80 now). I am on BHRT with my anti-aging doc. We have run many labs, the latest being a Nutra-Eval nutritional panel. I took the nutri-panel from Genova after cleaning out my system, and the first part of the nutritional panel showed a high need for magnesium, B-1 and Alpha-Lipoic Acid. I am waiting on the amino acid profile. I started Ortho Biotic on the advice of my hormone doctor and began to take Magnesium Taurate a few weeks ago. I feel shittier than ever. I have episodes of blurry vision, too, especially in fluorescent lighting.

My headaches are different now, not as intense, but still every day. Part of these I think are attributed to a compressed nerve on the left side at C5-C6 causing issues with my neck and left arm/shoulder. I have been in physical therapy, but it has not helped. My next stop for that is an epidural in November.

Now I have widespread pain and stiffness, especially in my elbows, forearms and hands, but just basically all over. If I stay still for more than a few seconds, I stiffen up like the tin man with no oil. I feel like I am losing strength in my arms. There are things twitching that shouldn't be. I feel these random vibrations under my skin, mostly in my thighs and girly parts. I wake up at 4:30 every morning, 15 minutes before my alarm with this feeling of bad anticipation and my anxiety level has been pretty bad of late. I have a really hard time concentrating on my work. My allergies are up after 1-1/2 years of not needing any sort of anti-histamine. I am a software support specialist by trade and work from home. I go through these small bouts of depression now, because everyday I wake up hoping that this will all get better and it never does.

Mayo Clinic's answer was anti-depressants, debilitating migraine meds and Cymbalta. According to their labs I should be a perfectly healthy 50 year old woman. None of their drugs worked and I weaned off of EVERYTHING. I started over with just my BHRT and a probiotic about a month ago. I went back on BHRT because my hot flashes were so bad I could not sleep or function. The hormones appear to help my headaches somewhat also. I do acupuncture once a week. Not sure if it helps, but it's cheap, so I do it. I had a massage last week and the therapist said my "connective tissues feel dehydrated".

My diet is fairly clean. I no longer consume gluten, which a test showed I have an intolerance to. To sum it up, I am on Estrogen, Progesterone, pro-biotics and magnesium taurate.

I am not sure where to go next, what to look for next. My hormone doc is very open to discussions with me on what I want to do or check or try. Any thoughts on my next move? It's been three years of suffering and trying different things to resolve this and I am worse than ever. It seems that the more I do to try to feel better, the worse I feel. All my blood and urine panels have always come up "normal" according to Mayo. I have an appointment with a new PCP outside the Mayo. They are too tunnel-visioned to help me, unless it involves taking a drug to mask the symptoms.

So my path right now is a new neurologist, new PCP, and the pain doc for the epidural. Again, my BHRT doc is open to thoughts or suggestions that I bring in, so, any thoughts on where to go/what to do or try next? I feel lost at this point.

Let me add that two weeks ago I had to have a molar pulled - it was the very back one on the bottom that had a root canal years ago. It was decayed under the tooth bridge it was holding on, so now I am missing two bottom teeth in the back.

Karen from Arizona

### MsYo — 2013-10-29T17:58:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/now-everything-hurts-whatever-this-is-it-is-progressing.8593/post-99356>

Do you have copies of your labs? "Normal" isn't always optimal as you know. Do you CT? Eat Epi-Paleo? Maybe post your labs in the Optimal Labs forum. I take SAM-e for my stiffness but upped the dose to 800mg to help me with moodiness. You own your destiny..... if your current physician isn't a match.... Keep looking. I suffer from adrenal fatigue which is similar to fibromyalgia I think. I was on a wait list for a doctor for 6 months and within the first month of revisiting the Leptin reset, emf reduction and some adrenal support I am improving. Keep the faith...... believe.... never give up.

### Melinda — 2013-10-29T20:25:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/now-everything-hurts-whatever-this-is-it-is-progressing.8593/post-99367>

Hi Karen,

I'm eager to hear what replies you get. I've been Kruse directionally accurate for a long time. I'm post meno and on bio hormones. I was feeling so great this last spring and summer as my sleep improved and we transitioned to much lower EMF, I threw myself at projects that were physical, re-tiling a few rooms. The oddest thing developed: the usual post exercise muscle soreness never left. I'm sore in large areas of my back and neck; my inner thighs are useless and it's hard to get up and down from a chair/car. But the morning stiffness is the worst, like rigor mortise. I'm trying trigger point massage and it helps but I do not get how this could happen out of the blue. We are all eating clean and drinking the same water but this only happened to me. Not sure where to go from here.
I know this is not enough info to get meaningful advice but I just wanted to share with you that you are not alone.

Also, from Arizona

### mafixit — 2013-10-29T20:43:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/now-everything-hurts-whatever-this-is-it-is-progressing.8593/post-99371>

I can pinpoint the day the pain started in my right elbow- July 4th of this year. Thought I had tennis elbow. That pain has moved into my right hand, and now my left hand and arm are also extremely stiff. Getting in and out of bed or the car is painful. All my toes hurt when I flex them and two on my right foot burn like they have carpet burn. I described it as you did - it feels like I worked out yesterday for the first time in a year.

Saw a doc for RA. Nope, not that. Saw something this morning on using Chlorophyll in your water to make yourself more alkaline as acidity can cause issues. Wondering if I should head out and look for the urine test to check my pH

What part of AZ are you in? The Valley or the High Country? PM me if you want to on who you see for your BHRT.

.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/now-everything-hurts-whatever-this-is-it-is-progressing.8593/post-99419>

I have eliminated gluten and grains. I did have the hs-crp lab done, twice in the past year. The latest was 1.6 mg/L, which is better than the previous results. Lower range, but I feel worse. I am on fish oil with my supplement pack. I have not tried Resveratrol. My husband takes it. I do peruse LEF.org often. My BHRT doc has their magazines all over their office.

I did test for EMF's and found my clock radio that was plugged in under the head of my bed was atrociously bad. I removed it. That is all I found in the house, unless I stand directly in front of the microwave. My meter is on the other side of the house outside of the garage. It's the furthest away it could be.

I am incorporating the MELT Method into my daily routine. I have the roller and foam/rubber balls and am waiting for her book to arrive. I have scheduled a massage therapist to come in once a week. I had a good massage yesterday afternoon and felt good when he left, but woke up in the middle of the night with a horrible headaches. Headaches are waking me up at night along with the pain all over.

I did notice that when I am on a small dose (in the morning when I wake up) of Nature-Throid, I seem to sleep better, but that is the only difference I feel.

Karen from Arizona

Now I am feeling like I am choking, hard to breathe but I can take a deep breath. Chest and upper abdomen feels tight over the past week. Which of course pushes the anxiety level way up - Tightness came first, not anxiety, so it's not anxiety related at least initially. Not sure if it's hormone imbalance again, or stomach issues. Or all muscular from the pain in my back and neck from the compressed nerve. Oy Vey!

On Day 82 of my cycle, one of the longest time periods between periods. Maybe menopause is finally kicking in.

Karen from Arizona

**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 2

### Hemming — 2013-10-29T16:42:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clarification-on-janszs-question-about-olive-oil-in-ask-jack.8595/post-99347>

We're supposed to eat a lot of seafood and eat mainly MUFAs with it such as olive oil and avocado as saturated fat blocks the absorption of DHA. But how does that tie in with that Jack says that olive oil is not a staple for him? If we eat a lot of seafood then we would automatically be eating a lot of olive oil/MUFA with it. Should we instead eat tallow or lard with the seafood?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-10-29T19:24:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/clarification-on-janszs-question-about-olive-oil-in-ask-jack.8595/post-99363>

Once your are LS, ie low cytokine levels, high tissue O2 content, the fats can and should move to the saturated fats like butter........but MUFA's are perfectly fine with seafood......because it has the other parts MUFA needs to remain stable......iodine and DHA.

### healthman — 2013-10-29T20:42:13+0200

### Inger — 2013-10-30T07:18:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lambs-heart.8601/post-99400>

Oh yes. That is what I eat every day right now because they are "in season" here. I eat it for my meat, almost everything else I eat is from the sea right now. The hearts are very cheap, I got 1 organic lamb heart, 1 kidney and 1 liver for only 4 â‚¬ package. I got me 10 of them, going to freeze something too. This is so cheap food, and it is so good for me. I do not care too much what it tastes like. I usually add some organic balsamic vinegar from 100% grapes, and salt and raw onion and dried wild mushrooms. Makes it delicious! That was my breakfast today... with a huge slice of grass fed beef fat... bigger than my hand. That fat I just munched on raw and plain... tastes like puff pastry to me..yum!

You could try to slice it up in mouth pieces and fry on low heat just a tiny bit, so it still stays a bit raw, like medium. Just salt and pepper, maybe some curcumin to taste. I have tried that and the taste is great! Makes the hard fat around the heart melt a bit and get so yummy.

### healthman — 2013-10-30T13:03:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lambs-heart.8601/post-99408>

Thanks for the replies
I ended up cutting the rest of the heart into small pieces and swallowing whole with a mouthful of water. Same with liver until I get used to eating it raw will have to make do with swallowing small pieces down
I am now eating 3 raw oysters every day and that took some getting used to...But eat prawns, mussels, kelp, dulse seaweed and cockles daily without any problems

A few questions please

How much raw liver can you eat each week/day safely?
The same question regarding lambs heart?
Anyone tried raw kidney?

Many thanks

### veelansu — 2013-10-30T19:27:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lambs-heart.8601/post-99437>

I love raw oysters and raw scallops, they taste about the same. I have never eaten raw lamb heart. I am trying to find a place to buy these things.

### endless — 2013-12-07T06:56:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lambs-heart.8601/post-102678>

I agree with Inger...organ meat is an aquired taste, especially raw, but making it like a tartare or ceviche with added flavor helps! I think I will try your method of swallowing with water for the liver though....I love it cooked but raw it's a bit strong for me.

I think raw liver 2-3 times a week is plenty. I'm guessing 2oz or so at a time? Heart I think you could eat every day with no problems....
Raw kidneys? Nope. I can't stomach kidneys, they taste like pee to me

### Inger — 2013-12-07T10:22:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lambs-heart.8601/post-102693>

Just had a breakfast consisting of raw grassfed beef heart plain with just a few grains of salt (I actually like it better without salt), some thin slices of raw onion, and sliced raw grassfed beef fat plain. A wooden plate full... eaten as finger food. YUM!
I think too, heart you can safely eat every day. Liver you will feel when you have enough, so no worry. Some get loose stools when they have too much liver so maybe the body just do not absorb it when too much, IDK? It will just not appeal anymore. That is how it works for me at least. I eat liver in periods, sometimes a lot and sometimes no. I sometimes eat liver for dessert..lol plain, raw and sliced, so sweet like chocolate! Yesterday for dinner I had a big glass of raw fish head smoothie, then a glass of seaweed smoothie, and for dessert a cup of sliced raw liver.. and an hour later some Brazil nuts but they was not satisfying me and I felt I should rather have left them out so I made me some thin raw beef fat slices and that was sooo yummy! haha.. pretty weird menu

Raw kidneys are not my favorite either.. tastes like pee to me too! I think you could put them in water for a while to lessen the pee taste but I have not tried that. I eat them seldom and when - I usually hold my nose.

I think it can take some time to get used to a new way of eating, and new tastes, so never give up if it tastes weird to a beginning. It usually changes when you keep eating it and all of a sudden you like it

### SeaHorse — 2013-12-07T14:53:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lambs-heart.8601/post-102714>

I've found that pork kidneys taste the best raw to me....lamb is good slightly cooked only, or swallowed without really tasting. Any kind of heart still remains my favorite for eating raw...

# Thread: Altitude Training or Enhanced Oxygen Training with Cold
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/altitude-training-or-enhanced-oxygen-training-with-cold.8605/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ColinGorham — 2013-10-29T21:59:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/altitude-training-or-enhanced-oxygen-training-with-cold.8605/post-99382>

**Question: Altitude Training or Enhanced Oxygen Training with Cold?**

Question: I have an Altitude Machine which can simulate oxygen concentrations at 1) Sea Level to 21,000 ft or 2) Increase the Oxygen concentration of Ambient Air up to 40 percent. While peddling on the Elliptical & wearing an Ice Vest, I practice Intermittent Hypoxia, Hypoxic Workouts, or Exercise with Oxygen Training (EWOT). (Everything done simulating low altitude/ mildly hypoxic air. Always use a pulse oximeter, never go below 85 percent blood oxygen saturation) From the perspective of body re-composition, what exercise scenario would be optimal?

Note: Not asking for medical advice, I just want to hear Jack Kruse's general feeling about this from a theoretical perspective.

More Info:
Hypoxic Workouts (HW). HW involves riding a stationary bicycle or running on a treadmill three times per week while breathing hypoxic air. HW can substantially lower your blood oxygen saturation, which may stimulate additional production of red blood cells. Mountain climbers benefit from hypoxic workouts on inclined treadmill with backpack to simulate high-altitude hiking.

Intermittent Hypoxic Training (IHT). In IHT, you repeat short intervals (typically 5 min on, 5 min off) breathing moderately to highly hypoxic air (as little as 10% oxygen). Interval length and oxygen level are adjusted to achieve a target oxygen saturation in the blood, typically 85% to 90%. You measure blood oxygen saturation with a pulse oximeter.

EWOT. Exercising while breathing oxygen concentrations higher than ambient air.

### ColinGorham — 2013-10-29T22:01:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/altitude-training-or-enhanced-oxygen-training-with-cold.8605/post-99384>

I plan to add grounding to the protocol in the future.

### RickD — 2013-10-30T03:55:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/altitude-training-or-enhanced-oxygen-training-with-cold.8605/post-99397>

Colin,

I think I know what Jack will say, but since I own an altitude tent and have used it extensively for both training and mountaineering I figured I'd toss in my two cents as well. The standard way to use it is to sleep high, train low. For training I'd set it to about 8000 ft and the results were great. Living at Sea Level I had no altitude acclimation issues using that protocol. For Mountaineering I'd set it to 14000 ft and again had zero acclimation issues once on the mountain. For me if I increased the altitude further I had problems sleeping which defeats the purpose of using the tent.

To research your question further look to the mountaineering community. That's an activity that literally destroys your body no matter how good of shape your in. After 1-2 weeks above 14000 ft you literally start to waste away because your body simply can't recover from even mild exertion. My advice is then not to do intervals at altitude. Training is hard enough and any serious endurance athlete is always on the edge of becoming overtrained. Hammering out intervals with limited oxygen will just put you that much closer to being overtrained. And take it from me crossing that line is something you want to avoid at all costs.

I also wear an ice vest while doing some indoor interval work. Most times it's on my kayak ergometer and I find the cooling actually helps the interval performance. Not sure though this is really that helpful from a CT perspective.

### ColinGorham — 2013-10-31T17:08:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/altitude-training-or-enhanced-oxygen-training-with-cold.8605/post-99534>

Thank you Rick D for the great perspective on altitude training. I tend to agree. In my opinion, interval training (HIIT) while breathing hypoxic air is not necessary and potentially dangerous. HIIT on its own drops oxygen saturation quickly during the short intense burst section of the workout, and coupled with hypoxic air you could bring oxygen down too low to the point where you could pass out. Instead, I use Hypoxic Workouts (steady state cardio while breathing hypoxic air) or Intermittent Hypoxia (stead state cardio while intermittently breathing hypoxic air). From a cardiovascular & oxygen saturation standpoint, doing steady state cardio while breathing hypoxic air intermittently is somewhat similar to a HIIT workout.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: BJâ€™s Roller Coaster Ride
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 277
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BJK77 — 2013-10-30T16:23:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-99420>

I finally decided itâ€™s time to start a journal and stick with it . . . though Iâ€™m sure I wonâ€™t be updating it too often since I really try hard to limit my computer time. That being said, I feel like Iâ€™ve hit a plateau recently and am just â€œstuckâ€ in my journey and could really use some help sorting some issues out.

Iâ€™ve been blessed to see some amazing improvements in my health in the two years Iâ€™ve been following Jackâ€™s blog. Prior to that I had no success on the GAPS diet, a strict anti-candida diet and just plain paleo. In fact, I actually think I got worse on GAPS  Some of you may remember my post earlier in the summer where I said I was finally getting back to my healthier days. I was feeling great, had increased energy and everything just seemed to be going well.

The problem is it never seems to last for me, which is why Iâ€™ve called this journal my â€œroller coaster ride.â€ I feel like Iâ€™m constantly on this loop with so many ups and downs and right now Iâ€™m definitely on one of the low points. Though I have seen steady improvements in certain areas there are other areas where I feel like Iâ€™m just constantly chasing my tail and they reappear over and over again!

It also seems like every time I remove anything from my life, be it related to diet such as a specific food or limiting EMF, when I reintroduce it in any way I find I am now SUPER sensitive. This has caused me to be EXTREMELY limited with my diet for the last 2+ years. Iâ€™ve had (and still do have AFAIK) problems with oxalates, sulfites, salicylates, histamine, etc, etc. I can honestly say my diet is about as good as it can be and I do not stray at all from what I know I need to do.

I know diet is only a small step in this journey, but just to get it out of the way Iâ€™ll detail what/how I eat because I canâ€™t imagine what Iâ€™m consuming could possibly be part of the problem so Iâ€™m hoping it will be easy to eliminate this as a cause for the recent issues:
\- All grassfed/pastured organic meats â€“ mostly beef, some pork and lamb
\- Lots of wild King salmon sashimi, oysters, shrimp, scallops, sardines, etc.
\- Only carbs are avocado, mushrooms, sea veg
\- Thatâ€™s pretty much it â€“ NO dairy, nuts, eggs
\- I drink a combination of Spring, well and mineral water ~ 1 gallon/day

Iâ€™ve been eating all my meals outside while earthing â€“ even now when the mornings are 32 degrees! I strictly follow the Leptin Rx and have for nearly 2 years now. I havenâ€™t had a single snack that entire time. I try to finish eating 3-4 hours before bed, so I think Iâ€™m pretty good when it comes to the Leptin Rx.

Hereâ€™s one of my problems Iâ€™ve had consistently since starting the Leptin Rx â€“ I get extremely warm after some meals â€“ there seems to be no rhyme or reason to this. One day sashimi will make me start to burn up, the next day it will be after some pork roast, but then Iâ€™ll eat the very same thing the following day and nothing! It also doesn't seem to be an effect from my field either as it sometimes happens at work when I'm surrounded by wifi, but sometimes doesn't; sometimes it happens at home when wifi is turned off. I live in an area with 2-3 acre lots and while I can detect my neighbor's wifi, it is a very low signal. We only allow ours to be turned on for about an hour a day at the most.

My sleep was great over the summer, but in the last month it has royally sucked! Worse than itâ€™s ever been. I wake in the middle of the night burning up. My bedroom is a nice, cold 59 degrees at night and I wake up HOT at 2 AM! We cut the breakers for the bedrooms, sleep on a 20-gauss Magnetico and keep the window open so it's nice and chilly, but none of this seems to be helping.

Iâ€™ve also started passing gallstones and/or liver stones again, which causes me to become constipated. I've had this happen several times in the past as well. I asked Jack about gallstones in an Ask Jack post and my takeaway is the problem is related to dehydration, possible gut bacterial infection (H. Pylori?), low vitamin A/melatonin â€“ problems with the circadian cycle.

So how do I fix this?! It seems like a neverending circle. My sleep sucks right now, so of course my melatonin is out of whack. I had a negative H. Pylori test. I know my BUN/creatinine level sucks, but I'm drinking at least a gallon of good water/day. I am religious about wearing my blue blockers. I put them on as soon as I wake up in the morning and donâ€™t take them off until I go outside in the freezing temps to eat breakfast and wait for the sun to rise. They go back on anytime Iâ€™m on my computer or inside with the lights on. What else can I do?

Two things I changed before the stones recurred: Decreased my Naturethroid because I thought maybe I was taking too much and this was causing the excessive overheating. Turns out it wasnâ€™t, so Iâ€™m back on my normal dose and getting labwork done later this week to confirm. Second change was increasing the amount of fat I was eating. As soon as the weather started to cool I found myself craving it like crazy and I would eat 4-5 tablespoons with my BAB, but then the stones started . . . how is all of this related? I canâ€™t figure it out.

I woke at 2 AM last night and just cried. I really thought I was getting somewhere with my health. I knew sleep was important before Jack even hit it in the webinar, but now that I know just how important it is I'm a mess when I wake up at night because I know what it's doing to me to lay there for hours and not be able to get into a good, deep sleep. What I find strange is that I have no problem falling asleep and am not usually quite as hot then. it's when I wake in the middle of the night that I'm totally burning up and just can't fall back to sleep.

If you've been able to read this far, thanks. I'd love to hear any suggestions you may have. I'm not the most patient person and don't do well at taking 2 steps back for every step I move forward. I'm so tired of being elated by progress only to nosedive into a massive depression because it doesn't last.

### BJK77 — 2013-10-30T16:27:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-99421>

Another thing I thought I should add is that I eat 2 meals per day that are seafood. I also eat sea veggies with my non-seafood meal and with one of my seafood meals. I think I should be getting plenty of iodine . . . question is am I assimilating it?!

Also, I did have my house checked for EMF and readings were very low. I am so sensitive though that even typing on my grounded laptop for 5 minutes causes my right hand to go ice cold and numb. I am exposed to more EMF at work, but I only go in 3 days a week so I was hoping I could manage that by offsetting it at home with CT, earthing, ketosis, etc.

How nice you have a journal now BJK77! I am so sorry your sleep sucks, that is a nightmare for sure.

59 degree F is too hot for me to sleep in... that is about the summer nights here and I like it way colder. I have around 44 degree F in my bedroom at night right now - with a thin duvet - but when the temps get colder it goes below freezing in there because I have no heating on and window wide open always. Is it possible for you to get it colder in there? 59 degrees F is what I have in my apartment living area all winter long

Maybe you also would sleep better alone, I do. When hubs stay over I sleep not so well at all... he turns around a lot and goes to pee and we try to both fit on a single magnetico pad lol I still manage to sleep somehow. But I get so hot when close to him. Alone I sleep so much better to be honest. Good is I mostly sleep alone!
You should try to sleep in a tent BJK77! Could you do it in your garden maybe? Under some trees?

Are you eating lots of avocados? I know some people do not do well on those, I have forgotten why. What else are you eating for fat.

### BJK77 — 2013-11-04T17:16:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-99804>

Thankfully my sleep is getting a bit better! I think it may be due to wearing my blue blockers more often. I am abit confused about how/when to wear them though. I just got a new pair for me and DH from lowbluelight.com and they came with instructions basically saying you should ONLY wear them a few hours before bed and that you need to be very consistent with when you wear them or else you run the risk of trying to reset your circadian clock too many times and that can cause even more damage. I thought I had read on here that we should always wear them when exposed to blue light though?? Am I remembering wrong? For the last few days I've been wearing them whenever I'm on my laptop or using a light other than an amber bulb. I'm pretty sure this is what's helping my sleep, but I don't want to be making things worse!

It also helps that it's getting a bit cooler in the evenings (low 40's) and we keep the bedroom window open, so I'm not quite as warm as I had been at night. It's strange though because I do still find myself getting warm nearly every evening after dinner. And even with the window open and my room feeling like it's freezing, I still can only use one light blanket and I wake very warm. I don't know why this is happening! Body temps are supposed to drop at night, right?!

I'm still strugging to fully understand the concept of transition metals too. I read the latest blog once, but need to go back and re-read it again and again. In my transition metals post, Jack said my work environment (next to a machine shop that does welding/grinding) is probably my main issue. I KNOW I feel worse at work. In fact, I often find myself burning up and getting totally overheated in the office and by the end of the day I'm just exhausted.

My gut says I need to just up and quit, but of course life isn't quite that simple  I've got a lot of soul searching to do to figure out where I need to go from here . . .

### BJK77 — 2013-11-04T21:33:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-99830>

Part of me senses that this is all related to me not being able to get into the stinkin' PPP! I listened to the PPP webinar earlier and Jack said there are 2 reasons people can't enter the PPP: 1) carbs are too high; and 2) dehydration.

My BUN/Creatinine ratio sucks, so yes I guess I am intracellularly dehydrated. No surprise to me really as I often feel very dry and thirsty when I'm around too much EMF. The thing is I'm hardly exposed to any major EMF anymore! My house is good. Work is the problem . . . 2-3 days a week is all I go into the office though. Am I being unrealistic to think and \*hope\* that 4-5 days without that exposure is enough for my body to strengthen itself to be able to deal with the few days I am at work?!

ETA: I asked Jack about not being able to enter the PPP and just saw his response that it's either serious EMF risks or really horrible glutathione levels due to poor cysteine utilization. ### Bonniebelle — 2013-11-04T22:22:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-99834>

I think this is part of that methylation pathway and I don't completely understand the methylation pathway and I haven't seen the PPP pathway webinar yet. But I did find this article.

[http://www.immunehealthscience.com/cysteine.html](http://www.immunehealthscience.com/cysteine.html)

In order to make glutathione in our bodies we need 3 amino acids...glutamate, glycine, and cysteine. The article says that many diets are deficient in cysteine.

"Your body can and does produce some cysteine on its own from another amino acid – methionine, which is also a sulfur-containing amino acid. Methionine is an essential amino acid, meaning it is not produced by the body but comes from diet only.

The process of transformation of methionine into cysteine is a multi-step process, very complex and requires the presence of certain enzymes and B-vitamins. The process of transforming methionine into cysteine can be interrupted by a number of things.

Some of the cysteine that does get produced from methionine can be used for manufacturing glutathione inside the cells. Unfortunately, methionine also converts into homocysteine in the body.

Not sure if any of that is helpful or not. What was your homocysteine level? Since some of the methionine gets converted into homocysteine rather than cysteine, maybe that is what Dr. K is talking about??? I would like to learn more about this so I need to definitely do some more reading!

Is there anything that you can do in your work environment to protect yourself from EMF exposure?

What does that mean? How does that happen and how do we correct it?!

Are you drinking wildcaught fish broths? I think that could be a great idea....... But I really think you need to eat more raw as cysteine is sensible to heat

So maybe in the ends it is the work place who is the beast  because the EMF is what makes the malabsorption making you get too little cystein from the beginning. any change you could stay home, or change work?

I think what is so frustrating for me right now SeaHorse, is that I felt like I made a lot of headway with the leaky gut too, but now I seem to be right back where I was 3 years ago and I have no clue what caused the regression. I'm wondering if it could be just the seasonal change. Now that you've asked me about fat to protein, it makes me realize I had just been increasing my fat A LOT due to the cooler temps when all of this broke loose again. I wonder if my leaky gut couldn't handle the extra fat for some reason?!

I still can't tolerate magnesium - no idea why. No one has ever had any clue about this, but every single type of Mg (malate, chelate, citrate, etc, etc.) all cause low back pain for me and I end up with black, tarry stools. Not fun!

I think perimenopause is an issue for me, but my (leaky) gut just doesn't think the overheating is as simple as menopausal hot flashes. I could be wrong though.

### Inger — 2013-11-05T18:45:30+0200

That is what he recommended, medium to high protein. I so get why, because in the protein are the cysteine and other stuff we so need too... ### BJK77 — 2013-11-06T18:27:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-99994>

Finding myself getting more and more depressed lately. I dreaded this happening all summer long. I felt better over the summer than I had in years and I was afraid once I lost the sun it would all be gone . . . and I think it is  I don't know if it's just the change of seasons or if something else is going on, but I feel like I'm right back where I was 3 years ago and I just can't make sense of anything.

My leaky gut is obviously rearing it's ugly head again . . . no idea why. I'm afraid something is up with my liver. Smells (perfumes, chemicals, etc.) are once again making my extremely sick - headache, nauseous and actually leading to a constant sore throat. Why am I unable to detox these things?! I fear I'm not absorbing nutrients like I should be. Two epi-paleo meals a day should have me loaded with goodies, right?!

I'm also assuming what's going on with my right hand/arm is a form of neuropathy. I just can't figure out why it's getting worse now. Could it be tied to my liver/leaky gut issues? It used to be just my hand would get ice cold and tingle when using an electronic, but now it's actually radiating up all the way to my elbow and it lasts for a very long time even after I stop using my mouse/laptop.

I had made so much progress in the last couple of years and I don't want to go backwards now! I'm finding myself so depressed that I just want to up and leave - everybody and everything! This is not like me at all.

### sooperb — 2013-11-06T18:47:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-99997>

Hold on there, all is not lost I'm sure, you maybe need some investigations. It's possible that the problem with your arm is a mechanical one, maybe research it a bit to see if you can tie it in with overuse or a strain. At the moment it's isolated so it could just be something simple that can be fixed. Maybe your feeling down is an anticipatory reaction (is that a word lol?), you expected to feel bad and now you do, a kind of self-fulfilling prophecy? Hang on to how good you were feeling, you can feel good, you did feel good, hey, that feeling is still in there, dig around a little and recover it

Have you added anything new to your diet or have you dropped something, maybe that has had a knock on effect? I don't know what you are supplementing with but I notice you don't eat nuts, maybe your selenium is low, would a couple of brazil nuts a day be too difficult to add in? Cantweight said she has/had the same issue with her mouse hand so you're not looking at some rare new syndrome lol, my mousehand goes cold too, I changed to a track pad, it's a better way for me so maybe you too? Don't let this defeat you, have a go at finding an answer. I'm sure someone else will be along with more suggestions, you aren't alone with this you know.

### cantweight — 2013-11-06T19:12:13+0200

BJ I am struggling along with you...scratching and clawing not to fall apart right now. I really think magnesium is a major culprit and vit A. I cant get my magnesium level up to save my damn life. Have you tried either soaking in it or spraying mag oil on your skin? I am going to try the oil next. I just get crazy loose stools from mag and that is really unhelpful in my attempts to hydrate. So freaking frustrated right now. I am so dry it is uncomfortable my skin is scaly and eyelids are so dry it hurts. My legs seize with cramps, any amount of physical exertion is met with extreme muscle fatigue....Ive even had some fibro like burning in my back....something I never thought I'd see the return of. Ugh. I want to sleep constantly and my sex drive took a nosedive...that was my biggest source of fun so now I feel a little depressed and deflated.

I have been mixing things up trying find that sweet spot again....I made a green smoothie today in a desperate attempt to feel anything better and it just made me feel hungry...grrrr. Sigh....

I have been trying to recall when I felt great and mimic what I was doing at that time. If I stumble upon something wonderful i will let you know....hang in there

### BJK77 — 2013-11-07T15:57:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-100070>

My sleep is finally improving. I'm getting a good 7-8 hours per night and I'm actually dreaming every night! That is so rare for me. I'm attributing the change to wearing my blue blockers more frequently. I still wake warm, but at least I don't wake until 7-8 hours after falling asleep, so I'll take it

This is what I find so frustrating though - I'll see an improvement in one area, but everything else seems to be crashing. I've had trouble for the last year or two with my toes swelling and literally turning dark purple/almost black. Yesterday the toes on my left foot were so swollen and sore it hurt to walk and put any pressure on them. My son accidentally and lightly bumped them last night and it hurt so bad I screamed! I think I can finally correlate this with eating any food that contains a moderate to high amount of oxalates and then going to work! Strange as it sounds - I had a bit of dark chocolate yesterday morning - and by a bit, I'm talking one tiny bite! - and then had to work most of the day in the office. If I eat a tiny bit of dark chocolate and don't go into the office, they might swell and discolor a tiny bit, but nothing like this. This is also the absolute worst it's ever been, so something is definitely going on. I've eaten chocolate before and gone to work and they swelled somewhat, but I can't even begin to describe how bad this was!

I assume it's all tied to the fact that my gut is a mess again and my liver is unable to function properly. That of course leads me back to square one again and asking what changed to make this suddenly come back when my gut was healing so well?! I finally got through the Tim Jackson webinar and sent him a request for info about working with him. He seems to really know his stuff and I need to get a handle on methylation/leaky gut/hormones, etc.

### Flora — 2013-11-07T20:14:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-100111>

Hope you don't mind me chiming in BK77, but have you thought it could possibly be gout? It's caused by a build up of uric acid and would def. tie in with you passing stones as well as having really painful toe joints. Apparently it's more likely as we age and can be caused by eating foods high in purines - which include liver and sardines - and I imagine you probably eat those a fair bit?

I read this link to the UK health service site but I'm sure you'll find lots more on Google. [http://www.nhs.uk/Conditions/gout/Pages/Introduction.aspx](http://www.nhs.uk/Conditions/gout/Pages/Introduction.aspx)

Just a thought - but hope it may help.

I really enjoy your journal and I do feel for you, as it's so frustrating when you're trying to do everything right and you hit a big boulder on your path!

### Shijin13 — 2013-11-07T22:56:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-100124>

BJ

I've been meaning to drop in and give you my perspective on what's going on but it seems Life is getting in my way these days! ughh.

Like you, and a lot of other people, I have great successes followed by a downward spiral. I've just accepted that that's how my body is dealing with everything I'm throwing at it. I'm doing everything I can in this given moment, and I've decided in this moment that's enough, b/c if I give any more I'm going to be stretched so thin - my body's only priority will be pregnenolone steal.

My last set of labs I've got some great improvements, and some really crappy nose dives. but hey I'm still making progress. I too have a very low homeocystine level - and in a consult with Tim Jackson - he pointed me to my CBS defects - [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cystathionine\_beta\_synthase](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cystathionine_beta_synthase) I got a lot of good info out of my consult with Tim and I'm going to be looking in to a few other things again - like parasites (it looks like my little friends might be back), and looking at viruses - that I haven't cleared. So I've got more things to work on... but right now my priority is focusing on Chaos and Havoc and their issues. I'll continue on my journey - but I need to get them better.

Its a challenge - what with Chao's hashi's dx, next set of labs are being run this weekend (but ped endo won't run full labs, just tsh - I did badger him into running TPOs & TABs). And we've reached a point w/Havoc that we have to go have tubes put in b/c he's not clearing the chronic ear infections at all with or without antibiotics - so the docs have just said no more antibiotics for him; add on to the fact his hearing has decreased by 50% since last summer...not a good thing - we don't know if its permeant.

i'm finally out of the Pentagon on a daily basis but sometimes I need to go back in - depending on client needs. It seems there are times I have a good vitamin A cycle, and other times its bad. I think as each of us keeps pulling strings - we find as we fix one thing we knock out another. its a delicate balance trying to get it right and sometimes we all feel like the only thing successful we're doing is running into the same damn brick wall...

but we're not... we'll get there. I think Tim's webinar is going to give us some additional things we can all do to get into optimal health.

hang in there...

### Bonniebelle — 2013-11-07T23:13:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-100127>

Sorry you are having these challenges! I know that is soooooo frustrating when you feel like you are taking a step forward and then taking several steps back! Not sure whether your problem is a mechanical issue related to repetitive motion or related to EMF/Environment....maybe it is a little of both. Could the arm pain be carpal tunnel syndrome? or perhaps it is coming from your neck? (neck pain can cause radiating pain down the arm as well) Have you ever had a neuromuscular massage? It is also sometimes called Trigger Point Therapy. It is a form of deep tissue massage which consists of alternating levels of concentrated pressure on the areas of muscle spasm. The massage therapy pressure is usually applied with the fingers, knuckles, or elbow. Sometimes pressing on one area of the body actually produces a sensation of pain in another site. Sometimes that can give you a handle on where the pain is actually coming from. Not sure if massage therapy would be in your budget, but it could help eliminate toxins, and help with muscle pain.

I had never heard of magnesium oil until finding this site. Sounds interesting, I just heard about it from a sleep post on another site and they recommended Ancient Minerals Magnesium Oil and Lotion. Has anyone tried that before?

I drink dandelion tea for my liver and take liver supplements. I figured that my liver could use a bit of help, don't know if either of those are really necessary though.

You are doing great though with all the changes you have made and following the diet! Don't get discouraged! Just put on your detective hat and try to figure out what may be related to the symptoms you are having. Have you tried icing your arm?

Hang in there!

We may be doing everything perfectly - w/Jacks protocols and anything can take us off the rails. viruses, parasites, stresses (financial, emotional, physical), nutrition (food/supplments), environmental toxins, mold, non-native EMF from our environment. like I told Jason over on facebook:

"Jason's question to Jack is epic, as is Jack's answer. Everyone should read it. My take on JAck's response is that in the athletes its the drastic change in temp during the exertion when warm adapted - the body can't support the quantum tunneling, and the proper unfolding of proteins to leverage the water in the mitochondria to power cellular functions like a superconductor - and the cell is stuck btwn trying to activate cytochrome 2 and bypass cytochrome 1, but b/c it can't its stuck some where "in-between" with the result being an inability to use the krebs cycle - which causes cellular death b/c there's no ATP production AT ALL. For you & Chaos - I think its tied to both Cold and Light. the light signals a seasonal change - impacting your Vitamin A production, and your T4 Conversion to T3 so you can leverage Vit A & T3 to convert LDL to your steroid hormones. the problem comes in as temperature changes and your body attempts to transition to the PPP - it can't b/c you don't have the Progesterone to support entry b/c of dehydration. You also don't have enough Iodine/Iodide to support T4->T3 conversion. You need water, iodine/iodide, & fat to enter ketosis. You need ketosis, and Pg, and structured water to activate the PPP so you can make NADHP+ and bypass Cytochorme 1 and activate cytrochrome 2. **For those of us who are off within the Quantum Health model - we can be perfect in all we do - but one thing can knock us out. Light, Water, Magnetism in balance support sleep/autophagey within the parasympathetic nervous system. Anytime that's off - your guts, cortisol, hormones and thyroid will be off... any stress can knock you out.** for you I think Light is one of your biggest triggers which means - your vitamin A cycle is directly impacted and with that your efforts to reach ketosis are challenged b/c you're body is attempting to tell time/space but can't. which means you're struggling to bypass cytochrome 1 to leverage cytochrome 2 - but can't - so you end up in limbo - just like the athletes in Jack's response....."

Given your experience when you were on vacation - I think Light and EMF are your two biggest drivers - but the next question is what else is off for you? what is making those so pivotal for you. You know what I find really easy is looking at others experiences and slicing through them - but when I look at my situation or Chaos and Havos - I have a harder time - mostly b/c its so personal. I think that sometimes its hardest to look at yourself objectively. At least it is for me. There's so much I want for not only myself health wise but for my family. Trying to balance all of that its very important... and I'm still trying to figure all that out...

I don't think any of us are chasing our tails - I think we're each on our own individual journey - and while that journey isn't going where we want it to go in the time we want it to occur - its exactly what we need - physically, emotionally, mentally and spiritually. we will all get there. I know Jack's told me that I've gone off the rails - a couple of times. and I told him yeah? so what its my journey. My path won't be exactly like his - its mine. Just as your journey is yours. Like the Dr Seuss book "OH, the Places You'll Go!" - we have bang ups and hangups and sometimes end up in the waiting place. But its possible that happens to allow our bodies to recover and prepare for the next assault of healing.... nothing is ever a straight line. its an intricate fascinating beautiful dance - we've forgotten in the hurry up need this NOW environment of the 21st century. Which I think is what Peter talks about in his posts.

You and I will get there. We'll set our children up for success and we'll figure out how to mitigate the crap we're living in. We'll reconnect with nature, we'll reconnect with other people on a level we're desperately seeking. we'll change not only our sphere of influence, but we'll change our world as well. all because we're on this journey.... hang in there...

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-11-11T17:39:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-100429>

If I were your boss, and knew that you had been sick over the last few years ...... then I heard that you were considering quitting your job, that might persuade me to let you work from home fulltime. The rationale is having "most" of you is better than completely losing you.

IMO, you should approach changing your job location like this:

1) spend time meditating over ways you can make a creative offer to your boss so that it is win / win
2) a creative offer might include you working from home full time ... but ... offering to come to the office for important meetings as needed (i.e. several times per week for an hour or so ... or some modification) ...... In reality, many people find that working remote 100% is not good for their career. As has been said in the forum recently, there is no replacement for face to face contact. .... You are creative; you can develop a good proposal .... consider it an interview for a new (and better) job.
3) have your meeting, and offer your proposal. Do not say it unless really necessary, but you should be prepared to give your 2 weeks notice if your proposal is rejected. Your health depends on it.

It is kinda like... a bad circle... bad job... money for treatments... more bad job... more money for treatments... as all the treatments might not even work as the bottom issue is still there...

Is there any change to get another job? Maybe like in a kindergarten, where you can be outside with the kids much every day? There must be some other options... if not... could you just stay home and heal, and live very cheap for a while? Then you could be so much outside everyday, just sleep as much as you like... just relax! So your body could finally heal. In the summer you could tan nude on the grass a lot and stuff like that. And your hubby could work and you could do all the homework of course but that is not too much and you could still have lots of time to concentrate on your healing.

And I think if you have borrelia you might want to go zero carb. Very strict. That is what I would do anyways. No veggies even, nothing. I know about someone who have healed 100% from very serious borrelia doing this. She still eats only beef. She does so well now! She cannot eat anything else at all or she will not feel well but that is ok, their whole family eat only grassfed beef for years and they are crazy healthy! You should try it! I bet seafood would be great too of course, but just no carbs at all.

You know... it is pretty funny but it is actually very nice to not be so attached to food, it is very liberating I think! Especially if that WOE makes you super healthy too!

Oh... and do not ditch CT if it is crazy cold outside, you could roll in the snow? Just have socks and gloves and a hat on! I dip in the river even if it is -20 degree C. I just dip short but I do it.

Okay.. these are just some suggestions IDK if they are way too crazy or weird but they are just what popped into my mind what might help you get better...
I know there is a way BJK77, there always is (((hugs)))

Then you could be so much outside everyday, just sleep as much as you like... just relax! So your body could finally heal. In the summer you could tan nude on the grass a lot and stuff like that. And your hubby could work and you could do all the homework of course but that is not too much and you could still have lots of time to concentrate on your healing.
>
> And I think if you have borrelia you might want to go zero carb. Very strict. That is what I would do anyways. No veggies even, nothing. I know about someone who have healed 100% from very serious borrelia doing this. She still eats only beef. She does so well now! She cannot eat anything else at all or she will not feel well but that is ok, their whole family eat only grassfed beef for years and they are crazy healthy! You should try it! I bet seafood would be great too of course, but just no carbs at all.
>
> You know... it is pretty funny but it is actually very nice to not be so attached to food, it is very liberating I think! Especially if that WOE makes you super healthy too!
>
> Oh... and do not ditch CT if it is crazy cold outside, you could roll in the snow? Just have socks and gloves and a hat on! I dip in the river even if it is -20 degree C. I just dip short but I do it.
> Or just be outside, bundle up and spend time in the daylight outside at least 15 to 30 minutes / day. I think this is huge in winter. We really really need it. But I think you are doing this already AFAI remeber
>
> Okay.. these are just some suggestions IDK if they are way too crazy or weird but they are just what popped into my mind what might help you get better...
> I know there is a way BJK77, there always is (((hugs)))
>

Hi Inger!

I didn't see your response earlier. Thanks for your always encouraging words  I know it sounds like I should just quit my job and I know Jack has said I need to "sh!t or get off the pot," but it's always easy for others to tell someone else what to do!

I'm currently on a medical leave, so I'm going to use the next few months to really try to get myself better and see if I can't then continue to work. If not, well then I'll know for sure. For now, however, it seems to me I'd only be replacing one stressor with another. If I lose my job, I can't afford the dental surgery that I'm told I need to have done, which will be over $10,000!! I'll also lose my health insurance and the insurance for my kids.

I have been looking for other jobs, but literally everything seems to expose the workers to some level of EMF! All the schools around here have wifi. I was offered a job in the cafeteria at my son's school, which of course isn't exactly my dream career or anything, but I would have done it in a heartbeat if it meant less exposure.

Like I said, I am currently on medical leave at least. I only go into the office 4-6 hours a week and we have also moved my office so I'm no longer near the machine shop that Jack said was probably causing my problems.

So, we'll see what happens . . .

### BJK77 — 2014-02-06T23:23:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-108754>

I should update too to say that I saw the Klinghardt-trained practitioner who did ART testing on me and says I am "infested with mold." He claims it's all through my body and of course put me on a ton of supplements. He's sure this will "do the trick." I don't know if I'm quite as convinced.

After reading EE12, I'm a bit concerned about taking more detoxing agents. I've always known I just don't detox well. In that blog, Jack says "Clearing a toxin does you no good if you cannot generate a redox potential." Well, my redox potential sucks! This I know for sure. Problem is I don't know how to fix it. I've been listening to the webinars - and plan to ask Jack some questions despite him thinking I ask too many  I've gathered that I need to do the following to improve my redox potential:

-   Stay ketotic (my diet is totally tight)
-   Get hydrated (I'm working on this. Like an idiot I had been adding Celtic sea salt to ALL of my water. I thought I had asked Jack about this before and he said he did that when going into the OR. I assumed this was a good thing if you were going to be exposed to non-native EMF and went at it full force. BIG MISTAKE. I now know better!)
-   Make sure I'm getting enough cysteine
-   Somehow I need the power of sunlight right now too, which is not easy to do this time of year in the Northeast! Suggestions?!

Those are the four key areas Jack said are tied directly to redox potential in the webinar: water, ketosis, certain amino acids (cysteine) and sunlight. So, I'm going to go full speed ahead with each of them.

Is it too much to think that I can get rid of this mold in my body, do all of the above and then be able to continue with my job? Is that unrealistic? I thought that's what we all were trying to do here - get optimal and live a good life. I'm not being facetious or rhetorical here. I'm really wondering whether I'm being unrealistic. I guess if I need a reality check, in my mind at least, it's that this may not be able to happen in the next few months while I'm on medical leave. I'm guessing I need more like years to accomplish this, huh?!

### BJK77 — 2014-02-21T16:37:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-111547>

Just some rambling thoughts for the day. It helps to put this down so I can look back on it later and if anyone has any input, please chime in!

I've been brainstorming why I crashed so hard when Fall hit this year. This summer I felt the absolute BEST I have in years - still had issues with some foods and a limited diet and some other issues, but my energy really rocked! Sleep was good too. Fast forward to the beginning of Fall and sleep went to hell, energy levels dropped, temperature regulation became a MAJOR issue - before when my thyroid was off I'd be chilled, now I'm almost constantly in a state of having my upper body feel like it is totally burning up and the night sweats are unbearable. I had a few week time period where I had a constant sore throat, a strange pins and needle/sore feeling in the soles of my feet in the mornings, motion sickness, neck stiffness, heart palpitations and I became chronically constipated.

Had Lyme testing done and one lyme MD says it's positive (even though only 3 bands were positive) and another lyme MD says it's not. I did also get a rash on my foot at the end of summer, so I don't know if there's a connection or not. I also had a blood smear done that clearly showed parasites in my red blood cells. The one Lyme MD says that was babesia and it would explain the night sweats.

What I keep coming back to is that my redox potential must have been improving over the summer when I was feeling so good. What happened to make it slide back down so fast once Fall hit? Could the changing seasons be enough of a factor for that to happen? And so severely? I know I never feel AS good over the winter, but I can't remember crashing so hard either since I first got sick.

I kept telling myself the last two years that I was at least improving somewhat, but looking back on it now I'm not sure that I was. Instead, I was cutting more and more out of my diet and life and still getting more and more sensitivities. I guess if the Klinghardt practitioner is right and my body is loaded with mold (which I'm really beginning to believe), that would make sense that I'm just so overloaded with toxins any additional burden is too much for my body. Considering the fact that I cannot seem to detox anything, this "stuff" just builds up and has nowhere to go!

The Klinghardt guy put me on a ton of supplements (lots of binders - clay, chlorella, Pectaclear, some homeopathic drainages and supports and he wants me to take some anti-yeast things that I haven't even been brave enough to start yet). I'm also taking Welchol from the Lyme MD.

I feel like I'm starting to get some of my old symptoms back that I had before we did the mold remediation in the house - air hunger, more ear/throat congestion than normal. So I'm wondering whether all of these binders/detox agents are stirring things up again. I also put out some MMS in the basement as was suggested to me to kill mold spores. I didn't really think it through though and am now wondering if that would have just killed the mold spores and released mycotoxins and that's why these symptoms are getting kicked up again! I've been told though that MMS should get rid of everything. In fact, it's one of the things the Klinghardt guy wants me to take to detox the mycotoxins from my body, so I guess if it removes it from my body it would remove it from the air too, right?

It just seems very coincidental that I got very sick the last two days after putting the MMS out. However, I also only recently added in the Chlorella and Lithium that Tim Jackson suggested too and stopped my coffee enemas . . . so maybe it was just a build up of toxins. I hate not having a clear answer!

Nothing I can do, but keep moving forward. My dream is still to move south. I need the sun. We had hoped to do this through my job, but obviously that's not going to happen now. DH may have the opportunity through his work, but that won't be for another year or so. In the meantime, I need to get through this winter somehow. I'm wondering if I got so much worse because it's been such a damp, snowy winter and it's so hard to get outside or air the house out. Regardless, I'm formulating a plan to figure things out and keep trucking on:

\- I'm going to have the house tested again for mold so I know for sure if it's a problem here or not (Jack always says it's the environment and it may be, but we've had the house gone over twice now for EMF and it comes back very low and now that I'm not at work more than 4-6 hours a week I think it has to be something else)

\- Work on improving my redox potential. I know this is huge. I realize if my RP was decent I would be able to deal with mold or anything else, so this has got to be priority #1. I'm going to go back through the RP blog and look at each item and make a plan for how I can work on the ones that I know suck for me!

\- I'm trying to find a doctor that will follow Ritchie Shoemaker's protocol to detox the mold.

\- Need to consult with Tim Jackson again to find out why I can't detox. Everytime I've tried in the last few years I end up crashing. I honestly can't say I understand all the methylation issues . . . it's just too complicated for my brain right now.

### Josh — 2014-02-21T17:07:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bj%C3%A2%E2%82%AC%E2%84%A2s-roller-coaster-ride.8610/post-111551>

For MMS consider that it is essentially bleach. It will beat you up if your liver is already overloaded. COffee enemas are rough without enough transition metals such as molybdenum and manganese. Sounds as though you may have gotten reinfected at the end of the summer. This is hard to catch when one is already in the thick of long-term stuff. Having practiced radical detox for years with partial results, I now understand that the blessing is in immune regulation. Detox costs a lot of energy and immune regulation requires a good deal of energy so it is a trade-off. One needs to detox enough so that it does not interfere, but the overall energy balance needs to be maintained to have immune regulation/function and congitive function on top of regular physical function. Detox is the easiest to sell because people tend to notice something quickly enough to believe it is worth it. Tonification and strengthening sound good but are much harder in practice and finally balancing is quite elusive. Dr. Kruse is showing me a path to the "simple but not easy" path of health creation and maintenance that I have suspected is possible. The devil is in the detail....
