### QuantumRadical — 2013-11-04T00:10:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-6-the-ancient-pathway.8644/post-99746>

It will be a generation or two before "they all" get it. Even after the earth was discovered to be a sphere and not flat, I am sure the schools back then took a couple of generations to switch from "the earth is flat" to "the earth is round". Too much BIG MONEY is tied up in the status quo. Even smart people will deny reality if it doesn't match their beliefs. And we have been significantly brainwashed by media, marketing, advertising, etc. to believe what we believe about food, medicine, and health. Let's face it, to be healthy just do the opposite of what everybody believes is the truth. Fat makes you fat--so eat more fat and get skinny. Exercise burns calories so you lose weight--exercise just makes you hungrier so you eat more and gain more weight. Cold will make you sick--cold improves your immune system so embrace the cold tub. I could go on and on about what many believe is common sense, but is not. Being contrarian may not make you more friends, but may save your life.

I have been reading this forum and Jack's Blogs for a good 2.5 years. I got it immediately because my nature has always been to challenge/question authority and I have a scientific/technical background. But I also checked out every cite from the Blog's to make sure that Jack's interpretation was on the money. So far, 100%! Most people go with the flow, and would never even think to challenge the expertise of a doctor, or an institution, or even a government. But, that is not how we make progress. Those who challenge are the ones that make progress. But also remember, the explorers are trampled by the pioneers who are subsequently shot in the back by an arrow by the settlers. And so the world goes around.

Ultimately, the laws of physics prevail. We are just starting to realize the implications of what that means from a biology, health, and medicine perspective. Ultimately, those implications will ripple through everything in our lives just as a round earth transformed the world back in the day.

I ramble.

Q'Rad Randy

### caroline — 2013-11-04T00:23:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-6-the-ancient-pathway.8644/post-99748>

Thankyou Lahelada, Nicole, Inger, Martin and Randy for articulating that so beautifully - you are amazing, intuitive, kind people \[ not to mention a whole lot of smarts\] that I am so happy to share space with here and call a friend ....if that is okay....

### caroline — 2013-11-04T00:34:43+0200

Those people wont change until they are on the brink.......and when they get there it maybe too late. Just visit someone with Alzheimer's and spend 5 minutes talking to them........so see what non native EMF is capable of.

The thing is ..... They always say to me - but people are living much longer these days ....and I don't have a good enough response.

I was up close and personal with dementia \[myDH\] that is not something you ever, ever want a loved one to go thru. It is heartbreaking and tears you apart - for ever. To see a strong person slowly lose everything they know and hold dear is not something you ever want to see .......but the odds are stacked against us if we don't change the state of our existence. We really need to fight for our life and the life of our loved ones...if only they would let us.

Someday it will become crystal clear to them - but will it be in time......

We may be living longer but definitely the quality of life is just not there. Like your DH, my dear FIL died of Alzheimer's and it just broke my heart. It took me almost 6 months after he went in the nursing home to see him since my grandfather also died from it. They were both once strong proud farmer who were bed ridden and did not know who anyone was. It is one of the worse diseases EVER. Then when my MIL died on heart failure the SIL took out a huge box pill from her house that kept her alive at one point. And then the SIL is not doing much better. She is just in her early 60's, has had a stroke already and is diabetic and basically disabled. She is great motivation for me to do everything I can to get optimal. Love her dearly but I NEVER want to be like her.

### Inger — 2013-11-04T08:05:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-6-the-ancient-pathway.8644/post-99782>

Yes, the Marino book is it. Everyone should read it.

Most in my family are not able to read that kind of a book in English... sadly. And I think no one in my family is really interested in science..? sadly. But they do believe when I tell them. Their issue is more discipline maybe? .. and that you cannot force on anyone.. So I have come to take it not so much to my heart when they do things different than ideal. I try to help them when I can and I just bought dad and mom Silica and told them to take it with lots of well water.... to not get Alzheimer. My grandma died from it. My daddy especially loves to get all those "health stuffs" lol. I also make them fish for dinner and things like that, once in a while when I have the money. They love fish.
My dad is 66 and my mom 61 and they are both pretty healthy, thankfully! I always encourage them to be outside, get sunshine and light, going to sleep early, no alcohol in the evening etc. I think my parents are that healthy because they never used technology much, never had TV and just recently got cell phones they use not too much. And they both like to be outside too. And they stay away from doctors... never eat medicine... they do not even take antibiotics when they "should"....lol (like when doing dental work / having infections and dentist or doc says they must take.. they just are cautious and let it heal itself)

I really am learning to be more relaxed with the whole thing, you can only do as good as you can, and I am sure even the small changes and things they do for health will make a difference!  I think we can make a lot for our loved ones / family and they do not even recognize  just one small thing like fish for dinner more often will help, I am confident. And I guess as long as I am not close to optimal yet I have more than enough work to do with me  (I need to remind myself of this every so often)

# Thread: Changing just for me for life
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/changing-just-for-me-for-life.8646/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Justfourme — 2013-11-04T02:47:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/changing-just-for-me-for-life.8646/post-99760>

I've planned on starting a journal for months and have heard so many recommend journaling, now is the time. The best place to start in any story is the beginning, but first a few basics. 53 yo female, wife and mother, LR, overweight and menopausal.
Born, raised, and living in the Midwest (USAHeartland). For the first 18 years if my life, I lived on a farm where we raised our own beef cattle (mainly grass fed), pigs, chickens (not caged), milk cows, and had a fish stocked pond. We also had a garden that was probably an acre in size and canned or froze just about every type of veggie and fruit. No fertilizer other than what the animals provided! We were outside rain, sun, snow or sleet doing chores or just playing in the sun. We had raw milk straight from the barn - although I've never been a big milk drinker. Most of my carbs came from veggies and fruit. We made everything from ingredients and didn't really eat anything that came from a box. I was probably 16 before I even tasted pizza! We didn't have much TV time as that was reserved for when after all the chores were done. (Yeah, I agree that was pretty sweet). I was typically tan, fit, and never overweight for my height.

Age 18 came with moving, getting a job, getting a loan for college. Married at 19 and a mom at age 20. Never got back down to pre pregnancy weight but was around 10 lbs over that. Last baby was breast fed for a couple months. Most of my 30's and 40's I was eating SAD and my weight stayed between normal to +30.
Tried WW (about 4 times) and even got my lifetime membership status, then gained it back. Tried exercise, weight came off, then back. Had my gallbladder removed. Next we'll fast forward to 2010.

### Justfourme — 2013-11-04T03:14:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/changing-just-for-me-for-life.8646/post-99763>

Fast forward - peri menopausal, overweight, overworked, stressed and bad diet. I gave up soda. But had terrible cravings and replaced soda with chocolate. Fatigue, low energy, some migraines. Weight kept creeping up and nothing would allow weight loss. Everything that I had tried before, now would not budge the scale. I was miserable and decided to try this diet I had never heard of before. That was the beginning of my HCG journey. It was like a miracle! When I was about 15 lbs from goal weight, I started hearing about this new thing called leptin, found Dr. K. And this forum. About 3 months after meeting my goal, I started eating paleo, did the BAB, Leptin reset, avoided night shades. I was feeling the best I had in decades.
Now let's add in some stress - two divorces of very close peeps, lost my job, mom passed away all w/in a couple months. Still felt good and was maintaining. Tried to do CT and just couldn't stand the cold. (Later found out from the wise Dr K that I needed to get labs and get hormones and thyroid fixed). Yes that's right - get your labs and hormones fixed first. I found a wonderful doc and started to get labs.
Add in a new job and more stress, official menopause, and the new job is in a building full of EMF. First 6 mo of new job = + 10 lbs, ditto second six months. Double the work load and I'm at my desk 12 hrs per day. Next 6 mo, 20 lb gain, ditto for the next 6 mo.
Still fixing my low thyroid using supplements, started BHRT, and fixing my low cortisol.
Posting labs next...

### caroline — 2013-11-04T03:15:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/changing-just-for-me-for-life.8646/post-99764>

Hi Justfourme and Welcome!....

I am so happy to see you starting a journal.... Dr. Kruse likes journals so he can get some context. He has no chance of answering any of your questions unless he knows something about you!
He said the other day ..... it is like wanting a mechanic to fix your car - but you leave the car at home!

Also - if we don't ask questions .... he doesn't know what we don't know! This is some pretty tough science stuff for some of us non- geek people. I have to keep reading things over and over and over and that is what he expects us to do - if that is what it takes! My brain is in pain all the time these days .... but I love it .....I love trying to connect the dots. It does get easier and sometimes you will read a blog that you read a year ago ....and the lights will finally go on

Please go to the home page and read the easy start guides- they are excellent.

Have you read Dr. K's book - it is a great way to get to know him and what he is all about and trying to do with his blog and this forum. It is available in Kindle or paper. It has a really good section on labs etc.

Also - listen to his podcasts - they are a quick way to get information and he is easy to listen to.

If you can manage it ..... become a gold member .... you will then have access to all the webinars at anytime that is convenient for you and there is a follow up live Q&A a few days later and you can ask a question on anything - He often goes for a couple of hours - one went for more than 4 hours!

There is a "fix your hormone" bootcamp starting tomorrow with Dr. Tim Jackson. He is one of the good guys! This would be invaluable for you as you are entering the mine field of menopause. You can listen to a free community wide webinar that Dr. Jackson just did a week or so ago. Please do listen to that.

### caroline — 2013-11-04T03:28:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/changing-just-for-me-for-life.8646/post-99765>

Oh - I didn't see you hadn't finished yet!! Please post your labs in the lab section - They might not get seen here in your journal.
Go to the top of this page and click on forum and go down about 7 categories - I think!

How did your pregnancies go? that is a big tell for Dr. K. too.

It sounds like you have a lot of this figured out already - good for you!

Light cycles are really, really important .... as is grounding. You need to rise and set with the sun! Do you have blue blocker glasses?

Non native EMF is hugely important. Can you turn the breaker off to your bedroom? get rid of your TV and alarm clock and anything else that emit EMF where you sleep. Do you have a smart meter?

### Inger — 2013-11-04T09:02:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/changing-just-for-me-for-life.8646/post-99787>

Welcome to journal land Justfourme

Sounds too bad, your job... could you look for another kind of job?

### Justfourme — 2014-02-12T00:11:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/changing-just-for-me-for-life.8646/post-109702>

Help please...heart issues...I normally just read a lot vs posting much, but I really need your input. I mainly follow the paleo diet and have intentions of moving more toward the epi-paleo. Since I do most of the cooking, my husband more or less follows my diet with the exception of some occasional bread. I also try to stay away from all processed foods, dyes, chemicals, preservatives, try to eat local and organic, GMO and gluten free. I also am opposed to using statins. Both of us were "prescription free".

He had a heart attach last week. 100% blockage of the RCA, cardiac cath, angioplasty with one stent.
A few stats: age 53 male, non smoker, BMI of 31, total cholesterol 200, LDL 131, HDL 48, trig 105. BP around 120/78.
He doesn't eat as much of the healthy fats as I do on a daily basis.
So now we are trying to navigate thru the throngs of cardiac rehab and dietitians -who of course are pushing the normal food pyramid, wanting to eat lots of grains, eliminate fats, butter, coconut oil.
They are very concerned where we are getting our fiber from...uh...fresh veggies? Fruit? No breads??
I did get a small concession on their part on omega 3's. They want breads, grains added for fiber.

They also automatically put him on a statin to get the LDL lower. Thank goodness I already had him on co q 10. They have agreed to discuss the possibility of reducing the statin in 6 mo and seeing if he can eventually go off of it.

So here am I, the bad guy who wants to give her spouse healthy fats, no grains and no statins.

Advice on how to navigate thru this mess?

### Justfourme — 2014-06-19T04:08:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/changing-just-for-me-for-life.8646/post-131340>

Test, test and more tests. Have you ever read things on the forums and thought "well, I don't have that"? Guess what - tests reveal leaky gut and problems with methylation. So in addition to no gluten, add no dairy, eggs and most beans to the list of foods to avoid. Eggs were probably the hardest to deal with. Hardly had much dairy, except for some cheese, and didn't eat many beans before so those weren't very hard to give up.
Brain fog, fatigue and choc/carb cravings are now gone. Thyroid function is back up. Eating more seafood, leafy greens, And taking glutathione and methyl B's, Resveratrol and probiotics.
Going back to re-read all the blogs for leaky gut.

Now if someone could point me in the right direction to tell when the leaky gut is healed...

### Josh — 2014-06-19T04:31:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/changing-just-for-me-for-life.8646/post-131350>

> [Justfourme said:](/goto/post?id=131340)
>
> Test, test and more tests. Have you ever read things on the forums and thought "well, I don't have that"? Guess what - tests reveal leaky gut and problems with methylation. So in addition to no gluten, add no dairy, eggs and most beans to the list of foods to avoid. Eggs were probably the hardest to deal with. Hardly had much dairy, except for some cheese, and didn't eat many beans before so those weren't very hard to give up.
> Brain fog, fatigue and choc/carb cravings are now gone. Thyroid function is back up. Eating more seafood, leafy greens, And taking glutathione and methyl B's, Resveratrol and probiotics.
> Going back to re-read all the blogs for leaky gut.
>
> Now if someone could point me in the right direction to tell when the leaky gut is healed...

Well "repair" reduces and or ends the persistent chronic version. Our intestines are designed to leak to gather DNA and any stressor that overwhelms mucosal immunity which is redox dependent will open up the guts and possibly the brain anytime for some period of time. Think about a rash on the skin....

### Inger — 2013-11-04T08:38:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-you-think-ct-and-movnat-dont-have-history.8648/post-99785>

Haha... this video made me smile! Looked like so much fun  Hubs was a great boxer in his youth in Estonia (back then it was Russland BTW) and their training looked just like in the video, from what he has told me! Must have helped some, because he does has a very strong body at 54 yo even if he smokes and drinks now....

My mom has told me as she was young and went to school in northern Norway they always had to walk or ski to school, in any weather! It was pretty far too! And he told me in winter in much snow and storm they was skiing and it was so hard that when she arrived at school she was bright red in her face...lol

Imagine what difference from today's kids that are driven by car everywhere...... Just 30 years ago when I was a kid we had to walk 4,5 km (one way) to school and back every single day, independent of weather too! Take this with the heavily increased use of technology... no wonder illness is increasing with frightening speed

It was just plain instinct....and life affirming and beautiful to watch and we followed suit...

### Benny Hammond — 2017-08-07T20:09:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-you-think-ct-and-movnat-dont-have-history.8648/post-221099>

Hey Gang, I want to get your opinion on my routine. I have been doing CT for 28 minutes at 43-45f around 3pm and then riding my bike up to this park to do a series of exercises in the MovNat style - sprints, push-ups, pull-ups from a tree branch, dips, deep body squats, and then I get creative carrying logs over head doing split squats and all sort of other movements. Then I ride back and wait an hour then do CT again the same way. I like to wait an hour before doing the second CT session based on what I read from Dr. Rhonda Patrick about allowing the muscle autophagy response to signal before hitting the cold. I feel like get more power and more reps when I do the CT before my workout circuits. What do you guys think of this routine? It feels really good!

# Thread: Where to start on educating people on the dangers of non native EMFs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-start-on-educating-people-on-the-dangers-of-non-native-emfs.8649/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PaulG — 2013-11-04T05:15:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-start-on-educating-people-on-the-dangers-of-non-native-emfs.8649/post-99774>

Someone I know has asked the question can you get sick from electricity / EMFs?

To be honest I am at a loss as to know where to start?

1) Verbal arguments. Its hard to convince someone with words without coming across as a mad person (which is generally how I am perceived in my family as they cant be bothered to research).

2) Link to websites - trouble with this is that you can find plenty of people saying there is no evidence etc. Some websites such as Jack Kruse might be too advanced for a teenager or someone first considering the concept.

3) Appeal to authority - what organisations are coming out against mobile phones/towers, WiFi? The World Health Organisation

I sure people can relate to this situation so would be interested in approaches and resources to help bring enlightenment.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-11-04T05:48:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/where-to-start-on-educating-people-on-the-dangers-of-non-native-emfs.8649/post-99775>

Very Simple........have them read Andrew Marino's treatise on his and Becker's work and all the testimony they gave in the book "Going Somewhere." I have yet to see one person who has read it come away not getting it.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blocker-prescription-glasses.8650/post-99784>

Has any one got blueblocker prescription glasses? I have to get new glasses so want to get this.

I was pretty confused....

do I just want a yellow tint? antifatigue? U.V. guard?

there are some new lenses called "blue control" they apparently have a bit of a blue tint and are for use with computers, ipads, iphone etc. The optometrist seemed to think they are a gimmick.

He isn't so sure about all of this - but is lovely - and will go with what I want ....but...what do I want???
I just asked him to pretend that he does agree with me!!

any help would be greatly appreciated..... thanx

### Ralef — 2013-11-04T21:13:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blocker-prescription-glasses.8650/post-99825>

i have blue blocker glasses from the optometrist. Usually they are used to help with retina diseases, if you have such a disease they are prescribed here in germany. I had paid it on my own. I'm wearing them at the computer at work and on my way home when its dark after sunset. i paid 600 € for them. manufactorers for the glasses are Multilens, Zeiss, Rodenstock and Essilor.

@caroline: i think the glasses are from essilor, i must search for the bill. The glasses work very well for me. First day it was strange and i have to get used to it, but after that i feel much better when i use them, especally because i have to use the computer much at work. Co-Workers finding it strange at first, but get used to it fast too. My optometrist is specialized on that glasses for eye diseases. Blue Blocking for prevention was new to him, but he listened and finds it interesting. the glasses are in a middle dark orange, i choose a good grade of blue blocking. They aren't t really dark like sunglasse. they are blocking the relevant range of blue lights.

Here is the website of my optometrist: [http://www.optikbaart.de/](http://www.optikbaart.de/). It is in german language. Look for "Kantenfilter", perhaps the site is helping you? Or look at the manufacturers site, i think they have information in english.

Sure you can ask more questions, i check internet sometimes daily, sometimes every other day.

### freesia — 2013-11-05T19:08:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blocker-prescription-glasses.8650/post-99953>

No you don't need to know what time zone he's in - you just need the **start time** in the zone specified \- and the **current time**. Then, the difference is how many hours you have before it starts.

### Fish — 2014-12-02T05:21:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-blocker-prescription-glasses.8650/post-148990>

Since a mother works in IT, I can’t ignore my eye problems. I have to stay in the front of computer for more than 8 hours, and also feed my baby girl. After I checked the danger of blue light for human health ([http://www.taimeopt.com/dangers-of-blue-light-with-human-health/](http://www.taimeopt.com/dangers-of-blue-light-with-human-health/)), I chose T'aime Dinodon eyewear to block blue light when staring at a computer. It states to block 97% of blue light. Actually I haven’t seen the whole test process, I just refer to a video which uses a spectrometer to test how the glasses work to filter the blue light from a computer screen.

I really enjoy it. As for the anti-fatigue and UV guard you talked about, Dinodon is more than enough. Just hope it helps.

# Thread: EMF noise from LED lamps shuts down airport-to-airplane communication near airport
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-noise-from-led-lamps-shuts-down-airport-to-airplane-communication-near-airport.8651/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Optimalbound — 2013-11-04T14:40:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-noise-from-led-lamps-shuts-down-airport-to-airplane-communication-near-airport.8651/post-99794>

**EMF noise from LED lamps shuts down airport-to-airplane communication**

A building in the flight path to and from VÃ¦rnes airport, Norway, was equipped with 13,500 LED lamps (with switching power supplies, no doubt) and generated so much EMF noise that it shut down the communication between airplanes and the airport.

[http://translate.google.com/transla...tp://www.adressa.no/kultur/article8555323.ece](http://translate.google.com/translate?sl=no&tl=en&js=n&prev=_t&hl=no&ie=UTF-8&u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.adressa.no%2Fkultur%2Farticle8555323.ece) (there's a video there, also)

[http://translate.google.com/transla...ressa.no/nyheter/trondheim/article8561581.ece](http://translate.google.com/translate?hl=no&sl=no&tl=en&u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.adressa.no%2Fnyheter%2Ftrondheim%2Farticle8561581.ece)

Nothing in the newspaper story about health issues, but I suspect it would be "interesting" to track the labs of the poor people working in that building.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-11-04T15:24:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-noise-from-led-lamps-shuts-down-airport-to-airplane-communication-near-airport.8651/post-99795>

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-11-04T15:44:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-noise-from-led-lamps-shuts-down-airport-to-airplane-communication-near-airport.8651/post-99796>

# Thread: High AST/ALT

**Subforum:** Optimal Labs

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### enyaw — 2013-11-04T20:46:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-ast-alt.8653/post-99823>

These are my wife's labs. She's worried about the high ALT/AST. I'm concerned with her BUN:Creatinine ratio; it's approaching 20. Dehydration?

### Bonniebelle — 2013-11-04T21:33:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/high-ast-alt.8653/post-99829>

I'm sure someone else can probably answer this question better, but I will give you my feedback.

**About elevated liver enzymes:**

I know they are part of the liver function test. I remember having elevated levels at one time when I was working with a personal trainer and had really increased my protein intake. I had been to the gym right before having my labs drawn. My doctor seemed to think it wasn't anything to be alarmed about and the next time we checked my levels were normal. Other than that I don't remember specifically what each means so I had to google it. Looks like AST is associated with muscles as well as the liver so if your wife really increased exercise or protein intake maybe that is the reason. But I would certainly check with your wife's doctor to rule out any other issues. My mother's liver enzymes were very elevated and that is what led to the discovery that she had pancreatic cancer. The elevated levels led to a sonogram, then CT scan and they found the pancreatic mass.

What do high (elevated) liver tests (AST and ALT) mean?

AST (SGOT) and ALT (SGPT) are sensitive indicators of liver damage or injury from different types of diseases or conditions. But it must be emphasized that higher-than-normal levels of these liver enzymes should not be automatically equated with liver disease. They may mean liver problems or they may not. For example, elevations of these enzymes can occur with muscle damage. The interpretation of elevated AST and ALT results depends upon the entire clinical evaluation of an individual, and so it is best done by physicians experienced in evaluating liver disease and muscle disease.

Moreover, the precise levels of these liver enzyme test do not correlate well with the extent of liver problems or the prognosis (outlook). Thus, the exact levels of AST (SGOT) and ALT (SGPT) cannot be used to determine the degree of liver disease or predict the future.

[http://www.medicinenet.com/liver\_blood\_tests/page3.htm](http://www.medicinenet.com/liver_blood_tests/page3.htm)

Elevated liver enzymes may indicate inflammation or damage to cells in the liver. Inflamed or injured liver cells leak higher than normal amounts of certain chemicals, including liver enzymes, into the bloodstream, which can result in elevated liver enzymes on blood tests.

The specific elevated liver enzymes most commonly found are:

Alanine transaminase (ALT)
Aspartate transaminase (AST)
Elevated liver enzymes may be discovered during routine blood testing. In most cases, liver enzyme levels are only mildly and temporarily elevated. Most of the time, elevated liver enzymes don't signal a chronic, serious liver problem.

[http://www.mayoclinic.com/health/elevated-liver-enzymes/my00508](http://www.mayoclinic.com/health/elevated-liver-enzymes/my00508)

**About BUN/Creatinine ration:**

Yes, I remember Dr. Kruse saying that a high BUN/Creatinine ratio meant dehydration.

I found this calculator for BUN/Creatinine ration because some of my labs did not have the ratio listed.

[http://www.miniwebtool.com/bun-to-creatinine-ratio-calculator/](http://www.miniwebtool.com/bun-to-creatinine-ratio-calculator/)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paying-it-forward.8655/post-99863>

Today I learned of how paying it forward can have great benefits .... if a person is ready. This story should give hope to members that get frustrated by family and friends that just don't "get it".

Awhile back I had a conversation with a relatively young guy who was scheduled to have hip replacement surgery. He said that years of playing sports had resulted in a bone on bone issue. I barely knew this guy, but had been introduced to him by one of his employees.

I shared a wide variety of JK ideas with him .... inflammation, diet, CT, EMF, etc. He seemed interested. As the conversation was ending, I encouraged him to "question everything" and get a 2nd opinion. I emailed him the JK website and included a link to Pre-Op blog.

Today I saw him, and he said he was very glad that we had the conversation. He got a 2nd opinion, and was told of a protocol, if followed to the letter, would allow him to postpone the hip replacement surgery for 5-6 years. Needless to say that made him happy.

Then, I challenged him again. I mentioned that hip replacements are typically transitional metals, and that he would be become an EMF antennae. I said why not try to make the 5 years into 10 years or more (or never).

Bottom line ..... we never know who is ready for a new viewpoint .... until we share it. This success story truly made my day!!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-11-05T17:36:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/proof-of-biologic-coherence-and-macroscopic-human-qed-in-tissues.8668/post-99945>

The apparent regularity of the heartbeat conceals abundant variability, and contrary to common intuition, the more healthy the heart, the more variable the beat.
In 1993, Eugene Stanley and Ary Goldberger, and their collaborators at Boston University and Harvard Medical School in the United States published a paper showing that while the heartbeat records from healthy people look superficially very variable, there is a longtime correlation in the intervals between beats in the healthy heart. For example, shorter intervals tend to be followed by longer ones, and the effect occurs on a wide range of time scales, from seconds to many hours. In contrast, heartbeat records from heart disease patients look superficially much more regular, but are significantly less correlated when analysed mathematically.

In other words, there was less dynamic order in the heart rhythm of those afflicted with heart disease. Since then, there have been numerous studies aimed at extracting the mathematical structures that characterise the rhythm of the healthy heartbeat, in order to clearly distinguish it from those of people with heart disease or at risk from heart disease.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-11-06T02:02:45+0200

### Jack Kruse — 2013-11-08T03:34:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/proof-of-biologic-coherence-and-macroscopic-human-qed-in-tissues.8668/post-100161>

Fish oils prevent AF recurrence following PVI (pulmonary vein isolation)
You might not know that atrial fibrillation is a common sequel to coronary artery bypass grafting (CABG). In 2005 a team of Italian researchers reported that supplementation with 2 grams/day of fish oil (EPA + DHA) for 5 days prior to CABG reduced the incidence of postoperative AF from 33.3% (control group) to 15.2% (fish oil group). Now a team of researchers from St. David’s Medical Center in Austin, TX, Northeastern College of Medicine in Canton, OH, IKEM in the Czech Republic, and McMaster University in Hamilton, Canada reports that fish oil supplementation before and after a pulmonary vein isolation (PVI) procedure reduces the risk of AF recurrence.

Their retrospective study included 1500 afibbers who underwent a pulmonary vein antrum isolation (PVAI) procedure at St. David’s between 2004 and 2007. Two hundred and eighty-five patients (19%) had supplemented with a minimum of 655 mg of fish oil (in capsules) for at least a month prior to their PVAI and continued to do so during the entire follow-up period. It was possible to closely match 129 of these patients (70% paroxysmal and 30% persistent or permanent) with 129 patients (control group) from the remaining 1215 patients who had not taken fish oil. The two groups were matched for type of afib, age, gender, left atrial size, ejection fraction and the presence of type 2 diabetes, hypertension, coronary artery disease and stroke. The two groups, fish oil group and control group, were scheduled for follow-up (including 48-hr Holter monitoring) 3, 6, 9 and 12 months after the procedure and were equipped with a cardiac event monitor during the first 5 months following their PVAI.

During the blanking period (8 weeks following the procedure) 27% of patients in the fish oil group and 44% in the control group experienced afib recurrence; in other words, fish oil supplementation reduced the risk of early recurrence by 39%. Procedural failure (afib recurring more than 8 weeks from the PVAI) was 23% in the fish oil group and 32% in the control group, or a risk reduction of 28%. The research team also noted that the fish oil group had a significantly lower pre-procedure level of C-reactive protein (CRP) than did the control group (2.1 mg/L vs. 3.0 mg/L). CRP, an important marker of inflammation, measured 48 hours after the procedure was also lower in the fish oil group (14.3 mg/L vs. 18.0 mg/L). Complication rates were similar in the two study groups.

The research team concludes that patients treated with long-chain omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids (fish oils) prior to and following a PVAI experienced a lower incidence of early recurrence and procedural failure compared to untreated patients.
Shaheen, PD, et al. Omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acid supplementation reduced atrial fibrillation recurrence after pulmonary vein antrum isolation. Indian Pacing and Electrophysiology Journal, Vol. 9, No. 6, November 1, 2009, pp. 292-98

### Jack Kruse — 2013-11-08T03:46:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/proof-of-biologic-coherence-and-macroscopic-human-qed-in-tissues.8668/post-100162>

### Jack Kruse — 2013-11-08T03:49:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/proof-of-biologic-coherence-and-macroscopic-human-qed-in-tissues.8668/post-100163>

Progression of paroxysmal AF ( a story of collagen)
Paroxysmal (intermittent, self-terminating) atrial fibrillation may over time progress to persistent or permanent afib (episodes lasting 7 days or longer). It is not clear why some paroxysmal afibbers progress to the persistent variety, while other remain paroxysmal for decades. A group of American and Italian researchers now provide at least a partial answer to this question.

Their study involved 330 patients with a history of paroxysmal AF (mean age of 70 years, 61% male) who had had a pacemaker implanted to deal with bradycardia (slow heart beat). Most study participants had underlying heart disease, but 21% were lone afibbers. The pacemaker (Medtronic AT501) automatically recorded the daily burden (duration) of afib and tachycardia for an average of 400 days. After a mean interval of 147 days, 24% of the patients progressed to persistent afib. The researchers made the following interesting observations.

The prevalence of lone atrial fibrillation (LAF) did not differ between the group that remained in paroxysmal afib and the one that progressed to persistent afib.
Patients with congestive heart failure were significantly more likely to progress to persistent AF.
Patients destined to progress to persistent AF experienced a higher daily afib burden and a higher probability of experiencing afib on any given day than those in the paroxysmal group.
The mean daily afib burden in the group destined for progression to persistent AF increased by about 14 seconds/day, while it stayed relatively constant in the group that remained paroxysmal.
Lone afibbers experienced significantly more PACs (premature atrial beats, ectopics) than did patients with CVD. However, the incidence of these ectopics decreased over time.
The conversion to persistent afib occurred suddenly and was often preceded by a period of normal sinus rhythm.
It is possible that treatment with ACE inhibitors or angiotensin receptor blockers (ARBs) may slow down the remodeling that underlies progression to persistent AF.
The researchers conclude that, “Our results suggest that functional electrical remodeling may not impact all patients or inevitably lead to increasing AT/AF burden and persistent AF. In fact, a large proportion of patients may not increase their AT/AF burden, particularly in the absence of CVD.”
Saksena, S, et al. Progression of paroxysmal atrial fibrillation to persistent atrial fibrillation in patients with bradyarrhythmias. American Heart Journal, Vol. 154, November 2007, pp. 884-92

The mean daily afib burden in the group destined for progression to persistent AF increased by about 14 seconds/day, while it stayed relatively constant in the group that remained paroxysmal.
Lone afibbers experienced significantly more PACs (premature atrial beats, ectopics) than did patients with CVD. However, the incidence of these ectopics decreased over time.
The conversion to persistent afib occurred suddenly and was often preceded by a period of normal sinus rhythm.
It is possible that treatment with ACE inhibitors or angiotensin receptor blockers (ARBs) may slow down the remodeling that underlies progression to persistent AF.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-11-08T04:45:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/proof-of-biologic-coherence-and-macroscopic-human-qed-in-tissues.8668/post-100169>

Ok........so you got a big lesson on collagen.........what happens when collagen loses energy or increases its entropy? It forms a scar.......we call that fibrosis.

Role of fibrosis in atrial fibrillation
According to the late Professor Philippe Coumel, three conditions must be met in order for atrial fibrillation to occur:

The myocardium (heart tissue) must be capable of being triggered into and sustaining an episode. In other words, it must provide an electrophysiological substrate that is suitable for AF initiation and maintenance.
1\. The autonomic nervous system must be out of balance. (parasympathetic vs sympathetic)
2\. A trigger or precipitating event capable of initiating an episode must be present.

Researchers at the Montreal Heart Institute believe that cardiac fibrosis (formation of _scar tissue_ in response to injury) is an important feature in the development of an “a-fib friendly” substrate. Tissue fibrosis results from an accumulation of fibrillar collagen deposits which themselves are formed in a repair process aimed at replacing degenerating myocardial tissue. Fibrosis is associated with aging, dilated cardiomyopathy, mitral valve disease, and possibly myocardial ischemia (angina).

However, fibrosis and increased collagen deposition have also been observed in lone a-fibbers. Fibrosis interferes with the normal progression of the sine wave from the sinoatrial node to the atrioventricular node by impairing the transfer of the impulse from myocyte (heart muscle cell) to myocyte. The researchers point out that the renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system (RAAS) is _involved in the formation of myocardial fibrosis_ and that patients with _primary hyperaldosteronism_ (Conn’s syndrome) have a significantly increased incidence of atrial fibrillation. They also point out that locally produced angiotensin II is associated with the formation of collagen deposits and fibrosis. Angiotensis II decreases the side chains on collagen ability to bond water by diffraction and MRI studies.

Mechanical stretch of cardiac muscle fibers induces collagen synthesis and increased angiotensin II production, thus creating structural remodeling that further promotes a-fib ( it means AF begets AF).
The researchers reason that, if the production of collagen deposits and fibrosis could be slowed or even reversed, then it may be possible to eliminate or at least control AF. They suggest that ACE inhibitors (lisinopril, enalapril, ramipril), angiotensin II type 1 receptor blockers (valsartan, irbesartan, losartan), and aldosterone antagonists (spironolactone, eplerenone, canrenone) may be useful in preventing fibrosis and thus denying the atria the electrophysiological substrate necessary for initiating and sustaining an AF episode.
Burstein, B and Nattel, S. Atrial fibrosis: mechanisms and clinical relevance in atrial fibrillation. Journal of the American College of Cardiology, Vol. 51, No. 8, February 26, 2008, pp. 802-09

Light at night and heart disease links are strong! This was just published this today in JAMA Network Open.

Using 13 million hours of personal light exposure data in >88,000 people, they found that those with the brightest nights had significantly higher risks of developing coronary artery disease, myocardial infarction, heart failure, **atrial fibrillation,** and stroke.

These associations were robust after adjusting for established cardiovascular risk factors, including physical activity, smoking, alcohol, diet, sleep duration, socioeconomic status, and polygenic risk. Keep your homes dark at night and get in the sun for photorepair of your arteries and heart!

[https://jamanetwork.com/journals/jamanetworkopen/fullarticle/2840489](https://jamanetwork.com/journals/jamanetworkopen/fullarticle/2840489)

How long do the ice packs stay optimal out in the sun on a hot day?

Could I potentially throw these guys in a cooler while I'm at work for a few hours and then pull them out and get after it?

I'm cold adapted as I CT in 40f chest freezer water for 40-50min daily. Will the CFB and CGB work for me even if I don't shiver?

Thanks, these look like very versatile and functional products.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-via-the-cool-fat-burner-vest-increases-adiponectin-by-62.8672/post-224861>

> [JoeT said:](/goto/post?id=223355)
>
> Eric, I'm looking to try to use cold to get my blood sugar, which is "normal", down to very low levels. (i.e: HBA1C below 4.7 ish? ). Do you have any idea as to whether the vest might be able to help me vs having to sit in a CT tub? Thx.

Back when I was constantly monitoring and measuring this stuff, my resting BGL was in the high 60's or low 70's. Even if I'd do one of my crazy refeeds, the BGL wouldn't go too high, and would return to normal within a few hrs, max. Granted, I was strategically doing those cheat meals, along w/ CT and exercise.

Regardless of the method (tub, vest, sparse clothing in cold environment, etc.), the changes to insulin sensitivity, glucose uptake, BGL and so on, appear to pretty constant per intensity level (non-shivering, pre-shivering, various levels of shivering), regardless of the method used to induce the particular level of cold.

# Thread: New labs option: Blueprint for Wellness
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-labs-option-blueprint-for-wellness.8677/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### scottconnerly — 2013-11-06T00:17:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-labs-option-blueprint-for-wellness.8677/post-99971>

I'd like to offer up another possible source for personal laboratory testing: Quest Diagnostics has started offering [direct pay personal lab tests](https://my.blueprintforwellness.com/direct/) nationwide.

I think the best test for us would be their Heart and Diabetes Screen. It includes

-   Total cholesterol, High Density Lipoprotein Cholesterol (HDL), calculated Low Density Lipoprotein Cholesterol (LDL), Triglycerides, calculated Cholesterol/HDL ratio
-   Diabetes (Pancreas): Glucose
-   Blood pressure
-   Body Mass Index (BMI)
-   Waist Circumference

If anybody tries them out, let me know how it goes, how it compares to the other labs services.

Low creatinine is a lab sign of decreased muscle mass (i.e. being under weight)

Cortisol levels high out of range = lots of stress ...... number one cause is your workouts

DHEA level is very low .... the rest of your hormone panels probably are horrible

If you did the HS-CRP test, I suspect it would show your inflammation is through the roof.

Your symptoms and labs do not bode for a healthy future .... when you will make the required changes???

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-stage-of-adrenal-fatigue-i-feel-like-its-2-or-3-neurotransmitters.8678/post-99992>

Megan - I use the following to help me understand the steroidal hormone chain. [http://fdnmanager.com/steroidal\_hormone\_balance.pdf](http://fdnmanager.com/steroidal_hormone_balance.pdf)

Jansz really hits it on the head. If you want to stop feeling fatigued - you're going to have to cut back on your exercise until your body heals. Everything you're doing exercise wise is nullifying all the efforts of following the LRrx, Adrenal Rx and eating epipaleo.

Many have told you this before... I think you need to really sit down - and identify what your goals are. Do you want to be an elite endurance athlete - with serious health issue? if so, keep doing what you're doing. Or Do you want to get your health back? If you want to get your health back, you might need to put your athletic goals to the side until you get your health back.. once its back, then you will probably want to slowly re-enter your training - and monitor closely w/labs - probably on a monthly basis to determine if you're sending your body back to where it was before. The reality is it will probably take a while to right this course. you not only have to deal with the stress of EMF and your environment, but the additional stress you're putting on your body through the excessive endurance training.

As Jack has said - the truth sucks - The question is you've got two options, which one are you going to choose? Stay the course of your current effort - and continue feeling fatigued and having issues or change the course and focus on healing yourself...

DHEA 69.8: 6am-9pm..... Reference Range 23.8-291.1

Get blood test for
DHEAs,serum

You may be low, take DHEA pills, aim at
Women 275-400 μg/dL
Men 350-490 μg/dL

I do not know your age. Do not be misled by laboratory range for DHEAs that is age adjusted.
If you are low on DHEAs, 50-100mg/day supplementation is not out of question for women, much more for men.
\--------------------------------
On day 2-4 of your menstrual cycle, get blood test for:
pregnenolone
progesterone

You must have at the minimum progesterone=1.5ng/mL or real close to it.
Otherwise, if pregnenolone is low, supplement with (Pregnenolone Micronized Lipid Matrix from Nutricology).
\-------------------------------

Get 24hr urine test for cortisol, you may be high.

\-------------------------------
Creatinine 49.6 .... Reference Range 28-259

Lowish, hopefully because you are underweight, not enough proteins, high stomach acid.

> Low-Protein Diet
>
> Diets that are low in protein can cause low creatinine levels. Foods that are high in creatine phosphate include salmon, pork, tuna and beef, all of which are high in protein. Because these foods are not included in a low-protein diet, there is less creatine phosphate to break down, resulting in reduced creatinine levels.
>
>
> Pregnancy
>
> Pregnancy may cause low creatinine levels in the blood. Once the fetus starts developing in the womb, nutrients are transported from the mother to the baby via the umbilical cord. Pregnant women may have less creatine phosphate to break down due to the changes in the body. Reduced creatine phosphate breakdown results in reduced levels of creatinine. Once childbirth has occurred, creatinine levels should return to normal unless other causes of low creatinine levels exist.
>
> Decreased Muscle Mass
>
> Creatinine levels are usually higher in men because their muscle mass is higher than that of women. In people who have decreased muscle mass, creatinine levels may be low. Aging causes a natural decline in the amount of muscle mass in the body. Some medical conditions also cause wasting of the muscles and a reduction in muscle mass. Muscular dystrophy and myasthenia gravis are examples of diseases that can cause decreased muscle mass. Disabilities that limit movement can also cause reduced muscle mass due to lack of muscle use.
>
> Liver Disease
>
> Liver disease can contribute to a decline in serum creatinine levels. When liver disease becomes advanced, the organ stops producing as much creatinine. According to Dr. Kevin Pho, who is certified by the American Board of Internal Medicine, liver disease can also result in decreased muscle mass. Diseases that can affect the liver include fatty liver disease, hepatitis and metabolic disorders such as Wilson's disease and hemochromatosis.

### RickD — 2013-11-06T18:31:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-stage-of-adrenal-fatigue-i-feel-like-its-2-or-3-neurotransmitters.8678/post-99996>

Meghan,

I'm not going to tell you to quit training because I probably better than most understand how important it is to you. But from the symptoms you list above and your labs my guess is your results have not met your expectations so you need to do something different. One size does not fit all when it comes to training. Some elite triathletes, cyclists and Nordic skiers can thrive, at least in the short term, on 1500 hrs of training a year while others break down with more than 6-700hrs. My advice is take a break and get your health back then go out and get a coach who will work with you one on one to maximize your potential.

### RickD — 2013-11-07T05:21:40+0200

Meghan,

I raced hard until 50 when all that training finally caught up with me and trashed my adrenal gland and hormone panel. Fortunately, I had accomplished most of what I set out to do and with my best times behind me so it was an easy matter to back it down and just train for fun. Took a few years but I healed and now my focus is on staying fit and healthy so I can still do all the active things I enjoy doing for the next 50 yrs.

You have to decide how important the Olympic Marathon is to you and if you decide to go for it what you're willing to sacrifice to achieve that goal. Unfortunately, only you can answer that.

### prAna303 — 2013-11-07T10:39:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-stage-of-adrenal-fatigue-i-feel-like-its-2-or-3-neurotransmitters.8678/post-100057>

Ok, just my idea...

The problem is that i do not know you in person, i can not see you with my own eyes, i can not see your music if we put it that way.

I do meet a lot of elite persons, in sports, in dance, in music and in many other branches. I feel your problems on a daily basis and the big thing is that we are different persons with different triggers. With so many factors behind illness today one must find the things that makes your own life going down... Today we see people with great passion crash and burn because they can not get it right, we think alot, read alot, listen alot but mostly to factors made up by others, by golden rules. When did you have a chance to listen to your own inner voice?

The training is a trouble for you now, at the same time your passion. This combination is making your head hurt also and the chaos inside of your is rising, so you need to calm that storm. Every time i talk to someone who is on the same course as you i feel that deep inside there is a call for help but not the outside world help, a call for letting the master piece become alive. This is a problem today because rules of the modern world often point towards a locked up creature in a cage. It is time for you to come out now.

So my way would be like this:

First of all, before anything, start with fixing your conductor in life, feed your brain. Food is just a small part but right now for you quite important. Put yourself in places that promotes wild food, fish, fats and good water. Second, life is energy and everything around us is some kind of that BUT make shure you think in a different way... You are not a combustion engine, by training you add entropy in your unit, by living you add entropy and maximum entropy is dead. Food can change the rules a bit but the big things is how and where you place the unit YOU. If your passion is training right now, put it in places where you reconnect with nature. Make your runs in the woods, mountains... Make your swimming in the sea not in the gym. The list goes on...

How do you live your days? Are you working the white collar way? Do you use technology? Are you living in a family? The point is that when your signal is sending at a frequency, your life is doing itÂ´s music it must match the world you are living in and with.

Do you get analog vibrations? If your passion today is training, go get energy from seeing performing arts, blend in with that kind of persons, you vibrate together, get that magic resonance the old school way. Remember that life feeds on negative entropy and music, singing and dancing is just some way to get that.

Get yourself on a trip, a hike on your own. Your body will hurt and in that process things will break down, your mind will be confused and stressed BUT in a natural way. Energy is in the nature and you feed up. Remember that we need sun, water and magnetism as pillars of life... Fix these three legs and you can over time build the fourth, the fifth and so on. Now you can see your training as a natural way of life. I guess you have lost some of the three right now and first your brains hurts, then your big muscle functions and so on... When the three parts is filled with natural energy you start to see life as sublime, the details gets clear because your mind is working at light speed...

I think that when someone like you get the chance to get the fundation worked up you see how your training either is good or bad for you. To say what you should do in the state you are in is difficult because the world is full of noice, and alot of noice is also loads of entropy.

I hope you post some clues about yourself, not only labs but the things that you really are, like what makes your soundtrack of your life. The big changes is in the world you put yourself in, what kind of fields you act in.

For you i share my own passion with these words:

\- Emotion thru motion -

### MeghanK — 2013-11-07T17:08:22+0200

You sound like my therapist and parents... I am close to my parents... they are in the burbs and I in the city but I have gotten "Life360" for them on my phone so they can track me......
I have tried to read EMF #4 and all the blogs but I get COMPLETELY OVERWHELMED by the info and then get OCD about the details and will feel guilty if I don't try CT even though it's miserable for me.... etc.....

The sad thing is, I know I am so much more than an athlete... I am SOOOO FREAKING CREATIVE.... I make cards and scrapbooks and crafts that would take your breath away but the exercise and athlete thing took over..... I am an identical twin who was the fighter... born at 1.9lbs...and had to fight 3 months in a incubator to SURVIVE while my sister thrived so I have spent my life trying to "SURVIVE" and also be UNIQUE....we always got attention... made EVERY TEAM, MVP, every boyfriend we wanted.... I NEVER FAILED... until now.....NOW, my sister is married, pregnant...lives in the cutest house and is a teacher.... ME... no boyfriend, no friends.... in and out of jobs, currently working at Trader Joes... and the only thing I have to show for the last 10 years is winning marathons, half ironmans, races etc. but NO BALANCE OR LIFE and now skinny, destroyed hormones etc......

Of course you will need discipline... but I think that you have

Something I read on Jacks facebook site some time ago - start to love yourself dearly from today, because you deserve it

> Do not be afraid to be bold about your love for you. We can only love others as much as we love ourselves. Become immune to shame. Just as the sun does not shine for certain trees and flowers, love has no boundaries for any of us. If you are not good enough for you, who are you really good for?

The world desperately needs leaders.. and to become one we must get healthy in our mind, body and spirit... we are all on our way here, only the direction counts, not where you are on the road in comparison to others. Never compare yourself to others, but only to you

### cantweight — 2013-11-07T18:16:18+0200

It's about control yet you are not the one in the control. If you want control take it...its there just waiting. The only power your negative thoughts have is the power you give them every time you think them. Be done. Move on. Want to do something hard? CT.

I have an eight yr old son with neuro issues that gets in the CT tank with his little goggles on and practices how long he can hold his breath under water. He is the CT rockstar in our house and he is so proud of himself.

Do something to make yourself feel proud. It is an upward spiral, just get it going....it is amazing

### MsYo — 2013-11-07T21:27:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-stage-of-adrenal-fatigue-i-feel-like-its-2-or-3-neurotransmitters.8678/post-100118>

This thread touches my heart closely. I am a recovering exercise addict. I was overweight and after gastric bypass I found a new joy in competitive running and the gym was my "high". Much like the document Gretchen provided, I didn't realize the damage until my performance began to decline. My family life, career, and finally exercise was a source of stress. I discovered this blog in 2011 after I ran the 2010 Chicago Marathon with no training (also, lost my job 30 days later on the eve of my birthday). I kept trying to do cardio and added HCG to try to get back to what I thought was "normal"--and I realize the damage that I continued. Sometimes we have to do what doesn't feel "normal" in order to get on track to optimal. Read the link Gretchen posted, it really does serve as a reflection of both extremes in Adrenal Fatigue and personality types.

### caroline — 2013-11-07T23:20:40+0200

So much wonderful advice here Meghan - from people that care about you...

Could you drop out for awhile? maybe move back home with your parents? be with the people that love and support you the most......reconnect with your sister. Their heart is breaking for you and they would love an opportunity to hug you every single day for awhile!

You could run barefoot thru the streets in the "Burb" and hug trees .......makes friends with a bunch of children and teach them some stuff - you have so much to offer...

Hi Everyone!

We've seen a lot of chatter about the webinar we had with Dr. Tim Jackson and the upcoming "Heal Your Hormones" Bootcamp. We know many of you missed part of the webinar or the whole thing so we wanted to make sure you could listen to it in it's entirety before the bootcamp starts next week.

You can listen/download to the free webinar here: [Community Wide "Heal Your Hormone" Webinar with Dr. Tim Jackson](http://www.jackkruse.com/watch/community-wide-hormones-webinar-with-dr-tim-jacksonkdkocqqozdeov9382ie/)

And if, once you listen to the free webinar, you want to sign up for the bootcamp you can do that [HERE](http://www.jackkruse.com/heal-your-hormones-bronze/) (Silver & Gold members should have gotten an email, if not let us know.)

Once you sign up, you will have access to all the webinar replays in case you miss one.

We hope you'll take a listen and considering joining in on the bootcamp!

# Thread: Nature-Throid and Sleep - Am I imagining this?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nature-throid-and-sleep-am-i-imagining-this.8680/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mafixit — 2013-11-06T19:19:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nature-throid-and-sleep-am-i-imagining-this.8680/post-100001>

My BHRT doc put me on a small dose of Nature-Throid even though my Thyroid Labs are "normal". I swear that when I am on this med, I sleep deeper. Is this possible? I am on 1/2 of the 37.5 mg dose 1x per day in the morning.

I can't explain it, but I just feel like I am in a heavier state of sleep. I still wake up once a night to go pee, but I fall right back to sleep. And I find myself waking up to the alarm at 4:45 a.m., which is unusual, and I am not as anxious when I do wake up. When I am not on this stuff, I tend to wake up 45 minutes before the alarm with anxiety. Am I on to something?

Nothing else has changed med-wise. Still on my BHRT.

Karen from Arizona

### Shijin13 — 2013-11-06T20:52:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nature-throid-and-sleep-am-i-imagining-this.8680/post-100009>

I've slept better since being on Armour that coupled w/BHRT has helped with my migraines as well...

For me, open enrollment comparing options started with figuring out where I could find the information to begin my comparison. Iâ€™ve put together a matrix you can download to help you compare your options as well. /www.yourpaleohealthcare.com/open-enrollment-comparing-options/

### Hope — 2013-11-11T23:55:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/open-enrollment-is-in-full-swing-newest-blog.8683/post-100458>

Gretchen, I must add to the choir..you are amazing!! 100%. I don't know how you find time to do all this....work a job, take care of you and fam and dog etc......wow. When do you do this? It pops out in your sleep, from your brain onto the website?

### Shijin13 — 2013-11-12T00:04:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/open-enrollment-is-in-full-swing-newest-blog.8683/post-100459>

### Destiny — 2013-11-12T13:14:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/open-enrollment-is-in-full-swing-newest-blog.8683/post-100495>

My employer offered only one plan for next year, down from 3 plans last year. So I only had to pick from this one plan or go on the exchange.

I picked my employer's plan even though my plan jumped from monthly premiums of $225 to $425. My deductible went from 2K to 6K and out of pocket went from 4K to 9K. This will be paid for by before the tax dollars.
The affordable exchange plan was $600 a month and deductible 12K. Not sure if there was any out of the pocket maximum. All must be paid by after the tax dollars.

While the employer's plan is expensive, it is still cheaper than the "affordable" health plan.

### JanSz — 2013-11-12T16:23:00+0200

### Gracelind — 2013-11-15T08:18:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/open-enrollment-is-in-full-swing-newest-blog.8683/post-100761>

Plan through my husband's employer has just announced that the PPO premium beginning 12/6/13 is $29,000/year, up 20% from last year. Don't know the deductible, but previously it was $1500 with $2500 annual stop loss. A few years from now this kind of "Cadillac" policy will be subject to a tax of 40%. Time to look elsewhere, which I couldn't do before due to pre-existing condition. That's the only benefit we've detected so far from ACA. Didn't need a humungous bloated law to accomplish that.

[http://notjustpaleo.com/podcast-52-dr-jack-kruse-food-light-cycles-meditation-biohacks/](http://notjustpaleo.com/podcast-52-dr-jack-kruse-food-light-cycles-meditation-biohacks/)

### Martin — 2013-11-08T20:56:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-podcast-up-with-evan-brand.8694/post-100202>

In many ways, one of your best to date. Evan did a good job of asking some Q's and then letting you speak. About halfway, there was a 30 minute stretch where you were on a _roll!_

Your podcasts and interviews are some of the best communication tools in your belt. We read the science and absorb as much as we can, but when we hear it spoken with passion, intention, and clarification on the main points, we can mentally refer back to what we've read, and, like a CD player, we're able to "sample" the complex science and get the overall gist. If we want more clarity, the references you give, like which blog post, article, book, etc., help fill in the gaps that are tough to do in an hour podcast.

Thanks for taking your time to do these, Doc. Keep 'em comin'!

Martin, BGH

### caroline — 2013-11-08T23:08:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-podcast-up-with-evan-brand.8694/post-100204>

### prAna303 — 2013-11-09T16:55:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-podcast-up-with-evan-brand.8694/post-100268>

Just to chime in, in the last three years i have gone more analog for each day. I felt that the binary world is not mine and even if i will be alone in the wild, the digital world is not for me...

Things i have done:
\- Killed my phone, mobile and mobile devices. I have i GSM, turned off and used only in emergency.
\- Killed Wifi and gears with antennas. No smart connect any more.
\- Dropped usage of computers, not listening to music that way, not seeing movies that way...
\- Interact with the ones that i resonate with, found them always in surfing, climbing, and other sports of elements but now also in the performing arts. I found that i can blend with top dancers and be like them without being a dancer, once i started there is NO WAY BACK to digital again.

So bottom line: Go Analog, ditch the digital...

### laker — 2013-11-08T07:21:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kidney-health-supplement.8696/post-100170>

Worried about my 1.3 creatinine in recent labs. 26 yr old male. 68 gfr. Is the gfr measured on its own or only calculated from the creatinine levels? Could this be low because of not being well hydrated before the blood work?
96 fasting blood sugar. Following Paleo diet now for 10 months

UPDATED \_\_

I was fasting before the blood work

non black, low body fat, don't lift weights

UA was negative

BUN wasn't tested it seems ( these labs were one due to follow up @ kaiser )

I will get my BUN tested asap though!

What does this mean

### caroline — 2013-11-08T07:56:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kidney-health-supplement.8696/post-100172>

Hi Laker ... and welcome! It would be great if you could start a journal in the journal section ..... go to the top of this page and click on forum and then go down 5 sections and start a new thread in the journal section.

Dr. Kruse likes journals - he really needs a lot more context ...your lifestyle? other labs? your non native EMF exposure?

It is all very, very important.

Have you listened to Dr. Kruse's podcasts? there is a new one today that is excellent. You can find them on the home page.

There is also a great free webinar by Dr. Tim Jackson - that would be really helpfull for you I think.... and he is doing a whole bootcamp series - starting next week.

Have you read Dr. K's book? It is a great overview of all of this and a really helpful section on labs. It is available in kindle and paper.

### Jude — 2013-11-09T01:32:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kidney-health-supplement.8696/post-100215>

> [laker said:](/goto/post?id=100170)
>
> Worried about my 1.3 creatinine in recent labs. 26 yr old male. 68 gfr. Is the gfr measured on its own or only calculated from the creatinine levels? Could this be low because of not being well hydrated before the blood work?
> 96 fasting blood sugar. Following paleo diet now for 10 months

Dear laker, perhaps you could post all your labs and ref ranges. JK says context is crucial and we need to look at everything. iirc the BUN/creatinine ratio gives a better picture of ones' hydration status, so in isolation yr value can look strange but must not be taken out of context
Caroline's advice is good...more info better advice....all the best.

### JanSz — 2013-11-09T02:09:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kidney-health-supplement.8696/post-100224>

> [laker said:](/goto/post?id=100170)
>
> Worried about my 1.3 creatinine in recent labs. 26 yr old male. 68 gfr. Is the gfr measured on its own or only calculated from the creatinine levels? Could this be low because of not being well hydrated before the blood work?
> 96 fasting blood sugar. Following paleo diet now for 10 months

If you have very low body fat, lots of muscles, exercise heavily, supplement with Creatine monohydrate, eat lots of proteins, creatinine may go up, eGFR may go down and all that may be normal.
Also eGFR is calculated with correction for African Americans.
I just got my labs. I am white. Got
eGFR=85 if nonAfrican American
eGFR=98 if African American

I got above after I stopped eating Creatine monohydrate.
While eating Creatine monohydrate I had
eGFR=58 if nonAfrican American
eGFR=67 if African American

your lab:
Creatinine=1.3(0.76-1.27)
eGFR=68>59

\-----------------------------------

.....................

### laker — 2013-11-09T04:21:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kidney-health-supplement.8696/post-100235>

thanks, non black, UA was negative, i don't supplement that, i do eat high protein diet, my BUN wasn't taken but ill be sure to get that check asap

TESTOSTERONE, TOTAL 249 - 836 ng/dL 705
SEXHORMONE BINDING GLOBULIN 14 - 48 nmol/L 37
TSH 0.35 - 4.00 mcIU/mL 2.86
FSH 1.4 - 18.1 mIU/mL 3.9

# Thread: Its the little things...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-the-little-things.8700/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### cantweight — 2013-11-08T17:01:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-the-little-things.8700/post-100193>

Ive been grumbling about feeling crummy and I am not alone. I have read some wonderful posts this morning that have really reconfirmed the humanity in all that we are trying to achieve. Despite setbacks we rally in a most impressive way.

4 years ago I had sick children. My then 9yr old daughter had terrible migraines, zero energy, strep in the gut, random fevers, hypothyroid, pathogens and parasites to boot. She had a tonsil and adenoidectomy, (her tonsils grew back), missed school constantly and was on a ridiculous amount of medication. My then 7yr old son was diagnosed MCTD, JRA, he was in pain constantly, fatigued, underweight, hypothyroid, high strep, pathogens, parasites, hormone imbalances. He also had the tonsils and adenoids out same day as his sister (that was fun...ugh!) also missed school constantly and was on a ton of meds.

These 2 awesome kids that could barely get through their days not that long ago are running their first 5k this weekend....a Turkey Trot! I am so proud of them!

This is after successful softball and soccer seasons. My daughter just tried out for a tournament team and my son scored his first goal last weekend.

I know these are things that other families experience on a regular basis and are probably no big deal, but I am grateful for our journey and that all these "little" things get to feel so special to us.

(My youngest is not running the race but definitely worth noting he scored the most goals on his soccer team this season....this kid that could have been stopped in his tracks by neuro tics is kicking ass!)

(Also...no one is on any prescriptions and they are doing great!!!)

### Shijin13 — 2013-11-08T18:17:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-the-little-things.8700/post-100198>

great successes Christi!!! just awesome....

### nonchalant — 2013-11-08T18:28:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-the-little-things.8700/post-100199>

It's so wonderful to hear of small children being able to live life fully again.

### caroline — 2013-11-09T00:03:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-the-little-things.8700/post-100206>

I just love to hear about all the improvements in your children Christi ..... Next up - YOU....

We take it for granted sometimes that our kids should be healthy and happy...... It is so unjust and unfair when they arent.

You have an awesome family unit ..... And you are the captain of this magnificent ship.....you are a force to be reckoned with. I would bet on you every, single time....

### Hope — 2013-11-09T01:59:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-the-little-things.8700/post-100220>

I agree with everyone. And I would add to Audrey's words....you are the captain of a magnetico and magnificent ship!!!

BTW, I would love to hear how you detoxed the kids from their biotoxins....ugh.

CONGRATS!!!!!!!!

### caroline — 2013-11-09T02:08:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-the-little-things.8700/post-100223>

Christi - have you talked to the folks at magnetico about why you are having such a struggle on your son's magnetico?
I would guess that they would have some imput - especially since you want to get one for you...

### sooperb — 2013-11-09T16:19:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-the-little-things.8700/post-100263>

I really applaud your commitment and your bravery to fight against "the system" for your children and yourself. Being a square peg in a round hold and sticking to your principles is a tough stance but you are winning, very well done Christi. I wish I had found the backbone years back (I did ban MacDonald's eventually lol) and started my children on the road to optimal, now I can only heckle them from the sidelines and I'm not convincing at least one of them that needs to listen. I won't stop though, it's too important.

### cantweight — 2013-11-12T16:25:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-the-little-things.8700/post-100504>

Thanks for the kind words! They did great and their Dad ran with them, it was awesome

Audrey...I think my problem with the magnetico is that I am loaded with metal and I was laying on it during the day which Jack said is not useful because we need to be in a chemically reduced (sleep)state. I am curious about upcoming blogs that hit on that. If we cant get to that state it seems to be a major issue although I feel I am getting there more and more. Weight still coming off slowly, sleep is decent, feeling much better than i was.

Hope....we detoxed with a ton of stuff!!! We know how different we all are and I was trying to find N=1 x 5...lol! It was nutty, but everyone is doing so well. Chlorella was the first and most constantly used, spirulina, and seaweed. I fed the kids nutritional smoothies with Opticleanse GHI or Brain Sustain has rice flour in it I believe so we havent used either in quite some time. L-glutamine, Swedish bitters, aloe. Green juices and smoothies constantly lots of cilantro and parsley and lemon. I personally used Welchol as a binder for some time. DesBio homeopathic drops Detox 1,2,3 and Metox. Lots of lemon. Trampoline jumping to help move lymph fluid. Nux vomica, berberis, NDF....so many homeopathic drops it was a bit nuts. I just wanted to leave no stone unturned. Coconut oil for antiviral properties. FNG drops for yeast, candida plus drops. Tons of probiotic...threelac, fivelac, theralac, dr ohiras, now raw milk. DE capsules, parasite cleanses, coffee enemas, supplements with bentonite clay and pectin....one was called Toxi HMF. Thats all I can think of right now.

I liken the process to more of an exorcism than a healing...lol. My poor kids....the crap I made them drink! They will all be in therapy one day...lol. But they will be healthy!!!

### SeaHorse — 2013-11-13T03:12:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-the-little-things.8700/post-100585>

Such a great success for you and your family.....thanks for sharing with everyone...I think it makes all of us feel wonderful to read these stories!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-11-13T04:05:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-the-little-things.8700/post-100590>

There are many things that seem impossible only so long as one does not attempt them. To overcome this, you need to let the little things that would ordinarily bore you suddenly thrill you. The small things in life are often so much bigger than the great things . . . the trivial pleasure like cooking, one's spouse, little stories, especially sad ones, solitary walks, funny things seen and overheard. Sometimes the little opportunities that fly at us each day can have the biggest impact. Pay attention to Mother nature's nudge's, she'll protect you. Night.

### MrPinkies — 2013-12-12T21:26:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/its-the-little-things.8700/post-103238>

\*Tears\* awwwwwwwwwwwwwwesome!!!!!! xoxo

### caroline — 2013-11-09T00:56:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jks-latest-podcast-with-evav-nrand.8703/post-100208>

I really get so much out of the podcasts.... They are so great for us non- geeks. I must say tho .... I like my head hurting on a daily basis too ....self inflicted pain via jackkruse.com what's not to love.

Jack talked about some interesting things we hadn't heard before. His meditation room sounds awesome especially as it sounds like it is built for two....how lovely.

I am even more confused about transition metals now ... So a really dopey question.....Jack talked about using crystals in surgery ...... Crystals , with their transition metals, attract non native EMF - right?

So that means we are attracting EMF to us and the crystals are absorbing them? All of them coming our way?

So what I thought about my copper bracelet wasn't correct?? I thought I was attracting non native EMF to myself by the boatload .... And then becoming very dehydrated....

Is it the same for rings, earrings, necklaces etc? I am not thinking this thru correctly..... I need a grade 5 version I guess ......

Y'all.... What was your take home from this podcast? Do you have a special meditation place?

We need more details! Lots more.....

Wouldn't a meditation place outside be more beneficial?
