### caroline — 2013-11-09T01:23:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jks-latest-podcast-with-evav-nrand.8703/post-100213>

I can't wait to meet you in person Martin .... One day soon..

But ......Jack talked about 2 dimensional relationships on the net...... say it isn't so Martin?

In my mind - you are beautiful and your mind/ heart are actively engaged to the nth degree.....and you are very 3D to me..... I know I am right about this.....

### caroline — 2013-11-09T01:39:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jks-latest-podcast-with-evav-nrand.8703/post-100217>

Let's talk about this .... Do we only see a person in 2 dimension on the net/FB etc....... Or over time .... Does a person's true self display itself?

I know I have experienced this personally .....someone from the net just wasn't who I thought they were. Are they actively downplaying their true personality? Or - is that just how it is?

I realize some people say rude, stupid, hurtful things on electronic media ..... But surely some of us are real??

Over time .... Do we become 3 dimensional? Surely after 3 years of this .....we would inadvertently display our true personality? Or am I full of crap? Head in the sand??

What about Skype - surely that is somewhat real?

But the touching thing is so important ..... When we transmit and receive energy.....when I am out everyday - I get the most glorious smile usually ..... But some people look away .....so much negative energy. Generally older men only give you a brief look or a nod..... But Jack talked about this in the sex webinar .....men communicate side by side.

Oddly - I find younger men are much better communicators. - why is that? They will often say something very pleasant.
And it is not as if they are hitting on me....I am old enough to be their Mother.....

Older men seem to almost be scared of life ....is that a fair characterization? I think I am too intense these days....

So - the takeaway for me ..... I need to hug you one day Martin.....apologies to your DW - who I know you adore!

### prAna303 — 2013-11-09T10:29:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jks-latest-podcast-with-evav-nrand.8703/post-100245>

> Do we only see a person in 2 dimension on the net/FB etc....... Or over time .... Does a person's true self display itself?

\- I think that the only way to make a person 3D is to be "live" with them. Deep inside us there is so many signals that must get their resonance otherwise we can not really connect. The problem with the net is that it is digital and based on digital ways of making the natural / analog things sendable, it that process we loose every little odd overtone, the crisp details, i would say the whole thing that makes a LIVING organism. In fact you could be speaking to a computer who makes answers based on some logarithm (think google). If you meet someone live and then connect thru the web you can get a memory from him or her but you will never sense the person right here and right now.

> I know I have experienced this personally .....someone from the net just wasn't who I thought they were. Are they actively downplaying their true personality? Or - is that just how it is?

\- The big problem today is that many live their lifes on the web, they can not sense their own ways of being anymore... So when someone talks out on the web you should always think twice about what they are saying. I mean, if you never been in a live situation where you hurt someones heart, how should you be able to sense the things we do not say in words?

> Over time .... Do we become 3 dimensional? Surely after 3 years of this .....we would inadvertently display our true personality? Or am I full of crap? Head in the sand??

\- Never i would say, the thing you can see is the persons way of acting over time. Does he or she behave in a regular way or do the words they say mean one thing today and another tomorrow, do they change standpoint over time without any good reason and so on.

> What about Skype - surely that is somewhat real?

\- In my life, ONLY if you have been seeing someone live then you can add that to the situation, it is not live but better than facebook. For me, the big problem once again, IT IS DIGITAL. Where are the small things that you can not see, smell but SENSE?

> Older men seem to almost be scared of life ....is that a fair characterization? I think I am too intense these days....

\- For me? I would say that many, both men and women, young and elder are scared. They are scared of real power and energy. When you spend your life wobbling thru life, scratching your iPhone you get a big disturbance, a big error in your system when you see pure energy. At first they find it exciting but pretty quick they tend to go the other way because they can not keep up with someone who is powerful. The world is as you are, when you have energy you transmit on a different plane than the other, because of that you must look for the ones that resonates with you, stop looking at the ones that will make you sad, look at the stars. But remember that everyone has a chance to become a star also, so you can not be rude to others because you feel that you have nothing in common with them, that is how the www works, not superheros.

### prAna303 — 2013-11-09T11:15:23+0200

> how I became so afraid of losing everyone
> never been afraid of being lonely
> now Im becoming the one Im most scared of being
>
> I dont know where this fear comes from
> this fear of failing fear of letting everyone and myself down
> its growing deep into my soul
> making me all paralyzed and cold

### SeaHorse — 2013-11-09T15:13:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jks-latest-podcast-with-evav-nrand.8703/post-100257>

Wow.....Peter, that was a great meld of words and images. Circus is where art and fear come together...amazing.

Audrey...I will add to what Peter has written above....I think all the connections that we make online, through the forum, etc. have significance...of course they do, but they are not full relationships and what isn't there is what is most important ultimately. Just speaking of the forum, think how much knowledge we've gained reading it over the last few years....but that knowledge still has to be activated in order to have significance for us, and in our "friendships" too...I'm feel certain that we would have a wonderful time if we ever met, but think how little we know of each other apart from our own isolated words floating around in space. There is a lot of room for distortion. One of Don Miguel Ruiz five agreements is Don't make Assumptions. We need to be in the presence of a living body to really feel the essence of someone. Of course relationships exist in a spectrum and as I said, online friendships can be a significant, I just don't think they hold a candle to the experience of being with someone. I was taught that 90percent of communication is non-verbal.....so that gives me an idea of what I'm missing by just dealing with words. At the same time words are powerful, so we can't dismiss them in any way. Some people are verbal and can paint a picture of themselves that has nothing to do with reality....others are mostly mute and hold great truths that are only expressed through action....

I think in a perfect world we would be engaging in this healing path face to face with our teachers and our fellow travellers.....yet it is also true that our modern world has created many of our individual challenges, and we must use what is available to meet these challenges....so thanks be for the Dr. Kruse, the internet, magnetico and all of you!!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-11-09T17:42:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jks-latest-podcast-with-evav-nrand.8703/post-100270>

It all comes down to what you treasure, in my opinion. Any treasure worth having, you have to dig for it. Gold is what most people 'pan' for. There's "gold" in people too, but sometimes you have to move a lot of dirt to find it. But it's always there, and it's worth digging for. This makes everyone worth treasuring. So how do you find that treasure? The greatest treasure map is found in our hearts. That is the place we should dig first and yet many don't have the courage to do it. They dig anywhere but in their hearts. Today find the gems in your life: passion, love, hope, desire, faith, strength, and knowledge. When "you know" what is buried in your heart no thief can steal anything from you........

### caroline — 2013-11-09T01:53:25+0200

I know we have talked about silica briefly - but it is so hard to find things these day.....

I bought some liquid silica yesterday .... The instructions are to put one tsp in a glass of water daily.

I really like the taste it gave to the water - it was very pleasant.

I paid $29.95 for 500ml. It is probably dirt cheap in the U.S.?

Is this a good idea?

### Hope — 2013-11-09T01:57:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/silica.8704/post-100219>

Hi Audrey, I haven't looked into it yet.....but a health fair I went to in October, they talked about it wrt aluminum....it also supposedly helps with aluminum removal? It's also great for hair and nails. I remember seeing lots of ads in Germany talking about it.

A quick google search brought up this: [http://www.naturalnews.com/034756\_silica\_anti-aging\_heavy\_metals.html](http://www.naturalnews.com/034756_silica_anti-aging_heavy_metals.html)

Good luck.

Can you import your own cheaper supplements from overseas maybe?

### caroline — 2013-11-09T02:14:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/silica.8704/post-100225>

Hi hope ....sometimes we can - but often things aren't allowed. If they do come into the country they can be horrendously expensive! I want to get some great lakes gelatin but it costs more than twice what two containers cost you...

### Hope — 2013-11-09T02:30:47+0200

### CTforlife — 2014-10-21T23:27:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/silica.8704/post-144637>

Is silica important? From a cellular depletion standpoint or does it just help? I heard it has effects with collagen so it sounds important to me?

### Penny — 2014-10-23T06:51:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/silica.8704/post-144809>

silica - also known as horsetail - chelates aluminum... Fiji water has the highest amount in a bottled water and in fact if you want to use it to remove aluminum, the protocol is one bottle of Fiji water per day for 3 months... safe, but kinda pricey -

For all you Aussies, iherb has horsetail (silica) for less than $6.00/bottle...
[http://www.iherb.com/Nature-s-Way-H...sc=0&lc=en-US&w=horsetail&rc=471&sr=null&ic=1](http://www.iherb.com/Nature-s-Way-Horsetail-Grass-440-mg-100-Capsules/1978#p=1&oos=1&disc=0&lc=en-US&w=horsetail&rc=471&sr=null&ic=1)

malic acid also chelates aluminum - take it with garlic and it binds even more crap

I take a magnesium malate every day -

### Lahelada — 2014-10-23T17:00:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/silica.8704/post-144853>

Penny,
Silica is not equivalent to horsetail,it is just more marketable than getting you to swallow a capsule of sand as we are so far removed from nature.It is a component of sands, just plain ole bird sand has a lot of it .
Horsetail is the plant that contains a lot of silica but is a diuretic....
I have taken it but thinking about how much it costs us to get the water to go where it needs I am thinking that horsetail should stay a medicinal herb for specific use not a daily supp.

### Lahelada — 2014-10-23T17:06:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/silica.8704/post-144855>

For Germans reading Kieselerde is the capsulaeed sand you cannask for in any Droguerie at a fraction of the cost.

### Penny — 2014-10-28T00:34:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/silica.8704/post-145353>

thanks for that - I think I'll just shut up about horsetail from now on... but I think Fiji water would be safe...

# Thread: Pandora's box is open and I need to shut it :)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pandoras-box-is-open-and-i-need-to-shut-it.8715/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thenursisin — 2013-11-10T02:56:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pandoras-box-is-open-and-i-need-to-shut-it.8715/post-100299>

Hi everyone:

My name is Donna and I am a registered nurse for over 34 years. I have always been healthy and naturally thin. I have run marathons and half marathons and always have been active.

In the last 20 years, I have developed Hashimoto's disease after my second pregnancy. With that I have had a partial thyroidectomy due to a huge growth and and fear of cancer. Thankfully, no cancer. I have been on synthroid, developed anxiety and insomnia and was placed on Paxil. As time progressed I developed skin issues, asthma, GERD, and huge digestive issues with a special reaction to WHEAT but no celiac per blood tests and colonoscopy. I have developed IBS. I have developed rosacea. I have developed severe ragweed/mite allergies. I have gained 40 lbs. No matter how much I exercise, I can't lose weight. I also have been in alcohol recovery for 8 years.

I switched to Paleo lifestyle and felt better but feel huge cravings to sweets and chocolates. I wondered if I wasn't just switching addictions. After several Whole 30s I felt better but couldnt stick to the plan on a constant basis. After several months of large carb/wheat binge eating, I have raised the white flag. I am **DONE**. I can't eat like this anymore and feel like this anymore. I receive acupuncture twice a week and have switched to walking/nordic walking/elliptical training and weight training twice per week. No more running.

I have begun to read Jack Kruse and feel immediately drawn to his writings. Eureka!!!! I have stumbled upon a solution that might work for me. I want to get off of Paxil and lose the weight and feel like my pre Hashimotos self again. I have begun to take Probiotics for the last month and that has greatly helped me overall.

I need help and think this forum is a great start. Any advice would be greatly appreciated.....

### jib — 2013-11-10T03:32:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pandoras-box-is-open-and-i-need-to-shut-it.8715/post-100303>

Hi Donna, Welcome.

For whatever reason, no one can see your thread or get to it if you use these parentheses ( ) in the thread title, so don't think we don't see your post. You have to go into the string at the top and delete them to see the post.

I see Dr K has already given you an answer to one of the posts. I hope you are able to see his answer

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pandoras-box-is-open-and-i-need-to-shut-it.8716/post-100300>

Hi everyone:

My name is Donna and I am a registered nurse for over 34 years. I have always been healthy and naturally thin. I have run marathons and half marathons and always have been active.

In the last 20 years, I have developed Hashimoto's disease after my second pregnancy. With that I have had a partial thyroidectomy due to a huge growth and and fear of cancer. Thankfully, no cancer. I have been on synthroid, developed anxiety and insomnia and was placed on Paxil. As time progressed I developed skin issues, asthma, GERD, and huge digestive issues with a special reaction to WHEAT but no celiac per blood tests and colonoscopy. I have developed IBS. I have developed rosacea. I have developed severe ragweed/mite allergies. I have gained 40 lbs. No matter how much I exercise, I can't lose weight. I also have been in alcohol recovery for 8 years.

I switched to Paleo lifestyle and felt better but feel huge cravings to sweets and chocolates. I wondered if I wasn't just switching addictions. After several Whole 30s I felt better but couldnt stick to the plan on a constant basis. After several months of large carb/wheat binge eating, I have raised the white flag. I am **DONE**. I can't eat like this anymore and feel like this anymore. I receive acupuncture twice a week and have switched to walking/nordic walking/elliptical training and weight training twice per week. No more running.

I have begun to read Jack Kruse (EpiPaleo on Amazon) and feel immediately drawn to his writings and beliefs. Eureka!!!! I have stumbled upon a solution that might work for me. I want to get off of Paxil and lose the weight and feel like my pre Hashimotos self again. I have begun to take Probiotics for the last month and that has greatly helped me overall.

I need help and think this forum is a great start. Any advice would be greatly appreciated.....

### Jack Kruse — 2013-11-10T03:10:17+0200

# Thread: Pandora's box is open and I need to shut it
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pandoras-box-is-open-and-i-need-to-shut-it.8717/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thenursisin — 2013-11-10T03:55:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pandoras-box-is-open-and-i-need-to-shut-it.8717/post-100304>

Hi,

I am Donna and am glad to have found this forum. I have had Hashimoto's Thyroiditis for over 25 years. I had a partial thyroidectomy in mid 90's after a pregnancy for fear of thyroid cancer. It turned out to be Hashimoto's. After this, I have developed anxiety and depression and been started on Paxil.
Since then, on Paxil and Synthroid all hell has broke loose! I have developed rosacea, IBS, GERD, asthma, severe ragweed/mite allergies, dental problems, joint pain etc...the worse of problem of them all is 40 lbs weight gain despite running 25 miles per week. I suspect I have a wheat allergy due to severe GI reactions when I eat it yet negative celiac panel.

I have done Paleo (Whole 30) yet crave carbs and go off the food plan and my health spirals. Insomnia is a chronic issue for me. I recently began bioidentical Progesterone and Estrogen to help with menopause symptoms. This has greatly helped my insomnia.

I stumbled upon Dr Kruse and bought the Epi Paleo book on Kindle this week. I have so many questions and don't know where to start. I am financially strapped so cannot afford the boot camp, unfortunately. Would love to hear from anyone who can direct me to the first step!

### caroline — 2013-11-10T04:19:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pandoras-box-is-open-and-i-need-to-shut-it.8717/post-100305>

Hi Donna- and welcome! I am so glad that you started a journal! Dr. Kruse needs context to help where he can. At least you have a head start with the terminology!!

If you go to the home page you will find the easy start guides. Start the leptin RX using the epi paleo template.

Start reading the blogs that pertain to your health first - there is a lot - but just start slowly!

Please listen to Dr. K.'s podcasts - you will find them on the home page. The podcasts give you a lot of information quickly and Dr. Kruse is very easy to listen to. The last one he did with Evan Brand is great.

Have you listened to the free webinar with Dr. Tim Jackson? That would be really important for you.

HAve you looked at your non native EMF exposure? That is critical for you - that needs to be at the top of your list. There are some great podcasts that Jack did on that.

Keep asking questions - we all try to help each other. There are other folks with your problems ....so it would be great to connect with them in their journals.

When you start the leptin R/X - especially pay attention to the BAB (big ass breakfast) ... It knocks out cravings pretty fast. - I promise!

You need to eat within 30 minutes upon rising and leave at least 4 hours between meals and 4 hours before going to bed and no snacking what so ever. Follow Jack's protocols exactly as written

Avoid all fake lite.....no lights on after sunset ....And wear blue blocking glasses when on a computer/iPad/iPhone/TV etc.

Take everything electrical out of where you sleep and cut the breaker if you can .... Really important.

Lastly - read the C/ T easy start guide....

### thenursisin — 2013-11-10T14:15:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pandoras-box-is-open-and-i-need-to-shut-it.8717/post-100312>

thank you Caroline:
I just read your story at the end of Jack's book....very hopeful for me as I can relate to your story the MOST. I actually came to find Jack Kruse by listening to Tim Jackson's webinar! I am trying to eat my BAB right now but only ate it 45 minutes into awakening. I pray and meditate for 45 mins every morning upon awakening but I need to switch the order of that with my eating first.

Looking into blue blockers. I fall asleep with a sound machine but I think you are saying get rid of it? I need the white noise to drift....

This site has offered me the most hope to date. Thank you for responding and I will peruse your recommendations.

Donna

### nicld — 2013-11-10T18:53:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pandoras-box-is-open-and-i-need-to-shut-it.8717/post-100332>

Welcome Donna,

Caroline (aka Audrey) is a wealth of information and well as many others on this site.

You can get some blue blockers off of Amazon for about $10 and they really help. I have so sleep with a sound machine too but have switched from having it run on electricity to battery. Don't know if it is better but I cannot sleep without it (need to drown the sound of the hubby out).

Like Caroline said, I would start reading one of the series of blogs like the CT ones or all the Brain Gut ones. There is so much information that it will make you head hurt.

Also, get outside and get some grounding in. Not sure where you live but where I live, winter is fast approaching and I can already tell that I need to figure out how to get some time outside.

Hang on, this is going to be a journey.

### thenursisin — 2013-11-10T19:20:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pandoras-box-is-open-and-i-need-to-shut-it.8717/post-100333>

Nicki,

Thanks so much....My head is spinning. And I am a master's prepared registered nurse (master of science in nursing)....but it's because I am sooooo anxious to learn so much, so fast. Driving is like the Indy 500 around here

He loves the Paleo food plan....

I think I found my home......thanks for the welcome

~Donna

I like to think of fractal as being the same pattern, but not necessarily identical. The pattern appears to be the same (or similar) at any dimension or perspective, but not necessarily identical. A tornado, a hurricane, a galaxy, all have a similar pattern but are not identical. But one can make the case that they represent a fractal similarity. I saw a picture of neurons and a picture of star/galaxy clusters and they looked exceptionally similar. Different scaling for sure, but surprisingly similar. That is why I think it is possible that the universe is self-aware on its own dimension. We just cannot fathom that from our perspective, but then, I can't think of any law of physics that would prevent the universe from being itself self-aware on its own time and distance scaling.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-11-10T16:11:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-largest-biologic-experiment-ever-done-on-humans.8719/post-100317>

As incredible as it seems, much of the relevant biology, including the quantum physics of organisms, has been ignored and excluded from mainstream discourse.

Today, we are in a tsunami of the wi-fi revolution, the installation of wireless facilities in airport lounges, universities, schools, and entire cities, threatening to saturate the airspace with microwaves. This is happening just as incontrovertible evidence has built up over the health hazards of Microwaves exposure, including cancers increasing three- to four-fold in proximity to mobile phone transmitters, acoustic neuromas for people using mobile phones for more than ten years, decrease in sperm count and quality in men using mobile phones for more than four hours a day, sterility in mice, disappearance of birds and bees, and high incidence of microwave sickness syndrome including headache, lack of concentration, nausea, fatigue, and severe allergic reactions.

The reason the molecular mechanisms have skated past biology? Biologists homogenate and ground up cells to study the molecular mechanisms........and in doing so immediately loose the ability to see how water really acts in a cell as a living liquid crystal semiconductor.

I now know that the biological effects of electromagnetic fields are mediated through the large amounts of ‘biological’ water molecules associated with the cells and tissues of living organisms. This was discussed at length in the November 2013 webinar.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-11-10T16:16:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-largest-biologic-experiment-ever-done-on-humans.8719/post-100318>

What biology does not realize is that living organisms are in general much more sensitive to weak magnetic fields than to weak electric fields. There are times in the lifespan of all living things where these weak fields is completely tied to the amount of entropy in the cell........

You might ask why I believe this to be true? The answer is based in Maxwell's 4 laws of electromagnetism.

Time-varying magnetic fields will induce electric currents in the organism. Static magnetic fields, however, cannot induce currents, so how do they produce biological effects? Static fields affect organisms by their ability to orientate magnetically sensitive molecules and by deflecting endogenous electrical currents. This is why the magnetico pad works as it does.........It acts a pole in the ground of cell in order to polarize its own intracellular water when the electric currents we surround ourselves with consistently alter that balance.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-11-10T16:18:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-largest-biologic-experiment-ever-done-on-humans.8719/post-100319>

Why is the above comment critical?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-11-10T16:23:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-largest-biologic-experiment-ever-done-on-humans.8719/post-100320>

The answer goes back the Quantum electron blog post......of two yrs ago. [http://www.jackkruse.com/do-food-electrons-impart-a-quantum-effect/](http://www.jackkruse.com/do-food-electrons-impart-a-quantum-effect/)
And now you layer in what you just learned about transition metals...........Some molecules can orientate themselves in a magnetic field. Like water and biologic proteins with transition metals in them. Remember in QED electron pairs have spins that oppose one another. As a consequence of their electron spins, electrons behave as though they are **miniscule bar magnets** each possessing a magnetic moment, called the Bohr magneton, and is of the order of 10−23 J/Tesla. As I mentioned in the November 2013 webinar , the electrons in an atom are arranged in shells about a central nucleus. I told you about the D shell electrons being really special in transition metals in EE 7 and 8.

A complete electron shell always contains pairs of electrons with opposite spins. This is a quantum rule, not a Jack Kruse rule. Hence a complete shell has no net magnetic moment when the electrons are present because the spins oppose one another. In the transition metals such as iron, cobalt and nickel, there are unpaired electrons, **not only in the outer shell but also in an inner D shell.** In iron, for instance, five of the six electrons in the n = 3, l = 2 subshell have parallel spins, so that iron atoms have appreciable magnetic moments and are referred to as ferromagnetic. This is what makes these metals special for biology. These electrons become able to do some amazing things when they can freely float in a semiconductor lattice. Water and collagen are that lattice.

### Martin — 2013-11-10T16:42:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-largest-biologic-experiment-ever-done-on-humans.8719/post-100321>

_"Static fields affect organisms by their ability to orientate magnetically sensitive molecules and by deflecting endogenous electrical currents."_

_"Some molecules can orientate themselves in a magnetic field. Like water and biologic proteins with transition metals in them."
_
_Boom!_ Critical, indeed!

Big

### Jack Kruse — 2013-11-10T17:20:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-largest-biologic-experiment-ever-done-on-humans.8719/post-100322>

So lets go back to EE 7.......and the comment I made about super nova's and about earthquake lights that occur just prior to an earthquake.......and explain why both effects are tied to the QED actions of these transition metals as they undergo phase transitions due to environmental alterations.........

Transition metals are the only metals that can be magnetized. And on being magnetized, the domains of these electrons and in their respective shells, progressively align themselves until all are oriented in the same direction in parallel with the external field. This is called reducing entropy........because order is being brought to bear by the external magnetic field either on a star, the tectonic plate, or for biology it is the Earth's magnetic field.

SuperNova: Beyond a certain temperature, the atomic alignment within the electron domains disappears, and a ferromagnetic material becomes merely paramagnetic. The temperature at which this happens, the Curie point, is 770Â°C for iron. Well the temperature in the core of star that has burned all its gaseous fuel certainly exceeds 770 degrees C. And when this happens Nickel begins to emit energy from its atomic nucleus. This energy is due to negative beta decay of its D shell electrons. This is tied to the video from the Cal Berkley cosmologist I linked to in EE7 for those of you not following the details along......As the star continues to exhaust all fuel......the core of the star becomes iron and it begins to collapse in on itself due to the high temperatures. Here is where the Curie point matters. At extreme temperature or pressures all transition metals lose their atomic order and their electron domains begin to roam freely in the core of the star.........This creates massive nuclear energy in the form of electrons flying all over the place, but it also makes the inner core of the star very very chaotic. This is called an increase in entropy. November 2013 webinar alert. ....These emitted energized photons from these chaotic transition metals then begin to delocalize and create nuclear collisions to change the atomic structure of iron and nickel.....and the star explodes.......In the explosion the electrns cause atomic nuclei of the transition metals to fuse to make new elements........They make all the heavier and lighter metals most of you know about like Mg, Ca, gold, and platinum. This is the stardust we are made from. This also means that the information from that explosion is carried in those new elements and remains there until a cell can order and re energize the atomic nuclei once again in the new environment. On Earth, we use the photoelectric effect, water chemistry and the Earth magnetic field to create this set of requirements to regain order. (think entropy) Now go listen to the November 2013 webinar.............

OK now come back and read the rest of this.........thread.

Atoms and molecules with fewer unpaired electrons do not form electron domains at ordinary temperatures, they simply become paramagnetic or diamagnetic. A paramagnetic atom or molecule has a permanent dipole moment ( a magnet all the time) , and therefore tends to align itself in the direction of the applied field it finds itself in. A diamagnetic atom or molecule, on the other hand, has **no permanent dipole moment** ( means it can be magnetic when the field allows for it, but normally it is not)

When an applied magnetic field is added to the new environment of these atoms ( OK now think the transition metal proteins or water in you) This immediately affects the orbital motion of the electrons in such a way as to produce a magnetic moment in the opposite direction to the applied magnetic field. Remember Maxwell's 4 laws of electromagnetism show us that the electric and magnetic fields are at 90 degree angles to one another............This is why this can happen. An MRI takes full advantage of this aspect of these atoms in you.

Want to see where all this knowledge becomes really interesting...........for biology when you understand that the atomic physics and the action of electrons is primordial to all biochemistry? **_Pure samples of membrane lipids are known to align themselves in an external magnetic field._** Hey, did you know the human brain has more lipid membranes than any other organ? Think EE 5 now.......maybe stop and have a re read here.

OK next step in understanding:

It has been suggested that the effect of static magnetic fields (think Earth or magnetico but both do vary a small bit) on the permeability of lipid vesicles to drugs is due to the summation of diamagnetic alignments of large molecular aggregates to the external field occurring near membrane phase transition temperatures. I talked to you about phase transition temperatures when we spoke about water......a phase transition is gas to liquid and liquid to water etc.......all of these are coupled to what? TEMPERATURE

Guess what else biology does not yet realize? Proteins are also diamagnetic due to the planar peptide bonds, which, in the α-helix, in particular, are all aligned along the axis, and this will give rise to substantial diamagnetic anisotropy. Stop.......and re listen to the November 2013 webinar even before you look up what diamagnetic anisotropy means..........
Anisotropy is just another fancy word for making order from the atomic chaos naturally found. You can use many things to improve anisotropy and transition metals that become diamagnetic become ordered. Remember from our above lesson that a diamagnet is not a permanent magnet!!!! These actions are important.......as you will see as the blog continues on because diamagnetic atoms allow us to create Maxwell demons. (November 2013 webinar alert) This is why all biologic proteins have transition metals in them. They have the innate atomic ability to create order using no energy. This allows them to skate around the second law of thermodynamics when they are coupled in reactions. All biochemistry is coupled ironically. See Evolution really really really understands transition metals well. She rubbed off the print of the dollar bill when she used this trick in natures laws to create energy from nada. It turns out all conductors exhibit an effective diamagnetism when they experience a **changing magnetic field**. I told you we are made up of tons of different kinds of semiconductors. This means with the EArth's normal small varying magnetic field fluxes and currents can be made within those semiconductors. guess what else. With time these cycles (think circadian or thermohaline, or vortices) also become coupled......naturally without using energy, while continuing to bring order to a cell by reducing its entropy!!!!

How? The Lorentz force on electrons causes them to circulate around forming eddy currents. (think whirlpool, hurricane, tornando, think EE 4, and the Quantum sleep webinar when thinking of eddy currents.) Eddy current of delocalized electrons are small tiny vortices of electron energy that biology can use to never break the second law of thermodynamics. This is how life orders itself using the laws of nature you learned about in EE4. The electron "eddy currents", are a type of electric current. What did i teach you above about electric currents? They induce a magnetic field themselves at 90 degree angles. (think how the circle of willis acts in your brain from EE 5) Electron eddy currents then produce an induced magnetic field **opposite the applied field**, resisting the conductor's motion. What did i tell you about "resisting molecular motion" in the November 2013 webinar using Ling's experiments? Life stops Einstein's Brownian motion (Think QB 8 have a re read for the folks not keeping up) on proteins to create negative entropy or reduced chaos.........when chaos is reduced life can self assemble base upon what is contained in the DNA code. You begin to realize something more profound.........and see why biology is really clueless at the core.........they believe the answer to life is all in DNA.......yet I just showed you it is not. The DNA code is only a partial story to life because it requires the conditions of existence that only exist on Earth. What orders biology? Quantum mechanics................is the short answer. What did EE 8 say? Does physics dictate biology? Short answer? Yep.

So diamagnetism is another example of a Maxwell demon. It gives order to atoms by giving it the property of being **directionally dependent**, as opposed to isotropy, which implies identical properties in all directions. Here you can see this is another fancy way to describe order and disorder with no transfer of energy required. It can be defined as a difference, when measured along different axes, in a material's physical or mechanical properties (absorbance, refractive index, conductivity, tensile strength, etc.) An example of anisotropy in nature is the light coming through a polarizer. (Think your sunglasses or better yet think about your retina or a chloroplast membrane (November 2013 webinar alert) And another example of anisotropy is found in ordinary wood we burn, which is easier to split along its grain than against it. That is the inherent anisotropy in this material.

So why is this big?

### Jack Kruse — 2013-11-10T17:20:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-largest-biologic-experiment-ever-done-on-humans.8719/post-100323>

All life organizes using these simple ideas of fractal design. Similar anisotropies exists for other regular arrangements of the peptide bond as well as for nucleic acids in biology. Thus, chloroplasts, the outer segment of the rods in the retina , photosynthetic algae and bacteria, the purple membrane of the latter and nucleic acids have all been successfully oriented in moderately strong static magnetic fields of several kiloG (1 Gauss = 10−4 Tesla) in many many experiments, yet biology has no clue.........because they don't understand how physics arranges for life to happen using Maxwell demons and transition metal electrons.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-11-10T17:59:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-largest-biologic-experiment-ever-done-on-humans.8719/post-100329>

Because an altered electromagnetic field has massive implications at the cell membrane because of the QED principles in this thread.
Proteins embedded in membrane lipids belong to a large class of molecules called liquid crystals. Water is the best example of a liquid crystal but there are tons of others in physics. Liquid metal superconductors like helium or hydrogen are great examples. Superconductors may be considered perfect diamagnets (χv = −1), since they NATURALLY expel all fields (except in a thin surface layer) due to the Meissner effect. (Taught you about the Meissner effect in EE 4, 5, and 6.)
To be clear, however, this effect is not due to electron eddy currents, as in ordinary diamagnetic materials. Water becomes a superconductor when it interacts with the proteins embedded in a lipid membrane.

Water chemistry: If a powerful magnet (Think your transition metal proteins in the lipid membrane) becomes covered with a layer of thin water (that is thin compared to the diameter of the magnet) then the field of the magnet **significantly repels the water**, naturally with no use of energy using QED. This action creates and exclusion zone. And exclusion zone is an empty space where no atoms exist. In this exclusion zone water begins to have some amazing properties that physicists and electrical engineer's take full advantage of for your modern tech gadgets. The simple proof that this happens naturally is that this action causes a slight dimple in the water's surface that may be seen by its reflection.

So water, protein and membrane lipids can exist in a number of mesophases, (phases that are neither solid nor liquid, but in between). These mesophases are characterised by long-range order in which all the molecules are aligned in quasicrystalline arrays based upon the field of action they are in......... Liquid crystals are relatively easily aligned with electric and magnetic fields, which is the basis of the liquid crystal display screens that come with modern TV's, Iphones and Ipads, watches, laptop computers and every computer game made today. Biology does the same thing with with different substances........you just don't realize it yet but you will. (Novemeber 2013 webinar alert)

# Thread: How microwave energy heats water.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-microwave-energy-heats-water.8721/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### CindyB — 2013-11-10T18:46:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-microwave-energy-heats-water.8721/post-100331>

Currently reading "Cross Currents" from Becker. He references a Scientific American article written for "The Amateur Scientist" column by Jearl Walker. Found the article on-line here:

[http://jesseenterprises.net/amsci/1987/02/1987-02-fs.html](http://jesseenterprises.net/amsci/1987/02/1987-02-fs.html)

"THE SECRET OF A MICROWAVE oven's fast action is that water in the food rapidly absorbs the energy of the waves. At first thought this is surprising. In most cases where a material absorbs light or other types of electromagnetic radiation there must be a resonant match between the energy of the radiation and the energy changes possible in the atoms and molecules of the material. Water absorbs infrared radiation resonantly, but the frequency of microwaves is far too low for a resonant match. How, then, is energy transferred from the microwaves to the random thermal motion of the molecules of water? Several hypotheses I shall discuss represent a groping toward the answer. First one needs a bit of background on the physics of microwaves and the water molecule (H2O), consisting of two atoms of hydrogen and one atom of oxygen."​

Understanding how imparting (thermal) motion can happen with 2.45 GHz irradiation helps me understand how other frequencies (e.g. harmonics, et.al.) might impart change on cellular chemo-dynamics (e.g. diffusion, stearic hindrance, surface adhesion, etc.)

This jesseenterprises.net website is a treasure trove of articles explaining deep science to the amateur scientist. Fun!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-11-10T19:47:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-microwave-energy-heats-water.8721/post-100334>

everything in that link is in the Thread I made this AM on biologic coherence in the doctors forum ironically. Must be quantum biology at work. I describe precise how it happens........

So I was searching for something else and then this comment on EMF8 hit me so much, I had to share. Love these snippets.....I mean bombs.

Carbs do not hold water.......they cause us to dehydrate. This is why glucose laden drinks stimulate thirst. Since carbs are ATP oor foods most come packed in their evolutionary packages with a high water contents to offset their lack of electron power. This is why carbs grow in longer light cycles when the photons from the sun are most powerful........to draw us out into the sun to acquire the fruit so we can gain directly from the photoelectric effect. Today humans have radically disturbed that balance. If the water is treated or fluoridated the water is essentially useless to a biologic system and will be urinated out and not used to rehydrate our collagen carbon nanotubes. When this happens we also lose magnesium because mag is hydrophillic as many people seem to have forgotten. This is another reason why people who eat too many carbs, like a T2D, become Mg depleted and dehydrate easy. The fluoride is a dielectric to DHA in neurons and displaces iodine in the brain's cell membranes and especially at synapses and it causes avalanche collapse of the semiconducting current. This is why it screws our ability to water superconduct. Re read Brain gut 12 and the top ten paleo supplement blog post carefully.......the info is there and the next few blogs are going to open that discussion more. ~ Jack Kruse

**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbs-in-broth.8730/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jasmin — 2013-11-11T08:25:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbs-in-broth.8730/post-100405>

Hope that this is not a stupid question, but when I make broth I use bones from grassfed animals or the bones and heads from fish and I use carrots celery, garlic, onions and herbs for flavor. I remove the vegetables when the broth is finish, but is the carbs from the vegetables now in the broth???

### yewwei.tan — 2013-11-11T10:38:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbs-in-broth.8730/post-100408>

Yup. Assuming you boil the broth for a long time, the broth ends up tasting sweet, and I suspect that any soluble carbohydrates would get into the broth (in the case of onions, probably half the total carbohydrate).

### Jack Kruse — 2013-11-11T14:47:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbs-in-broth.8730/post-100418>

i do the same thing.

### Jasmin — 2013-11-15T09:15:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbs-in-broth.8730/post-100765>

I boil the broth for a long time and it taste sweet - I will have to keep track with the amount of vegetables and then calculate with half the total carbs.
Thank you so much

# Thread: Not sure if I am leptin sensitive yet?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-if-i-am-leptin-sensitive-yet.8732/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Roman1 — 2013-11-11T19:24:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-if-i-am-leptin-sensitive-yet.8732/post-100434>

I am not sure if I am leptin sensitive ,want to continue HIIT exercise\\ heavy lifting.
I am 27yr old male ,underweight ,5.67 ft and ~100LB.
I began doing leptin reset on april or may of 2012 and some time afterwards added c.t... for some time.
Epi-paleo when it first appeared. 2 meals a day. .I am not cold adapted yet. I have a thyroid issue, probably hypothyroid.
Still looking for a doc who could do the needed tests.
I watch the circadian cycles: look at the sky in the morning, use glasses. Watch EMFs in my apartment.

SIGNS IT’S WORKING:
\-Change in your sweating pattern: probably less ,I think ,but hard to tell b\\c of the wheather...
\-Energy levels ,not sure but sometimes it feels like they rose a little bit however
my feet (legs?) still get so tired after some walk.
\-Hunger and cravings did disappear ,probably 6+ months ago ,but it seems like cravings have
somewhat reappeared ,after I began lowering my carbs, in preparation for winter?
I usually can hold off eating easily for 6+ hours.

\-When I awaken I am in hurry for the BAB but maybe I am a bit more refreshed ,if my sleep was good, which usually is not ...
\-Men: quicker weight loss?? I think my body 'looks' a little bit better than before I started, like by 1-2% ?
less bloated all the time however I have indigestion or something. Still somewhat skinny fat b\\c of the stress...

Are there any other signs? Any advice would be very appreciated.
Thank you!

EDIT:
Changes in appearance: hand nails got so much worse in presentation. Less dead skin on my feet.

### caroline — 2013-11-11T22:05:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-if-i-am-leptin-sensitive-yet.8732/post-100450>

Maybe hang in there for a while yet ......You still have stuff to sort out - thyroid etc. weight gain....

What about walking, swimming? Are you grounding? hug a tree or two? Walk in a forest? go camping....

what about non native EMF? at work?

### Roman1 — 2013-11-12T18:54:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-if-i-am-leptin-sensitive-yet.8732/post-100519>

Thanks. I do walk but it starts to hurt after a short while... esp. feet probably b\\c I have a Flat Feet condition ......
I sleep on a 5g magnetico SLEEP PAD if that counts but the mattress is an old 8"+. I did grounding before ,
not much now thanks to the increased stress outside which is rarely bearable. I do try to touch a tree but unsure if
I do it right or long enough.

I have posted yesterday but it has been deleted or not posted and now I can\`t remember it.
Fifth night was ruined by mosquitoes. Last night's sleep was better.

I do not have a smart meter, nor do the neighbours ,However, a lot of wifi signals everywhere.
There is a cell tower 120yard away from my bedroom. No electrical device plugged in at night. Computer in room.

I did work in the past for a short while but it was too demanding for me..... Now looking for a new one.
I want to try flyers home delivery job ,but I worry that I won\`t have enough strenth and such for it,
hence a good reason to begin HIIT exercise asap.

### caroline — 2013-11-12T23:47:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/not-sure-if-i-am-leptin-sensitive-yet.8732/post-100542>

Roman - in my opinion you should wait to exercise .... you really need to assess your EMF situation and improve your conditions of existence. Is your computer wired in? if not - it needs to be.

I am pretty sure that Dr. K. would tell you to do everything possible to fix your space. That will be a lot of your problem

Stress outside?? what does that mean? Hugging a tree is simple - just hug a tree, lay under a tree - especially a conifer and absorb it's energy .... be in nature as much as possible - walk barefoot as much as possible wit as little clothes as possible.

I will get back to you later after I reread your posts.....

Where do you live?

Can you get your computer out of your bedroom? You need to!

# Thread: WAPF President Sally Fallon on Robb Wolf.......interesting.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wapf-president-sally-fallon-on-robb-wolf-interesting.8736/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2013-11-11T21:29:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wapf-president-sally-fallon-on-robb-wolf-interesting.8736/post-100447>

I have no idea why it took Sally 3 years to review this book. There must be some trouble in paleo paradise with WAPF members. I think most of what she says in here can be interpreted as sour grapes but I think she is spot on when it comes to her complaints about the Vitamin A issue in the brain and what she pointed out about fish oil. While she correctly pointed out the dietary decoupling she whiffed badly on the real paleo problem with Vitamin a recycling in the brain, non native EMF and blue light destroy the photoperiod and this is a double whammy to those who do not realize it. Robb has said on his podcast he may have overdone his rec's on fish oil but interestingly in later printings of the book it still has not be removed.I also think calling it the original human diet shows complete lack of understanding of neurobiology and its evolution. A refresher course on Dr. Cunnane and Crawford's work should have eliminated that from the title because you can not have a human brain without a ton of seafood. Grass fed meat is not that ticket.

I personally think Ms. Fallon is going to create a war with Robb's followers. Paleo people are quite harsh when you attack one of their leaders, even when the criticism is worth a discussion.

The timing sure is odd.

[http://www.westonaprice.org/thumbs-down-reviews/the-paleo-solution-byrobb-wolf](http://www.westonaprice.org/thumbs-down-reviews/the-paleo-solution-byrobb-wolf)

### Danco3636 — 2013-11-12T01:45:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wapf-president-sally-fallon-on-robb-wolf-interesting.8736/post-100468>

Interesting find....

### smart cat — 2013-11-22T16:22:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/wapf-president-sally-fallon-on-robb-wolf-interesting.8736/post-101354>

I believe Sally Falloms war agaist the Paleio Community is becaue WAPF is not very succesful in attracting new members while Paleo is becoming more and more popular. It's about market share I think.

Here is a nice blogpost with lots of comments about the debate:

Why Weston Price and Paleo Will Not Survive Without Each Other

[http://www.ancestralizeme.com/2013/...nd-paleo-will-not-survive-without-each-other/](http://www.ancestralizeme.com/2013/11/11/why-weston-price-and-paleo-will-not-survive-without-each-other/)

# Thread: Lustig's new video......extremely bad.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lustigs-new-video-extremely-bad.8743/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2013-11-12T06:56:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lustigs-new-video-extremely-bad.8743/post-100484>

I was willing to give him rope after his first video.......it was good but he made a lot of mistakes in it by leaving out leptin.........then in this one he adds it and completely botches it BADLY. He is using this fructose, to get a political solution using horrible science.

[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ceFyF9px20Y#t=4413](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ceFyF9px20Y#t=4413)

All of slides can be explained by many other factors.......like EMF's effect on the hypothalamus and the inner mitochondrial membrane. When he showed the fat kid with the brain tumor who was irradiated and said the neurons were missing I could not handle it. X-rays are high energy EMF's. Geopathic stressors of the Middle East countries........he just misses the boat. Causal medical inference? WOW. He needs a serious dose of physical chemistry and QED. The pathways he shows all use proteins with transition metals at every step. Every hormone he mentions does too..........A lot of what he says is true but the conclusions he draws.........does not support the the things he claims are true.

When he mentions stress.......and then FB........dopamine. WOW he is brain stem clueless..........

At AHS 2011 after hearing him speak live, I was concerned about some of the incorrect things he said about leptin. In this talk he goes even deeper into the mess. I came away saying we need to give him some rope to see where he is really headed.........in this video he shows us he is 100% completely this generations Ancel Keys..........really really disappointed in where he is going. Dr. Feinman pegged him correctly in 2011. He was sure but I was not. Now I am too.

I am no fan of evidence based medicine but this talk is exceptionally bad in trying to peel the onion skin back to find the truth............

### nonchalant — 2013-11-12T17:43:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lustigs-new-video-extremely-bad.8743/post-100515>

He lost me when he called for government action to stop people from eating sugar. Ancel Keys? Ouch. But yes, they are following a similar path for resolution of their interpretation of the problem.

Not sure how accurate this is, but wow. I have to minimize my GPS as of today.

[http://www.novamind.com/connect/nm\_...82B2D92-AFE7-4A1C-A570-ADC4133A1FB0/880294264](http://www.novamind.com/connect/nm_documents/show_branch/Power/B82B2D92-AFE7-4A1C-A570-ADC4133A1FB0/880294264)

I wish those Tesla folks would build a low EMF car....

Whenever I see those ads about how great smart cars are - and now with more smartness to track teenage driving - I wanna puke.

### nonchalant — 2013-11-12T17:33:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-in-cars.8744/post-100513>

Interesting. Anti-lock brakes, even the radial tires?

### cantweight — 2013-11-12T17:46:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-in-cars.8744/post-100516>

In 2007 I bought a honda minivan. It was really nice all the bells and whistles. My kids were 7, 5, and 2...it was perfect for us. I told my husband every time I started that car it felt like it was sucking out my soul. We sold it after one year I couldn't take it. I thought it was my ego that didnt want to be a "minivan mom" but I am sure now it was the EMF exposure. I am still happy as a clam in my almost 10 yr old suburban.

### Martin — 2013-11-12T20:19:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-in-cars.8744/post-100528>

My '98 Dudge Ram truck (gasoline engine) is fairly low-tech, but still got an average of 0.5 gauss reading at my knees with the engine running. But turn on the radio, heater, or wipers - in other words, anything with an electric motor or big magnets(speakers), and it spikes up to over 1.0 gauss.

I'm checking DW's late model car when my RF meter that I rented arrives. NOT going to be pretty, methinks.

BGH

### Shijin13 — 2013-11-12T20:31:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-in-cars.8744/post-100530>

I've got a 2011 Subaru Outback - MT. No bluetooth or WiFi in it. thought it does have seat heaters (which I don't turn on), Radio, Wipers, heated mirrors etc. I haven't been turning on the heater  CT in the car.

I only drive MT. Find one that's used in good condition is a PITA. took my husband 8 mths to find a used truck w/MT.

I'd be interested in seeing the difference btwn the same vehicle - w/a MT and an AT.

then look at the same vehicle from 1980 w/a MT and one from today w/a MT.

(MT = manual transmission)

I can't wait until the kids are old enough to start using a compass and learning orienteering. I'd love to do geo-cashing with them as well. but I really don't want to use a hand held gps.

### JanSz — 2013-11-19T19:17:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-in-cars.8744/post-101034>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=100710)
>
> I think it is a double whammy of EMF and also not using your spatial sense. I know when I take an alternate route it can be a challenge, especially if the sun is behind clouds and the roads are curvy. I feel a real sense of accomplishment if I arrive without taking wrong turns.

Spatial sense when driving is good thing to have, but to a point.
In rural area there is no question that it is helpful,
but in metropolitan areas may not be as useful due to changing traffic conditions.

[http://www.siriusxm.com/siriusxmtraffic](http://www.siriusxm.com/siriusxmtraffic)

My subscription to SiriusXM Traffic will expire soon.
**What is your experience with this service?
**
My daughter and her husband do not care for it, but they have smart phones and are able to get better service with their apps.
I am newer sure how good is the advice from SiriusXM. (Feels annoying constantly changing roads).
Today around 6AM I-80 (E-W)was blocked solidly both ways, many people had to reroute via RT46, I-287 (N-S) is always busy.

If you decide to drive an older car with few or no chips, your mitochondria will still be under attack from other cars self driving radars, wi-fi's and all the microwave towers that are next to all major roads.

### Martha Ray — 2019-07-24T05:15:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-in-cars.8744/post-274909>

Interesting tread. I am driving a ’99 CRV ‘Silver Cord’ that I love and my DH is driving a white ’10 Hybrid Ford Fusion ‘Snowball’. I name cars and computers. We have lighter colored cars because we believe they are safer due to better visibility. DH loves the gas mileage of the Hybrid but I’ve wondered about it with my EMF sensitivity. Strangly as a passenger it seems to charge me up but when driving it I do not feel as energized. As Jack Kruse mentioned “**the amount of EMF present is very close in a car cockpit.”** My DO doctor said he has noticed issues with some of his patients--some drain and some charge. He feels it is good for me…but I am not so sure. Also feel it may be a factor with DH bouts of Afib. He got the car, had cataract surgery and our little 15 yr Italian Greyhound all passed shortly before his Afib attacks began 5 years ago.

I’ve wanted a Ford Flex for about 5 years. I rented one on one of my annual trips to Texas and felt it was more of a vacation than the trip… and I loved the trip. I felt so safe in Houston traffic, even in San Antonio and Austin where I don’t know the roads nearly as well. On trips I depend on a Garmin brand GPS… guess they are all the same kinda bad.

I have been examining most everything with the lens of ‘is this healthy’ for 10 years… a challenge for sure and discouraging at times but convinced that it is worthwhile!

### Dan2 — 2020-07-31T03:44:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-in-cars.8744/post-291276>

Mahindra Roxor

[https://www.google.com/search?q=mahindra+roxor&ie=utf-8&oe=utf-8&client=firefox-b-ab](https://www.google.com/search?q=mahindra+roxor&ie=utf-8&oe=utf-8&client=firefox-b-ab)

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mahindra\_Thar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mahindra_Thar)
"On March 2, 2018, Mahindra Automotive North America unveiled a variant of the Thar for United States market called the **[Roxor](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mahindra_Roxor)** at its assembly plant in [Auburn Hills, Michigan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Auburn_Hills,_Michigan). It is assembled from [complete knock-down](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Knock-down_kit) kits. Unlike the Thar, the Roxor is not street legal, and is positioned as a [side-by-side](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Side_by_Side_%28UTV%29) [off-road vehicle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Off-road_vehicle) with a top speed of 45 mph. The Roxor is powered by a 2.5L turbodiesel, four-cylinder engine producing 62 hp and 144 lb-ft of torque, mated to a five-speed manual transmission. An automatic transmission is available as an option. It is only available with two seats, a 148-inch body length, and a 96-inch wheelbase.[\[2\]"](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mahindra_Thar#cite_note-2)

Specs
[https://d3lwr7t4tsy765.cloudfront.net/Specs/ROXOR-2020MY-SpecSheet.pdf](https://d3lwr7t4tsy765.cloudfront.net/Specs/ROXOR-2020MY-SpecSheet.pdf)
No anti-lock brakes, says top speed 55 mph here, 144 ft lbs torque, 3490 lb towing capacity, max towing speed 15 mph. Maybe not the best for taking a camper on a road trip.
Starting price MSRP $16,599 US

How to make it street legal
[https://teamroxor.com/threads/what-is-needed-to-street-legal-a-mahindra-roxor.5/](https://teamroxor.com/threads/what-is-needed-to-street-legal-a-mahindra-roxor.5/)

How to remove the speed limiter so it can go 70 mph+
[https://www.dirtlegal.com/blog/2018...-your-mahindra-roxor-and-unlock-its-top-speed](https://www.dirtlegal.com/blog/2018/7/14/heres-how-to-derestrict-your-mahindra-roxor-and-unlock-its-top-speed)

### cantweight — 2013-11-12T20:58:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-labs-i-think-its-working.8751/post-100531>

In September my TSH was low .329 so I lowered my thyroid dose from 150 to 120 mg Armour. I gave it a month to settle and tested again. New labs below, I am thinking I will drop my dose again as TSH is still relatively low and have had several episodes of racing heart (my too much thyroid signal). I think this sucker is finally coming back to life. CT/iodine one two punch  Not sure about the antibodies though....any input on them and thoughts on lowering dosage? Thanks!

TSH .48 .40-4.50

T3Rev 13 8-25ng/dl

T3Total 89 76-181ng/dl

T3Free 3.0 2.3-4.2pg/ml

Thyroglobulin AB <20

Thyroid Peroxidase AB 14

### nicld — 2013-11-13T00:09:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-labs-i-think-its-working.8751/post-100548>

Very interesting.

When my TSH was below normal (.29) I felt fine and was sleeping though the night. The doc knock my Cytomel down a bit based on the last round of tests and that seemed to cause all sorts of issues and I am only on 60mg of Armour. I stopped sleeping through the night, 45 minutes of CT was really hard to do without shivering (used to be able to do almost 2 hours no problem) and I was getting cold again.

I just upped the Cytomel back to where I was when I was feeling good and almost slept through the night last night for the first time in almost 2 weeks. Plus I am just feeling calmer. When the TSH was showing low, my ratios were really good I think (43 this time versus 11 before). I am planning on getting retested in Dec so I will be curious to see what they are then and how I feel.

I am forever grateful for all the learning that I have received on this site. Can’t wait to see how much more I will learn in the boot camp. I just have to remember to have my labs spreadsheet open to see how everything relates to my N=1.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: New Cholesterol Guidelines issued
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-cholesterol-guidelines-issued.8752/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ATL_Paleo — 2013-11-13T01:09:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-cholesterol-guidelines-issued.8752/post-100560>

On first glance, it appears the new guidelines have ditched the above 200 number for total cholesterol. One of the 4 guidelines replacing it is above 190 for LDL cholesterol. This seems to make a bit more sense. ..... But I agree, statins are still bad.

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

[http://www.medscape.com/viewarticle/814152](http://www.medscape.com/viewarticle/814152)

The four major primary- and secondary-prevention patient groups who should be treated with statins were identified on the basis of randomized, controlled clinical trials showing that the benefit of treatment outweighed the risk of adverse events. The four treatment groups include:

Individuals with clinical atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease.

Individuals with LDL-cholesterol levels >190 mg/dL, such as those with familial hypercholesterolemia.

Individuals with diabetes aged 40 to 75 years old with LDL-cholesterol levels between 70 and 189 mg/dL and without evidence of atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease.

Individuals without evidence of cardiovascular disease or diabetes but who have LDL-cholesterol levels between 70 and 189 mg/dL and a 10-year risk of atherosclerotic cardiovascular disease >7.5%.

### Destiny — 2013-11-13T13:52:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-cholesterol-guidelines-issued.8752/post-100611>

> [ATL\_Paleo said:](/goto/post?id=100560)
>
> On first glance, it appears the new guidelines have ditched the above 200 number for total cholesterol. One of the 4 guidelines replacing it is above 190 for LDL cholesterol. This seems to make a bit more sense. ..... But I agree, statins are still bad.
>
> \*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
> It sounds to me that there is even a bigger push towards statins with these guidelines.

Anyone else familiar with this story? Pretty neat stuff... The epitome of the PPP?

I bet his cave is pretty awesome. Do you think he has the magnetico sleep pad, or just uses the earth pulse?

### Josh — 2014-03-29T02:20:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/prahlad-jani-the-yogi-who-hasnt-eaten-drank-for-70-years.8754/post-118597>

This guy is beyond mind blowing......

> The Shivapuri Baba aka Swami Govindanath Bharati was born in a rich Brahmin household in the Indian state of Kerala in 1826. At a tender age of 18, he decided to abandon the world and seek for absolute truth. Here, Bennett is talking of the time when "the future Edward VII was a three-year old infant in Buckingham Palace while the great Indian saint Ramakrishna was ten years old." Govindananda Bharati followed the "path of Rtambhara Prajna or absolute realization of God beyond all forms and images." The writer explains that this stage is not reached until there is a progressive elimination of all that support and comfort the mind. He further explains that when all is stripped away, and the soul enters the absolute nakedness that is beyond understanding can it make the final act of the Pure Will whereby all distinctions disappear and all the Individual and the Absolute are made one.
>
> Although an Indian sage, the Shivapuri Baba spent over 40 years of his life in Nepal. There is an interesting account of how he hoodwinked the Ranas and entered a country virtually closed to the foreigners. On March 1926, Swami Govindanath first visited Nepal, accompanied by a horde of Indian Shadhus on their way to pay homage to Lord Pashupatinath. The Indian sage entered Nepal at a time when the country was virtually closed to foreigners and pilgrims alike. Only during the holy festival of Shivaratri, Hindu pilgrims were allowed to enter the country on the condition that they would leave within one week.
>
> Govindananda was highly fascinated by the serenity of the Pashupati temple complex and the jungles lying in the outskirts of the city. On the day of his eventual return to India, He luckily met a Wilkinson who had then become the British resident in Nepal. It was he who coaxed the Rana rulers to let the Indian sage settle in the jungle lying in the outskirts of the city- the place where he would shed his identity of Govindanath Bharati and emerge as the saintly old man of the Shivapuri forest, the Shivapuri Baba. **_He breathed his last in the same forest retreat in the Himalayan foothills. He died at the great age of 137 years old. The Shivapuri Baba was the first modern yogi to transplant the wisdom of the Indian sub-continent to the West. While on a visit to England, he had no fewer than 18 audiences with Queen Victoria, who was deeply influenced by his ability to make her see beyond "the veil of death._**" His stay in England was the longest part of his pilgrimage although he was equally popular across the Atlantic. While analyzing the immense popularity of Govindanath, Bennett rightly observes, "His excellent English, enlivened with the apt stories from the Mahabharata or Ramayana, or even, I suspect, sometimes invented by himself, his quick wit, and his profound wisdom; and no doubt, most of all, the irresistible attraction that the power of sanctity exerts upon even half-receptive people-combined to make him sought after by the hostesses of the end of the century."
>
> **_Bennett has listed a number of amusing anecdotes about the Shivapuri Baba. Notably, among them is his visit with the great 20th century dramatist, George Bernard Shaw. Although a learned philosopher, he harbored a deep contempt for saints and their world. Therefore, during his encounter with the Shivapuri Baba, he said, "You Indian saints are the most useless of men; you have no respect for time." The latter's reply was something like this, "It is you who are the slaves of time. I live in eternity." No wonder, the Baba was blessed with a very long life, which, after his awakening at the age of 50, he dedicated entirely to the spiritual welfare of others._**
>
> Another anecdote concerns the visit of Indian President Dr Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan on the occasion of King Mahendra's coronation. Having disembarked from the plane, he straightaway headed towards the retreat of the Shivapuri Baba, of whom he had heard so much. When Radhakrishnan queried about his teachings, which he beautifully summed up in a sentence "I teach three disciplines- spiritual, moral and physical."
>
> **_The Shivapuri Baba taught the principle of Swadharma, which believes that as humans we have three principle duties: first, physical duty towards our self. It mainly consists of maintaining body and mind through proper livelihood, including the obligation to help one's dependents to accomplish the same.
>
> The second is one's moral duty, consisting in remaining sensitive to the obligation to seek the truth 24 hours a day. And the third, spiritual duty, means the worship of the Divine. He felt certain that if we attend carefully to the first two duties for a decade, we will naturally become able to fulfill the third duty. Physical discipline, he noted, brings pleasure. Moral discipline gives us serenity. Spiritual discipline yields deep peace and ultimate happiness._**
>
> The Shivapuri Baba was dismissive of conventional yogic paths, because he saw in them potential distractions that might keep a person from performing the three duties. On closer inspection, his own sensible prescriptions are in fact a form of yoga. Only rare individuals can devote themselves directly to the pursuit of enlightenment. "All of Yoga, in a way, is preparation for our final hour on earth. It matters how we exit from life, because death is only a doorway to another state of existence."

[http://www.bennettbooks.org/store/m...de=BB&Product\_Code=LONGP-GM&Category\_Code=211](http://www.bennettbooks.org/store/merchant.mvc?Screen=PROD&Store_Code=BB&Product_Code=LONGP-GM&Category_Code=211)

# Thread: Squatting for elimination
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/squatting-for-elimination.8755/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### baudoman — 2013-11-13T04:17:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/squatting-for-elimination.8755/post-100594>

I'm very glad to have found a place where people agree with the notion of squatting to eliminate.
(As per Jack's reference to it in his Brain Gut 17 blog post)

I find it too sad every time I see a commercial for a constipation-relief product and think that there are millions of people suffering from something that may be solved in such a simple manner, if only they were aware of it and perhaps were advised to try it.
I think about toddlers growing up and the fact that they squat naturally to use the toilet and then suddenly we 'potty-train' them and force them to do it wrongly.

I think the world would be a better place if people feel true relief when they eliminate.

I'm looking for ideas on how can we make this happen - awareness of such an important method for relief?

Can't wait till the thyroid one next week. Going to revisit stuff and have some question ready.

### tellmisty — 2013-11-13T22:17:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/webinar-with-dr-tim-jackson-bootcamp.8756/post-100663>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=100626)
>
> I thought it was EXTREMELY helpful.
>
> Did not really know the difference between Ox bile and Betatine HCL and that my pregnenolone was in the crapper. Now to dig deep and find information on that and what to do to get it better. I am going to hold of on trying supplements till I get my next set of blood tests ran, like Audrey is saying. The more I heard about Misty's issues the more I am thinking that I am dealing with some of the same stuff.
>
> Can't wait till the thyroid one next week. Going to revisit stuff and have some question ready.

I have so many "pieces" that are off. But nothing is egregiously off -- to mainstream docs. That's what's been so frustrating. I'm seeing an integrated medicine doc, and even HE didn't really seem keen on running labs and digging deeper, because I do have some labs that seem pretty good and I don't have major symptoms. I just have a body that's mutating -- thyroid nodules, endometriosos, left ovary removed (those are the biggies). And docs give me these diagnosis with an accompanying, "And all your labs are normal! "

Makes me want to punch them in the throat.

So all along, my hunch has been: Something's not right with my thyroid. Something's not right with my hormones. Something's off with my liver.

And having serum labs was just ... kind of confirming something wasn't right, but it was reading the tea leaves, ya know? So I supplement, get rid of EMFs, have water delivered, do the orange glasses, eat squeaky clean ... and I just feel like those things keep my labs looking good enough that docs think everything is okay.

The Genova Complete Hormone Panel shattered the glass ceiling for me, thank God! I now have a REAL picture of what is effed up. And when Dr. Tim started breaking down for me why my cortisol was so high, and how it affected everything else, and nothing was going to get better until I figured out what the stressors were, that was HUGE. He went through my 23andme test and helped me to understand what all that crap meant (!!!) and how I needed to supplement to support methylation and a whole host of other genetic things. According to Dr Tim, until I get rid of the immune stressors, I won't be able to fix the other problems. He's mapping all of this out for us in the Heal Your Hormones Bootcamp.

I can't even put into words how freaking ECSTATIC I am to finally have a picture of what the f\*\*\* is wrong with me so I can get about the business of righting the ship. His sequence is awesome ... makes total sense, and is such a compliment to what Jack's been teaching us for the last few years.

As a side note, once I had these labs and started to understand precisely where I was positioned in this health labyrinth, I started combing through the blog and re-reading Jack's post. I was able to actually start to fit these posts around my own situation, which was so huge. I feel like the puzzle went from black-and-white to technicolor ... and we aren't done! I'm still waiting on my GI Effects test to come back, and I've had several titers ran. Finding out which viruses are high and getting more clarity on what the plan of attack should be.

I'm really pushing Dr. Tim to create a "manual" to accompany the 23andme / MTHFR results so people can reference it when they get their MTHFR reports back. That would be SO helpful to people.

He's really brilliant at helping you put your plan together ... he's got the sequencing down, which is helpful because going through the blog and trying to piece together your own plan is daunting, and we can really spin our wheels and waste a lot of $$$.

### caroline — 2013-11-13T22:38:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/webinar-with-dr-tim-jackson-bootcamp.8756/post-100666>

So well said Misty.......Dr. K. has been telling us for years to get our labs done and then go from there! I really liked Dr. Tim's approach - his sequencing........makes sooooo much sense.

Just the fact that he said he assumes almost every one one has a leaky gut - that is where we should start....don't even bother testing - until later if you need it.

He is giving us so much detail ..... but he is easy to understand- it is hard to remember it all. I definitely need multiple listens ...and I need more hours in the day!

When one things clicks - it leads us to another path sometimes that we hadn't considered or we just overlooked .... and, as you said, it leads us back to blogs that we didn't relate to the first couple of times.....

This is going to be an amazing bootcamp .......I can't even imagine the time y'all have put into this. Thankyou for arranging this for us...what an incredible opportunity.......

# Thread: Hi Everyone

**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
