Isn't Manganese a known neurotoxin in excess? I seem to remember there is a really narrow range of benefit, which can be achieved by food, and the toxicity starts at a very low dose.

Seems like a very scary thing to be supplementing if you have unknown sources in the diet?

Compare that to magnesium, where it is relatively difficult to see a toxic dose from supplementation. Magnesium seems like a no brainer. Manganese seems to warrant a little more caution.

Is there a way to test manganese levels reliably, so you could see if your diet is supplying it?

### Josh — 2014-03-18T06:57:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/essential-cofactors-for-pqq.8810/post-116124>

Dude, you need to read the literature on methylation pathways and apply it to yourself, otherwise you are mucking around in the dark with regards to your own N=1. This is a huge factor in how "toxic/stressful" something will be in a given system. Almost no one gets what is understood to date and there is soooo much more to work out. Then you need to figure out what your inflammatory/oxidative stressors are. Then make your own plan or find someone to help you and do some work and make the experiment on yourself. For me, Manganese has been life changing over the last 6 months and I am not dead yet. Take to heart Dr. Kruse's insight regarding EMF having a disproportionate effect on transition metals which to me means that all bets on how much we need and whether they will work as planned/hoped is off. I will take my own risks having had a front row seat to medicine and dying doctors my whole life.

You might make a different evaluation such as what does life look like without enough manganese and what does life look like without enough magnesium. Apply it to your situation/N=1 and see what seems relevant. You might also look at the supplement companies selling doses as high as 40mg and figure they have done some risk assessment as they are seriously exposed. Dr. Yasko's work details the relevance and importance and I have seen recommendations from that world for 60mg/day. I take an average of 30mg/day. In that world they recommend alternating hair and urine testing. Good to start reading. I have read about 6000 pages since I got here.....

Best of luck as always.....

>

How much manganese are you supplementing, and when/how do you take it?

I don't know enough about manganese to be critical of it in any specific dose. The general point was that \*some\* supplements have no toxicity at very large doses and real benefits at lower doses. So those supplements are a no-brainer, a real "free lunch".

Other supplements have definite toxicities at relatively low doses, and those supplements just require you to have knowledge and exercise care.

That's not a put-down of manganese. It's just about risk management in general.

### Josh — 2014-03-18T07:24:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/essential-cofactors-for-pqq.8810/post-116132>

As I suggested, it is worth spending some time with the people who have identified the issue led by Amy Yasko and her work on methylation. You can read what Dr. Kruse said about transition metals in the blogs. It really has a great deal to do with phenotypic expression with regards to methylation, your environment and the larger health issues one faces. In my perfunctory research, it seems that molybdenum and manganese are involved in a huge number of vital metalloenyme complexes and that there are many black boxes in our understanding to date. There is no money in minerals because they can not be patented. Of course this has not stopped the drug companies from making a prostate cancer drug out of molybdenum that probably has no more efficacy than a non-prescription amino acid chelate. Nor has it stopped the prescription of Lithium Carbonate as a prescrition drug while a less toxic and probably equally efficacious form is available that can be bought over the counter in Lithium Orotate. Look up the number of iatrogenic illnesses and deaths from manganese versus perhaps aspirin or penicillin....Have you checked the MMWR for your profession? Your living area?

[http://www.cdc.gov/mmwr/](http://www.cdc.gov/mmwr/)

> The Dose Makes the Poison:
>
> Here is a small sample of well-documented side effects, provided by the National Institutes of Health:
>
> • Swelling of the eyes, face, lips, tongue, or throat • Wheezing or difficulty breathing • Fast heartbeat • Fast breathing • Cold, clammy skin • Ringing in the ears • Loss of hearing • Bloody vomit • Bright red blood in stools
>
> This list should scare you. It should scare you because these aren’t side effects of anabolic steroids. These are common side effects of aspirin.
>
> Ferriss, Timothy (2010-12-14). The 4-Hour Body: An Uncommon Guide to Rapid Fat-Loss, Incredible Sex, and Becoming Superhuman (Kindle Locations 2939-2945). Random House, Inc.. Kindle Edition.

### caroline — 2013-11-20T06:06:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-jacksons-boot-amp-week-2.8811/post-101086>

Thanks Dr. Jackson and Dr. kruse and Misty and her team.

This boot camp is excellent - so much valuable information. I now can see so clearly what Dr. k. Always tells us.....it is all about context. I don't see how anyone could successfully navigate this on their own,

The potential for really screwing up is huge .....and doing untold damage.

### Josh — 2013-11-20T18:36:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-jacksons-boot-amp-week-2.8811/post-101121>

As I see it, Dr. Kruse managed exactly that. However, he did a lot of reading and experimenting and had access to testing and technology that may not be available to everyone. The hardest thing is to find someone as motivated as you are...to assist and guide. When you are in the most delicate group which implies epigenetic limitations with extreme sensitivity and issues with assimilating nutrients and absorbing environmental insults and variability it often takes people who have a similar experience to get in the trenches with you.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-11-20T21:17:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-jacksons-boot-amp-week-2.8811/post-101140>

What motivates you to change........Guesses. When you make guess they are called hypothesis. When you do experiments (testing your labs) on yourself you see if the work or not. Then you must realize you are the easiest person to fool yourself........so if your experiments do not fit the theory......your theory is wrong. What branch of science uses this pathway? PHYSICS.......which one does not? Biology. This is why we have statins.

### Jude — 2013-11-21T23:55:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-jacksons-boot-amp-week-2.8811/post-101280>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=101248)
>
> I have had NO issues with starting and upping the iodine plus I had a test that showed I was low on it. Now I am just so confused about what to do. I have dropped down to about 50 mg 1X a day from 2X a day.

I do understand your confusion ncild...think its generic here!.......so much information and good too....however the problem remains is that, is this information right for you and your N=1?

We have the unfortunate ability to inflict harm upon ourselves with this info if not used wisely and with proper support and there's the rub....where do you find same? JK advised us from the start of this blog that we all should have a professional guiding us during our journey to health.

I supplemented iodine to 100mgms for over 3mths with absolutely no seeming ill effects(with all co factors). Different story for DH....ended up with Atial fibrillation and hospitalisation..very scary....what we didn't know (and nor did the drs) was that he had multiple nodules on his thyroid which were stimulated by the iodine...hence causing the prob. Probably an extreme case but that scenario is possible with any supplementation...so hence repeating what JK says....use food as it provides us with safe levels of iodine.

In 2011 a few members ended up with Vit D levels of 350!:.........I was one....how could that happen with low D levels to start with?...what we didn't know was that taking Vit K2 with Vit D causes a synergistic effect ...resulting in high D levels over a couple of months......my GP was not overly concerned but laid down the law about daily amts....guess I'm now into caution in a big way.

### elvey90 — 2013-11-20T11:06:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/checked-labs-help-intrepreting-please.8813/post-101095>

Hey All,

Just got my labs in and was wondering if I can get a bit of help interpreting the results:

Also BOLD means it's elevated according to the prescribe ranges

Vitamin B12 & Folate:

Vitamin B12: 468 pmol/L (150-700) Increased since my last check by 114
R.C Folate: 715 nmol/L (> 630) Increased since my last check by 125

Serum/Plasma Biochemistry

Sodium: 139 mmol/L (136-146)
Potassium: 4.3 mmol/L (3.5-5.0)
Chloride: 102 mmol/L (95-110)
Bicarbonate: **35 mmol/L** (22-31)
Urea: **11.8 mmol/L** (2.3-7.6)
Est.GFR(ml/min): 84 per 1.73sqm (> 60)
Creatinine: 107 umol/L (60-110)
Total Bilbirubin: 7 umol/L (<20)
Ala. Aminotransferase (ALT): 23 U/L (<40)
Asp. Aminotransferase (AST): 20 U/L (5)
Alkaline Phophatase (ALP): 82 U/L (35-110)
Gamma Glutamyl Trans. (GGT): 19 U/L (<40)
Total Protein: 75 g/L (65-85)
Albumin: 46 g/L (38-50)
Globulin: 29 g/L (22-38)

Thyroid Function Test (Serum)

Free T4: 15.4 pmol/L (10.0-19.0)
TSH: 2.22 mIU/L (0.50-4.00)
Free T3: 4.5 pmol/L (3.5-6.5)

**Iron Studies**

Ferriton: **458** ug/L (20-300)
Iron: 13 umol/L (10-30)
Transferrin: 2.2 g/L (2.2-3.7)
Transferrin Sat: 24 % (13-47)

**Serum Glucose**

5.1 mmol/L (4.0-7.8) taken at 7:50PM

**Ultrasensitive C-Reactive Protein**

<0.10 mg/L (0.00 - 10.00)

That's it, please let me know what all of this means and possibly point me in the right direction. I'm looking at the Ultrasensensitive C-Reactive Protein and looks pretty low, meaning I should be doing the Leptin Rx?

Any help would be very much appreciated

Cheers!

### caroline — 2013-11-21T03:33:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/checked-labs-help-intrepreting-please.8813/post-101198>

I am crap at reading labs - but someone will look when they have a chance!

Did you do Vit D, hsCRP, rT3,

You probably will have to talk your Doc into hsCRP - he will think you don't know what you are talking about and just want to do a regular CRP

You can get rT3 thru Healthscope .... they have a functional medicine section. You can also get melatonin thru them.
Your Doc can order these - you have to pay - around $50.00 each if I remember right.
My Doc has requisition forms for them but if your Doc doesn't he can use regular requisition forms. You can call them and get all the info.
They also do a saliva ASI ..... I get mine thru ZRT labs \[canary club} in the U.S. basically same price - but ZRT will give you your results.

Your Doc can also order pregnenolone - extra charge. Just be aware it is batch tested so the results could take 6 weeks.

Jack's book has a great section on labs ... it is available from Amazon ... in kindle or paper

# Thread: Just got my AGE levels measured... holy mackerel!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-got-my-age-levels-measured-holy-mackerel.8815/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 26
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Inger — 2013-11-20T19:07:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/just-got-my-age-levels-measured-holy-mackerel.8815/post-101122>

My dad just had one of his friends for a visit.. they was about to go to a health event and his friend had gotten a AGE Reader (DiagnOptics Technologies BV, Groningen, The Netherlands) that works using skin autofluorescence (SAF), for the meeting. It is a pretty new thing, this link is about a study done

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3672176/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3672176/)

He said it costs about 3000 â‚¬ when I asked. He measured all our AGE levels and it was pretty interesting.... He is around 74 yo himself and looks very healthy, is a health guy so to speak. The AGE machine told his age to be 25. My mom was 54 (real age 61) and my dad had about the same numbers, a little worse. My bro had 23 (real age 21), he has not lived a healthy life until now, but since a month or so he does great, eats and does good stuff. I am proud of him. Now... I was pretty exited what my number will be... I just had labs done last week and my cholesterol was so high and my glucose too

HDL was 119 mg/dl
LDL was 193,3 mg/dl
Triglycerides 43,4 mg/dl

glucose was 108 mg/dl

and I am eating so much fat and I though holey moley I will have so much AEG in my body....... but.. my biologic age according to the machine was 5...???! I had the best by far! They were all stunned.. but I figure it is because I eat almost 100% raw for almost 10 years? When I do not cook anything (almost) there will be way less AGEs? Or is it the lifestyle I am doing? The Kruse style? IDK but I am so happy now! You see... I do have been warned from some health (raw food) people that eating so much protein and fat and coconut oil will make huge amounts of AGEs in my body and it will be very bad for my health and I will age fast. I did not listen to them and now it seems my diet is just right. Maybe my high glucose levels are not bad at all either, maybe my body have just made some anti freeze to take me safe through the winter

I was not too surprised though to be honest - I really feel like I am 5 yo

That's awesome Inger... I bet is a combination of everything your doing, as well as where you living...

Inger your high glucose levels are due to the CT......below 62 F insulin wont work in humans......so this is why BG goes up......and it is a natural phenomena in the part of the world you live in.......but when you warm up like a modern humans and have the high BG from carbs......you get Diabetes which is why the Norwegian countries have the highest rates of T1 D in the world. BG matters little in a cold environment.....it is actually an epigenetic adaptation to cold. See The evolutionary friend or foe blog for the details

# Thread: Butters' Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 951
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Butters — 2013-11-20T21:24:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-101146>

Hello,

this is a optimal journal start from me, a student of sports science from germany. I will do my best to live an optimal life and solve the problems appearing in a student's everyday life.
The entries will be very short, I will do my best to post only the important things.

Here are aims I am shooting for the next weeks/month:

\-improve sleep
\-achieving optimal body composition
\-lay optimal hormonal foundation for muscle building
\-improve resistance against the cold
\-prevent illness

I will do CT most days of the week using the protocoll and try to eat within the light cycle only (dinner may differ 1-2 hours).
I will try to eat good quality food, but often it isn't possible because of the limitted student's budget  but I will do my best.
I have to choose a weight-training routine, since I think I have a good leptin sensitivity, to build more muscle/strength. This must function with a ketogenic diet. I've been reading many articles and think to use HIT principles.

Until now I have skipped breakfast and have used a IF-regime. I'm not hungry at all in the morning hours. We'll see how I'll change variables.
For CT I'm using face-dunks in tap water sometimes with cubes. In addition I use cold showers and try to improve my time at the coldest temperature (I turn it colder while I'm standing under it). After just one week the body starts heating quickly.

First Question:

Is it better for the hormones to skip dinner if I can't eat until 7pm and eat the next meal not until the next morning?

### Inger — 2013-11-20T21:57:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-101155>

Welcome to journal land Alex!

I think it is better for your hormones to skip dinner if so late... and eat a breakfast instead. Because your sleep will be better, you should not eat 4 hours before going to bed. Good sleep = better hormones
But IDK... context.. if you are healthy and young and have no issues maybe it is not worst thing to do to eat shortly after 7 PM?

### Butters — 2013-11-20T22:14:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-101159>

Would have been 8.30-9.00. It doesn't bother me to fast either, because like I wrote I now can get away with skipping meals without getting hungry. So if there is a hormonal advantage (can be sleep) to skip the meal if it's so late I would to it.

### smart cat — 2013-11-20T23:37:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-101161>

There is a hormonal advantage: Growth Hormone, autophagy. Crappy house cleaning at night >> accelerated aging.

### caroline — 2013-11-21T01:14:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-101171>

Sleep is the most important thing as smart cat says.

### Butters — 2013-11-22T16:10:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-101353>

CT yesterday after a workout was good. I did it in the shower for 15min with almost maximal cold tab water temperature. Started to shiver really fast but also the body heats very fast now. Face-dunks were included right before, I start to add some ice cubes to the water.
Sleep last night was terrible. I wanted to go to bed 9.30 PM but lay awake some hours. In the middle of the night my body started heating up, I don't know why. Haven't had sleep problems for a long time. I ate very many brussel sprouts with lunch and my body didn't want it, maybe that was the reason...

Dinner: 7.00 pm: salmon, butter & onions

### Butters — 2013-11-22T22:55:49+0200

### Butters — 2013-11-24T12:43:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-101526>

23.11.

Lunch 12.30pm: lean pork meat and a lot of butter
Dinner 7.00pm: salmon, lean pork meat and a lot of butter

Had weight training yesterday. It wasn't the best training. I feel the glycogen depletion in my muscles, because I've been very lox carb/ketogenic for more than 2 weeks. But I'm feeling very good the whole day, sleep after this one horrible night came back and I feel refreshed in the morning few minutes after waking up. I hope I will get the "bam" - feeling, when you wake up and are crystal clear and ready for war from the first second, soon again regularly.
I read the intermittend fasting article, where Dr. Kruse mentioned Martin Berkhan's LeanGains. I tried LG before for a short timespan, but I always had the same problem after a few days. I couldn't get the carbs out of my head and tried everything to justify big carb binges on the reload days. I couldn't get them out of my head, despite I've always been not fat and should have had a good LS.
Everytime I went very low carb I started feeling amazing over the day, but got sleepy soon in the evening and often slept 9-10 hours. But it always was winter time. I thought there must be something wrong, because when I reintegrated carbs my sleep wasn't so long anymore. So carbs became more regular and I never went keto for a long time. After I read some articles here I now think it's perfectly normal to feel sleepy soon in the evening and sleep a bit longer than in summer times, coupled with training I need this time to replenish my ATP.
What I wanted to say is, I will give keto a try in this winter in connection with CT, because I don't want to deplete my stem-cells anymore. But I also want to achieve a good physique and I think it's also possible with ketogenic food. Not feeling "the high" in training like with carbohydrates, shouldn't hold me back.

Unfortunately in my circle of friends there are only people who live for the pleasure of the moment (don't think too much about food despite eating not so much meat ) and I live together with 2 other guys in a flat. So sometimes it isn't possible to go to bed early because of sounds and when there are some more people it's often difficult to sleep before midnight. I don't want to do all those things these warm adapted humans do, like drinking beer together, eating out together (food consuming things) and I know now which big importance food has in our society. You get food and beverages everywhere everytime to any event and most people consume the food in passing without focus because when you eat or drink you have something to do (think about parties and the "obligatory beer" for conversation). So the negative side effect of living an optimal life is that you have difficulties to wire socially, because humans use consuming food as opportunity to connect with each other.
Enough talk.

CT:

Morning: 5-10min face-dunks
Evening after training: 15min cold shower

### sooperb — 2013-11-24T13:53:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-101528>

Socialisation is a big problem; as you have observed most "occasions" include refreshment, hah, a bit of a misnomer, more an opportunity to pig out. Funnily enough I can quite easily not eat or drink at say parties because one look at the food that is provided is enough for me to smile inwardly because I know I don't want it. As I seldom drink alcohol and never fizzy sugary drinks, I find it much easier to stick to water. Dinner parties are a bit trickier when someone else has cooked and food comes out that it would be rude not to eat just because you know the beef isn't grass fed or the chicken organic. I guess if all of us made a stand about this sort of thing, we might get to convert a few more each.

The bigger problem is that decent, humanely reared food is expensive and there are a lot of folk on limited budgets who can't see the bigger picture that less can be more in terms of health so they fill their family up on crisps, biscuits, sweets and ready meals and aren't selective about where their meat etc. comes from.

### Butters — 2013-11-25T10:52:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-101592>

Yesterday I gave me the luxurity of eating pork belly in good quality, cooked it with some onions and brussel sprouts (not as many at a time as on the day I slept badly) and lean pork meat for more protein.

Lunch: 13.30 pm
Dinner: 7.00 pm after CT

I think about eating always after CT in the evening if I do.

### caroline — 2013-11-25T23:15:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-101636>

Have you thought of having your nice high fat dinner before you C/T?

### Butters — 2013-11-26T08:46:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-101661>

Yes, I've done it a few times but I wanted to try it only with water before because of what Dr. Kruse mentioned in Ben Greenfield's podcast. It is a better feeling to eat after CT, too. First you have all the shiverings and "workout" for your body, than you eat.

It's kale time:

Lunch: 12.00pm - kale, pork, butter, onions
Dinner: 6.00pm after upper body workout - pork, butter, onions

Last two week I have the feeling leaning out stalls a bit. I don't know if I'm overdoing it with the added butter. For the next days I will write down the amount of butter I eat and protein, carbs now are almost zero grams.

### Butters — 2013-11-28T11:55:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-101805>

Last Days I didn't have the willpower to do CT intensive. I run around with sweatshirt outside at 0Â°C and don't heat inside and tolerate it good, but when it comes to CT I seem to have no tolerance. Maybe I don't eat enough and my body is going into energy saving mode, because I don't have much fat. Are my energy needs going up high when I don't heat inside and wear minimal clothes?
Yesterday I trained legs and barely made 10min of a cold shover of 15Â°C, than had to go out. I ate 150g protein and I think maybe 300g of butter but I didn't forced myself to, my body seemed to want it (close to 3000kcal). I'm not a friend of calorie counting, but maybe I have to look that I eat enough to cold adapt?!
Any tipps? Should I rather stuff myself with butter in the cold adapting phase than looking to get leaner in the first place?

### Hemming — 2013-11-28T12:14:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-101806>

FWIW, I have an easier time being in the water when I eat more.

Did you really eat 300g of butter? That's a great amount, I would have thought that should be more than enough to last you for the day.

### Butters — 2013-11-28T16:02:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-101813>

Yeah in 2 meals. I was very hungry and the day before I didn't eat so much and was cold many hours after my bath until I fell asleep + I had leg training day.

### Hemming — 2013-11-28T16:19:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-101814>

Wow, after reading some of the different journals about how much people eat its no wonder I feel tired, cold and hungry

### Inger — 2013-11-28T17:23:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-101818>

Jack tells us to get "cold, dark and sleepy"... so maybe in winter and eating ketogenic it is natural to sleep more. I do too, and I do not stress about it. I guess in summer we sleep less, in winter more, at least here in the north. Many animals sleep the whole winter even...lol

Butters I would just eat if you feel like it! I do too, sometimes I eat a bit less and sometimes I eat so much! I bet it is natural to vary the amounts of food and listening to your body and its needs. I think aiming for being skinny / ripped is not the first choice. healing comes first I am pretty sure  that is what I live myself. I am not skinny but I am on my way to very healthy

### Hemming — 2013-11-28T17:31:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-101821>

I also sleep more in the winter - it feels quite natural.

I often feel that I want different things on different days so I cycle food naturally. Some days its tallow, some days its butter/olive oil or whatever.

Inger, I 'objectively' agree with you (still trying to fully convince myself of it) that skinny is not healthy. But even though you might not be skinny, you're not obese and I would guess you're at a very healthy weight.

Sorry Alex for stealing your journal a little

### Butters — 2013-12-02T21:33:48+0200

So surprisingly although I can't stand a long CT and shiver outside often enough it seems I've adapted somehow and feel comfortable in a colder environment.
I started again with CT today after the cold phase (still have a little cold, but I'm feeling very well) and had 10min shower of changing cold temperature. Tried as long as possible with 11Â°C than turned up to 16-17Â° (to warm up  ). Felt very good.

### Butters — 2013-12-03T10:32:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-102157>

Yeah right, but very exhausting for the mind. I've got enough "problems" with my friends and often me - and don't tell me I should quit friendship . Since I don't drink as much alcohol as before I have my difficulties with partying and women. Discussion topics are a bit rare with nutrition, health as hobbies and sports to study, when you're not drunk. These aren't topics you want to talk about at parties because you're always going to offend someone and damp the mood...my experience...and women it seems don't like it either.
So it's difficult for someone who is a little shy to adapt a lifestyle which basically disconnect from most of the people. Turns out this journal is a little psycho consultation-hour

On the one side you have all this benefits from your nutrition and lifestyle, on the other side you probably won't solve this shyness problem easily. Man...sucks.
Yesterday in public had the feeling all people started looking at me as I went into a room, tram or bus, weird...but looked normal in my opinion and wore a sweater so no looks because of only wearing a t-shirt  But hope to get them soon

### Inger — 2013-12-03T10:57:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-102159>

Alex, that i great about your cooler room! Exactly how I do too. I have it cool... and now I take warm shower in the morning and finish off with an ice cold one, just a for a few minutes. Feels so refreshing! I just do not feel like jumping in the river straight from bed... it is so cold in my bedroom already..lol Later in the day I take a 2 minute soak in the icy river. Trying to spend time outside every day too. When sun is out, I have started to jump softly on the big trampoline in the back yard as I feed on the last sun beams (sun gaze) before the sun drops behind the trees..... happens at 2 PM here now. Amazing.

I have found 100% organic chocolate here! It costs 6,99 â‚¬ / 100g. Not too bad. It is so delicious! I just had almost a half bar with my breakfast. It started to taste very sweet in my mouth... crazy! I will not eat the 85/90% anymore, they are way too sweet for me. I very seldom ate them but when I did I so wished they had less sugar. Now in winter sugar is out anyways. Totally out. The 100% chocolate do have some natural carbs from the chocolate beans, 4,7 g/100g so I guess it have to be a occasional threat in winter anyways, but glad I found it

Looking for a partner... I would not look at her eating habits, but at her mindset instead... that is all that counts for me. Because maybe she just do not know.....  If she has a open, evolving mind, she can climb any mountain with you

### Inger — 2013-12-03T11:03:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-102160>

Alex... I was the shyest person ever, I promise you! It will pass! When you get all these amazing electrons and your body heals and you get in tune with nature... you will not be shy anymore. Not in the bad way that hinders your life. You will have lots of courage and if any shyness left, it will be the sweet sort that is very appealing

I think the best thing is to show your friends with your life the way to optimal... maybe you need not to try to convince them if they do not ask you. But believe me, when they see how you live they will probably ask..lol Lots of interesting discussions can evolve! Drunk people I avoid... wasted time. so when they drink too much just go to bed or do something else!

### Butters — 2013-12-03T22:40:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-102217>

Today was a good day. Best CT ever. Took cold shower again, longest ever. Switched between 11Â° and 16Â°C most of the time to warm up  . This time I wore a hat. After the regular starting issues - getting wet is the worst moment - it quickly felt warm. I couldn't believe it, felt like a freak. Even 13Â° felt warm to me. Does the hat have such a high influence?

### elvey90 — 2013-12-04T06:58:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-102248>

In terms of the social aspect, I think a certain kind of acceptance is required when it comes to meeting new people and current friendships. Some may be really supportive and some may just give out ignorant bashings. Either way, the most important aspect is your own personal conviction to be in optimal health or in this case eating epi-paleo. Conviction can be then transformed into confidence, a form of inner strength so that when you do interact with others, they see the changes and the convictions you hold are strong and some may really resonate with you and some may not. Either way, it doesn't matter what other's think of what you should do, what matters is what you want to do.

Sorry for the hijack, philosophy rant over

### Butters — 2013-12-04T23:54:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-102349>

Yeah you're right.
People in the university always say to me "get a jacket, you're already having a cold" or "I know why you have a cold, because you don't wear a jacket". It's a little difficult to counter, I don't want to explain everytime. For now I mostly say "It is not the cold, it is the virus/bacteria".
Today I thought about what to say if they say again "these cold showers etc. get you nowhere you have a cold anyway" and it made click. I just would ask them if they would stop to exercising when they wouldn't gain their maximum genetic potential after 4 weeks and it made me smile, because that's the answer everyone can understand.

Today I ate a lot more. Definitly think now chocolate under 99% cause cravings. I was exercising today and ate really much food in comparison with the last days/week. Really much Protein and fat but a round off makes no sense. The positive is I don't think about it too much, I slowly begin to accept that my body will burn it off if I'm doing the right things.
CT was not bad, but not as good as yesterday. Have had shower for almost 20min but felt cold afterwards, that could be the reason I ate some food again. But as I said, I exercised and so it is good invested food
And I also went outside with just a t-shirt and a thin sweater at 0Â°C and didn't feel cold most of the time. So I think CT is following a waving improvement and some days might be not so good like others.

### caroline — 2013-12-04T23:59:18+0200

### Butters — 2013-12-08T00:07:07+0200

Back to topic. I added Vitamin D supplementation (5000 IU) per day yesterday. For 1,5 months I have been taking iodine supplementation about 100 mikrogramm per day and try to take 350mg of magnesium.
Today I had a short cold shower in the morning, was good and a short CT of about 20min. Destroyed very much chocolate. Definitly think its addictive to me. I mustn't buy it.
Plan for next week is to eat 250g of salmon per day (because their is a offer) and try to restrict some kale and butter to. I've overdone it the last week with kale and butter and got bloatings (probably form the kale).

### Butters — 2013-12-12T12:23:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-103203>

Update:

CT isn't running so well in my opinion. Don't have the willpower to stand a long time under the shower. I have a quesion: How does the effect differ when I take two shorter showers in the morning and evening than take one long only in the evening? Is the longer better for adaption?

Interesting things with chocolate. I gorged at chocolate at monday. Had a little stress and destroyed 3x100g of 90% Lindt in the evening at 9pm. In the night I couldn't sleep well. It clicked to my, because I remembered that Dave Asprey mentioned chocolate in the same breath as coffee for mental improvement. So chocolate must contain caffeine. And first thing in the morning I looked this up - with this amount of chocolate I consumed a caffeine amount like 5 cups of coffee contain. Man...no wonder I couldn't sleep. This could me the reason for problems with falling asleep on some other days when I consumed not so much chocolate, too. So the last 2 days I didn't consume chocolate in the night and at all + limited coffee consumption - I slept 9-10 hours the night. I think things are going in the right direction now.
I definitely think now that chocolate under 99% has a addictive effect to me. I have a problem with eating because some kind of boredom, too.
So main priority is to limit - better cut the dark chocolate in the evening and consume most of the time together with coffee.

### Butters — 2013-12-13T12:57:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-103308>

Thank for the tips. Are you german or living in germany (because you mentioned this company names ).
Yeah I don't think it's a good idea to buy goose Schmalz, because it is conventional fat and you will have a lot of omega 6 fatty acids in it in my opinion. I rather think about organic pork lard but that's more expensive than kerrygold butter, so most of the time I go for kerrygold.

I haven't tried raw cacao powder yet, but it is definitely a thing I will buy in the future. For now I think it is enough to cut the chocolate out of my diet late in the day like coffee, because of the caffeine. The last 3 days I found myself sleeping way more better, falling asleep faster and sleeping longer. But sleep time increased - I'm going to bed at 11-12pm most of the time and wake up 9-10am, when I don't have to go to university. Sadly I don't wake up an "boom" feel like rocking the day, I have to go out of the bed but then within a few minutes I am feeling great. So the difficult thing is to get up immediatly after waking up.

### sooperb — 2013-12-13T13:36:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-103309>

I subscribe to a fitness guru called Mike Geary, he is very much on the same wavelength and he occasionally mails out recipes for "treats" that are ok for most sensible dieters. Here's his hot chocolate which he advised in his newsletter we could share, yum;

Buy some unsweetened organic cocoa powder (NOT the pre-packaged hot chocolate garbage that is usually loaded with sugar, corn syrup solids, and hydrogenated oils) and mix a heaping Tbsp of organic cocoa with 10-12 oz of unsweetened almond milk (or if you prefer, you can use water, coffee, or a brewed unsweetened chocolate tea as the base liquid) in a small saucepan over very LOW heat.

Using a whisk can help blend the cocoa evenly. I like to add 2-3 tbsp of coconut cream from a can to add more healthy fats (a source of MCTs) to this delicious healthy hot cocoa. You can find canned organic coconut milk in the Asian section of almost any grocery store. Add some stevia and a touch of vanilla extract while stirring over the low heat, and in a few minutes, you’ll have a delicious, creamy, frothy mug of healthy hot cocoa -- WITHOUT all of the sugar and trans fats that are loaded into a typical pre-packaged hot cocoa mix.

You can also add a half scoop of your favorite vanilla or chocolate protein powder while stirring this mix together on the stove. In the final drink concoction, you’ll get the satisfying shot of chocolate along with muscle-feeding, fat-burning protein, a nice dose of fiber from the cocoa, healthy fats from the coconut cream (including powerful medium chain triglycerides), and a mega-shot of healthy antioxidants overall too!

I haven't tried it yet but it sounds awesome.

### Inger — 2013-12-14T07:44:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-103393>

Alex, do not fall prey to believe food is not important! It absolutely is in our screwed up world of today! The 10% means the other (environment, sleep etc.) are even more important but it does not say food is not. I would not eat grainfed organic beef if there where another choice around..... Lamb? I bet lamb or mutton is way better, and pretty cheap too!

....... I have lots of knowledge on eating cheap and well, some ideas for you

Write this very friendly women at max metzgers; [angela.galbierz@max-metzger.de](mailto:angela.galbierz@max-metzger.de) Ask for grassfed beef and fat and hearts, you might get liver too. Very cheap! You can buy the cheapest cuts, just ask her she will know. I have ordered form her for many years, they even send me stuff to Finland! Das Paket wird bei dir in ein paar Tage sein und die Versandkosten sind echt guenstig auch wen du viel bestellst

Then... eat lots of Pollock, look that it is caught in North Atlantic, not Pacific if possible. Prefrozen Pollock is very cheap and tastes just nice! I eat it raw, but for sure you can cook it gently too if you like (I eat all my fish raw always, no compromises there, because I do not want breast cancer... there is something in raw fish that protects against it ;-) )

Herring? I guess you can get raw and whole herrings for cheap? Or other small fishes? You can make a fish head smoothie out of the heads. Very filling and cheap! Your mitbewohner might want to try too ;-)
If you have a fishmarket close you can ask for heads and bones to make broth too. They might give it to you for free even.

When I was at my poorest I ate raw meat and organ petfood. Made of moose. Tasted not good but I just hold my nose and swallowed it down. Made me feel awesome, helped me stay healthy even in very stressful situations. When I did that I could live for 4 â‚¬ / day for food only and was perfectly fed

If there are still greens, eat wild greens! They are for free... Linden leaves are even good to fill the stomach with, tastes awesome! Not bitter at all... and great for your digestion

I might move to Germany this Spring, to the North close to Hamburg I think. If - and if you are close then, we need to meet up and I will show you what you can eat for free in the nature. Super healthy too, full of minerals

### Jack Kruse — 2013-12-15T18:01:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-103510>

Our beliefs are what got us in trouble with Mother Nature and her cousin Lady evolution. Just realize that food being your medicine is also contextual. When the primordial environment of this planet were changed it means the context of food has also now moved and changed. This is the seismic shift I am showing people. The reason is simple........food at the core issue are broken down to electrons in our mitochondria.

But we have biologists, clinicians, and theorist like Dawkins telling us that genes are the key to health. Riddle me this........is an electron smaller than a gene? If so then does life happen really at a genetic level or a sub atomic level? Now for closure of the circle of thinking about this all.........If you get that the environment has changed dramatically in 200 yrs it means understanding how these sub atomic particles change their behavior under a stronger electromagnetic force. This then tells you really where food 's context is today. What your grandmother and your ancestors did may no longer be relevant to your conditions today. Chew on that.

### Butters — 2013-12-21T20:05:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-103974>

Yes, I am talking more about health now and not only body composition. The people only look at your body fat and think they can tell you how "fit" and "healthy" you are.
When I think about many guys who study together with me...they are ripped but have to eat every few hours and can't go without food longer than 3-4 hours. And for god sake when they forget a meal...

Other topic. I started reading "Wheat belly". A very interesting book. Did Kruse mentioned it? Also bought "Mastering Leptin" and I'm very excited to read it.
My sister, 3 years younger than me, isn't doing so well. Very often she has headaches, this week everyday. She has an autoimmun disease, a bloated stomach... All sympthomes of gluten intolerance. I'm talking so often about it, my mother is a doctor and we have the book wheat belly on the table. They don't want to listen to me. They always look for other reasons for the problems of my sister. I don't know what to do. I always say she has to try it for 14 days and look how she is doing without all the rubbish... Nobody ist listening to me. It's freaking me out, I feel so bad. Same thing with my grandparents. Despite my father has read "wheat belly" they give cookies as a christmas gift. How can people do that??? They think they can't change anything, they think it is too late to change people at the age of 75 and if they try they are robbing them the last pleasure. I like my home, but that is freaking me out. It hurts so deep I can't describe, when you're own family don't want to listen...
I hope that my sister will listen to me this year. How bad it has to be that they will listen...

Yes, the book is called Grain Brain.
\--------
“If you could make just three simple changes in your life to prevent, or even reverse, memory loss and other brain disorders, wouldn’t you?”

So asks Dr. David Perlmutter, in promotion of his PBS special Brain Change, coming soon to your regional affiliate. Three changes. Simple ones. Wouldn’t you?

The 90-minute special is a companion to Perlmutter’s blockbuster book on how gluten and carbs are destroying our brains. In November it became a New York Times number one bestseller. Since its September release, as Perlmutter told me, “It’s never not been on the bestseller list, frankly.”

Avoiding "vegetable" oils should fix the issue, but it might take a year for your body to detox what is already incorporated into your cells.. I'm not sure the small amount of omega 6's in conventional meat is really the problem. If you eat plenty of natural saturated fats, natural omega 3's (seafood), and yes, some natural omega 6's (nuts) your skin will come around. Plus of course plenty of good water.

When I started eating a lot more seafood, daily at the beginning, I started, in a couple months, to feel a difference in my skin over my hip area. Later I felt this smoother skin spread to more and more of my trunk, and still later my arms & legs and head skin also changed. It was really interesting. But the composition completely improved to a texture I was unfamiliar with. Soft & smooth like baby skin, but also tough.

### Butters — 2013-12-30T14:41:03+0200

I really think about buying that thermometer gun. For now after christmas, I ordered some books and one of them is the "Epi Paleo Rx" yay
I also talked about my living conditions in the past, that will change too. In 2-3 months I have to search for a new flat/room etc. because my mates and I moving out. Best would be a flat alone for me, but flats in Potsdam are so expensive, it is not realistic. I will write about this.

So christmas hasn't weakened me like last year. My consume went up, but highest carb intake was yesterday with about 100g from chocolate and vegetables. I could prevent a carb binge, because "it's christmas". I think it wouldn't be the worst thing, because I have many things to work on and shouldn't try to work on all things at the same time. The stress will be too high and I will fail at nearly all of them - that's what the past tells me. But if I can dodge bullets I try. Books like "Mastering Leptin" are motivating too and I think when I read Dr. Kruse's book more things will change.
I realised that eating too much too late at night is bad for my sleep and the last days I really felt leptin rise after dinner and make me feeling full until bedtime. I only eat after dinner, because I am sitting in front of my computer and watching films. But it is strange, that in these times I feel some kind of overall motivation I don't feel in the daytime. Motivation to do something...It happend also in the past, when I ate many carbs/did carb-backloads.

That are my problems that bother me mostly - a lack of motivation over the daytime, somehow. I know it would go away with carbs but I don't want to eat much of them especially in the winter. ### Inger — 2014-01-07T09:39:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-104954>

Chicken get crap feed.
I buyed organic chicken feed for our hens and it was made of soy, wheat, pea protein, vegetable fats.... just crap.

Maybe the eggs are good if you have no autoimmune condition because they do have lots of nutrients. Especially if eating the yolks and raw. But Jack always says to eat pastured eggs. They need to eat grass, you know. It is important to make the eggs healthy. Most hens are not getting grass, not even the organic ones. At least not here. But if you get great quality I sure believe eggs are great food. I eat the duck eggs raw, I get almost 1 each day now and I feed my ducks only fresh wild caught fish and whole oats and sometimes sunflower seeds, flax seeds, raw meat/organs. Those eggs are so costly one eggs is about...4 â‚¬ or more if I calculate it..lol A financial disaster

If you buy organic chicken it will be way better than regular ones, but still way worse than other meat sources I guess.

### Butters — 2014-01-07T14:11:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-104974>

But, if I'm only eating the lean chicken meat without the fat, this isn't so bad like chicken with fat/conventional chicken fat which I avoid isn't it?

### sooperb — 2014-01-07T16:04:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-104980>

Butters I think you are missing out the essential lesson, fat is good, animal fat is good so cutting it out means missing the goodness. When I boil a chicken carcase for stock I don't skim the fat off I leave it, it adds flavour and goodness. True to say though, there isn't much fat on a chicken really, it's mostly in the skin which I don't eat simply because I don't like the taste or texture.

I totally agree about organic eggs Inger. I think in the UK most organic egg producers adhere to the code drafted by the Soil Association with regard to organic feed. The feed will all be organic but as you point out, the ingredients may not be what chickens should be eating. We have 3 at the moment and they roam freely, eating all the grass and bugs they can get plus any suitable kitchen waste but we do also put food out for them and it's not organic. That said our eggs have the yellowest yolks because the hens get sunshine and grass. I am happier eating them than any I have to buy although one hen hasn't laid for a long time and the other two have moulted recently. There was an egg this morning for the first time in at least 2 months. We could do with a couple more hens really. Ducks would be just too messy for a domestic back garden, the hens make enough mess.

Diet right now consists of a cup of bulletproof coffee + 20-25g whey isolat or eggs and bacon + carots in the morning right after awakening. For lunch usually nothing or 1-3 teaspoons of coconut-oil or big lunch after I trained hard right before. Usually I eat a big dinner in the evening. ### Butters — 2014-08-24T12:04:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-138756>

Most problems aren't present anymore. Since April I live in a new flat and socialisation changed radically. There's now a girlfriend and I have more freedom. So psychologically problems are rare.

I am a sport student and now I have holidays but do a practicum in a rehab facility. So I'm working 6-7h a day and like I said often afternoon till evening. I try to not stress me at work. Sleep is improving since wednesday and I sleep through till morning and wake up refreshed. Today I saw my stool improving radically, I think it was the eggs. I now eliminate them for minimum one week and then when reintegrating I look to not overdo it with the amount.

### Butters — 2014-08-25T12:46:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-138831>

And again without the eggs my stool is nearly perfect, if you know what I mean. I don't like it but they were non pastured eggs anyway and maybe it's good I don't eat them. Yesterday I trained at 2p.m. and ate till 3p.m. . After that I was feeling very hot, drank some ice-water and went bathing in the river. It had 19° C and air was 15°, quite comfortable. People around had pullovers and jackets
Felt so good, I ate 1 tbs coconut-oil after that but wasn't hungry and fasted till this morning 9.30 when I drank a bulletproof-coffee with cinnamon. I didn't get hungry, so I think I am leptin sensitive.

I plan to eat like a pig the following month, because I want to gain muscle as I said. So I will consume lots of fat and very little carbs. Also I try to bath regularly in the river as temperature gradually falls. Want to sleep without covers this winter and run around only with a t-shirt.

### nonchalant — 2014-08-27T19:11:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-139056>

In winter, if I have enough energy, I will notice that my hands and feet are cold, but only if I pay attention to them. They are cold but it doesn't bother me. If I haven't been outside much, or I'm hungry or dehydrated, that is another matter. My cold hands and feet make me miserable. I've also noticed that if I'm on my Magnetico, without covers, my cold hands or feet also don't bother me.

So it all depends on my energy level.

### Jonathin — 2014-08-27T20:56:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-139060>

Personally, I have found that my perception of being cold in my extremities and actually being significantly objectively cold can be two different things. Recently I have found my feet and hands to actually be nicely warm at times. Other times not so much. It is a cause and effect mystery in the short term and a no brainer when I think of the long term history of physical abuse. Like NC, the comfort factor is often a non-issue especially when energy is good. This year I have taken up waking barefoot to change irrigation water (yes, I'm a farmer) twice a day since April. It is a up to a 1/4 mile one way walk on dirt, weeds and grass with standing water, mud and sometimes light snow. In the late spring my feet were numbish when I made it back to the house. I can walk on a coarse gravel driveway if I have to. The point is that behavior can change your reality, at least over time. I can't image not doing this routine now.

### smart cat — 2014-09-12T12:46:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-140722>

> [Butters said:](/goto/post?id=138754)
>
> Fresh fish is difficult, I live in Potsdam.

**Wild caught fresh** seafood is hard to find even in Berlin if you can't fork out a lot of money and even then....
But with frozen products things are a bit better. Unfortunately they are very often farmed seafood (like loup de mer, scallops) but not all of them are.
I have found wild caught salmon, shrimps, mussels and mixed seafood packs in Edeka, REWE, aldi, Kaiser's, Karstadt...you name it. Also canned fish (sardines, maquerels) and mussels. So take a 15 minute ride on our fabulous S-Bahn  if you can't get the stuff in Potsdam. But maybe you can.

[http://fisch-eck.com/hauseigene-traditionelle-manufaktur](http://fisch-eck.com/hauseigene-traditionelle-manufaktur)

[http://fisch-eck.com/filialen](http://fisch-eck.com/filialen)

I wrote that I thought I had an egg sensitivity in the past. Maybe it always was the wrong time to eat ( too late) the evening before in combination with too much food that brought me bad stool the morning after.
Now with finishing dinner at 7-8pm (7 is better) I recognize my bowel movement is much better. I sleep better and wake up almost at the same time, most of the time refreshed. I think I slowly get it how timing is very important.

### Butters — 2014-09-29T18:16:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-142160>

The day was a disaster. Startet so good, but I ruined it.

I had a really, really BAB. Think it was too much. I ate 5 Eggs, 500g spinach, 4 carots, 125g salmon, much ghee (think min. 50g), 120g Lindt 90% chocolate, 200-250g cream and drank 2 cups of coffee. Than 1 hour later I made a CT session. Till after the session I felt really good. Then I went by bike to buy some kerrygold. On the way back it hit me. I felt horrible. Felt like many little stitches on my skin for 5min. I heated up very sick. My mental clarity began to decline and I barely could concentrate to arrive home safely. Like a really big brain fog. Plan was to train in the light hours as usual, so I made a 3 cup of coffee iced. Than I went to training and during the bus ride a was very tired and felt like headaches would come (I have never ever had headaches for more than 8 years!!!). I went to training anyway, despite feeling like crap. First exercise was leg press and I barely could motivate me. After the work set to max, I thought I would pass out. Blood was rushing through my ears, I had to put the legs up because otherwise I thought I would fell out of the machine. I needed >5min to recover and tried a smaller weight for the last metabolic sets (the weight usually is very light after the work set) but I barely made it through the set, because I again felt like passing out if I continued. So I ended the session. I went under the cold shower and immediately felt better, but on the way back I had to fight for mental clarity. Was so awfull. Never happend ever.

It had to be that disaster breakfast. Can't imagine an other reason. I had no breakfast the last week (despite of one, that consisted of 3 eggs) so I think it adds. I thought I had to eat very much, because the day before the last meal was 1pm and I went to bed without dinner.

Someone has an explanation for this?

I would just suggest to not eat so much different things at once... I bet you would digest just fine if you had, say, a 500-600 gram steak or raw salmon? I do this at times and I have no issues........
but I bet I would not feel well after that breakfast of yours....
Spinach I never eat either.... it can make one sick... especially if it was cooked and cooled..... I got once really sick from it
I eat greens that have been growing in the sun, preferably...... they are much different, I promise  Wild greens are the best, you could pick linden leaves, or chickweed [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stellaria\_media](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stellaria_media) .... it is my favorite veggie now, I LOVE it, grows everywhere where it is a bit moist and not too direct sun
You could do a nettle smoothie
All for free

### Butters — 2014-10-06T17:40:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-142980>

So, weekend is over. My girlfriend has got a cold since friday and I slept every night beneith her. Had no signs of sickness so far, today I thought I have a little scratching in my throat. Hope it doesn't get me this time. But I think my immune system is a lot better now because I'm doing CT every day 1-2 times for 45-90min per Session. It is not optimal because I'm doing it with my frozen gelpacks and they only become frozen one time a day. Water would be much better. I try to have 5-10min of cold showers before the session to precool the body and raise the intensity. I think it works pretty good.

Yesterday I reread the NASA Cold Thermogenesis script and now know more. To do something against my cold hands and feet I simply could soak them regularly in ice water I conclude from the script. Am I right that carbohydrate consumtion and CT induce shivering faster than ketogenic nutrition and CT? That says the script too and it makes deep sense, because in winter there are no carbs. So more heat is generated with ketogenic food through UCP and BAT. So that could be the important thing for reducing shivering and really stimulate BAT?
Its also said that glucose uptake is raised independantly from insulin plus insulin production is downregulated.

But they too say that a part of cold tolerance could be from chronic hypothermia. Is it that hypothermia that come with leptin sensitivity over time and what Dr. Kruse spoke about?

### Butters — 2014-10-09T16:02:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-143321>

3rd day of my cold. It hasn't beat me yet. I'm feeling good, not tired despite of a little scratching. Think my immune system is getting better. Next act will be the checking of my Vit. D levels. Its the cheapest and I think best test in my situation. My libido is getting very strong although I am not carb loading for T3 and T3 levels  like conventional weight lifting believe says.

Today I had a 30min cold tap bath without ice. Ice is to expensive. Didn't check the temperature but will in the future. Last year the temperature in Winter was 11°C, this is not optimal because I will heat the water and don't have constant skin temperatures of 10-12° C but it is better than nothing. I can say I tolerate it very well. For the first 15min it was very comfortable than the shivering began but wasn't bad. At min 30 I went out because I didn't want to go to far but next time I want 35min or more. Skin was red, but not red enough I think because of the temperature. I didn't get cold urticaria like last year but red skin-yeah!!! It's getting better, I love it. Outside temperature was 18°C so it wasn't so bad to get in the mood to take the bath and rewarm afterwards. Looking forward to cooler temperatures and how I feel than about taking the bath

### Butters — 2014-10-17T11:39:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-144095>

Hm.
Yesterday I was with friends till 1am (played games). It was very warm in the living room. I think it was very warm despite someone said I should shut the window because its getting cold^^. I think at 11-12pm I felt like heating up (despite it was very warm in the room) and it was getting stronger till I went to bed at 1.30am. I was very strong radiating heat then and I almost couldn't sleep.
My question: Is it possible to activate the fat burning pathway (UCPs and heat radiation) once adapted and leptin sensitive without early sleep at night?

Today morning I ate 250g fat, 50g Protein and some low starch carbs^^
Priority for me now is muscle building and browning WAT to BAT. Once more cold adapted I think I can go to more moderate calorie consumption for body composition.

And more:

I slept 6-6,5h this night but woke up really fresh. For me the sign that my sleep quality was good is when I can not longer afford to stay in bed even if I want to.

Now I have a reason to use my blueblockers at night more:

"
**Summary**
A worldwide increase in the incidence of obesity indicates the unsuccessful battle against this disorder. Obesity and the associated health problems urgently require effective strategies of treatment. The new discovery that a substantial amount of functional brown adipose tissue (BAT) is retained in adult humans provides a potential target for treatment of human obesity. BAT is active metabolically and disposes of extra energy via generation of heat through uncoupling oxidative phosphorylation in mitochondria. The physiology of BAT is readily regulated by melatonin, which not only increases recruitment of brown adipocytes but also elevates their metabolic activity in mammals. It is speculated that the hypertrophic effect and functional activation of BAT induced by melatonin may likely apply to the human. Thus, melatonin, a naturally occurring substance with no reported toxicity, may serve as a novel approach for treatment of obesity. Conversely, because of the availability of artificial light sources, excessive light exposure after darkness onset in modern societies should be considered a potential contributory factor to human obesity as light at night dramatically reduces endogenous melatonin production. In the current article, the potential associations of melatonin, BAT, obesity and the medical implications are discussed.
"

### Butters — 2014-12-09T12:28:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-149597>

I have concerns about my gut.
I eat 2 meals a day most of the time. For the last 3 weeks almost every morning 4-6 eggs with butter and a big cup of coffee. In the evening lots of protein and fat two + 500-1000g green vegetables. Thats it. No snacking.
But nearly every morning when I have to poop my stool is kind of liquid not solid consistency. Yesterday it even was yellow.

I wonder if I have a kind of leaky gut. I eat now Gluten, wheat, milk products (despite of butter) so its more confusing.

I wrote this some time ago and there I thought it could be the eggs, because its such a mass of them over the time.
My second idea is the coffee, because its most of the time happening when I drink a second cup later.

My plan is again to cut the eggs from my diet a while and when adding them back up to limit them.

Another topic:

For the last 2-3 nights I have been dreaming a lot, I mean really a lot. The times before I think I didn't dream at all. What does it mean?

Also -and this may be tied to the gut thing - I don't wake up refreshed anymore, despite I don't use much technology and blue light before sleeping and wearing blueblockers as soon the sun sets.
In the summer time I fixed this, but now I simply can't leave the bed so easy like back then and tend to fall asleep again if I stay lying down.

But CT is going forwards. Wearing the cool gut buster and fat burner 1,5-2h every day if I have the time and the packs are cold enough. From day to day the skin got more adapted. Hurts not so much anymore and shivering begins later.
The skin/fat hurt a little bit, but that getting better, too. Maybe its the apoptosis effect.

### Butters — 2014-12-19T10:48:11+0200

Yesterday again cold tub with 4 CFB gelpacks in it. Getting in the water without the gel packs was very boring, seems tap water is not so cold here. Also the bathroom is heated and I stick my legs out - no need for a cap and socks so.
At the end at minute 42 I got out because shivering was getting very intense. Half of the time there was not shivering, I am getting better. Rewarming also was faster and shivering wasn't so intense.
I try to meditate a little bit during the bath and breath deeply. Most of the time I am listening to a podcast or so.

It is a little funny. I try to eat large to bulk, but when I eat large I can't eat much more later in the day  This is how leptin is supposed to work but I want muscles. Probably I can't build so much muscle in the winter, because hunger is declining.
Another thought is that CT maybe a point to back up a little bit, because its burning to much calories.
Eric has a solution in the new CFB experiment with 2 days a week of intense CT and Body recomp. But he eats lots of carbs after training. I don't because of winter. But he made me think about the excessive CT use and maybe inhibited muscle growth.

### kovita — 2015-01-13T12:30:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-152631>

you always have to adjust your lifestyle to your individual difference/needs. I keep ketotic even on 100g carbs a day. Last winter. i was on zero carbs and even as the seasons changed I have not went much higher than 50g most of it insoluble. It was not particulary good for me. Not everyone can do longterm ketosis nonstop, it always depends on you how you tolerate it. The winter solstice is over and the days are getting longer. You can steadily start to add more seasonal carbs (even in the european winter there is plenty to choose from - different tubers, pumpkin, onions....) and see how it goes. It is not breaking the seasonality rules. I would say people always did the max to make their diet more variable and searched for carbs depsite of the winter, animals do that too (looking on the non-hibernating animals). Or you can supplement melatonin and serotonin precursor, but if I were you, I would avoid this option. I experienced this problem and it took quite long time to sort it out, mainly because I am so stack in ketosis, it has been quite a work to be able to move to something like 100 g a day, my body ignored glucosis big time. You see what is the best for you.

Best Wild Salmon is from Iglo. Taste is Note 3,5 (befriedigend). Parasites is Note 4,0 (ausreichend).

[http://www.costa.de/nachhaltigkeit/zertifizierungen/Seiten/Zertifizierungen.aspx](http://www.costa.de/nachhaltigkeit/zertifizierungen/Seiten/Zertifizierungen.aspx)

Maybe buying sardines, hering, maquerel is better. Good wild salmon is very expensive and farmed is not the same quality in Omega 3.
This whole salmon issue really sucks.

Good post from **Mark Sisson: Factory Salmon vs. Wild Salmon:**

_Because the farmed salmon are largely confined and fed a steady diet of formulated protein pellets, they’re inevitably fattier. “But isn’t that a good thing?” you might ask. “More omega-3s per serving, right?” The answers are “no” and “not really”_

[http://www.marksdailyapple.com/salmon-factory-farm-vs-wild/#ixzz3TtGIV7th](http://www.marksdailyapple.com/salmon-factory-farm-vs-wild/#ixzz3TtGIV7th)

_Because the farmed salmon are largely confined and fed a steady diet of formulated protein pellets, they’re inevitably fattier. “But isn’t that a good thing?” you might ask. “More omega-3s per serving, right?” The answers are “no” and “not really”

Many assessments have found **fewer omega-3s per ounce in farmed salmon compared with wild salmon**, but we know the farmed stuff also comes with a hefty (not healthy) wallop of other fats including omega-6s. We then deal with the problem that the omega-6s and omega-3s compete for the same receptors in our bodies. Consequently, the “net” omega-3 gain will always be less than what you’ll get with a wild serving. Here’s a nifty chart that compares the fat content of some popular wild versus farmed fish varieties (including salmon) from this_ [PDF](http://www.puresalmon.org/pdfs/human_health.pdf).

### Butters — 2015-05-27T13:10:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-165540>

I recently found my old analysis. Was 3 month ago. Wasn't dehydrated. But slightly above the norm. Now I am more above the norm. But My weight increased and my muscle but my fat decreased. So slightly body recomp. But I wanted muscle building. I think now I have to up my fat a little bit when I do CT every day.
I have eaten a lots of crap food with many carbs the last time. I am cutting this now and go to a approch with 70% fat, 20% prot and 10% carbs. And will add fat for building. I have a better time with that. My sleep is better without carbs in the mass.

### Butters — 2015-05-28T10:33:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-165640>

Yesterday I eat crap, again . But not so much as before.
As counter action I had 40min cold tub and 2-3hrs CFB use afterwards in the evening and lots of lots of ice water. Shiveringtime was I think 1-2hrs, almost all the time I think shiver surfing so a bunch must be burned off.
1hr after going to bed my girlfriend came to bed and said I was hot like a oven.
I usually takes some time for me to get that hot after a session and going to bed. Seems the body first is rewarming and than overshooting through brown fat activity.

I am doing an early breakfast after waking up again, because I feel this will couple me more than everything what I do in the evening with blueblockers etc.. Last nights I slept through, dreams were there but I didn't felt that optimal as in some other times before.

I thought about the issue I can't get much weight anymore. I think I have to eat really much fat. I am doing CT like crazy in my view, almost everyday and I am fairly lean. I notice I shiver faster than before - coupled to BF percentage I think. And I also think warmer water in that range right now is more uncomfortable than colder water.

### Butters — 2015-06-17T09:57:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-168553>

Monday I had my little surgery at my face.
Doc came and put local anestetica under my lip from inside the mouth  Right the moment I felt the stitch (really uncomfortable) I got flickering in front of my eyes, cold hands, sweat and felt like passing out. Said it to him and I made me lying horizontally. Anyway he said I passed out for 2 sec.. Man that was quick. I sometimes got it when there were taking blood from me, but not so fast.
But surgery was good and now lips are going back to normal size

I didn't need any painkiller afterwards, indeed I even had no pain so far! Maybe my condition isn't really as bad as I think. Next monday they take a look at the patch. Hope I don't see a scar. And if I will treat with DHEA cream.

I noticed when I added a BPC or fat coffee now (because I can't eat firm things and chew so good) I feel much better in the morning. My morning poop is better again.
Sleep is improving, too.
This week no training.

### Butters — 2015-06-24T11:27:11+0300

### cinnamon — 2015-07-13T18:28:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-171834>

Hi Alex,

About the green tea - you can reduce the amount of caffeine in it by steeping it twice. The first time, use only a small amount of hot water, and steep the tea for a minute or two. Throw out that water and add new hot water to steep it for a second time. The resulting tea will still have caffeine, but the amount will be reduced.

### Butters — 2015-07-13T22:35:54+0300

### Inger — 2015-11-29T15:31:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-182829>

Alex I know for cholesterol labs you should be completely fasted. I think otherwise the labs will show up weird....... I guess

If you tan in a high nnEMF environment your body will not effectively make vit D.... where have you been tanning? In a city?
That you are not sick all the time might be because you are doing many things right  and you are still young so your redox is higher because of your age

I do not think supplement is wise at all if you live in high nnEMF. I think you said you live in an apartment building in a city? Not so good....... I would not take supplement.
I would eat lots of fatty wild caught fish instead as it has lots of vit D too... okay not so good as getting sun...... but better that artificial supplements I would say
Maybe you could go on a vacation for a week or two to a sunny place on the beach away from population and make some vit D  there are always cheap trips to buy if you are not so picky
and if you go to a tanning salon (with UVB) it would help too. But I am not sure how safe it is if your vit D is very low. Jack has said to go to a tanning salon you need to have a decent vit D status
otherwise it is risky. I use tanning bed but I have a good vit D status I am sure. It is a while since I measured it but back then it was very good. But I also have been tanning nude for many many years... and I always was a tanning freak (practically never used sunscreen... and always loved to be barefoot in the sun)
Maybe a terrarium UVB lamp would help? It is not as strong as a tanning bed.....
Maybe just go through this winter with low vit D (winter is the time of year when vitD is lower naturally anyways) and then as spring comes start to tan nude out in the wild as much as you can and your vit D will raise fast
many options to consider

### Butters — 2015-12-14T00:00:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-184043>

New things:

Try to take the glasses instead of the contacts, when I am at home. Try to look 30° next to the sun on the left side for as long as possible, when practical right after getting up in the morning.
When I don't look at the sun and do stuff at home, I use a 25W black light bulb desk lamp.
I think my sleep has remarkley improved, when other factors are right. (not eating to late)
Crazy things happend. Im almost not hungry. Often I dont feel like eating breakfast anymore, despite of having dinner at 5-6pm. And I do sport for muscle building again. I am not a much hungry as I have been.
Bad things, I eat to much carbohydrates for this season (now 8hrs daylight and temperature 0-10°C). Not regularly CTing. Didn't do any cold tub this winter season so far. Only did CT with CFB.
I will fix this over the holidays. My familiy isn't addicted and uses some stuff I bring to them. They already use normal incadecend bulbs and flux.

### Butters — 2016-02-24T20:00:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-189715>

I just realized after a lecture of Alexander Wunsch, that its the near infrared light that makes the exclusion zone.
I always wondered if infrared was good, so that actually was saying heating and warm showers are good. But in his lecture he said internal heating is just IR-C radiation, no IR-A at all.

But black bodies radiation has all. So that means the conventional bulb has IR-A as well but the saving lights have none. And thats the special about the sun too.
Also when Dr. Kruse in podcast spoke about IR I thought about just heat. But I didnt realize that there is different kinds of IR radiation and that only IR-A penetrates deep to the body core.

### Butters — 2016-03-10T12:39:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-190589>

I do the leptin reset again. Because I recognized I woke up later in the morning and going to bed later. So clearly a switch in circadian rhythm.
So after just a week of leptin reset I went from going to sleep at 0.00am to 10-10.30pm and waking up gradually ealier every morning.
Also went from drinking 2-3 cups of coffee to 1 cup today and didnt like it - it was suddenly to strong.
I take my blueblockers every day when sun goes down (6pm for now) and go for longsleeve in the evening.
Gym time is as short as possible. Dont like that feeling inside. I also go when the sun is strong, so at least in circadian fashion. If I cant make it, I dont go later. Meals are 2 per day with the last meal often finished 5pm. That comes naturally. I just eat in the late afternoon and be full till next morning.
