### Butters — 2016-04-09T08:49:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-192399>

Book arrived yesterday. Good tip Jack, thanks. How did you know about it?
I think I got a friend an board. Its a student friend and he is like me 3 or 4 years ago: open minded, listens excited to new things he hear, dont call people dumbasses or call out quackery or something. He looks things up and checks both side. Yesterday I presented him some books, also the new "Better Brain, Better Body". I showed him all the fluxometer things, some things about pollack and because we were some time in the sun I told him what I know so far about UV-light and the eye from you. He instantly downloaded f.lux for his computer and was excited to get some blueblockers, despite he was saying before he can sleep good. So maybe we have another part in the friendzone who can contribute to the whole thing. My feeling is the first thing people will change is the blueblocking at night. First they will do it only at home, you know because of peer pressure - I know those things. But Im improving on giving a shit about that.
When more and more people of the same group going to change something I think the others will have to question themselves, what is wrong with them. Its like you can use peer pressure once more people get the truth for the people which have too low dopamine to change. Im seeing a constant change in my nearest friends. It is a little change, but its growing. I think this book will get a lot more people into thinking, because now they can read it easily on their own. Until know I was a info box between your blog and some people, but that is more practically.

No to me: I really like the changing. It seems with every sun hour I get I improve more in my thinking abilities. Its like I can connect dots more and more while I speak to people. I felt it yesterday, I can explain things better and in connection. I hope this is only the beginning. Its like getting a glimpse of what I am able to do when things are going natures way.
It recently came to my mind, when I saw all these people on the streets with sunglasses AND jackets. All covered up, so not a single trace of sun, despite on the face and hands, could get through. "When you mess with mother nature, she will hit you in the face, again and again - until you stop." Most people are lucky to have a normal eyesight, not me, and they are covering their clock in the daytime.
This is the thing I got unlucky with: A really bad eyesight of -7 diopters. Mothers nature hits everyone in a different place - until you stop messing around

### Butters — 2016-04-15T09:21:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-192716>

The book is really great. Got 2 others friends in and my girlfriend got excited  I just let the book laying around in the living room...
I am up to 2 or 3 meals with fish a day now. I have sometimes little brain freeze when drinking a big glass of ice water in one big swallow, but not always.
I can now tell from my pop when my meals before where shit. There is almost now smell, when I dont mess up with carbs etc. But when I am eating too much carbs I can tell the next morning. Bacteria seem to produce more H2 then. When I stick to keto/low carb and/or just fish it is fine.
I am more awake after waking up. I rarely wake up after 7, I should say it never happend the past 2 weeks, even not the one time I got not enough sleep.
I am a little bit unsure because it seems my skin gets pink really fast now in the sun. Last evening with probably UV1 I got a response after 1min on my forearms. My forearms seem to be really sensitive for UV light. I carry a little tan now. I try to go out on midday when we have strong sun as much as possible.

### Butters — 2016-04-15T17:07:55+0300

Im starting regularly training again. 3x a week and Full Body routine. When its nice outside I will go out, otherwise I go to the gym.
Im looking at some of Menno Henselmans ideas on training. He got interviewed on Primal Edge with Tristan.
For my training I try to go the scientific approach at least when I am in the gym. Since Im studying Rehab in sports I have plenty of knowledge. In the past I didnt use it, instead I went for brosience. Thats changing now.
The science in my university is lagging behind. No one mentioned even Leptin. Next week we have a seminar to thermoregulation  thats going to be funny. I have the feeling since the start of the seminar, if I ask some question, the people will break and have no answers.
We got the topic "paradontitis" and the connection with diabetes. Lol people dont get some. I mean I am not the most intelligent guy, but clearly people only look on single points and are not connecting. Its a so mechanical viewpoint of the body, its really funny.
Im working on a tactic to get some of our "teachers" in thinking. I really would like to change something. But often people are irritated from my questions and dont answer the question, but tingling around.
Also we got the ATP synthesis from carbs and fats and the "teacher" even said there is so few ATP made from the energy of the food. I am not so good in the whole ATP thing from Gilbert Ling, but would like to read about it.

Nice. German community is growing
I got cold urticaria the first time I tried CT. That was 2 years ago. But I had all other factors wrong: I ate no fish and I didnt do it carefully enough.
I have two blueblockers. One is from Prisma, a german company, but very expensive. The quality is very good, but it costs too much. And then I bought some fit-over glasses, because Id like to wear my glasses more in general and especially in the evening it was very complicated to take out the contacts without light exposure  They cost like 13€ on ebay

[http://www.amazon.de/Uvex-S0360X-Sa...&redirect=true&ref\_=oh\_aui\_detailpage\_o04\_s00](http://www.amazon.de/Uvex-S0360X-Safety-Eyewear-Extreme/dp/B003OBZ64M?ie=UTF8&psc=1&redirect=true&ref_=oh_aui_detailpage_o04_s00)

Butters you are slowing engaging the world within you by turning on the power by using the sun. Soon all around you will be drawn to your illumination like bugs to light.

Is your life aligned with your principles, if not you will continue to struggle? Is what you think and what you do coherent? Great things are not done by impulse, but, by a series of things brought together to act at once to create a new effect. The whole becomes greater than the sum of its parts. The task for us is to create an alignment of our strengths, making our weaknesses irrelevant. Alignment begins with a crowd of one person, you. People who live this way have an essence that is real, pure and non-negotiable. They share their weaknesses and fears to complement their strengths. They become comfortable weaving all parts of their lives together into a quilt. Our level of effectiveness, contribution and integrity to others are correlated with our level of integration, self-actualization and total alignment for body, mind and spirit. The sun and seafood are going to make you a coherent laser for your network soon. Keep going.

Yeah. Woke up 5am today. Not so refreshed as days before, but ate a little too much in the evening, without much time before bed. Whatever, I fell asleep in 5min I think.
Had a seminar today. About heart insufficiency. Prof talked about a study they do at the moment about insulin resistance during sickness in healthy people. They found healthy people with flu or cold have a similar insulin resistance like T2 diabetes. But he told, they do not know why.
I have an idea, which I didnt tell him:

Insulin resistance = less carbs in the cells = less electrons on cytochrome 1 = more on cytochrome 2 from fats = less ROS, better electron flow = stronger magnetic field = more O2 and DHA come into tissue = also more reduced state - and sick people are more sleepy often - maybe a need for a more reduced state.

Thats my 2 cents. Makes sense to me.

Congrats! I am happy that **Anja** took the time and effort to write a book.
There are a lot of Germans who can't read sciency stuff in English. Not even simple language like popular Paleo books or blogs.
And definitively not **your blog** or those books you recommend as basic **"textbooks"** for the community for example.

Everything on the web I can really recommend for German health problem sufferers or health optimizers is in English.
And I don't mean German bloggers who write in English.
I tried to find quality information in German language from MDs, health practitioners, movement experts etc.
The results? Not impressive. A bit watered down Functional Medicine and "Mitochondrial Medicine" and a bunch of Paleo Blogs (you better stay with the US Paleo bloggers, they are smarter), ............
A handful good blogs. Maybe two hands. Like **Alexander Wunsch, Ulrike Gonder, Reiner Klement, PhD.**

What sells like Bratwurst and Bier in this country ?
Precisely in the German "self help/Bio Hack/Paleo/ Functional Medicine/Alternative Health/crossfit-andwhat not- population?
The German editions of Davis' _Wheat Belly_ & Cookbook, Perlmutter's _Grain Brain_, _Grain Maker & Cookbook,_ Hartwig's _It Starts With Food_ and more and more and more Paleo books. So Anja's book is a badly needed step forward.
What is in German urban bookstores toda_y?_
Piles of Paleo Lifestyle books and Vegan lifestyle books. Plus the _Reader's Digest_ version of uhm...Evolutionary Medicine and Ancestral Health.

And for "Fitness Training"?
1\. Running. 2. Running. 3. Running. 4. Aerobics. 5. Yoga.

### Butters — 2016-05-09T21:40:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-194427>

Im sensing declining hunger, the more I am out in the sun. 2 meals a day seems perfect to me often.
I try to match my carb in take to the sun exposure. When I have very heavy sun exposure, I eat more and when not I have no problem to stay fairly low carb. One could say carbs are a reward for sunbathing.
I think I noticed my skin gets more red when I get morning sun than getting mid day sun. So maybe the mix of IR and UVA in the morning has more effects on the blood (especially porphyrines). When I understood right, AM sun is jumping and loading the battery through charge seperation.
One day last week I had almost 6 hrs sun I think. From 6-9am and 10.30 - 1.30pm. I got red-brown on my whole back till the evening and used coconut on my whole skin. Was a little frightened to have a horrible sunburn, but the next day it was all gone. Only brown was left.
I used no sunscreen what so ever this season so far. It went very good. It seems I built up some shield.

### Butters — 2016-05-10T20:10:12+0300

I get some biohacks going soon. I use my magnesium oil every evening. It has 30% MgCl2 and destilled water. I just need to measure how much sprays are 1g.
I got an Epsom Salt delivery going and will use it for foot soakes and CT bath.
Im searching for leather shoes. I got an outlet in my city, so the chance to get all leather shoes for a good price is high. Im going to get some old fashioned italien style leather sandals.

I will quit the gym member ship and go for outside calistenics. I just dont see the sense in getting big through bodybuilding anymore. My aim is to get fit with my body now and being able to move, get some fat melting and weight falling maybe. Just being lank and lean. Now with a coming kid the training approach is not important for me anymore. I have to admit, I maybe did it for the girls . Whatever I dont want to get lazy and fat. I just switch to a functional movement approach like Ido Portal or Erwan LeCorre. Throwing weights in the studio is so far from reality. The last times I got in I didnt even like it. Its just the lights are so bad inside, it stinks, there is motivation falling when you could simply be outside in the sun and getting your UV at the same time. I think its just loosing quality time when you go into the gym. Outside you can ground, sunbath and train, all at the same time.
The thing to is also, I have a knee problem for 4-5 years now almost. No pain, but the patella is snipping over something or out of track when I am between the angles 110-70° flexion. I am frightened I ruined my knee in the first study years in which I did everything possible to scew my body up. I have the hope my university has a good doctor on that (my prof) who can bring some light. The last doc didnt have any clue and wanted to order a CT after a MRI, so I ditched him.

### Butters — 2016-05-11T09:11:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-194539>

It fixed itself through conditioning. It doesnt burn anymore.
I got approx. 1-2 g per spray. I figured out the molecular weight part of Mg is 25% of the MgCl2. So 1g = 300mg MgCl2 = 75mg Mg. The error is pretty high because my kitchen scale only shows with 1g accuracy. So minimum is 1g but it could be higher. So I would say per spray 75-150mg Mg is realistic.
Can too much Mg acually destroy sleep?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-198937>

Thats cool. Most amazing thing is, bushcraft really is so close to nature and an optimal life. In the past the only thing holding me back to do adventurous things was always my strong nearsightedness. Im always in fear to loose my glasses or contacts and be helpless. Thats why I didnt do many of those things in the past. I always dreamed about it while reading Karl May books, survival adventure books nature... but I didnt trust myself enough to try it out. This thing has been a real pain for my whole life and is a big reason for me staying at home and such things.
Problem is, I cant to many of those things in the future times, because a little baby is coming. So if we arent forced to flee into the nature (hopefully not), I wont have much time to practise and really no time to sleep in nature.
Whatever I started prepping with little things with my little money. Start practising essential skills like knife sharping etc.. Just learn to make many things alone and be prepared to have to do them alone and reparing things.

### smart cat — 2016-07-26T17:22:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-198943>

> [Butters said:](/goto/post?id=198937)
>
> I always dreamed about it while reading Karl May books, survival adventure books nature... but I didnt trust myself enough to try it out. This thing has been a real pain for my whole life and is a big reason for me staying at home and such things.

Strong myopia shouldn't be a big problem. I know a freeclimbing instructor (Deutscher Alpenverein) who couldn't see shit without glasses. Serious myopia. He is almost 70 yrs and still going strong as a climbing instructor. And he does other outdoor adventure training stuff as well.

I am sure you know about sports glasses. Or how did you train? There is a variety of glasses for every purpose. Even for bushcrafters:
[https://www.bushcraft-deutschland.d...1-Für-alle-Brillenschlangen-Progear-Eyeguard/](https://www.bushcraft-deutschland.de/index.php/Thread/16901-F%C3%BCr-alle-Brillenschlangen-Progear-Eyeguard/)
[https://www.amazon.de/adidas-Gletsc...&qid=1469542091&sr=8-1&keywords=Adidas+Tycane](https://www.amazon.de/adidas-Gletscherbrillen-Tycane-Pro-Brille/dp/B00FF3V1RI/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1469542091&sr=8-1&keywords=Adidas+Tycane)

But yes - as father of a baby you won't have time and energy left for outdoor adventures.

### smart cat — 2016-07-26T18:36:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-198945>

> [Butters said:](/goto/post?id=198937)
>
> Whatever I started prepping with little things with my little money. Start practising essential skills like knife sharping etc.. Just learn to make many things alone and be prepared to have to do them alone and reparing things.

Honestly: I don't believe the _zombie apocalypse_ is lurking around the corner in Germany.
But even if there were the invasion from armies of bad guys, terrorists or Klingons: do you really believe you can hide in a village? Or in nature?
Uhm....what Nature?  In GERMANY? In 2016?
Well maybe in 1525 (zu Zeiten der Bauernkriege).
I watched all those Westerns when I was a child. From John Ford to Howard Hawks to Samuel Fuller..... I practically lived as a cowboy in the Old West. In my fantasy.
[https://www.amazon.de/Cowboy-Legend...469544866&sr=1-2&keywords=Heinz-Josef+Stammel](https://www.amazon.de/Cowboy-Legende-Wirklichkeit-amerikanischen-Pioniergeschichte/dp/3570054381/ref=sr_1_2?s=books&ie=UTF8&qid=1469544866&sr=1-2&keywords=Heinz-Josef+Stammel)

In Germany you can't walk even 20 miles in the woods without meeting other people. Around Berlin not even 5 miles.....
It's a small and extreme population dense country. No place to hide except the high Alpes. Hoch droben auf der Alm. You are not Old Shatterhand and I am afraid die Sächsische Schweiz is not the Great Plains, the Rockies or Northern Canada.

Don't get me wrong: I don't want to make fun of your plans. Or your fears. And there are good reasons to be scared about the state of affairs in the world. But unless you are a professional survivalist you won't survive long in "real nature".

### Butters — 2016-07-27T12:11:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-198976>

Thank you for your many replies.
Of course I am not talking about pure nature survival and no zombie apocalypse.
But I think the next big crisis is on its way. The puppet masters are already pulling the strings for that. Some think it will begin this year, some believe not now but at least in 1-2 years.
How big is another question, but watching the trend I too believe it. At least we have to be ready for a time with food shortage at the market. More and more people think about civil war.
I honestly dont want to believe it, but now I think its just looking through pink glasses if I would believe everything is going to be fine without difficulties like the past years.
Sadly I think a crisis is the only way to get things changing here. Or better said: When things change for the good there will be a crisis.
This is what I try to prepare for. I dont want to talk about politics here, but man, its really obvious that things are out of control. And I just want to bring my family through this. I hope I dont need all that stuff. But better I am prepared and nothing happens than not prepared and there is chaos.
There are so many things, so many problems. The invasion of foreign men, the euro will collapse, the 2. Cold war, "leaders" in germany who are out of their minds (or like I think, know well what they are doing). Look how so few men can bind so many police men. What happens when more men per big city are going amok? When this happens they have to shut down all the transports (including food transports). They shut down the inner city of munich for several hours or more because one single (like "they" said) man went around shooting. Look at turkey and about how many migrants here in germany support the dictator. Its all creating a large powder keg. You need one single spark and the whole thing is blowing of. Its like 1914+.

### Butters — 2016-08-10T14:24:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-199474>

I ditched studio training 3 months ago. I was simply not so funny working out in a zoo. I now do home exercises in brief manner with BWE and strand pulling, you could say old strongman training with low equipment. Makes more fun. I am not so stressed out.
I lost something around 5-7kg I think. Some fat some muscle I think, because I dont do heavy squats and deadlifts anymore. But thats ok. I realized I just did it because I thought I must do it to look good or whatever to be healthy or strong. Not anymore. Im a growing father, and I want to be independent of a studio.
Weight is now around 82,5kg in the morning fasted. I look a little skinnier if one can say that with that weight  but its only the comparison with my former ego.

Soon I will pick bow shooting. It was a dream since I was a teenager. Now Im doing my first course and plan to buy a bow. Than of course shooting in the outdoors. My first hour starts tomorrow I am very excited, happy like a kid before christmas eve.

### smart cat — 2016-08-11T11:11:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-199513>

> [Butters said:](/goto/post?id=199474)
>
> I ditched studio training 3 months ago. I was simply not so funny working out in a zoo.
> I lost something around 5-7kg I think. Some fat some muscle I think, because I dont do heavy squats and deadlifts anymore. But thats ok. I realized I just did it because I thought I must do it to look good or whatever to be healthy or strong.

A wise decision. I never went into a gym. It always felt like an ugly place to me.
I grew up with "working out" in nature outdoors: living on a small farm (my grandparents) and working in summer on a big farm (my godfather).

"Exercise" on machines that makes you dumb , in toxic blue light and nnEMFs from all the tech gadgets always felt weird to me. And producing singlet oxygen ROS instead of triplet oxygen ROS doesn't look like a good idea.
This podcast with Jack Kruse and Ruben Salinas has some nuggets about outdoor training vs. gyms and crossfit boxes.

[https://itunes.apple.com/WebObjects/MZStore.woa/wa/viewPodcast?id=1139825099&i=373647233](https://itunes.apple.com/WebObjects/MZStore.woa/wa/viewPodcast?id=1139825099&i=373647233)

That's why I am a big fan of Ido Portal, Katy Bowman, Erwan LeCorre's MovNat, Rafe Kelley and the parkour guys etc.
Looking good on the outside and being a bad mover and frying ones connective tissue with blue light and EMFs and no sun? All show and no go I'd say.

**Katy Bowman: This is the year I challenge you to exercise _less._**
**Exercise Does Not Equal Movement**

[http://breakingmuscle.com/mobility-recovery/this-year-exercise-less](http://breakingmuscle.com/mobility-recovery/this-year-exercise-less)

[http://www.goodhousekeeping.com/lif...man-family-trades-mattresses-for-monkey-bars/](http://www.goodhousekeeping.com/life/parenting/a36553/bowman-family-trades-mattresses-for-monkey-bars/)

**THE MOVEMENT! Ido Portal**

**Interview mit Joseph Bartz - Schüler von Ido Portal - Movement Culture**

**The Workout The World Forgot | By MovNat**

**Rafe Kelley, martial arts, gymnastics and parkour .**

[http://www.evolvemoveplay.com/returntothesource16](http://www.evolvemoveplay.com/returntothesource16)

**Natural Movement Podcast: EMP with Kelly Starret and Juliet Starret Mobility Wod**

### Butters — 2016-09-15T11:40:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-201471>

Its like the little one has got clue of day and night. She drinks more at daytime and has longer breaks at nighttime. Its a very easy baby. Drinks and sleeps. Some problems with bloating after the real milk has come.
We only use redlight at nighttime for a nursing light, its a tiny astro map light. And himalayan salt lamp to wind at night. I am surprised, we get plenty of sleep.
She has a little yellow face but her bilirubin value in the blood was ok. I will go outside with her today and try to do every day, since I read Otts book.

### Butters — 2016-09-16T23:53:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-201517>

Went outside today second time. Little baby was quiet the whole time. Of course it had close on, so I pulled of my t-shirt and took it to the chest with skin contact, grounding during that time. I got some little smiles, which are unintentional of course, but I think she loves grounding and daylight.

### Butters — 2016-09-17T22:13:02+0300

So. It only was in the evening. I had the best night for 7 days. It happens regularly that the little one sleeps 4 hours after eating. ### Butters — 2016-09-30T12:56:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-202116>

Little girl is gaining. Almost 200g last week. The midwife said she is fast forward in eye movement, because she already can fixate things. Again very little problems. Almost no screaming, only when hungry or when having some bloatings, or need a change of diapers. But the bloatings fade.
I only have to get up twice at night to change her diapers. I get pretty good sleep periods. Changing and nursing is done in himalayan salt lamp light or just led redlight for nursing sometimes.

Wow Alex so nice to read about how good you are doing   and the baby

### Butters — 2016-10-31T16:00:33+0200

Thanks. We dont cover her up so much. She doesnt like it. She tells us most of the time when its to warm for her.
I now look on eye color. I hit irodology stuff, because I have a strange mix of color in my eye. With natural light is outward gree/blue going brown/green around the pupil. Read about that being eventually sulfur from the stomach.
Maybe there is some connection between my eye color and bad skin I have since puberty.

### Butters — 2016-11-04T22:28:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-203616>

I just heard a part of a somewhat new podcast with Jack. Brought me to write something down.
Now that I realized really in my head that mitochondrial DNA is coming from our mothers I begin to look closely at my mother and of course of my girlsfriends mother.
My mother has always been very slim. She is not very hungry most of the time and hasnt had many big problems. Same about my grandma, but she now is overweight in her 80s, but same here no big illnesses.
Although I have to mention my sister has an autoimmun disease "collagenosis". So she must have the same mitochondrial DNA like me. I always asked myself why that is that she got this at the age of 16 all of a sudden and I got nothing. I am 3 years older than her and I never wanted to have a mobile phone, because I liked to be independant and all other friends had to call their parents (that was a big pro for me, not to have one ). She bought herself a phone I think with 14 or 15 and I think she had it switched on the whole time. I switched my phone of every night from my first phone on, but the purpose was like above independance and silence, no stress when sleeping. But I have to admit I wore it some years switched on in my pocket. Whatever their was no Wifi on my phone and no internet. Coming back to the story...
I dont know why the hell she got this. She had almost no hemoglobin with 16 and could have died. Now she works in a hospital as a nurse, man.... I really cant do much about it. At least I think I got her to switch of her phone at night, but she is so addicted to her phone and pad in her free time. I have to submit Im doing things on the laptop big time of a day, but at least I have f.lux on on daytime and in the night plus bluebockers with long clothes and all the time switched of wifi going with LAN connection and my phone is off most time of the day.
I just realize writing this blog my brain is not working good. Writing all different stuff together without a red line. I have some work to do.

Coming back to the podcast. I just realized with a bang what a bitch blue light from devices is in the daytime too. I instinctively had f.lux on on 2700K during the day and twillight on the phone, because I found that the normal screen is way to blue for my eyes and its disturbing even in daytime. Now I got why. Its the damn configuration of the light without all other parts of the spectrum hammering on the pituitary gland.
On that topic my kid gets it. I just have to look on my kid. She is looking at the windows for a long time of being awake. And she really get calm when I open the window and let her watch out. I try to get her a morning light every morning - no direct sun though, but shes closing her eyes anyway when direct sun hits them. Although my girlfriend said, when they were outside one time with blue sky the girl turned her head into the sun. Kids getting it instinctively till idiots tell them the sun is dangerous or give them technical devices

I have still some books to read. But I am searching for some german books on mitochondria and the whole topic.

Main theme of today- Trump won.

### Alper — 2016-11-12T12:06:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-203859>

> [Butters said:](/goto/post?id=203780)
>
> Just found that: [http://www.rsarchive.org/RelArtic/Marinelli/](http://www.rsarchive.org/RelArtic/Marinelli/)
> Stating that the heart is not a pump.
> Together with the video of the helical heart, all makes more sense.

Rudolf Steiner was a smart ass  I´m studying anthroposophical medicine in my free time and he had some pretty genius speeches / texts written. He discribed the process of "building matter from sun" long before vitamin D etc was founded, just by making conclusions from the lifecycle of a butterfly. Pretty cool stuff.
And as you mentioned, he was the one who predicted that the heart is not a pump in the early 20th century!!!!! He followed the "particle physics" of his time in the early 20th century pretty accurately, so he was "one of us" deadling with the physical body of the human. The folks of his time (and our time as well) tend to analyze the human body down to it´s smallest particles but he also suggested not only to microscope the human body, but also to have a much bigger perspective ("makroskopieren" in german) to unterstand processes of the human body and the human soul and spirit. You can compare it to the idee of Jack(in one of his podcasts) to understand obesity by comparing it with a star getting bigger, loosing energy as it gets older. We now know that those are mechanically comparable processes just understanding and applying the laws of thermodynamics while in his time he would have called it a more "cosmic view" on the understanding of the human. He was a natural scientist as well as a humanities scholar. He tried to bring together both scientific discplines even though they seem so diverging for the understandment of the human nature.

I can just recommend anybody to read his books and speeches. They are all available online or you can at leat in germany buy them as a book. He had some really crazy ideas. The more I read his books, the more it seems plausible to me, because all his predictions are following certain rules which are equivalent no matter if you study the human body, education, society or anything else. It´s all based on the same laws and rules and thats sometimes hard to admit on your own mind, because we aren´t taught and educated that way.

### Butters — 2016-11-21T15:08:40+0200

### Butters — 2016-11-22T12:38:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-204203>

Second day after ditching those headphones. Damn I feel much better. Its like a dizziness is fading. I feel more motivated, more clear. I didnt believe this is gonna be a big difference but I have to admit it could be.
We got a sports medicine seminar today. Some EKG things about right heart hypertrohy. Along with training regimes. Thousand things came into my mind and I began to daydream (because it was so boring) about UV dilatating vessels and grounding reducing viscosity and improving zeta potential. And when I heard about O2 saturation I thought about jeremy and what he can do and has to do therefore.
But I dont think the whole institute is going to be a help on my way. I hope I can write my master thesis about some sunlight effects or grounding.

### Butters — 2016-11-28T14:17:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-204415>

First Cold bath after months. Drank 700ml ice water before, no headaches. Seems like good hydration/DHA in the body.
Also no much peeing in the water. Good BUN/Creatinine ratio? But I realize when I am outside in the cold, I tend to pee more.
Cold bath was 20min. I start low in. Although I didnt freeze in the water and didnt shiver.

### Butters — 2016-11-28T19:47:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-204437>

We had a power test in university today. I volunteered.
I got cold hands and arms through the test, some cold sweat and after my maximum of 260 watts I never got good anymore at the end I had kind of vasovagale synkope. The Watts was not very good but I think in the normal range.
Tomorrow there will be an echo for the heart, because someone else had it in the lesson before today. I hope this is nothing about the valves. I had an infect 6 months ago and after that some episodes of weakness during training.

### Butters — 2016-11-29T21:14:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-204505>

Tomorrow I will have my blood taken. They want to look if my CRP is high because of an infection. But I dont feel bad. Day after tomorrow is ultrasound of my heart. I think they will look for valve actions and ejection fraction.
Other thing I watched my teeth and I can recognize that I can look slightly through them on the front teeth. Is that a sign of demineralisation? Can I reverse that? I dont know maybe
I brush to hard. I brushed very seldom in the past and I had no problems with cavities whatsoever. But I think I have weakend enamel or so, because I have some little broken spots in front. Maybe that is because of grinding and because I have a closed bite for what I have to shove my jarbone back, so that it fits.

I am really frightened about that heart issue. I tend to think about bad things all the time. It can even get me so far that I am near of fainting. Its seldom but when I really have some imagination in my head about blood drawing or so I can obsess about it so much I get a vagotone and blood pressure is dropping. So I am a little prone for psychosomatic things.

I just decided I will up DHA even more. Because there is no moving till next year out of the city.
I will feed here the news about my consultation.

### Butters — 2016-12-01T14:19:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-204581>

I had an echo of the heart today. Doc says all is ok. All valves ok. Ejection fraction is 75. But pulmonary pressure was 20.5 mm Hg. Thats a little high although in the normal range.
But I heard from some sides including Jack I shouldnt worry. So I try not.

I use magnesium oil again on me at night. The last 2 days I used it I rocked the sleep. I almost didnt woke up when the nursing occured. Wished my girlfriend could sleep that good too.
Also I skipped the coffee for the last 2 days. I will try that again. I used green tea instead. I try to use bottled water for all my water I drink now, for months.
My DHA content in the brain seems to be good according to the ice water drinking test. Although I am a little sloppy with the carbs and sweets. I try to eat at least a fish meal for every meal I have such things and of course every other meal too
Really upped that mackerel and salmon, sometimes some blue mussels. I cant get oysters here.
I try to eat a tablespoon of coconut oil every meal for my gut.
Im in search for bones for broth, but its difficult here to find some organic.
Grounding is difficult for me with sort of raynauds. Im constantly watching for leather only shoes on a budget for that.

Edit:
I just did a short unintended biohack. The one himalyan salt lamp is broken and I switched on the blacklight in the dark room. I had blueblockers and glasses on and had weird sprinkles in my field of view. Took blueblockers of - much better. Saw fluorescens around me. Looked over my glasses in the light- much better and brighter. I just saw that my glasses must block the UV of the lamp. Then I held them in front of white material and look dark shadows where the glasses block the light. I mean I knew it before thats when I just made the connection with my eyes. And I saw that glasses alter my reality and field of view. That was amazing.
And that must tell me, that the blacklight really is a UVA light.

### Butters — 2016-12-15T00:19:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-205279>

I posted this in optimal labs but I'll post my BUN/Cr here too, because its important.
I just found in europe there is not BUN measurement but urea instead. So I looked some things about and translated urea to BUN:

urea: 4,5mmol/l - 27,03 mg/dl
creatinine: 91 µmol/l - 1,03 mg/dl

I found, you can calculate BUN= urea x 0,467. That would be 12,62.
That would be a BUN/creatinine of 12,2:1.
I hope its all correct. When its correct that is not a too bad point to start off in a city.
I found 10:1 should be better and best would be about 7:1.

### Butters — 2017-01-03T22:29:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-206095>

I decided to try eyesight training from today on. I want my eyes to become better. Clearly UV is needed but I researched a bit and found that some people did improve eyesight with continous training with or with weaker glasses. I got weaker prescription glasses from home, I said for emergency when my other glasses break. But I plan to take them all the time at home or when I dont need good eyesight, like when I read or at the computer.
I noticed my eyes try to focus more and can make a sharp picuture although the worse prescription. And although intuitive it should be more strenous on the eyes it is not so bad. Indeed a bit more relaxing than before. I can feel some pressure is fading from the temples when I relax.

### Butters — 2017-01-16T00:01:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-206880>

My fitness and wellbeing has improved since I do weight training and HIIT regularly. I do not stress myself, but I make time for it.
Since Im doing eye exercises, for now just eye movement a few times a day, my tension in my eye muscles has reduced. I can roll my eyes more freely than before.
I try to use my weaker glasses when reading or in front of the computer and be in a slight blurr to get the eye to focuse.
I dont know if its true what Bates said about the eye muslces being responsable for focuse. I read it has been disproved. But although I read that the muscles can become so strong and tight, that they squeeze the eyeball, what could elongate it for myopia. Than there of course is sunlight. I think it is a bit of all.
My little daughter seem to have eagle eyes at 4 months, I am surprised how far she can see us smiling. Thank god, she has no such bad sight like me. I will do my very best to keep that.

### Butters — 2017-01-22T12:38:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-207201>

22.01. News from the front

I realized this week that my dandruff is almost gone. I mentioned it in a dandruff thread already. Question is, why?
I've been taking brazil nuts (2-4 pieces) per day for my selenium for 1,5 months now. I used a neutral shampoo to wash every second day, but I used it for a while before + before that I used urea shampoo and noticed no improvement. Also I had dandruff on the eyebrows and I didnt directly wash them in the shower, that had also improved massively - 1 point more for the selenium theory.
Its more likely that the added selenium was important for me.
Maybe there is a connection to the thyroid. Because I have had knots since puberty and my TSH could be lower. So probably iodine and selenium is low. I started taking brazil nuts in preperation to taking lugols, which I integrated yesterday first for 1 drop of 5% lugols (2,5mg iodine, 3,75mg potassium-iodine per drop). I realized though that my seafood eating (despite of eating every day) is not enough for iodine.
I definetely thank Jack for mentioning the selenium hint.

But there is also bad things in the news. I dont know if its worse than before but it really annoys me that my fingers get cold so fast. Thats is definetely raynauds syndrom. Maybe there is hope in improving with iodine supplementation.

### Butters — 2017-02-02T23:22:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-207809>

I noticed that I grind my teeth again. I hadnt had it before, but since I am a father I seem to grind more at night. I dont know if its the sleep, more stress or the sleeping position. I think I grind more when I lay on the side than on the back. I have to stop that, my front teeth are becoming a little shiny.
I again will ditch the coffee, because I think it makes my teeth yellow. I use coconut oil almost everyday but nothing happens to my teeth. I add a Vitamin K supplement to my diet now.
Also I maybe need to see some different doctors in the next time:
I will look for a sono for my thyroid to get an idea of the goiter, which I think I have.
I need a doc looking at my right knee, because the patella is somehow cracking and jumping now for some years but now it disturbes me because when I drive bike there is always this snapping feeling on every cycle.
Also I need to see a dentist and maybe an orthodontist. The dentist for routine checkup and for making me maybe a biting devices for the night as long as it fixes itself. The orthodontist because I have a deep bite and my front teeth are getting grinded of more and more and the enamel gets bad (little pieces break out). Also because I have to move my jar distally to get occlusion and that will maybe a problem with the years coming.
I hate it. I feel like an old man or so. And because I dont like it I push it away everytime. I dont know why. It seems I dont want to know what is happening with me. Because when I dont know it doesnt become reality or so.

Although those bad things (for me), I noticed I became more open for criticism over the last weeks/months. Also I can question my currents beliefs without a big "mind barrier" and adapt to new information and facts.
My sense of tradition and values of my people has risen over the past weeks. I want to know everything about the past and what shouldnt be told in history class. Also about what is "forbidden" to think and know. Because the things I discover are so big I get more involved in that instead of optimal living. But optimal living is running in the background and maybe gives me the strength to change my mind again and again. I realized there are things that are bigger than selfish optimization and neglecting everything else. I use it as a tool for my mind and body to make me able to do the things for my people that do matter. Because optimal only is useful in a world where you can live in peace. And what is optimal living worth when you are in good shape and healthy but you have no fundament to build your life on or no way to go but to live and die some time. Luckily I discovered that optimal living is more traditional in my people and deeply connected to that values I than I thought. More I come to a thought of the technical revolution as a tool for the little international clique that want to control the world and split the people apart . Because when you have no deep values you just get stuck at the phone or laptop or tv. You have no big aim in your life, you just think of yourself, of the moment, of the joy of the moment and joy over everything. So you are stuck at those dopamine stations, because you cant get dopamine from the things that should matter.
(philosophical thinking and bad english writing out for today  )

### Butters — 2017-03-03T12:06:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-209549>

For the last weeks I really have not found the time and silence to update my log. But recently I have found some more time to educate myself.
So I will update now:
I integrated a more regular bodyweight routine into my life. I have 3 times a week a calisthenics session. Currently Im doing it at home. I have no a door pullup bar, so I can really do everything at home, when its raining outside or the weather is simply to bad. Also that is practical because with a little kid I simply dont have allways time to go outside and train there. Hopefully that will change this summer, when the little daughter is more mobile.
When I can I add 2 short HIIT sessions with kettlebell swings on 2 other days. These sessions are 15-20min long and throw them in between when I have time.

I experiment with lugols at the moment. Iam currently on 3 drops of 5% per day and 1-2 brazil nuts. Slowly working up. I got a swallen thyroid from some days on 6 drops. But that faded. So I am experimenting right on the edge. Also was taking 1000mg of K2, because I wanted to improve my teeth together with coconut oil - no improvement so far.

Now some not so good news for me:

I got some 2 or 3 eye floaters all of a sudden over night 2 weeks ago. They really annoy me. I dont know why and where are they coming from. Last time I checked I had no issues with fasting blood sugar and BUN/creatinine ratio. I read Jacks blogpost about some eye issues and its not fitting in there.
Question I have to ask now, because its coupled slightly to lugols and K2 usage, can that affect formation of eye floaters? Or can it be a change process in the eye, because I noticed a slightly better vision on the snellen? Of course I have to raise the question, if the winter low sun exposure period has something to do with it together with laptop work (although I use f.lux on the lowest seeting even during the day and using blueblockers general in the evening)?
So at the moment I see the only method to improve this is to get more sun in the future spring and summer time at the eye, together with cutting laptop work even more. Beside that I dont know what to do about. I could try to improve my blood sugar more with carbohydrate restriction, but like I said I had no problem with blood sugar testing.

### Butters — 2017-03-06T17:28:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-209760>

My dandruff has or say scurf, because its not that strong, has come back. It seems that it wasnt the selenium. But also not the shampoo, because I still use it.
I maybe need to see a orthodontist, because finally my vertical standing front teeth are causing some problems in the jaw, because it has to move distally with every bite. My old dentist said in my teens I should do anything because my teeth were so good. But now thats hitting back. I dont know if the teeth are regrouping with my wisdom teeth came. I dont know. In my teens some orthodontist said the would saw my jaw and make it shorter, thats of course what I didnt want because there werent no wisdom teeth out. I hope when I need some thing, a normal sublingual bar is enough. Of course there is the thing with EMF seeking metal, but I wont live here the whole time.

I need to cut back on coffee. It seems I developed an addiction, because I regulary drink it and the baby is causing some sleep disruptions. I am at 1-2 cups per day till 2pm, so I will cut back first to one cup and than slowly more.

### Butters — 2017-05-10T12:48:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-213803>

So Im updating once again. Life had me for a while. Im updating also, because I think I am on a downward spiral of health. I noticed some things that are stacking up.

But I will start with positive things:
My sleep is has not worsen. I dont sleep so much, because of the kid but I can sleep through and dream. I dont have sleeping problems in general. Once again I think my dandruff has become better, but thats not for sure. So that are the good things.

Now the bad things:
1\. I got from my perspective many eye floaters from february on. Im not sure if they appeared all of a sudden or I just noticed them because outside its brighter now when spring began. They are very disturbing when outside, I dont recognize them much when Im inside. They are mostly long strings some dots can I see when I blink. I tried to get a seeing at the opthalmologist, but they have 6 month or more waiting time and just told me "if the floaters more, there is nothing to worry about, when there are flashes or a dark curtan I have to go immediately to the ambulance". So no real help there. I will try to go to another doctor, maybe there is an opportunity. Im a little bit scared because I have -7 diopters and the floaters happend to come suddenly. But they came on both eyes, not just one. No accident before that.
2\. I went to the dentist and I have caries on all of my 3 wisdom teeth in the back  . I dont know right now how bad it is, but it seems I didnt brush enough in the back of the mouth. On wisdom teeth is still not broken through, I hope I dont need surgery. My teeth are slightly yellow. I dont know if that is from coffee or bad redox. I have a location on a front tooth that is breaking out in small parts. I maybe need that covered. But I am frightened that I have weak tooth enamel.
Also it seems my front teeth are kipping more back. I started teeth grinding more often at night and often got problems to really close my jar.
3\. I have a small knot in front of my right ear. It seems to be a parotis tumor. I have not yet gone to the doctor, because I recently discovered it. But it seems it grew pretty fast. Im scared also, because my dad had an parotis tumor surgery in his mid 40s. I surely have to see a doctor fast for that
4\. An old problem is disturbing me. I have had patella noises for some years on my right knee when moving through the ROM. Its a popping noise, like the patella is jumping over an obstacle everytime I extend the knee. Recently I experienced light swallen knees on both sides after walking for 1,5hrs. I dont know if this is normal, because Im usually not moving much this time and maybe indicated weak muscle endurance.

Now to the things I did wrong since my kid was born:
I ate very many carbohydrates again. Sometimes 200+ grams per day. But I had an fasting glucose check of under 80 in november. So I though there would be no problem. But it was paired with not much movement and winter time. I didnt eat past 7pm most of the time, upper end seldom was 8pm. I didnt eat so much fish like I used to to (at least one meal a day). Sometimes I just hit 1 meal of fish a week. Also its pretty hard for my to cut the carbs now. I ate grain things more often during that period.
I had almost no grounding during that time. I can count on one hand how many times and that was in spring .
I have been using my laptop very long time a day and got pretty addicted. Explanation is, that I use the internet to get information etc. I used f.lux in daytime with 2700k setting though and blueblockers after 6pm. Maybe thats the explanation why my sleep is not wrecked. My laptop is not grounded and Im sitting in front of that.
I do not use wifi in our flat, its all hardwired, although we are living in a appartment building and I can sense 3-5 strong wifi signals and more very weak signals. I live in the 4. floor 12m above the ground. There is a cell phone tower 150-200m away from our flat. At least I dont use my cell phone much and it is always switched off.
I dont get morning sunlight through the open window regularly. I experience massive tears when I look in the bright sky now.

It seems I messed myself up big time since my child was born.
That thing that disturbes me most is my floater problem. Im scared most of my parotis knot.
I know what problems are and what I can do to midigate. But can these things actually cause floaters and teeth stuff?

### Butters — 2017-07-26T17:03:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-220139>

Found something about my eye flashes:

"The entoptic phenomenon of momentary phosphenes or flick phosphenes can occur in the eye with quick eye movements in normal, healthy individuals, as described by Nebel3 and personally observed by Verhoeff. These phosphenes that occur in normal eyes share a common pathophysiology with eyes having pathology: mechanical stimulation or irritation of retinal receptors. A detailed clinical history, family history, and dilated funduscopic examination will help differentiate the ominous causes of flashes of light, especially in patients with a normal-appearing fundus. A unifying principle in evaluating the differential diagnosis of "flashes" is that, while unilateral photopsias most often point to local ocular causes, bilateral symptoms typically describe chiasmal or retrochiasmal etiologies and systemic processes."

**BILATERAL CAUSES OF FLASHES OF LIGHTS**
Bilateral causes of phosphenes or flashes of lights can be grouped into vascular effects on the cerebral cortex, medication-related side effects, and bilateral retinopathies.

The most common cause of bilateral flashing phenomenon is migraine with aura, although it is unilateral in up to 70% of patients.26

Since bilateral symptoms often point to the cerebral cortex or postchiasmal pathology, it is not surprising that ischemia or edema to the cerebral cortex including the visual cortex can cause bilateral symptoms of phosphenes. Reported unusual causes of phosphenes include local cerebral edema to the temporal-occipital lesion in an occipital dural arteriovenous malformation,29 cortical injury to both occipital lobes after removal of a posterior falx meningioma.30 and infarct of the occipital lobe presenting as an atypical migraine causing photopsias in 1 patient in Japan.31

The bilateral perception of widespread flickering or "snow" on a television screen can point to various forms of drug-related side effects. Drugs such as digitalis, quetiapine (Seroquel, AstraZeneca), paclitaxel, quinine, and quinidine32-36 have been implicated in patients who may have an otherwise normal-appearing exam, although the pathophysiology remains unknown. Photopsias have also been reported in a patient with occupational contact with fish anesthetic M-222, which resolved after treatment.37

Bilateral degenerative retinopathies may have similar flickering photopsias, specifically hereditary tapetoretinal degenerations and paraneoplastic autoimmune retinopathies. Degeneration of retinal receptor elements bilaterally are likely most responsible for photopsias seen in 35% of patients with retinitis pigmentosa.38-39 Paraneoplastic diseases such as cancer-associated retinopathy (CAR) and melanoma-associated retinopathy40-41 and autoimmune retinopathies mimicking CAR without the evidence of malignancy present with bilateral photopsias. Damage of the inner plexiform layer or Mueller cells are among the pathophysiology for these autoimmune retinopathies.42-43

An uncommon cause for bilateral luminous images is the Charles Bonnet syndrome. In this condition, often seen in patients with vision loss from age-related macular degeneration, the source of light is a visual hallucination arising from the visual cortex after deafferentation from the central visual pathway.44

[http://www.retinalphysician.com/iss...just-a-pvd-differential-diagnosis-of-flashing](http://www.retinalphysician.com/issues/2007/nov-dec/not-just-a-pvd-differential-diagnosis-of-flashing)

I thought about another problem before. Because floaters as well flashes did occur in both eyes at the same time.

Edit: Found a site to test my monitor for flicker. And it flickers even at full brightness  :
[http://www.lagom.nl/lcd-test/inversion.php#invpattern](http://www.lagom.nl/lcd-test/inversion.php#invpattern)

Maybe it helps others. I have to cut screen time even more.

### Butters — 2017-07-27T23:17:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-220248>

I changed my training regime 2 weeks ago to a more sustainable and not so taxing routine.
I reentered kettlebell training in combination with bodyweight exercises. For that I used the "Simple and Sinister" program of Pavel Tsatsouline. That was my entry into training 10 years ago and now it seems to come back at me. Because I have not much time in one big piece I try to train every day. Then I make room for 30min, when I have more - good, when I have not more time, then be it.
The main part of the program are Swings and Get-ups with the kettlebell. Key is not to train till exhaustion, that makes it easier on the central nervous system and the bodies hormones. So I end every session a little bit tired but very fit. Volume is slowly building up. When I have more time I add some bodyweight exercises like Rows, Pullups, Pushups. Same principle here. As soon as velocity dropping significant on exercises I end the set, big motor units are giving up - more taxing on the system when one continue.
That seems to be very good. I am not tired at all the next day and can train every day. Also I get a HIIT every day.

### Butters — 2017-08-01T23:15:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-220650>

Had a follow up eye exam today. Everything was right. No vitreous detachment, nothing else. My sight got a little better eventually. The refractometer said left -6.5 right -6.00. My old glasses have -7.00. So at least when you count something of for the distance between eye and lense its to strong for my dominant right eye. I know use old glasses with -5.75 and get some new. Now that I have some good values I can order some glasses for the near work and distance.
We were on the parents home at the weekend. I took my meter with me and was positively shocked. There is almost no wireless radiation in the home, when the wifi is off. My parents use a eco phone that indeed shuts off every radiation when locked in the station. Even the wifi is very weak in the next room and seems to give away no strong radiation. Or the walls are blocking much radiation. So I slept two night with all LED green and the lowest wifi radiation I ever detected that weekend. Always had a good feeling about that house.

### Butters — 2017-10-16T14:57:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-225972>

I just gave thought about my mitochondrial fundament. Like Jack said, we want to know how are mothers and grandmothers were doing.
It shows, that my grandmother and mother never had any issue with heart disease. I think my grandmother doesnt even take pills for anything. Yet she has a bit more weight to carry, despite a very unhealthy lifestyle for the last 15 years. My mother is thin, but healthy thin I think and has no real issues, too. So my fundament is not as bad. But both of them got glasses with age and my mother needs them now for distance and near. She used to have no glasses vor 45 years. My grandmother also used to have no glasses for much longer. Despite that I was diagnosed with high myopia on one eye and high hyperopie in the other with two years and of course got glasses prescriped what may started the whole journey to high myopia on both eyes of approx. -6.00 diopters and now floaters and lightings with eye movements.
I should mention my mother has got unclear skin and I have it too, my sister too. So this is a connection. My mother used to have huge migraines when she used to work in a hospital. I think it went away with leaving the hospital practise. My mothers sight gets more bad fast now and I think it must be tied to working close up with glasses and now never seeing the sun. Maybe is started for her with the initial glasses for work.

So thats just some thoughts about my mitochondrial family history.

### Butters — 2017-10-22T23:21:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-226406>

Short thought to a thing that struck me while raising dopamine levels with a friend.
We were discussing heteroplasmy rates and how it ties to developing diseases. And I told him we have many friends how seems to have migranes very often, also my mother used to have them while working in hospital, my sister also had them, me not.
So my thoughts were: When the pain of migranes is related to vasodilating arteries, why are they dilatate? To bring more oxygen to the tissue, I think. But why do it need it? Maybe because there is not enough energy made in the ECT so the body tries to up the oxygen to pull more electrons through. Can it be related to higher heteroplasmy in the tissue? I know that most of these people are glucose depended and glucose has less electrons plus if the blood sugar drops there is a deficit straight away = electron deficit in the ECT. These people seem to do better with high fat and MCTs with more electrons. Is it tied to leakyness of the first complex due to glucose dependency?

Interesting thing:
Buddy told me he hacked is hangover some time ago with a cold shower straight to the head till it was numb. Pain was gone for the day. He wore his blueblockers all the time during drinking out  he told me.
This friend told me that he is very thankful that I once brought him on the way of quantum biology. Once he knew I he just cant go back.

When Im on the laptop at home I always use my blueblockers + Iris on 1200 K or so. Iris is always on that low and when I am in the library I even turn it lower (shitty lights there of course too). I realized I now react sensitive to flicker, I notice it when it is in my field of view. I try to get my morning sun when its out and no clouds are there. ### Butters — 2017-11-29T15:52:56+0200

New contact lenses. I searched for ones without UV protection and found an english vompany called "cooper vision" with "biofinity" lenses. I also see my sight indeed has improved or at least the cillary spasm resolved.
I think this is a better option for me than going without glasses and cant really do anything outside. Morning sun can be observed still without any glasses and contacts.
I recently noticed more dreaming and I can remember dreams.
Also something improved my raynaulds symptoms. I dont know what I did, but my fingers didnt turn white today at 4°C and nasty wind without protection. Cherry red skin on all fingers but nuthing more. And that despite consuming less fish than last year or maybe because? Maybe the blueblocking works, maybe the high dose lugols work, dont know.

### Butters — 2017-12-11T19:48:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-230017>

Too bad?   Would be too much argument
This winter weird thing happened. My raynaulds symptoms are completely gone so far. I used to get white finger tips when going out in T-shirt or with sweater only at say 0°C for a time. And I had it badly last year when I was shoving the babys buggy and exposing them to the cold. Now they get very cherry red from the tips to knuckles and after one hour may get rigid  but no white anymore.
Im really impressed. I dont know what I was doing, because seriously Im eating a lot more crap than last year and not so much fish.
Nevertheless I rigorously block blue light since summer like stinky dog shit. Not even at daytime I allow shitty lights when I can ditch them and more and more I feel how they hurt when Im in an environment that has LED shit etc. and Im out as fast as I can get out. Iris even at daytime on 0K plus most of the time blueblockers every time Im at the laptop.
When Im doing exercises at home I switch my blacklight on as I go almost naked. No that is winter time I go by two meals a day easily and despite eating crap sometimes I never,ever snack and didnt eat late in the evening. So maybe a big dinner with some crap but thats it. 4 hrs minimum till sleeping.
Another thing could be the lugols that I was taking some days in large doses.

### Butters — 2018-01-28T23:06:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-233700>

Im a bit frightened about my jaw and teeth. I have a deck bite with normal standing teeth but this is shoving my mandibula back in the joint everytime the teeth occlude. I have good occlusion when I eat but I think this is the reason for me teeth damage. Im thinking about some orthodontist look up but Im frightened about surgery.
Got back to glasses again, because I realized they are to strong for my right eye (I have only one set ordered). I can see 20/20 now with my right eye and the -5,75 glasses and 20/25 with my left eye and a bit time, good light and focus (the floaters disturb sometimes). It seems, that using lower prescription for near work and blocking blue light helps. I will see if I make greater results this year. I need a weaker prescription now for near work. The difference between outdoor sunlight and indoor lights is big.
Floaters persist, flashes of light also. Its some kind of weird that the flashes arent always at the same intensity. I only have them with movement and the last checkup by the eye doc said all was fine with my eye background and macula. They said it must be photopsia caused of the connection between eyeball and retina are pulling on the retina because of fast movement and the longer eyeball because of the myopia. But sometimes the effect is stronger and sometimes I even barely can notice something. That makes me hope that it can go away. Weird also I got them at the same times floaters, flashes and both eyes. That makes me think of a systemic reason. I also have the entoptic phenomenon when looking on bright surfaces. Jack mentioned it could be due to flicker effects on the muller cells. And that indeed could be because I was using my laptop before that a lot with dimmed backlights, which I tested on flicker and there was flicker. Iam using the laptop only with full backlight and Iris software since that moment. I hope this makes a difference. At least my camera cant film flicker at full brightness.

### Butters — 2018-02-15T12:55:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-234801>

So Im experimenting with weaker glasses for some time now. I got on that by endmyopia.org but I recently found a german webside that explains the same stuff and without any advertising and deals. So no thinking of making money here.
My researches show that indeed you can make animals and humans make myopic with minus lenses quite fast and reverse the process through plus lenses.
Also cillary spasm is a great deal, because it can make up to -1,25 diopters and that is huge. So this is I think the way I got this myopic.
So connecting that Jack said he reduced his prescription by -3 diopters only with sunlight and people who getting better with weaker glasses, there seem to be two ways to improve. My hope is indeed that I can improve even faster with using both ways.
After my last visit of the opthomologist, I give a fuck on this opinion. They gave my dilating drops and then measured my eyesight. But even then they would prescribe me 0,25 diopter weaker glasses then my last prescription. So in the most unnatural, unrealistic conditions they would prescribe me weaker glasses that would be way to strong in bright outdoor sunlight. And they were indeed somehow curious when they realized my strength didnt change from the last exam.
I myself made the observation that dry eyes can make up for like -0,5 diopters worse. Also with bright light I can see two snellen lines more. So there is personal evidence that the presciption standards are bullshit.
I ordered now weaker glasses for near work like reading to relaxe my cillary muscles to a max. I hope to make huge gains when now the sunlight is getting stronger. Some of the gains in bright condition may be due to pupil constriction but that is all natural and of course I have to adapt to that, because I even want a challenge then.
I would be my pleasure to shock the opthomologist next year with a 0,5-1 diopter reduction, that is my big aim.
Every mm eyeball shriking should minimize my risk for retinal detachment, what is my biggest concern and fear right now. So Im all in on that.

Going on more epi paleo keto has improved my stool again. I should note that to myself.
I took an epsom salt bath with my kid last week and that night I had the most relaxing sleep for weeks I think. The kid also slept very deep and calm. Im applying transdermal magnesium since then.

> Yes. The thing is, I did so much wrong that I dont know what could have made it better.

There is the challenge I had to give up.

I'm too glad to feel better to go experimenting with whether it was more grounding, CT, or sunlight. Instead of trying to isolate each so I can know, I've reorganized my life to get more of each.

### Butters — 2018-06-18T12:20:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-243653>

On a boat trip the weekend. Got some sunburn the day. Next day it was almost gone and now its completely gone. Is that a good sign? I got pretty red that day. So far no peeling in sight.
Also I get out with my daughter in sunlight in the mornings and midday/afternoon. When we can we go at least upperbody naked, I also pull her shirt off to get the morning light in and condition her for midday sun. She seem to have a lighter skin type than I, or it may develop as she grows. But not even in 45min sunlight midday she got a sunburn. She is better than her mother, who has also light skin.
I got a aha moment when we was at the lake one time in early morning till midday and not even my wife got a staying sunburn. Only for the day she got red, next day it was gone. That was my aha-moment for hybrid tanning, it must be that, because she got red so fast in the past.
I dont go for sunscreen for my kid, but my wife sometimes do. At least I convinced her to use no chemical sunscreen but one with minerals only. But she also seldom uses it now.

On the boat trip I was the only one who didnt use sunscreen. Its amazing how frightened they are about sunburn, but its no wonder if they dont go out in the sun and have the experience that it can fade away pretty quick.
Sometimes I even dont believe in myself, I was really red one that evening and I had to ditch the midday sun the next day, because I had a burning sensation, but now its gone. Amazing!

That hybrid tanning thing is really amazing, its so amazing its hard to believe! The was the biggest impact I got for the last month. Thank you Jack!

### Butters — 2018-06-22T22:41:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-244104>

Since I started some e-guitar playing some time ago, I looked for some pedal/amplification set-up that I could use when the kid sleeps without much EMF.
I ordered multi-effect pedal with headphones connection - yes headphones - to test it. But it should be possible to use with batteries thus low voltage and power to lessen the EMF effect on the headphones.
So far my readings show no EMF at all between the headphones where my head should be. Testing is progressing. Readings only appear when the meter is moved, but this happens also when the headphones are unplugged. Should be an induction effect on the sensitive meter coils.

I measured connected to my laptop to and there is also no EMF between the headphones.
Hm that is weird because I had 4 years ago a thread, where I posted this issue and measured with an phone app a big field. I sold them thereafter.

This is the thread.
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/headphones-at-night-responsible-for-bad-sleep.12234/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/headphones-at-night-responsible-for-bad-sleep.12234/)
So my phone app could be wrong, my meter now could be wrong or the headphones could be differ.
Another member posted a picture with a high field between his headphones.
So I am a little insecure here.

### Butters — 2018-06-23T19:03:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-244171>

So measuring made me curious. I measured my wired phone receiver and right where it sits at the ear its going quite high. That surprised me, but I came to the idea through Inger who tested one time her phone. Could that explain, why my ears feel hot during phone calls and why I intuitively changed the sides after a while?

### Inger — 2018-06-23T20:11:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-244176>

Sure Alex.
I do not like to speak long on my wired phone either… and when I do, I put on the speaker and keep the phone further from my head. It helps.

It does not emit any RF radiation, but quite a bit of magnetic/electric when you are very close to it. I still prefer to use it instead of my cellphone tho . Does not feel as bad. Especially with speaker on

### Butters — 2018-06-25T09:26:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-244275>

Yeah. Thats kind of weird. Because a wired phone is supposed to be safer than a unwired, yet it has other dangers.
But the headphone stuff is really weird.
I tested a bit and plugged it them into my stereo system, which is not grounded through the plug - and the headphones are on the end of the scale for electric fields.

### Lahelada — 2018-06-25T16:06:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-244292>

### Butters — 2018-06-26T11:20:09+0300
