I probably have no problem with vitamin A intake. I calculated and even with 5-6 eggs a day and 50g butter I am slightly above the recommended intake. And I dont eat it everyday. So I dont think its a too high intake problem. And I feel really good eating eggs and bacon for breakfast.
But I think too much fish for me has problems on the iron side, because fish has not so much iron and I really dont eat much red meat. Here and there ground beef and occasionally a steak but most is fish. And fish has DHA but almost no iron. So maybe there is a problem and I need to step back a bit on fish to get iron in. When I think about it, I have really not so much iron intake. So it could be the food. Im cooking on cast iron and steel but that should not make any difference because of the patina on it.

>
> I was stupid thinking the organs are the best foods to eat. In our poisoned word, and think about organs are the accumulators of poisons.. so how stupid is it to think it is good to eat those **on a regular basis**. Now very smart, but I am getting smarter
>
> I am probably lucky to have a sensitive body where things show up on skin, nails, wellbeing/mood, eyes etc way before I get any illness, so I am very healthy, but noticed my body trying tell me I am going in the wrong direction.

I feel good and my body craves the yolks it seems and when I think about paleo man or woman, I can't imagine them not eating an egg as soon as they found it, not sharing it with family. The taking of and consumption of large animals seems a bit more difficult with their technology. Not saying they didnt eat large animals, but I think they ate a lot more rabbits, eggs, mice, grasshoppers, fish and waterfowl. I read a book about the primitive Paiutes, who didn't have contact with the outside world until the 1800s during the California gold rush. They lived without horses, agriculture, tanning, clothing, pottery, metals. They lived in smaller groups called the Rabbit eaters, the cattail eaters, the duck eaters ect. They would hunt birds and rabbits with nets woven from sage brush fibers. Lots of large animals around but hard to hunt big game with the technology they had. Different from the Hollywood image of a native American on horseback, dressed in buckskin, slaying bison with a bow and arrow.
On the other hand, I can also fixate on something I think is good and over consume it, and we have access to plentiful eggs year round, which isn't natural in this latitude. I used to eat crazy amounts of raw spinach until I knew about oxalates. I also question research that begins with a statement that "it is well known that red meat causes cancer", but without data backing the claim or differentiating different types of meat, breed, environment, production method and the resulting meat composition.
Because like Richard and unlike Inger, I have yet to develop a taste for organ meat, I feed them to my other animals to up cycle them. I had some beef hearts and tongues in the freezer that I fed to my laying hens. I give lamb hearts to my dog and let the pigs munch on the heads. The cow livers around here can have liver flukes, a parasite, from river drinking water, so I give those to the chickens or throw them in the pasture and let the other animals eat them. If an animal dies here, there is nothing left but a little fur or feathers after a day. lots of hungry critters competing for not much carb, fat or protein.
Bon apatite, amigos!

In other words: sunlight can be turned directly into matter based upon what the cell needs when it needs it. Lavoisier has established a mass conservation law which is valid in chemistry. ### Butters — 2022-07-23T13:17:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-314560>

Since beginning of july I changed my way of getting to work. I reactivated my old roadbike and ride my 12km (one way) to work. It has not only the effect of saving money (not using the car) but I have the opportunity to get more activity into my every day plus a little more direct sunlight (despite Im not riding upper body naked). The first two weeks were very hard but at the end of the 3. week now its getting easier. My endurance was very bad. I hadnt done any serious endurance work for a couple of years. Im adapting to the regular stress better and plan to use it as an HIIT opportunity. Kid no. 3 is on hits way but not yet hatched and it will change quite a bit in a few weeks.
Although I begin to like roadbiking again I know I dont have the time to invest much time in it and dont see the sense in it to ride hours and hours for what. My time is rare soon, so I have to decide to invest in the talents I have and that is clearly the music, not sports and clearly not bicycling  (know it from vo2 max tests in university). But I feel much better with that bicyling every day.

I have not checked any blood work since April, but I see that sleep is a prime factor in my life. Because I feel very different when I go to bed earlier and sleep good. And I have gotten to bed later and later over the winter month. Also I noticed it is not good to bring the kids to bed and fall asleep AND wake up 1-2hrs later and do other things, then go do bed again at like 12pm. Its definitely not good and I try now not to fall asleep with the children. Of course evening time is now the only time I have time for myself and to do anything despite work and family, so I have to watch out. But no sleep time equals no quality time for me, that is clear. Because all I do without sleep is much more uneffective and more random so I better have a good nights sleep and use my free time effective.
Randoms thoughts dropped...

### Butters — 2022-09-23T23:19:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-315995>

Biking was a good idea although it brought out, that I might have mild visual snow symptomx or something.
Because I also have to ride in the dark (morning oder evening) I noticed symptomps more than with car driving which are:

\-bluefield entoptic phenomenon for 5 years since floaters in both eyes happended at the same time, mostly in bright light
\-long after images especially from strong light sources (sun, glare, reflection, incandescent blub etc.)
\-short after images (parts of second) after looking at an object and look on an even area
\-halos around light sources (especially at night) but for 5 years too I think
\-self light phenomenon of the eyes in the dark, I notice it here and then when I go to sleep and make it pitch black (otherwise I dont see it)
\-since beginning this year little black points when I look at dark objects at day time in bright light
\-when I look at a lamp in the dark a few second and then look in the dark, the after image has some white fizzling dots for a few seconds
\-after physical activity (bike riding) when I look at a light source and then into a dark background I have a flickering image of the light source for a few second, like the retina wobbles, the flickering gets weaker over the time of 1-2 min. after end of the physical effort (so maybe a different problem) and finally disappears but this is nuts when it happends in the dark

### Butters — 2023-07-03T23:28:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-323202>

So Im now in my 4th week of eating Epi-Paleo. I realized 3 weeks ago that with my 3. child if gone far of the road and I think it was longer. So eating omega 6s like crisps, peanut butter, lots of ice cream, sweets, drinking beer and so on. Even almost ditched my holy rule of not snacking in between. Almost, because I did not that regularly maybe I got no real weight gain and remain leptin sensitive?!
But I had an sudden allergy coming up I think, something that flared just a little the last years but not much. But this year I had I think one heavy strike. Also the last ice cream I ate, I got horrible brain freeze which according to Jack is massive O6/3 disbalance. My cold resisistance also decreased, so it was time to change some things. Also I noticed I even didnt get to the evening without eating lunch, a thing that generally wasnt a problem the last years. So maybe a little leptin resistant?
So I started to get to see the sun (not the actual sunrise because now that is 5:11 am and I have a big tree line and a hill in the way, but as soon as possible I was sure to see at least 1-5min. Then eating BAB. Was strange to me, I wasnt used to it anymore. But now when I eat BAB I can get to evening without problem. I took some time. Umfortunately I got some gut issues with loose stool (I drank much coconut milk) which hasnt been solved yet, but got a little better. Maybe gut problem?! Posted already and got some reading answer from Jack, thanks. But the feeling of fullness and not having to eat lunch is a big relief, because I dont want to eat at my works place anymore (O6, carbs, conventional meat etc.). I didnt do that the past 3 weeks. I used lunch time to get almost 1hr of midday sun (I have 1hr lunch break) or do some weight training. But whenever sun is out, I prioritize it.
I feel it is a long way to go and things are happening slowly, but slowly they got me in that position right?!

The big thing for me nowadays is the sleep. With 3 kids many things have to be done and there is only so much time. Cheers to drezy for one of his posts on playing the guitar  I now know what you mean. But the sleep is the big thing.

Today I had a big migraine onset. Never had that before. Usually when I had it, it started with aura which faded after 1 hrs and sometimes headaches and speech problems. Now I had 4-5 aura onsets like over 4 hrs, when one was gone the next started. No big headaches and not so much speech difficulties but that aura incident was huge. Made me frightened. I dont know what the trigger was. But it fits in the frame I think, because of O6/3 imbalance and blue light.
One thing that left my curious is why I have had a phase, when I didnt had these migraines at all. In my puberty is started like with 14/15 and it ended with 16 when I started to do sports at a regular base. Then it was gone for almost 10 years and started again at 27. In all that time, which was study time I didnt gave a shit about light and food but have done a lot of sports (no chronic cardio). Why started it after this acute phase with blue light, not much sleep often, shitty food etc.? Can the body only stand so much and one trigger it was to flip the switch to reactivate it?

I need to sleep...

### Butters — 2023-07-11T15:25:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-323528>

First morning with a crisp wake up. I woke up and was ready to go, it was 5.45 am. Sunrise is 5.18am. Must be the BAB and bright light after waking up. Havent had this a long time. A have not prioritized sleep a long time and worked up to a deficit. Also finally more dreaming. Maybe placebo or not but I think my thinking gets better and Im generally more optimistic to different things.

Ive done some bike sprints from work to home the day before yesterday and experienced eye flickering at the end. I begin to notice that this happens everytime I either enter high heart frequency over longer timespan or accumulated over intervals. Its flickering in both eyes. When I look into light and then close the eyes of look into darker backrounds it flickers afterwards in the center of the eye. The flickering gets less and finally disappears when heart rate is coming down or other factors of high intensity getting less in the blood stream. So at the beginning after flickering lasts some seconds (maybe 3s). Its both eyes the same, so could it be the brain/nerve etc.? Is there not enough blood circulation or to high blood pressure in HIIT exercise or nitric oxide release switching something on?
Also when I jump or run I have like one spot in my field of sight in the middle that get darker with the bounce, I think both eyes, too.
Also some more visual phenomena in both eyes. Blue field entoptic phenomenon, flickering black dots when look onto dark backgrounds when surrounding light is bright (black t-shirt in bright sunshine), pulsating retina sometimes with strong heartbeat. All both eyes, as good as I can tell. So something is coupled here in both eyes. Otherwise it would be a big, big coincidence. Melanin in visual cortex or POMC defect there?
I just re and relistening the podcast about POMC, which is mind blowing, but so far I cant decipher what my problem could be.

I have good melanin on my skin and hold my tan way into the winter, so not much is sucked inside I would assume. There I am lost.

### Butters — 2023-07-14T16:40:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-323736>

So Im waking up even earlier now. Last 2 days was around 5.20am, which puts me right at sunrise. Problem: sunset is 21.27 pm. So ruffly enough time to sleep for the ideal 7.30 hrs. Yesterday it was only like 7hrs. Because on sunset its not dark enough. But good thing is, my body is still managing the clocks.
I noticed suddenly I get often cramps when waking up. Havent had cramps for years. Is my body depleting mg for some reason? Sucking it from the muscles to somewhere else?
My digestion has become much better, but some smoked fish I dont tolerate (mackerel). Stomach ache and diarrhea are the result. Same with mushrooms .
I noticed that laptop time prior to bed has significant influence on my sleep, despite using IRIS AND blueblockers and dimming with iris. Must be just the illuminance or maybe flicker from screen. I sleep much better without it. I just play guitar in dawn time, as long I can see something.

### Butters — 2024-06-15T12:39:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-335806>

On the hunt for better O3/O6, low deuterium and gut health, I came to the conclusion to eliminate certain foods and reintegrate to test myself, how I behave. So my littel 1=1 of gut health. Im not under the african sun 12/7 so, I have to tweak the food also.
I eliminated grains and noticed less appetite, need to eat lower food portions and less gas, better stool. I would have eliminated it anyway, but I now want to test to gain experience. This is also true for german beer . So no beer for me. We are on alcohol, so I have to test wine next. But I tolerated wine good in the past with plenty of water.
On my path I tested food like onions, garlic and recognized, they are not good for me either. Much gas etc.
Also my gut is happier, when I only eat in the morning and in the evening, respecting sunlight of course. Let my body have some digestion rest.

Now I happened to stumble on the so called FODMAPs - "Fermentable Oligosaccharides, Disaccharides, Monosaccharides and Polyols" and realized all the foods are in there. And most of these foods we are not supposed to eat either for most of the time.

On the test list is milk products and coffee.

On this shitty post I realized that the "perfect" stool should be a normal thing. BTW we have 2 cats and they are the example, they have always perfect stool, no need for toilet paper  except the food was rotten or something. And so it should be for us. Brain is better with better stool. Money is saved with saving toilet paper and time is saved. Everything is better with perfect stool. And because its "perfect" stool for me, I realized its not normal for me otherwise it wouldnt be special. That has to change. Oh and sleep is also better. So its brain/gut axis all the way.

Other topic - Im on the hunt for all day blueblocking options for prescription glasses. Unfortunately its not so simple in germany to get glasses simply tinted like in the US. You can only get prescription glasses with partial blueblock and they are not even tinted, so no real thing.
I want to get some yellow like glasses for everyday use, especially for work environment.
Only option I found is from prisma, who sell glasses with magnetic front clip options and various filters. Prescription glasses are not tinted but you can fit them in the frame and front clip the filters you like. But its only 2 frames to choose from. So not many style options here.

Maybe someone know other options in german to get tinted glasses so you can customized frames?

### Butters — 2024-06-16T12:18:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-335821>

The acoustic guitar is still my go to solution to relax and let the mind rest. Grip the guitar, everything is literally gone.
I found a young band on youtube, which does incredible acoustic covers of rock songs, real inspiration. To me it even sounds better acoustic this way than electric + the outside vibe.
Unfortunately if you have a good acoustic guitar, you wont play it in the sun, because it will damage in direct sunlight. For that you have to downgrade a bit.
But the magic in acoustic guitar playing lies in the rhythm factor to me. Its like you play the drum the same time and then you get a feel of flow, almost decoupling the mind to another highter state. At least that it is for me, when I am playing. Its like flicking a switch.

### Butters — 2024-09-04T12:43:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-339237>

Experiment is running good so for. I now have some good homeostasis in my gut. My standard now is bristol 3-4. I know when I eat certain things (too much sugar or coffee). When I reduce it my stool is fine the next day. I now understand better that current situation means everything and a healthy gut can handle more than a unhealthy. I now can eat lots of coconut oil without getting the runs and without stomach ache. I eat more fish, try to eat at least 3 times a week a big portion like 300g each. Right now salmon is the best option so far. But it seems to have cured my "brain freeze" that I again got a few weeks ago when eating ice cream. According to Jack it was too much omega 6s.
What had I done? I stopped peanut butter completely AND ate more fish, sometimes shrimps.

Negative: I seem to have injured my knee a few weeks ago. Symptoms vary but I frighten a meniscus tear. Exam is coming soon. Medical consultation in rural regions of germany is difficult these times.

I bought a fire pit for making a fire here and then. Its also great for gathering with other people and will be nice in winter time for that.
And I bought more BTC. Try to take away a little every month.

### Butters — 2024-09-20T13:49:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-339755>

I noticed some things the past months. Despite I dont do any weight lifting, because of the knee issue, my body condition didnt changed that much or I am slightly more ripped. My hunger feelings are more controlable. Despite of me, most of the time eating only in the morning and evening I can go with little portion size and be satisfied. The was not always possible in the past.
May it be, that weight lifting (not crazy) was a more harmful stressor than I thought? My gut also is pretty good. I begin to get cravings after fish, which means its always the first thing that comes to my mind when I think about a meal.
My gut also tolerates products with higher fiber like onion, garlic and pumpkin better. It also tolerates some amount of coffee without giving me runs. Now its getting into autumn and I will change to more butter and fish, not so much coconut oil. Seasonally good, but good for my gut? We will see.
My knee is not rehabed, so I need to fix this first. Maybe I let the weight training go for winter and only try to do CT and find out how this changes me.

### Butters — 2025-05-24T11:21:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-353253>

For more than 1 months now completely sugar free. I need to eat more fat to compensate. But it works very good. For some time integrating again coconut manna and oil for fat energy and gut health. I think when I look at my past, I wrecked my gut pretty heavy. I now try to get everything to heal my gut, including sun of course, but also dont eat carbs other than vegetables in seasons (mostly). Situation in germany is becoming unfavourable. When once had a good health system. With massmigration and corrupt politics not anymore. So I have to care for myself more.
I still have the nail fungus problem and I think bad blood circulation in the feet is the problem here too. I tend to get some renaulds symptoms for some year now. Not heavy but cold feet often are violett (low oxygen). So that feeds the fungus, plus this is the reason I now have ditched the carbs completely because I think my gut is not alright and so my immunsystem. Otherwise I wouldnt have that problem.

I once bought a big package of grassfed bones to make my own bone broth and heal my gut. All went good, cooking not a problem. But somehow I dont tolerate the broth. I get bloating and gas and my stool is changing. So that is not as intended. I wanted it for my gut walls and stuff. When I drink a glass (250ml) I tend to feel very full but not satisfied. I doesnt fit well into coconut consumption as well. I can now eat more coconut oil without getting runs and stuff but broth AND coconut oil give me stomach ache. I tried to ditch onions but that wasnt it. Must be the mass of Kollagen? I dont know. Maybe I should try some other broth not self cooked but organic grass fed and see if its my broth that is giving me problems.
1-2 days after ending consuming the broth my stool got perfect. Like last summer. Not even coffee seems to intervene. I lowered my coffee consumption - also for the gut - leaving at least 2 hrs after waking up to not interfere with my morning cortisol, not drinking after 2 pm also.

Its now the time I begin to get some financial options so I invest in bitcoin etc. but money is still rare, so no major benefits. Back in the time 2020 when Jack said stack BTC I even hadn money for such things. Now I can do it - a bit. My long term aim is to get to the 6 figure Capital in 5 Years. In germany that is pretty tough because of the f\*\*\*\*\*g high taxes. Becoming socialist shithole here. My aim is to get out, but without capital no bueno - without health either.

God thank my kids are pretty healthy. Invested in my kids the past 9 years, thats the reason I had no major money supply. Gotta change that now. It all bounds together, Jacks posts make sense more and more now. I try not to look back, because past formed me as I am now.

### Butters — 2025-07-03T15:37:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-355661>

I noticed weight loss since last summer, from 83 to now 76-77kg. What happened? After my knee injury I completely stopped any weight training, also for upper body. Im still active at work, almost always standing, walking etc. light cardio due to my job is rehabilitation.
Since then I eat only two times the day. I try to eat much in the morning but I dont get to evening without hunger feeling beginning afternoon. I can push it to dinner, but then I cant and wont eat as much because I dont want my gut to work so much late in the day.
So no muscular stimulus + I think not enough calories got my weight down. I think I have to up my meal frequency to 3, so I can eat less in the evening.

### Butters — 2026-02-10T22:59:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/butters-journal.8818/post-366651>

One month ago I had enough of normal and mediocre. I see so many people in my daily practise with sickness I dont wanna have and same time I can not do much about in my Position, yet. I learned not yet or in German "noch nicht" is powerful phrase.
Whatever I constantly got brain freezes when eating cold Things Like Frozen berries which I Like very much and remembered Jack telling Something about Omega 6s too much, dadada. Also my gut Problem had Not yet resolved since summer. The lyme Thing and so, which made me really think.
I thought I was doing well, I wasnt obviously. I dont want to waste time anymore. There is nothing to waste here in Germany nowadays. Centralized med is fucked, I see it everyday. I dont want to Go there.
Back to story I try to optimize what is possible with 3 Kids.
First was canged to counter my Omega 3/6 Balance I had because of too much peanut Butter stuff and maybe shitty light at Work. So I aimed at fish every day to get 5-6 days mostly sockeye Salmon. I body voltage meter does not measure the actual electric field - it only measures the induced voltage potential to a reference point result of the capacitive coupling effect whilst being in the field. Most accurate way to do it is to use and meter in addition to BV measurements.

BUT given simple steps that they understand, they engage and take action.

I cannot tell you how how many calls I have had from people who had a Building Biologists (or other EMF Consultant) out to measure, received a VERY comprehensive 20 (or 50) page report . . . that they cold not understand so had no idea what to do to fix their nnEMF problem . . . So they DID nothing.

I believe it our responsibility to give people the information they need, in a way that THEY understand it, to the degree they are empowered to take positive mitigation action. # Thread: The easy guide to optimal road quantum mechanics
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-easy-guide-to-optimal-road-quantum-mechanics.8821/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2013-11-21T01:03:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-easy-guide-to-optimal-road-quantum-mechanics.8821/post-101168>

A Lazy Layman's Guide to Quantum Physics
Â·
Â· James Higgo 1999
Â·
Â·
Â·
Â·
Â· What is Quantum Physics?
Â· That's an easy one: it's the science of things so small that the quantum nature of reality has an effect. Quantum means 'discrete amount' or 'portion'. Max Planck discovered in 1900 that you couldn't get smaller than a certain minimum amount of anything. This minimum amount is now called the Planck unit.
Â· Why is it weird?
Â· Niels Bohr, the father of the orthodox 'Copenhagen Interpretation' of quantum physics once said, "Anyone who is not shocked by quantum theory has not understood it".
Â· To understand the weirdness completely, you just need to know about three experiments: Light Bulb, Two Slits, Schroedinger's Cat.
Â· Two Slits
Â· The simplest experiment to demonstrate quantum weirdness involves shining a light through two parallel slits and looking at the screen. It can be shown that a single photon (particle of light) can interfere with itself, as if it travelled through both slits at once.
Â· Light Bulb
Â· Imagine a light bulb filament gives out a photon, seemingly in a random direction. Erwin Schroedinger came up with a nine-letter-long equation that correctly predicts the chances of finding that photon at any given point. He envisaged a kind of wave, like a ripple from a pebble dropped into a pond, spreading out from the filament. Once you look at the photon, this 'wavefunction' collapses into the single point at which the photon really is.
Â· Schroedinger's Cat
Â· In this experiment, we take your pet cat and put it in a box with a bottle of cyanide. We rig it up so that a detector looks at an isolated electron and determines whether it is 'spin up' or 'spin down' (it can have either characteristic, seemingly at random). If it is 'spin up', then the bottle is opened and the cat gets it. Ten minutes later we open the box and see if the cat is alive or dead. The question is: what state is the cat in between the detector being activated and you opening the box. Nobody has actually done this experiment (to my knowledge) but it does show up a paradox that arises in certain interpretations.
Â·
Â·
Â· If you dare to think about it (you're not really supposed to), you have to believe one of the following things:
Â·
Â· MENU
Â· Your consciousness affects the behaviour of subatomic particles
Â· - or -
Â· Particles move backwards as well as forwards in time and appear in all possible places at once
Â· - or -
Â· The universe is splitting, every Planck-time (10 E-43 seconds) into billions of parallel universes
Â· - or -
Â· The universe is interconnected with faster-than-light transfers of information
Â· ----
Â· Full English Breakfast
Â· Coffee or Tea
Â· These are the results of the different interpretations of quantum physics. The interpretations all compete with each other. Otherwise respectable physicists can get quite heated about how sensible their pet interpretation is and how crazy all the others are. At the moment, there's about one new interpretation every three months, but most of them fit into these categories.
Â·
Â· What does it mean?
Â· The meaning of quantum physics is a bit of a taboo subject, but everyone thinks about it. To make it all a bit more respectable, it is better to say 'ontology' than 'meaning' -- it's the same thing. There are several competing interpretations and the one thing they all have in common is that each of them explains all the facts and predicts every experiment's outcome correctly.
Â· Copenhagen Interpretation (CI)
Â· This is the granddaddy of interpretations, championed by the formidable Niels Bohr of Copenhagen university. He browbeat all dissenters into submission (with the notable exception of Einstein) at a Brussels conference sponsored by a man called Solvay in 1927. Bohr thereby stifled the debate for a generation or two.
Â· The CI has a bit of a cheek calling itself an interpretation, because it essentially says "thou shalt not ask what happens before ye look". He pointed out that the Schroedinger equation worked as a tool for calculating where the particle would be, except that it 'collapsed' as soon as you took a peek. If anyone asked why this was, he would say, "shut up and calculate" (or he might as well have done).
Â· When you do try to take Copenhagen seriously you come to the conclusion that consciousness and particle physics are inter-related, and you rush off to write a book called The Dancing Wu-Li Masters.
Â· More recently, Henry Stapp at the University of California has written papers such as On Quantum Theories of the Mind (1997). Stapp's central thesis is that the synapses in your brain are so small that quantum effects are significant. This means that there is quantum uncertainty about whether a neuron will fire or not - and this degree of freedom that nature has allows for the interaction of mind and matter.
Â· What happens to the cat? You're not allowed to ask.
Â· Many Worlds Interpretation (MWI)
Â· The various paradoxes that the Copenhagen Interpretation gave rise to (famously Schroedinger's cat, and Einstein's dislike of "spooky action at a distance") led others to keep on trying to find a better interpretation.
Â· The simplest was put forward by a student, Hugh Everett, in 1957. He simply said that the Schroedinger equation does not collapse. Of course, everyone laughed at him, because they could see that the photon, for example, was in just one place when they looked, not in all possible places. But after a couple of decades, this issue was resolved with the concept of decoherence - the idea that different universes can very quickly branch apart, so that there is very little relationship between them after a tiny fraction of a second.
Â· This has led to what should strictly be called the 'post-Everett' Interpretation, but is still usually called MWI. It is now one of the most popular interpretations and has won some impromptu beauty contests at physics conferences. Unfortunately it means that billions of you are splitting off every fraction of a second into discrete universes and it implies that everything possible exists in one universe or another. This comes up with its own set of hard-to-digest concepts, such as the fact that a 500-year-old you exists in some universes, whereas in others you died at birth.
Â· In 1997, Max Tegmark at Princeton University proposed an experiment to prove that MWI was correct. It involved pointing a loaded gun at your head and pulling the trigger. Of course, you will only survive in those universes where the gun, for whatever reason, fails to go off. If you get a misfire every time, you can satisfy yourself -- with an arbitrarily high level of confidence -- that MWI is true. Of course, in most universes your family will be weeping at your funeral (or possibly just shaking their heads and muttering).
Â· What happens to the cat? It's dead in half the subsequent universes and alive in the other half.
Â· Pilot Waves, Hidden Variables and the Implicate Order
Â· David Bohm (1917-1992) was a very brilliant physicist and that's why people went along with him when he came up with an elegant but more complicated theory to explain the same set of phenomena (normally, more complicated theories are disqualified by the principle known as Ockham's Razor).
Â· Bohm's theory follows on some original insights by Prince Louis de Broglie (1892-1987), who first studied the wave-like properties of the behaviour of particles in 1924. De Broglie suggested that, in addition to the normal wavefunction of the Copenhagen Interpretation, there is a second wave that determines a precise position for the particle at any particular time. In this theory, there is some 'hidden variable' that determines the precise position of the photon.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-11-21T01:03:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-easy-guide-to-optimal-road-quantum-mechanics.8821/post-101169>

Â· Sadly, John von Neumann (1903-1957) wrote a paper in 1932 proving that this theory was impossible. Von Neumann was such a great mathematician that nobody bothered to check his maths until 1966, when John Bell (1928-1990) proved he'd bodged it and there could be hidden variables after all -- but only if particles could communicate faster than light (this is called 'nonlocality'). In 1982 Alain Aspect demonstrated that this superluminal signaling did appear to exist, although David Mermin then showed that you could not actually signal anything. There is still some argument about whether this means very much.
Â· Bohm's theory was that the second wave was indeed faster than light, and moreover it did not get weaker with distance but instantly permeated the entire universe, acting as a guide for the movement of the photon. This is why it is called a 'pilot wave'.
Â· This theory explains the paradoxes of quantum physics perfectly. But it introduces a new faster-than-light wave and some hidden mechanism for deciding where it goes -- to create an 'implicate order'. That's quite a lot of extra baggage, and scientists like to travel light. Worse still, Bohm went on to become a mystic, identifying his 'implicate order' with Eastern spirituality and spawning books like Fritjof Capra's The Tao of Physics . That's heretical behaviour in the eyes of any decent physicist.
Â· What happens to the cat? It's either dead or alive, of course!
Â· Consistent Histories
Â· The Consistent Histories interpretation, put forward by Robert Griffiths in 1984, works backwards from the result of an experiment, arguing that only a few possible histories are consistent with the rules of quantum mechanics. It's an interesting idea but not very popular because it still doesn't explain how a particle can go through two slits and interfere with itself. Roland OmnÂs, in The Interpretation of Quantum Mechanics (1994) wrote down 80 equations in a single chapter and came to the conclusion that the 'consistent histories' interpretation was pretty much the same as Copenhagen, with a few knobs on.
Â· What happens to the Cat? Again, you're not supposed to ask.
Â· Alternate Histories
Â· The Alternate Histories Interpretation is quite different, being similar to the Many-Worlds Interpretation, but with the insistence that only the actual outcome is the real world and the ones we're not in don't actually exist. Unfortunately this gets us right back to their being some kind of 'collapse'.
Â· What happens to the cat? Again, you're not supposed to ask.
Â· Time Reversibility
Â· Richard Feynman (1918-1988) was a genius who developed a new approach to quantum mechanics. He formalised its crowning achievement, Quantum Electrodynamics, which is the most accurate scientific theory ever devised. He also developed the Feynman Diagram, which represents the interaction of two particles as the exchange of a third particle. This diagram has time on one axis and space on the other and the interaction can be viewed as happening both in forward and in reverse time.
Â· An electron, on its way from point A to point B, can bump into a photon. In the diagram this can be drawn as sending it backwards not just in space, but also in time. Then it bumps into another photon, which sends it forward in time again, but in a different direction in space. In this way, it can be in two places at once.
Â· There is little doubt that a Feynman diagram offers the easiest way to predict the results of a subatomic experiment. Many physicists have seen the power of this tool and taken the next step, arguing that reverse time travel is what actually happens in reality. Victor Stenger of the University of Hawaii argues strongly for this ontology in his forthcoming book. Of course, for a layman, it is hard to understand why a photon bounces around in such a way that it appears in two slits at once.
Â· What happens to the Cat? It is both dead and alive simultaneously. We don't see this because of the macroscopic 'measurement problem'.
Â· Transactional Interpretation
Â· Like Stenger's, John Cramer's Transactional Interpretation relies on the fundamental time-symmetry of the universe. He argues that particles perform a kind of 'handshake' in the course of interacting. One sends out a wave forward in time, and another sends one out backwards in time.
Â· What happens to the Cat? Ermm...
Â· Gremlins
Â· A new interpretation, presented for the first time here, is that there are little green gremlins hovering around, going backwards and forwards in time, shaking hands and collapsing with mirth as they poke and prod subatomic particles in a way they calculate most likely to confuse us. This explains all of the observed experimental results, but it does introduce gremlins, and the need for a further theory about why they should want to confuse us. Using the principle of Ockham's razor, this interpretation will probably not find much popularity among the scientific community although it may be the basis for a new religion. Watch this space.
Â· What happens to the Cat? Depends on what the gremlins think will confuse us most.

### Josh — 2013-12-24T19:42:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-easy-guide-to-optimal-road-quantum-mechanics.8821/post-104210>

Somewhat shaky ground at the base...reminds me of the story where pundits were reputedly asked "what holds the Earth up?" They were answered something like "Giant White Elephants". When someone was intelligent enough to dare ask "and what do the elephants stand on?" The story says they were told "well, more Giant White Elephants..." I would be careful about writing Bohm off as a "mystic" until you make the attempt to digest J. Krishnamurti, also known as JK (ironic perhaps?) yourself. Forgive me the suggestion if you already have. He spent 70 years trying to get people to just take a good clear look at things. I am humbly amused that all exceptional physicists seem to reach the edge of the outwardly messy world of "mystics", yet few enter to take a good look around. Until everyone makes friends, the comedy will no doubt continue with physicists saying "he got lost in religion and mysticism" and so-called mystics bemoaning that "tragically so and so could not transcend the limits of the "mind"". Having had a peek from both sides, it is a great game that has been going on for a very long time...I do not use this as an excuse to not grapple with the physics of biology, I just need the perspective so that the "terror of the situation" does not depress me so much. A "mystic" once said that the easiest thing to do in the physical universe was to remove the neutralizing forces in things. He warned that it was an entirely different thing to put them back. Most of physics still looks like removing the neutralizing force and seeing what happens. In our attempts to find a mechanism-based rationale for "functional medicine" in quantum physics we are still largely bound to experiments that take away equilibrium in systems to see what happens. The results are then extrapolated back to what might be "whole" or "healthy" or functional". The question of the role of the "observer" is central to where most "mystical" systems start. Yi Lu Ping An....

### QiGuy1997 — 2013-11-21T02:40:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-cholesterol-for-my-mom.8822/post-101187>

My mom has recently got some blood tests done just to check-up and the tests turned up high cholesterol at 265 mg/dl . Her triglycerides showed 121 mg/dl, her HDL was 75, her non-HDL was 190 (including 166 from LDL and 24 from VLDL). I was thinking these high levels may be caused by low thyroid however her TSH was normal at 2.00 so that is probably not it. Any ideas on what could be causing this/ tips on what to do?

### nonchalant — 2013-11-21T03:02:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-cholesterol-for-my-mom.8822/post-101193>

Nice HDL. I would be a bit concerned about the triglycerides though. Perhaps reduce carbs a bit?

### QiGuy1997 — 2013-11-21T06:40:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-cholesterol-for-my-mom.8822/post-101220>

I've already thrown away all vegetables oils in the house and she's cut down her carbs. I'm working on complete elimination of wheat and what not. Hopefully when she gets retested the trigs/LDL will be down a bit.

### nonchalant — 2013-11-21T16:42:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/question-about-cholesterol-for-my-mom.8822/post-101254>

> [QiGuy1997 said:](/goto/post?id=101220)
>
> I've already thrown away all vegetables oils in the house and she's cut down her carbs. I'm working on complete elimination of wheat and what not. Hopefully when she gets retested the trigs/LDL will be down a bit.

I would be very surprised if her trigs are not down for the next test then. You should be prepared, however, for an LDL level that doesn't change much, or even goes up, QiGuy. LDL, if it is not oxidized/damaged, is actually good. The damage to LDL is caused by carbs and artificial fats like that vegetable oil. Do some research on the various types of LDL. Only small, dense LDL is bad.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-11-21T20:40:10+0200

### caroline — 2013-11-21T09:42:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-heart-warming-story-of-a-neurosurgeon.8825/post-101230>

Last night on TV there was a beautiful story about a young girl....

Up untill the age of two she had been perfectly fine and then, all of a sudden, she developed acute epilepsy.

She was seizing up to 200 times a day. It was the most heartbreaking story of parents searching for answers for their beautiful little daughter.

They finally found a surgeon in the U.S. .... a Dr. Mathern at California Best hospital. He agreed to see her as soon as they could get there \[from Perth\]. He had answered their email the very next day.

When he examined her he said there was a window of opportunity and it is now. He removed part of her brain. The concern was she would lose the use of one side - if she survived surgery.

It all turned out wonderfully well....

Everyone during this story was so choked up with emotion.

The Doctor said something I have heard Dr. Kruse say .... "when someone is down - I have to extend my hand"

I have tears running down my face as I am typing this .......how lucky we are to have wonderful, talented, and caring Doctors.....

I am not sure that we say "thankyou" enough. we are always quick to criticize and we do need to question everything ...... but we do need to appreciate the legions of talented and caring professionals who look after us when we are down.

### caroline — 2013-11-21T09:50:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-heart-warming-story-of-a-neurosurgeon.8825/post-101231>

I forgot to add ..... it really freaked me out to see a neurosurgeon operating on a child's brain. All I could think of is this is what Dr. K. does on a weekly basis..... I am in awe....

Dr. K. said not long ago that he leaves part of himself on the table every single time ...... thank goodness for your meditation room and your cold tub and your family and the people who care about you .... that would be us ...

sorry about the soap box - I never see it coming..... strange...

# Thread: Elvis Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elvis-journal.8826/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 62
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### elvey90 — 2013-11-21T10:52:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elvis-journal.8826/post-101233>

Hey All,

Decided to make myself accountable for my own progress hence starting a journal, hopefully this will help me and other people as well in similar positions.
Not sure how this works but I guess I'll start by posting my situation just to provide some context and I'll go from there.

Before getting into Paleo or Epi Paleo, I was eating unwholesome foods, cheap restaurants, soy sauce and lots of carbs (not the good ones), boozing, late nights etc and my energy, mood, inner psychology was going crazy, literally. Also, I was skinny bulk (does that even make sense lol?) for my size, aka, no muscular tone but still noticeable bulk. I was also very active playing soccer, tennis and weight training rotating them every day. However, by not eating right and not recovering body and always pushing my body to do more, I always felt tired and fatigued to the point that it was driving me mad inside because I wasn't able to sustain myself without knowing why. So my diet was a mess, and like I said it didn't help me mentally, moreover external situations made it worse and had bouts of depression. One day I got injured, snapped my foot in half (literally) and I have been out and still to this day (in terms of physical activity). I was still not eating properly until April (2013) when enough was enough and decided consciously to make a decision to better myself physically.

Hence I stumbled upon the Paleo diet, a friend of mine told me a few years ago about it and I couldn't believe that I still remembered it. So ever since I've been eating a Paleo and my relationship with food started to change. In turn I've started to appreciate myself as well, instead of trying to push/exert myself all the time, I started to appreciate my body and started to listen to it more and more. I gradually started to recover, my foot was healing and with a sense of clarity I started to investigate more and more into my relationship with food and in turn realizes the effects that it has on me mentally. Good food = smarter brain! (Yes, that was a huge realization for me....)

Long story short, I continued Paleo on an 80/20 principle cheating on certain days but until recently I made another conscious decision to stop that entirely. To be good hinders the progress of greatness, at roughly around the same time I found out about leangains IF and started to incorporate it into my lifestyle. I tried IF for a few weeks but it didn't feel right, was moody at times and energy was all over the place. Coming back to the present moment, I stumbled across Epi-Paleo and Jack Kruse website and holy heck, I fell in love (yea sounds a bit corny haha). Going through the blogs (not even close to reading and digesting all of it) has definitely been a humbling experience.

So here I am, starting with the Leptin RX, though I may not need it (according to my Labs - see Lab section), I started integrating the principles anyway. Switching from skipping breakfast when on IF and have instead a BAB and that lasts me till 4PM on average then I go home from my job and eat dinner before 6PM at latest when the sunsets (Aussie time). That feels sooo much better, much more fulfilled and my energy levels have improved quite a bit (though it's only been 4 days now...yes getting a bit eager hehe). Started to also be more EMF aware and started to do little things like turning off my phone, laptop when I go to sleep, but minimizing EMF's is difficult since I live in a shared room at the moment. I'm also planning on incorporating CT in my own way, cold showers and swimming in cold pools (looking for a pool now) and gradually move onto bath tubs when I actually find some time.

So far I've been researching and doing everything myself, having a lone wolf mentality. However I've come to realized that sometimes it's better to be vulnerable and accept the fact that I can't do everything myself. So I'm writing this journal with this purpose in mind, not only to improve but also not be afraid to share a bit of myself.
Wow that felt like a touching moment

In terms of physical goals, is to get that 6pac that I've always wanted and pretty much just have a better overall body composition. May sound vain but I do believe that if you really appreciate yourself, you will take care of your body which definitely in turn correlates with mental health.

I'm also hoping to maintain a state of clear, present mindfulness and mental performance through eating authentic and wholesome foods. Everyday is a new day, so why not make it awesome

Cheers All!

I've switched blue light with yellow globes, installed flux on my laptop, and I'm in the process of looking through get glass blue blockers, Greenwave EMF filter (anyone have experience with them?), and that watch that Ben Greenfield recommends (looks pretty good too imo haha).

In terms of leaving the wireless on, that can't be helped as my roommates have no notion of sleep....but gonna do what I can do.

Oh and one more thing, going to get those eye masks and earplugs for sleep

### elvey90 — 2013-11-22T12:37:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elvis-journal.8826/post-101332>

Today is a good day until lunch time but we'll get to that later.

So have been eating BAB's every day now, slowly finishing all the poultry I have in the fridge and leaving my organic drumsticks last. It consists of 3x eggs, 2-3 sausages, 2-3 chicken thighs (which I'm removing from diet), 1x avocado, 1 glass of coconut milk with organic cacao powder. Throughout the days I drink Nobles water or if I'm at work I refill with RO water.

I've been feeling good and I'm much more aware of the effects of EMF has on my body and the foods I eat.
Now at lunch today, my colleagues decided to go out for lunch, so out of obligation (worst choice), we went along to a Korean restaurant (Kimchi Hut). Everyone ordered their usual crap (harsh but lets face it, we all know it's crappy food haha, in this case it was rice, vegetable oil cooked meat, probably not pasture fed eggs etc) but I couldn't help but myself and ordered seafood. So here I thought yay something that I can eat and then the dish came out....IT WAS FREAKIN FRIED in what not (yea I don't even know what else was in it)...and you know what, I ate it anyway.

Yes I am kicking myself but at the same time I've accepted what I ate and face the consequences that follows. Was kinda on a high and felt floaty the whole day (but it had no sugar and I was on a high, maybe it was self induced), but thankfully nothing noticeable detrimental but did feel sluggish in a sense like my brain was slow (is this placebo?? or am I taking this out of proportion). Though I'm probably not leptin resistant (according to labs), I still wanted to follow this protocol cause I do have a sense of a somewhat carb craving (or maybe it's my own mentally arrghghghg). So I've added on another 10 days onto the calendar with this protocol to review, and made sure I understood the lessons which also relates to how I approach to life as well:

\- Never ever do things out of obligation, in every situation you have a choice.
\- Learn to accept what you did, there is no change without adversity.
\- To gain optimal health, turn inward and find out the reason why your doing in the first place and let it drive you.

\- And most importantly, ask the waiter/waitress how your food is going to be cooked or served raw (which is what I thought)...

With the third point, as I'm reflecting now, I'm re-questioning and re-affirming my reason of doing this. As JK says, there's no room for good, there's only great (is that what he said? Doesn't matter sounded epic in my head).

Yes this small little plate of fried seaweed plus other crap has got me more focused and discipline into achieving my goal and also my values for health. Ok Rant over

Yet despite this, I chose to have a good day and started researching on where to get seafood, the bracelet encoder that I'm going to purchase (seriously I think it looks good, think I'll even just get it because of that haha) and the Greenwave EMF filter. I've purchased Ghee and Coconut Oil online, will be making ghee myself after this batch thanks to a couple of individuals who showed me how and the best way to do it (thanks guys!). Before I purchase the EMF filter I've sent a couple of questions to them, one of them being whether they ship to Australia with it's relevant plugins, so waiting on that before I purchase it.

Tomorrow is a new day and will be heading into the markets looking for seafood and also the protocol resets

Excited

PS: Dinner, I had 1x GF Porterhouse Steak, with 1x serving of broccoli and coconut butter, made sure I had protein and fats with this meal, and I bought Blue Blocking glasses of Ebay

### sooperb — 2013-11-22T13:46:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elvis-journal.8826/post-101338>

Don't be too hard on your meal choice Elvey. One's life is always bound up with others, some you love, some you don't but all will exert an influence of some sort. Saying no and sticking out for what you believe in is never easy and you will upset even those you love along the way. I fail too often because by nature i want to help and oblige others, putting my wants first is quite difficult, I dislike confrontation. I am managing to make changes and stick to them, blue blocking glasses for a start. So my OH thinks it's bunkum, so what, I'm doing this for me and he can come along for the ride or he can stay where he is, it's about choices isn't it. If my choices aren't his choices I don't care, just as long as he doesn't make my sticking to my choices too difficult. On my side I have all the occasions when I've been right, which helps  Everyone falls off the wagon sometimes, you just need to dust yourself off and climb back on and try harder next time.

### caroline — 2013-11-22T23:20:03+0200

### caroline — 2013-11-22T23:29:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elvis-journal.8826/post-101403>

BTW ..... people do notice what you are doing - even f they don't say much. Your stickwithitnedness \[is that a word?\] is what they are observing and taking note of. My friends now know the way I am eating and living and are seeing all the positive benefits ..... how can you argue with that!

By all means, next time, go out with your friends and work colleagues - just have a bottle of water if that is all that is available. They really don't care so much as long as they can eat the crap!

I also just stick some dark chocolate or macadamias in my bag if I am rally stuck.

### sooperb — 2013-11-23T01:47:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elvis-journal.8826/post-101417>

I have found that trying to make healthy, informed choices while out dining with friends etc. makes them uncomfortable because they seem to take it as an unspoken criticism of their choices. Dining with anyone who doesn't care what they eat is difficult, more so with some than others. My hubby has just agreed to a venue for a meal out with friends in an Indian restaurant he knows I don't like because I am convinced they use MSG which invariably gives me palpitations and night sweats and they are very heavy on salt, plus I'm convinced that some of their chicken is pre-formed. I could refuse to go but I won't because he will be embarrassed and angry if I do. If I refuse food while I'm there he will be even more annoyed.

Then there's the temptation to eat things that you know you shouldn't like parathas or naan bread. I can fully understand why Elvey gave way, sometimes it's easier even though you know it's bad and you'll pay for it afterwards.

### caroline — 2013-11-23T02:33:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elvis-journal.8826/post-101420>

Sooperb ... just call ahead and ask about their menu and tell them you cant have MSG \[allergic\] and or gluten intolerant.

I wrote a post about this long ago. We had a restaurant for 35 years. Believe me - owners are very happy to comply. The last thing you want is having to call an ambulance ..... besides - you want happy customers!

Pick a time to call when the restaurant will be less busy and ask for the owner/ manager and just ask for help in choosing something from their menu.

Sooperb - you are right - some take it as a criticism ... but I really find that people are impressed that you have very specific things that you are doing for your health and well being and won't be swayed from your commitment. How could we possibly eat that way - now that we know so much better.

I never make a big deal of anything .... and when you say you are allergic - people do understand that.

The one thing I have found over and over......even tho my shape and size has changed considerably .... the thing people always say is that I look so healthy and fit .... my weight is never mentioned.

Make no mistake - they do pay attention....

I must have borrowed your soapbox ....LOL

### prAna303 — 2013-11-23T10:31:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elvis-journal.8826/post-101462>

> I've shut down my TV (hardly use it anyway), turn off my laptops, make sure all switches are turned off, I've unplugged my alarm clock as well so that I rely on my natural circadian clock.

One big thing many forgets is that just because one shuts off the technology with a switch do not mean it does not "live".

A simple way of describing it is like a speaker, when you listen to music thru a set of speakers you do not want to have any other sets in the same room. Even if they just stand in a corner they will start to resonate with the soundsource. Natural frequencies are analog but today we also use man made ones that acts like particles. This makes stand alone gadgets work in weird ways... It is a form of ghosting.

So i mean that you must always remove the complete source of technology, not just kill the power to it.

### prAna303 — 2013-11-23T20:22:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elvis-journal.8826/post-101481>

For example phone and loudspeakers and this can apply to many electronic devices...

> The type of interference can occur if the following things happen
> together:
> 1) a pulsing radio transmitter,
> 2) with relatively strong power,
> 3) in very close proximity,
> 4) to a non-linear circuit element.
>
> The non-linear circuit element is usually some sort of solid state
> device such as a transistor or diode. If the non-linear element is
> subjected to a strong pulsing radio signal, it will act as a rectifier
> and "detect" the pulsating waveform, i.e., convert the pulsations from
> a radio frequency to an audio frequency (if the pulsation rate is in
> the pass-band of audio frequencies.) For example, a hearing aid
> consists of a microphone, an audio amplifier and a small speaker. If
> a strong pulsating radio signal impinges upon the first transistor
> amplifier stage, the transistor will be driven into its non-linear
> range and detect the pulsations. If the pulsation rate is in the
> audio frequency range, the rest of the hearing aid amplifier will
> amplify this and deliver it to the speaker, to the great annoyance of
> the hearing aid wearer.
>
>
>
> Read the full text here: [http://mentalfloss.com/article/1758...speakers-go-blip-blip-blip-buzz#ixzz2lUhtwkR9](http://mentalfloss.com/article/17586/why-cell-phones-make-speakers-go-blip-blip-blip-buzz#ixzz2lUhtwkR9)
> \--brought to you by mental\_floss!

### elvey90 — 2013-11-27T11:47:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elvis-journal.8826/post-101719>

Alright time for an update, I've been busy studying exams and finished my first one this morning, feeling pretty awesome right now haha

Anyway so on the weekend bought 5kg worth of salmon at the markets and going to incorporate more and more seafood. My chicken batch is running out and probably after tomorrow will be all gone so no more chicken. So far my diet is looking good, on the weekend I had my BAB and lunch and didn't feel hungry for dinner, this was the first time that it happened to me and was pretty excited when that happened. My carb cravings seem to be dying down a lot, thought not totally gone, if I do have carb cravings, I usually have 1 glass of coconut milk with cacao powder and maca powder with my meal. Apart from that one day I had breakfast and lunch skipping dinner, every other day I have my BAB, not feeling hungry till 4PM then having dinner when I get home from work.

So far my carbs have been >30-50g (not counting) through green veggies but thinking of lowering it even more. So far my food choices are:

BAB:

2x pork or beef sausages
2-3 hard boiled eggs
2-3 chicken thighs (once these are gone I'll replace it with 200-400g salmon, or whatever fish I can find)
1 glass of coconut milk with cacao powder (I have these at dinner now due to time restraints I have in the morning)
1 cup of Bok Choy dipped in coconut oil and ghee

Dinner

400g GF Steak
Mussels or 200-400g Fish
2-3 eggs
Cabbage or Broccoli dipped in coconut oil or ghee.

I'm still eating my BAB within 30 mins but sometimes it may stretch to 45 mins though doesn't really concern me.
My water source is still RO water or San Vittoria/Pellegrino.

Overall I feel great, one thing I noticed is that when I really fill myself with fats like coconut oil and ghee, my brain just kicks into gear, it's the same feeling when I first got onto Paleo in April but this feeling is just even better, much more energy and just feeling pretty good about myself. Though I must say that I have been involved with heavy personal development and philosophy studies which combined with having a healthy lifestyle living, the process and feeling just accentuates.
I was reading Barry's thread with his keto + endurance + fasting experiments and there was one phrase that really struck me that rings true, it's been in harmony with oneself and understanding that all nature's law's apply, one aspect of life cannot exist without the other.

I'm still at the beginning stages, EMF is one of the issues I'm trying to resolve but quite difficult due to living in a shared accommodation, those Greenwave filters look very tempting to get first thing now.

I don't really count fats, I usually have a massive scoop of ghee and coconut oil in every meal and if I do have coconut milk, it's 1 glass so that's about 55g of fat. I do realize I have been eating a bit too much protein and working to getting it down and eating more fatty foods like seafood & offal.

Oh I forgot to add that I have 1x avocado in the mornings usually and if I miss that then I have it for dinner, so that's another 15g of fat approx.

But I honestly don't count to the extreme, it's more of a reference of how much I eat. I just eat whatever I feel like, and at the moment I am working to getting that protein down a bit and increase fats.

Quick Update:

Food Intake:

Breakfast:

\- 1 glass coconut milk with cacao powder and maca powder
\- 1 avocado
\- 2 sausages
\- liberal amounts of coconut oil

Dinner:
\- 250g minced meat
\- 1 broccoli
\- 1 onion
\- 2 eggs
\- 7 mussels (man these were good)
\- liberal amounts of coconut oil and ghee

Water:
San Vittoria (Italian Sparkling Mineral Water)
**Question: The label shows high amounts of bicarbonate, is this stuff good or bad for you?**

Now this is gonna be where I start experimenting a little, I'm going to try and reduce my BAB by removing sausages altogether, they are around 15g protein each if anyone is curious, but will be consistently having 3 eggs and of course my lovely fatty fats.
For dinner, that's going to stay the same for now but I will add another glass of coconut milk.
I'm also taking cod liver fish oil if I remember to take it about 1 teaspoon.

Still no exercise of any shape of form but I'm leaning towards swimming because it's water and its cold  (I've love being in water, feels good  ) and am going to start to experiment to change my body composition without going to the gym and do weights (been doing that for 3 years with each year plateuing end of each year, but was constantly sore and have niggling injuries), having said that I might go casually and do some kettlebells or battle ropes, something different.

Still figuring out the best EMF situation for my place, definitely going to get Greenwave filters and chuck em all over the place, and I'm going to replace my lightbulb with red amber bulbs if I can find them, at the moment I'm using yellow light bulbs. I am slowly removing the electrical devices that I don't need as well.

Other than that, constantly working on being present and aware.

Bit confused here, I thought the reset was only something like 75g of protein? That's roughly 3ozs which isn't a lot, certainly not as much as you guys and gals are ingesting. Maca powder I thought was a carb? Drinking coconut milk sounds great but again, isn't it a carb in that form? Italian sparkling water has a much higher salt content than most, San Pellegrino was always on our banned list because of this, how times are a changing

### cinnamon — 2013-11-30T20:20:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elvis-journal.8826/post-101906>

Hi sooperb,

The amount they are aiming for is the protein content in the food. For example, 100g of pan fried bacon has 38g of protein (try [this link](http://nutritiondata.self.com/facts/pork-products/7356/2) to nutrition data). What makes up the rest of the 100g is mostly fat (40g) and some water content. So, you would need to eat nearly half a pound of bacon (200g) to get to 75g of protein.

### sooperb — 2013-11-30T20:32:44+0200

### elvey90 — 2013-12-04T01:55:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elvis-journal.8826/post-102233>

Time for an update,

In terms of food, I've minimized my protein size for my meals (still eating 2 meals) and literally just rub them in Fat!

BAB:
250g of GF minced meat with chopped onions and red cabbage
3x eggs (hard boiled)
1x Avocado
1 Tbsp (approx) of coconut oil (maybe more, I take swigs of coconut oil at times)
1 cup of Coconut Milk with Cacao Powder & Maca Powder (gonna stop the Maca once I finish)

Dinner:
250-400g fish (fresh atlantic salmon)
2x eggs (scrambled)
1x handful of Kale
Loads of coconut oil and ghee
1x cup of coconut milk (if I feel like it)

I definitely feel a lot better than I was a couple of weeks ago and what I mean is by better is not the 'forever delusioned utopian happyness' but rather much more aware and the conscious changes of what's happening within me and around me. I definitely feel the EMF's affecting me, yesterday at my job, a colleage turned on a server right next to me and immediately a massive headache plus feelings of tiredness crept in.

I'm seeing a specialist tomorow for my foot before I start any form of excercises but still looking at swimming. The challenge is how I'm going to time when I swim and when I eat with the constraints of studying and my job.

It's been a heavy few days but it's also been very very fun!!

### elvey90 — 2013-12-05T02:22:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elvis-journal.8826/post-102359>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=102357)
>
> you are way ahead of me..... didn't take much did it ...

Curiously, there was a LCHF meetup (Low Carb High Fat) meet up (more like a conference) that happened recently, the presenters that were explaining the science did a very good job, explaining how the excess of carbs causes the insulin resistance and how high fat can help a diabetic etc etc. Interestingly none of them really spoke or talked about ketosis and the effect on the brain, also the benefits of seafoods and DHA for brain function, (though they did touch on a little on the benefits of ketosis as energy for the brain). In terms of food choice, it was mainly on finding and eating grass fed meats, poultry, offal, healthy fats and low carbs from greens etc.

What I found interesting was the examples of polysaturated fats (oils coming from plants and seeds), essential the human body was only meant to have 1:1 ratio, until we added grains and wheat it became 2:1. Now what was really scary is that now an average human takes in 36:1 of these plants and seeds, funnily enough these so called 'healthy' foods are approved by the 'Heart Tick Foundation'. I'll link the video once I get it.

Another interesting thing I found was the analogy of a paleolithic diet compared to a keto fuel based lion. A lion when hunts for food and catches its prey, the first thing it eats is the heart, then the liver, the rest of the organ meats and the last thing it eats is the muscle meat.

Food for thought!

Other than that, it was well presented to an audience who were mainly carb based, (can easily tell by the audience). It was pretty funny to see their reactions especially a diabetic doctor announced her disbelief that eating fats can help with diabetes haha

### elvey90 — 2013-12-16T05:46:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elvis-journal.8826/post-103564>

Heya All, it's been a while since an update, been busy studying and working through some stuff as well!

With regards to the foods I've been eating, I've cut out greens except for red cabbage, mushrooms and onions and I've minimized my protein intake as well (well kinda). I'm starting to notice that I get less and less hungry during the day especially if I drink lots of RO water. This weekend I managed to just eat breakfast and last the whole day without feeling hungry, no snacks, just water throughout. When this happened I got really excited, went to bed early on that day feeling pretty good. Some of my changes include eating more lamb/beef liver and heart, including random mussells here and there and putting more coconut oil on my foods.

On a side note: I go through ghee ridicously fast and yes I shoulda have listened to you guys (the ones in a separate thread that mentioned to make your own ghee), and have decided to make bulk ghee for myself.

The next morning, I had my BAB and I spilled pink salt all over my food and unfortunately ate through it all. Throughout the whole day, I had stomach cramps, constant burping and stomach grumbling. Was seriously such a tough day especially with my mood going out of wack as well. Anyhow, it's starting to reside today and I feel a lot better! I still have acne (white heads) here and there but not as often and severe anymore, probably a detoxing effect.

So far I must say, I'm really happy eating this way, it's definitely much more fulfilling when I eat and feels 'right' to me. Though there is one thing, sometimes my stomach grumbling plus randomly burping after a meal, is that normal? also with this acne, is there something I can take or do to at least minimize the effects much more effectively?

In terms of CT, I'm just taking cold showers and my skin is getting drier but I read up in the forums that it's probably a detoxing sympton. In terms of EMF, I've decided to get rid of my TV, PS3 and my games (not only an EMF reason but something bigger). Getting greenwave filters are much tougher than I thought since they don't ship to Australia, but so far I've got a couple of salt lamps placed around my room and getting a couple more to place on my desk where my laptop sits. This will have to do until I move out!

Until then I've looked at a water resonator to mitigate some risks with EMF - [http://www.greenfieldnaturals.com/resonator-research-program/](http://www.greenfieldnaturals.com/resonator-research-program/)
Anyone tried or have any thoughts?

It feels like there's so much to do and it feels never-ending sometimes but if there's one thing I learnt, is that be patient and enjoy the process!

### Sammy — 2013-12-17T03:02:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/elvis-journal.8826/post-103631>

Elvey, if you have time, go to the beach and just soak in the water for about 20 minutes. You can use the sand to gently exfoliate your skin. Do this about 3 times a week(the more the better) and your skin will thank you! All my life I had horribly dry scaly skin(have a history of eczema).... sure enough epi-paleo and cold showers definitely improved my skin ALOT(I no longer itch like a madwoman after a shower) but it was going to the beach that really took it to another level. I don't even have to use lotion/creams etc.

# Thread: Femlae Hair loss

### motivatedhealthseeker — 2013-11-22T04:17:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/femlae-hair-loss.8833/post-101304>

hello Everyone!
Ive had gluten issues for a while and noticed in college that the hair on the crown of my head were less than the rest of the hair. I wasnt diagnosed with gluten issues until almost 7-8 yrs later. Now all of a sudden I noticed the one side of head, the front, and the crown is also thinning more. I had a lot of hair loss in the last 2-3 months. I thought it was due to extreme stress from losing a sibling but it continues still. I am on the iodine protocol and have been taking 500 mg of metformin. Can the metformin cause hair loss indirectly due to low b12 levels? Does iodine have anything to do with it? I also take 75 mcg of cytomel and have been for about 1.5 yrs. I am 39. I also had a pretty low P/E ratio and had been using progesterone cream..1/4 tsp of Dr Lees Progesterone cream at night. It prob wasnt enough to get to optimal but it was hard finding enough skin to get all that cream into as it was. Pills scare me. So not sure what is causing this hair loss but I am very concerned. I am getting my iron and TIBC, ferritin, b12, free t3, free t4, tsh, and a CMET soon. Does anyone have any suggestions as to what might be causing this and how to deal with it. The two things I am worried about that I have added only in the last couple of months is the metformin and iodine protocol. I take 50 mg iodoral along with 200 mg magnesium, 200 mg selenium, and bcofactors.
Thank you in advance!

Dr. Kruse has always said that you can't out supplement a bad diet. Dr. Tim is going into excrutiating detail about starting with healing your gut first before you start supplementing willy nilly .... you can get serious detox and really set yourself back.

### motivatedhealthseeker — 2013-11-22T21:06:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/femlae-hair-loss.8833/post-101390>

I eat a strict gluten free diet..4 eggs with 1 cup of egg beaters with mushroom, kale, spinach, green peppers, onions etc in it with 2 turkey sausages for bfast every day and then lunch and dinner usually consist of steamed cauliflower with some kind of fish or chicken or a turkey burger with no bun. Dessert when I have it consists of 1/2 avacado with raw cocoa powder with almond milk and 1/2 banana and a spoon of gluten free protein powder whipped into a pudding with some walnuts.I will eat blue corn chips as a snack sometimes with picante sauce and 1/4 avacado and maybe some sour cream if its available. I dont eat any kind of added sugar or any fruit except the 1/2 banana. I am surprised my glucose is the way it is. I need to work on going to bed on time but lately its been extremely hectic as I am trying to leave town for a bit.

### nonchalant — 2013-11-22T22:03:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/femlae-hair-loss.8833/post-101394>

I know what you mean about the gluten, when I dropped it I started to get my health back.

I stopped losing hair, and regrew it back when I started eating large amounts of animal fats and meat. Then I started adding lots of seafood and my skin gradually changed as well, becoming very soft yet tough. Are you in the northern hemisphere? Perhaps you should really increase the fat. Shellfish, beef, pork, even consider tossing the egg whites and substituting yolks. You will probably have to gradually add in fat; your body has to adjust to digesting it.

I probably have less money than anyone here \[retired\] but I look at it as an investment in my health and an optimum life. I want to go out with my boots on!! My Dh was sick for many years and wound up with vascular dementia ....I can't let that happen to me - it is heartbreaking.
so - to me - a gold membership is a necessity ..... I need to soak up as much of this as possible and I love learning all this stuff .... well - trying anyway! .... not to mention all the inspiring people here that motivate me every single day to do better.

Dr. K. is shining his flashlight for us - what a rare opportunity ... and I want to take full advantage of every single second.

sorry about the soap box........

I was just listening to this podcast (again) yesterday & took greater note as I fly twice a week - abt 2 -3 hrs at a time. Not good for us!

\- bare feet on the metal as the avionics are grounded
\- 2-400 mgs CoQ10 prior to trip
\- then same dose every hour during the flight
\- big mob ? 32 Ozs cold RO water - and then 16ozs every hour thereafter
\- CT

He talks a bit abt long haul flights and some ideas for reducing these effects & also Melatonin use

I love how I am always learning more w every listen to Jack - bit by bit it's going in even w/out a science geek brain - just takes a lot longer !! Haha
