### MrPinkies — 2014-06-08T21:13:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-finally-feel-like-a-success-story.8975/post-130184>

Well friends.. 18 days until my extended abdominoplasty surgery.. I have my post-op appointment on Tuesday which I am thoroughly looking forward to. If there is one thing that is not talked about when it comes to massive weight loss (160+ lbs down and counting), it's the extra skin and the pain of it all.. It tugs and pulls.. have to wear spanx (shapewear) and at the end of the day.. I'm ready for bed.. CT has been my friend lately..

Other than that.. here in Michigan.. we have had great 75-80 degree sunny weather.. laying in the grass.. light breeze.. no sun burns- SURPRISE!

Last, spring/summer time.. bring on the carbs!!!! ok.. so.. I wanted to see what would happen if I went over 30 carbs in a day- first time in close to two years that I purposefully went over the carb limit.. I wanted to see if I would gain weight back.. Findings: as long as I time my meals- I'm good up to 50g of carbs... that was definitely not the case last year.. last year, if I went anywhere near 30g I'd gain weight back.. this makes me very very happy.. I found a very delicious raw raspberry pie..

Have an awesome day..  \*internet high five\*

### Hope — 2014-06-25T19:32:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-finally-feel-like-a-success-story.8975/post-132225>

> [Christopher said:](/goto/post?id=124064)
>
> David, I don’t know if you are still following this or not, but I shall respond anyway. I’m Chris, Kate’s (mrpinkies) husband. I have been professionally diagnosed with both Asperger’s Syndrome, and Social Anxiety Disorder, and I would like to add my perspective.
>
>
> Before my diagnosis, Kate and I first started to notice the traits of autism in Ryan, our first child, we didn't know what it was. Ryan had some behaviour that seemed to me to be anti-social. I knew I had social anxiety, and I thought that he was modelling his behaviour after my own. I felt guilty. I decided that I should make an effort to be a better role model for my son. I sought therapy for my social anxiety. That therapy lead to my diagnosis of Asperger’s. I was glad for the diagnosis, as it relieved much of the guilt I felt. Ryan’s diagnosis of Autism Spectrum Disorder soon followed.
>
>
> Kate and I both began our somewhat independent journeys to learn about autism. I was totally preoccupied with learning about my own condition, and at the time, I assumed that Ryan would turn out no different from myself. Everything I was reading about Autism was like looking in a mirror clearly for the first time. It gave me a new understanding of my personality especially my strengths and weakness. Like you, David, I am quite happy with many aspects of my personality that are derived from the Autism. I even went through a phase of thinking that being on the Autism spectrum was superior to being neurotypical.
>
>
> During that time, Kate was desperately trying to figure out how to handle Ryan’s diagnosis. She was learning that early intervention with therapy was important. And was trying to convince me that we needed to get Ryan into some kind of therapy as soon as possible. I resisted her. I was still on the stage of accepting myself. I was embracing who I was and figured we should also embrace who Ryan was, after all, I turned out ok, so he should as well. We went back and forth on the issue for some time. Despite my stubbornness, Kate was successful in eventually getting Ryan into various therapies.
>
>
> Eventually, the pride I had in my Asperger’s began to fade. My weaknesses were still with me, even if they weren’t my fault. I was still troubled by anxiety, and depression wich are both related to the Autism. To make matters worse, I was also dealing with some religious confusion at the time which was leading to denial, paranoia, guilt, and apathy. I was in a downward spiral, but I could not fully acknowledge it, nor even begin to understand why.
>
>
> Around that time, I discovered the paleo diet, which lead me to Jack Kruse. I started with the Leptin Rx. It made me feel great. And the rest is history. By history, I mean, Kate and I both have success stories, and journals on the forum here.

On the other hand. I need extra time to process communications from others. I tend to take things literally and have difficulty grasping the deeper meaning of what is said to me. This leads to misunderstanding, and unnecessary arguments. I also tend to get caught up on the details of things and lose sight of the bigger picture. Not to mention the anxiety and depression.
>
>
>
> Well, ok, lets mention the anxiety and depression. It should be known that anxiety and depression are both strongly linked to autism. Anxiety and depression are caused by both the social difficulties of autism, as well as by the physical state of the brain. Now if there is one thing that bothers me the most about my autism, its the anxiety, and second to that would be depression.
>
>
> After starting the Leptin Rx, I noticed a drastic improvement in my anxiety. Previous to the Rx, feelings of anxiety, including the dreaded rushes of adrenaline, were and everyday experience for me. Not just in social situations, but when I was alone as well. After the Rx, I only feel rushes of adrenaline in appropriate situations, such as when an angry customer yells at me at work. It used to be especially bad when I would first go to bed. But now, I embrace the darkness and have no trouble at bedtime.
>
>
> Over time, the lack of anxiety has helped me to explore some philosophical ideas I was previously afraid of. These new ideas, have been very helpful in alleviating my depression. I’m currently re-learning how to enjoy life again, with much success.
>
>
> Now as far as the social difficulties go, I have had no changes in the way I process communication. But my mind operates more efficiently now.. That means I still require extra time to process incoming communication, but it goes a little quicker now than it did before. Also, I used to have trouble dealing with transitions. For example when I have down time at work, I may end up surfing the web, reading a book, or just sitting in silence. It is hard for me to transition from one of these casual activities to trying to complete a job in a timely fashion. But since the Rx, I find transitions much easier. It helps that the physical sensations of anxiety are gone.
>
>
> I consider this to be a successful improvement to my life. To further define success, I would say that it has become easier to achieve my goals. One of my main goals is to just simply enjoy my life. I think its fair to say that most, if not all people have the same goal. Autism presents many different obstacles to achieving that goal.
>
>
> The obstacles my son faces are different, and it would appear more extreme than my own. It has been difficult for me to accept that fact, but it is true. Kate and I both love our son the way he is. We have no desire to change his personality. We only desire to help him overcome any obstacles that block the path toward the enjoyment of life.

Chris, I just read this post of yours. Wow. I do hope you guys decide to write a book about your story. You both are such great writers.....and could help great many people..not just here on the forum, but beyond. I am hoping to share this post of yours with my DH....he may resist me, again...but he sounds a lot like you....not ASD diagnosis, but he has admitted to being socially anxious....and it is very obvious anyway.

So, I also really appreciate your personal/male perspective on this. One thing that really hit me is this:

That means I still require extra time to process incoming communication

I think I am understanding my DH a little better through you. When I ask or talk to him, sometimes, it takes him what seems forever to respond. I bet this is why....same issue I mean. So thank you so much. Truly. If you had a book to sell now, I'd hit the buy button in a flash....

And of course, all the best to MrPinkies

### MrPinkies — 2014-07-08T23:06:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-finally-feel-like-a-success-story.8975/post-133555>

Had my first Post Op appointment today.. I got two out of three drains removed (YAY)! I got all the stitches/threading removed as well. I'm in a little bit of pain, but, nothing a huge bag of ice couldn't fix! My surgeon commented on how well everything is healing up. Honestly, I've been spending a lot of time outside in the sun, bare feet in the grass... sleep has been here and there just because I have to get up every four hours to drain the drains.. I just feel like a million bucks after being outside and hangin' out in the grass..

### caroline — 2014-07-09T02:15:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-finally-feel-like-a-success-story.8975/post-133579>

You are a rock star Kate! ......xo does the surgeon know about grounding and sun and seafood and wifi and bluelite?????

will you be able to sleep thru the nite now? are you still sleeping in the chair?

### MrPinkies — 2014-07-09T03:55:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-finally-feel-like-a-success-story.8975/post-133607>

My surgeon has no idea about the EMF/diet/grounding stuff.. I don't think? He was impressed about how I was healing up. He also asked me about why I felt nauseous or what it was from. I told him that it was probably from not having anything processed food wise, etc, and how my lifestyle has changed over the last two/three years... Then he asked if I was serious.. I could tell that Chris was about to explode in laughter.. He was impressed. Then again, he (my surgeon) said that I was one in a million out of the patients he normally sees. How often do you think he sees someone that has lost over 165+ lbs on their own without gastric bypass, etc..

I am just SUPER excited to go shopping after the swelling goes down.. by the measurements right now with the swelling, I should be a size 4.. They took off 5.8 lbs of skin of my stomach..

Going from a size 26 down to a size 4?!?! who does that?? THIS GIRL!

Still have one drain in, so, I have to still get up every 4 hours.. and I'm still in my chair too.. Can't lay flat yet either.. but.. very very soon... I can feel it!

### cantweight — 2014-07-09T04:24:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-finally-feel-like-a-success-story.8975/post-133617>

Holy shit Kate! 26 to 4 that is unfreakingreal! So proud of you and glad to hear your surgery and recovery have gone so well.

### kovita — 2014-07-09T19:18:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-finally-feel-like-a-success-story.8975/post-133738>

Kate, your journey is fantastic and your personal success should not stay unnoticed. I mean if you are not doing it already you definitely should make it public in some way (maybe send an email to a few magazines etc.) People are generally too comfortable to change anything in their lives, I think your story and the tools you employed on the way to your personal dream could and should inspire thousands out there. Just the other day I was talking to one of my school ex-classmates and she is desperate to become a woman she always wanted to be, she could be around 130kg now. She is professor assistant in physics, mother of 3 children and just finishing business university. To me it almost looks like she did everything possible to show to the world she is a special person and the same time justifying her body. We had a very long talk and I really am ready to help her to get on track, but I am athletic 55 kg woman and it is just too easy for her to say that I was never fat si I have no idea how difficult it is. To me, this is really funny, I was never fat but I was much sicker and apart of getting better my body moved to antic shapes without any effort. I know people must seek and make decision by themselfes, you cannot not push them, but your transformation could be a very important hint for many people who are looking for excuses. I say, everyone deserves to be healthy and to feel wonderfull.

### kovita — 2014-07-16T17:39:37+0300

### MrPinkies — 2014-07-25T17:57:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-finally-feel-like-a-success-story.8975/post-135547>

Roughly, four weeks post surgery.. I'm finding myself more and more bouncing back everyday.. I've been off all pain meds for two weeks now.. Sleep has been hard because I was getting used to getting up to empty the drains and I couldn't lay flat.. but.. this past week and a half I was given the ok to lay flat again and all the drains are out.. so.. I'm FINALLY back to sleeping through the night on my magnetico!! Oh how I missed my magnetico!! I'm finding the swelling is coming down tremendously too.. Walking 2-3 miles too! The more I move, the better I feel! Fresh air and sun never felt so good..

Things are coming together nicely.. Refocusing on getting back onto a regular routine- especially with my sleep and drinking water.. The surgeon reconstructed/tightened my stomach muscles.. with that.. I can't chug my 32 oz water bottle all at once a few times a day like I used to because my stomach can't expand like it used to before surgery.. It makes me sad, but, I guess spreading out my water isn't as bad as I thought it was going to be. Same goes to my bladder.. God gave me this awesome huge bladder.. It's forcing me to go more often.. oh well.. life goes on I guess...

So.. I have a few people in my life that have had this surgery done.. two of them were done a full year before I had mine done.. I am thrilled at how well I am healing up and how quickly I am bouncing back... but.. I can't help to think that they really really REALLY are jealous and hating me because of this... I know that I am SUPER conscientious about my timing of food, what I put in and or on my body, water, and being out in the sun as much as I can now.. There is no doubt in my mind that this is why I am bouncing back fast..

> and - one more thing.... you came to jackkruse.com .... and you took everything on board. You never once whined or complained or said you couldn't!
> I am very proud to be on this journey with you guys .....

I whined and complained for a while.. the first month or so of the reset was literally like a crack addicts rehab.. wait.. scratch that.. we can call it for what it is.. a sugar/carb addicts rehab... it was tough, but, well worth effort.. until I realized that I was losing 2-3 lbs per day.. LOL Took me a while to grasp the other stuff- three legged stool, emf, grounding, etc.. and even though I don't completely understand it all... all I know is that I feel AMAZING.. For the first time in my life, I love myself and the way I feel..

Chris has been (and always has been, even before my surgery) my knight in shinning armor! He has taken very good care of me.. and actually.. He took a full week off of work to take care of me my first week out of surgery.. It was nice to slow down for once and just enjoy each other... sit n' talk with each other.. but also, letting me take a break from being the almighty duties of being Mom.. It was a LOT to take on.. He did it.. and I couldn't have done it better myself..

Chris says hello, too!  I told him that he needed to come back on here because everyone is missing him.. He smiled.. He will be back soon. It's been a long week and a half. We went to a funeral today and that just sucked the energy out of both of us. A friend of ours, who we've known for over 15 years, committed suicide. In all fairness, losing our friend was a shock, but not.. He was either going to do what he did or die from from alcoholism sooner or later.. He will be greatly missed..

### MrPinkies — 2014-08-28T19:33:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-finally-feel-like-a-success-story.8975/post-139159>

Here's to starting year three of the ketogenic version of the epi-paleo diet, emf, grounding, with awesome spring water..

I'm still on my operation- get tiny.. more or less- firming up areas, etc.. I've never had a small booty before.. or small anything before for that matter... haha.. 9 weeks out post surgery and I am doing well.. I have another month before I can get the ok to go hard with HIIT or zumba....

Lizzie- we are doing well, so, keep on doing what we are doing for her..

Chris is having trouble with seafood.. he's becoming light headed and irritated at nighttime.. so.. he's taking a break from seafood and he started to feel better.. could be cashews too? He's trying to figure it out.. I'll keep y'all posted

and for Ryan.. We have gotten SOOOOO very very far with everything, but, it's time to go back to ABA therapy.. We have him at a calm steady level with the diet, so, it is an optimal time to do it and see what leaps and bounds we can make with the therapy again!

Well, it's sunny and in the upper 70s low 80s.. better go soak it up before we get 5 months of grey weather here in Michigan!!!  See ya around friends..

### MrPinkies — 2014-10-04T16:49:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-finally-feel-like-a-success-story.8975/post-142779>

Words about Autism (from Facebook) that are repeating in my head over and over... worth more than the weight in gold..

**"[Jack Kruse](https://www.facebook.com/jack.kruse?fref=ufi):** Its not the vaccines.......its the damn transition metals in them and how they steal electrons from your mitochondria to lower DHA in neurons.......when this happens the area in the Cerebellar and frontoorbital and parietal tracts become under developed........because the mitochondria there lose their magnetic fields because the current of flow of electrons on the inner mitochondrial membrane is low. Maxwell Laws, 101. Every current has an associate magnetic field. When that current is moving........it is called a Lorentz force. I have spent a lot of time talking about th delta psi of ECt but I have not tackled the proton motive forces....... ... When a magnetic field is applied to a moving electric charge, such as a moving protons or electrons or the electrical current in structured water or collagen crystals as Ling discussed, the force on the charge is called a Lorentz force. K+ turns our intracellular water into a structured liquid crystal that is the first step in TI construction."
**"[Jack Kruse](https://www.facebook.com/jack.kruse?fref=ufi):** movement of electrons is tied to the entire pathway working........its all in Tensegrity 5. Environment to DHA, DHA turns light to electric signal which travels along water and collagen to the inner mitochondrial membrane where said electrical signal must go back to light. DHA does that too. Here the light is kept inside the mitochondria only if there is strong electric and magnetic fields........so regression or relapse circa MS or autism is based upon a failure along the pathway at some level."
"**Jack Kruse:** The reason autism begins is because the hind brain is the last thing to develop and the mitochondria there wind up having less electron current flow and hence lower electric and magnetic fields in these mitochondria. The moms all have lowered hemotocrits. This lowers the amount of DHA and O2 delivered to these bad mitochondrial leading to chronic hypoxia. This up-regulates AMPK pathways the kids have poor functioning neural circuits. This gives you the autism phenotype."

I have some news.. coming very soon... few more weeks... very exciting..

### MrPinkies — 2014-10-31T15:43:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-finally-feel-like-a-success-story.8975/post-145788>

Soo.. many people (especially on Facebook) have been very curious what I've been up to these past two months.. so.. I'm ready to share...

I know that I have mentioned on this forum somewhere that my husband and I are starting our third year of healthy living, Kruse style.. It's amazing to see the transformations in many aspects of life... Our kids, especially our son Ryan, deserves the spotlight today.. Due to some of the laws that changed in Michigan regarding Obamacare, Blue Cross Blue Shield of Michigan now covers Autism treatments. Now, for those of you who have no idea what treatment costs out of pocket, with part-time ABA (3 hours a day, five days a week), Speech, OT, and PT, it costs around 7-8 thousand dollars, per month, per kid. We knew that I would not be able to drive for a good 8 weeks after surgery, so, that is why we have waited until now to get started.

Because Ryan's original diagnoses was over three years old, the insurance company required us to go through a whole new diagnoses process. This included 8-10 hours of paperwork and questionnaires, speech evaluation (waste of time), neurology evaluation (waste of time, although, I did catch the interest of this neurologist), parent interview (4 1/2 hours long), child testing which included cognitive test (4 hours long), along with the typical Autism testing- ADOS, etc, a multidisciplinary team meeting, and a result/feed back meeting. While most of you are thinking what BS that was because Autism is a life-long thing and the diagnoses was not going to change- trust me, I feel you.. this was the biggest pain in the ass thing to go through especially because they require us to go through it every three years to authorize services.. It's emotionally draining.. It's draining physically.. I'm so exhausted from this process I could potentially sleep for weeks if life allowed me to. Wishful thinking.. but that's ok.. On the other hand, I realized that it was a really good thing to go through because we can compare and see gains/relapse, etc and gives a clear path on where to go and what to do.

While I wish I could post something about changing his diagnoses or the severity of it, I can't. Autism part, didn't change, at all.. There are parts of Ryan that are still under two years old. However, there is a VERY exciting and interesting part to share... that is the cognitive testing results. Imagine a bell curve. From the far left to right: extremely low, low, average, above average, and whatever the last one was.. When Ryan was tested at 3 1/2 years old, most, if not all of his cognitive scores tested in the extremely low section. This year, at 7 years old, he tested all in the average and in one area, the above average range. It was within months of starting the ketogenic diet with Ryan, we caught him completely up educationally. We started to see him really start to learn and grow very quickly, especially after we spend an entire summer out in the sun and barefoot in the grass.. Took away most blue lighting.. Added the magnetico February of last year... This is the result of a year and a half of this journey... I can only imagine what this will all bring us a few years down the road because I am understanding and learning more and more each day that this journey is really just beginning!

What this tells us is that today, Ryan would be able to hold a job, but, not live on his own.. but.. because he has made HUGE gains cognitively, that if we put him back into ABA therapy, that he will most likely able to catch up to where he will be able to have a job or go to college AND live on his own... He has the capability... I LOVE LOVE LOVE this outlook on his future.. Our goal in ABA therapy is to work on his coping and daily living skills. We had great success with ABA in the pass, but, in combination of grounding, magnetico, ketogenic diet, etc etc etc, I am very very VERY excited to see the gains this time around...

### Ernest Mack — 2014-11-06T04:14:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-finally-feel-like-a-success-story.8975/post-146297>

Wow your story and results are amazing. Such an encouragement. Thanks for sharing.

> [MrPinkies said:](/goto/post?id=102418)
>
> Hi friends...
>
> I know, I know.. I went MIA on the forum... Life kinda got busy there for a minute.. but.. I guess... it's time to stop and smell the roses!! (right? well.. not so much in 30 degree winter weather in Michigan..)
>
> So... I've made the decision that my family and I are the ultimate success story..
>
> I am officially down ONE HUNDRED FIFTY FOUR POUNDS!!!!!!!!!! Yep.. 1-5-4... 154.. A little over 110 pounds of that was due to the Leptin Reset. Am I still dreaming?!!? No longer 3XL.. or a 2XL.. or even an XL.. or even a L.. Friends.. I went from a size 26 to a size 10 (and still shrinking!).. I am a MEDIUM!!! It took me a long time to get the hang of things.. but.. I think.. somehow my dear husband finally got through to my thick skull how this all works.. I had a lot of trial and error days.. but.. I never thought I would be under 200 lbs again... My brain still has troubles on occasion with the fact that I am under a size 12 even (which is what I was in High School and when I met my husband I was a size 14).. I've corrected my cholesterol, blood pressure, and the ever dreaded PCOS... I'm on the official count down of 15 lbs... 15lbs and winter in Michigan? No prob, bob, I got this! LOL
>
> My dear husband is down to 135 pounds (from around 180ish).. Still smaller than me, but, I am no longer 100+ pounds heavier than him.. which embarrassed me to no end..
>
> and... of course.. I have to include a section about Autism.. because that is how I operate these days... So.. One of these days, I would LOVE to shake your hand, Dr Kruse.. I hope I don't come off as creepy..
>
> So.. for my husband, the Leptin Reset, did a LOT for his Autism and anxiety issues.. Go read his success story and blog: Autism with a side of bacon..
>
> For me, all of my sensory issues for the most part are 98% GONE!
>
> For my kids.. all I can say is.. pure MIRACLE.. Life has certainly changed in the last year.. My son gained over 2 years worth of language.. His repetitive behavior is 95% of the time- manageable.. and the other 5% is rare and cured with a nap... He is in a general ed classroom with an aide and I can't even begin to explain the tears I had rolling down my face when the teacher was explaining to me that Ryan LOVES to partake in answering questions during class with correct answers.. and that he is starting to ask friends to play with him.. Lizzie is completely caught up, in a preschool class without help, and we are hoping when she turns 6 we can drop the label completely.
>
> Since we have changed every single light bulb in our house.. got rid of our cell phones, iPad, TV, and turned off the wifi.. MY KIDS SLEEP!!!!!!!!!!!!! They get tired and put themselves to bed every single night without a fight at 8/8:30pm. I am not joking.. I almost wish I was.. Not a single friend believes me!!!
>
> It's our goal to get the magnetico booster pads at tax return time.. I CAN'T WAIT!!!!!! I'm also looking forward to skin removal surgery in late June of 2014.
>
> Last... "impossible" is just an opinion..

### MrPinkies — 2014-11-13T03:23:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-finally-feel-like-a-success-story.8975/post-147221>

This week has been the BEST, even though it has been quite emotional one!

First, we started back up ABA therapy this week and the first week is always testing.. so I have a gut feeling that by the end of the week I am going to have some awesome results news for y'all... Still at war with the school, but, I'll save that story for another day...

Second, for quite some time now... I've been stewing in the fact that I have a need to share my story.. I've been looking into what it would take to write a book.. So much has been given to me and it's time to give back.. In a phone interview, I was asked to tell my story.. for a few seconds it made me stop in my tracks because it's been a while since I've really thought about where I came from... Two years ago seems like a lifetime ago now... I reminded myself about my thinking patterns and how they have changed over these past two years.. I somehow managed to start my journey with thinking and feelings like this: I remember how desperate and out of options I felt before I started this journey.. I remember my feelings of loss and disconnect with myself and others.. the darkness.. the pain.. the sadness.. parking the car where I knew the exact number of steps to get to a door of a store so that it would appear that I wasn't winded from such a short walk.. the fear of breaking the chair that I was sitting in.. or not fitting in a chair that was offered to me.. the embarrassment of someone asking me if I was carrying triplets and when I was due... losing the ability to breath because I was bent over putting my shoes on or clipping my toe nails.. or having to buy from a plus size online store for size 26 jeans because the biggest most stores carried was a size 22... My desire to live was leaving me.. I felt invisible until I heard the whispers and giggles that trailed behind me which hurt me more than any physical pain I've ever experienced (especially when it came from close family).. Which turned into this: I feel AMAZING.. I am alive and happy.. and what amazing things can I do next?

To get there, however, was (and is) a very long, winding, never ending, but fun, road... in my mind it had to happen... I learned that I had to make a definitive, black and white decision that I couldn't go back on. There was and is no option to go back, only forwards. The decision was to forgive myself as to where I was at and get clean. That there would be a time where I was going to go through absolute hell to get to the happiness.. My addition to sugar, processed food, blue daytime light bulbs, and wireless technology was extreme. It helped to have rules and guidelines. It did not take me long to realize that my addictions had made me miserable all these years... A few months in the leptin reset, my misery was gone.. I felt like I had finally done something that made sense, even though I didn't and still don't understand it all.. I think that is the absolute beauty in all of this.. One doesn't have to understand it ALL to make changes.. For me, understanding comes from how I feel and what I can see... I feel energized and awesome.. My body quickly changed before my eyes these past two years.. I look amazing, even though, there is a part of me that still wants to be smaller than I am now.. we shall see what happens this winter! (<singing> Let it snow, let it snow, let it snow! please let me sit in the snow! lol) I see my kids growing in the areas that I never thought possible or it was going to be possible, but, not for a long time... Hope and the realization that we're improving Autism symptoms and Sensory Processing and gaining weight (being under-weight to now to the 5th percentile on those silly growth charts) and muscle strength..

There are parts of me that are still wondering if all of this is really real and if what I have done is really that amazing... I have friends around me that lost over 100 pounds, but, they did it by killing themselves at the gym and feeling deprived for those slices of pizza.. Sometimes I look in the mirror and I realize everything is different- from my body, to my clothing, to my cleaning products, to new and different positive friends and family around me, right down to the fluoride-free toothpaste I use.. I have a whole new life.. somehow.. I don't miss the old one.. not even a little, smidgen bit, like I thought I would when I first started this journey.. No regrets or killing myself at the gym.. Don't tell anyone, but, I actually hate running.. I think I sometimes run sprints just because I couldn't before and it's one of the coolest things that I can do now... Food, that was easy.. I've always been a meat eater.. Lights.. that took some getting used to, but, not a biggie... technology.. tough one, but, I have managed to be more mindful of it... Spending more time outside when inside I would describe myself as an "indoor cat" or the excuse of being whiter than white walls and burning in 50 degree weather without sun... also a toughie, but, being more mindful about it and realizing that I actually do feel like a million bucks after being in the grass n' sun and knowing that I don't burn in the sun anymore... but.. the mind.. Changing my thinking has been the most challenging part of all of this.. the mind is tricky, stubborn, and powerful.. One of the number one quotes that has kept me on target- "if it's important to you, you will find a way.. if not, you will find an excuse". I wanted my results more than anything on this planet.. <-- that right there is the actual secret to all of this... at the end of the day.. it is YOU and the decisions YOU make... and somehow, I kept on going and going.. there is no stopping me from accomplishing what I dream... The people that have kept up with me on this forum are family to me and am grateful to have in my life..

Somehow in the mist of all of this.. got so much more than I originally dreamed for.. My dream is still growing and unfolding.. I have agreed to be apart of this film... I don't think I understand quite how BIG of an opportunity/thing this really is yet.. but.. I feel like my heart is beating out of my chest with joy and happiness and excitement for such an opportunity!

Kate, I think it will be tremendously helpfull to have any. aterial where someone who followed the weird things discussed on this forum found the solution for her family's multiple problems. I think you are a good example due to the many reasons. It is not so difficult now to listen to JK speeches in public places, you can read blog etc...but than the real people succesfull stories or nowhere to be found. most of the people do need real people stories to find courage to go against what is being sold as a life to be lived. In a way this forum is discussing deep scientific facts suggesting to seek escape fromt he modern lifestyle. The same time, people are being told every day, that the technological advances ARE the thing that will move humanity towards the ideal world free of problems and diseases. I found difficult many times to break this dogma. How can. i be suggesting to my friends living in cold and enbracing the darkness as the sun downs in a world od ever increasing comfort! They would not think a minute to swallow a pill sold in pharmacy without questions of course. So go on, girl, infect the people with doubts and make them questioning the always present sure truth!

### MrPinkies — 2014-12-21T21:14:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-finally-feel-like-a-success-story.8975/post-150744>

Soo... this is a double edged sword post..

After all this testing for new diagnoses and ABA therapist... SSI decided to send us to a mental health exam and a speech and language exam.

SSI declared Ryan no longer disabled. So we are losing all benefits starting February. I'm really scratching my head because at the mental health exam, Ryan was licking the walls.. and the examiner wouldn't allow me to stop him because he wanted to see what my son would actually do.. I, of course, got angry because I wasn't about to allow my son to continue licking the walls. He eventually did stop licking the walls... I think this might have been left out of the report.. not quite sure...

So.. I started thinking about this.. and I'm excited to no longer deal with SSI because they are just plain pain in the ass to deal with... but.. I'm starting to come around to the idea that the possibilities of catching Ryan up is really just around the corner.. Seeing my son in a new light this weekend.. Ryan is happy and fun to be around these days.. The more we do the less I think about my kids being "disabled".. Just a short two/three years ago I would pray that I would out live my kids to make sure that they are taken care of... Just like I had to think about being at a healthy weight and stop thinking of myself as fat, severely obese.. which at times, I am not going to lie, is still really hard thing for me, especially when going shopping for new jeans or whatever..

### MrPinkies — 2015-01-05T15:54:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-finally-feel-like-a-success-story.8975/post-151679>

So.. I've been struggling.. I wanted to switch things up.. and against my better judgement, I bought bulletproof whey protein kit.. I just needed something faster in the mornings because well, I could list all of the excuses of why, but, whatever.. Anyway, I've gained 10 lbs in 2 weeks... I don't think my body likes whey or maybe it's because it's processed and I don't have many processed foods in my life... but then again.. I just haven't really felt back to "normal" since surgery.. Each day feels closer to before surgery.. but.. I feel like surgery has done a number on me.. MCT oil is new to my diet as well.. and I made the mistake of adding 2 tablespoons of it from the start.. go ahead.. laugh.. stomach cramping IS funny when someone is on the floor curled up into a ball wanting to puke.. that's what I get for not reading the directions..

On the other hand... I've added tuna to my list of things I love to eat during this time.. FINALLY some kind of fish in my diet.. it's a very BIG step in the right direction.. and even got Lizzie, who is a very picky eater to like it too!!!

Later on today, I'm going to take more readings with my meters.. I have a sneaking feeling that something nnEMF has to do with my weight gain..

If there is ANYTHING I learned from this journey, lose your way... go back to the basics... strict leptin reset here I come! and... I think I am going to get some labs done too... few more weeks until we move!!!

Thoughts?

10 lbs in two weeks sure as heck indicates something is off in your world! Smart to go back to basics...

I went up 7 lbs over course of holidays. Have been back on track for 11 days and making progress returning to my former self

> me too .... no whey protein for this girl!
> it is such an awesome idea - but just doesn't work for me either
> and lately MCT oil sends me to the toilet with just 1 tsp.
> you must have spent a fortune Kate .......
> yep - back to basics for me too. Good news on the tuna!

Well, yes, it was somewhat expensive- 100 bucks.. I can use most of the kit for other things.. but the whey is completely useless to me.. oh well.. lesson learned.. Yesterday was my first day back strict on the leptin reset and I'm down 1.5 lbs of the 10 that I gained.. so.. yeah.. I'm not too worried.. I need to stop being lazy is what it boils down to.. lol

I was going to go to the gym today, but, after waiting for Ryan's bus outside for almost an hour in the snow, I don't think I need to do that.. lol We made snow angels... and snowmen.. and snowball fight...  It made my morning complete!

### MrPinkies — 2015-01-06T20:38:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-finally-feel-like-a-success-story.8975/post-151867>

Well, I don't think it was the fact that it was MCT oil, it was the fact that I didn't build it slowly into my diet.. I went straight to 2TBS right from the first time... Now that I've had it for a while, I've added more and more and I'm back up to the 2TBS... What really hurt my stomach more than the MCT oil was the whey.. I don't have a lot of dairy to begin with... I've even cut back the Kerrygold butter... I use lots of coconut oil and MCT... and animal fat too.. lots and lots of animal fat..

### nicld — 2015-01-10T19:36:42+0200

I am dealing with a stupid cold and cough that kept me up all night. But I did go and sit outside this morning -9F and stare into the sun while drinking coffee and doing a session of CT. Cold be GONE!!!!!!

### MrPinkies — 2015-01-27T22:02:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-finally-feel-like-a-success-story.8975/post-154022>

So.. The last month or so have been incredibly stressful... Looking back it was started by something that was said to me by a family member that just really hurt my feelings.. I have quite a few haters, which unfortunately, includes family members.
This past month or so also included:
\- I had the flu, then strep throat, gained 12 lbs back (back down 3 of the 12 lbs last night alone since this epiphany)
\- Trying to get a subsidized USDA loan, which pre-qualification came back yesterday as a "good to go" type situation.. So we FINALLY have some direction about finding a place to live...
\- School work..
\- Getting Ryan to and from ABA therapy/being everyone and their brothers taxi driver...
\- Being filmed in a few weeks (this isn't stressful, in fact, REALLY happy and excited for it, even though, I have no idea what to expect)..
\- Went back to my surgeon because I've been having sharp pains on my right hand side, 2" from my belly button.. Blood work yesterday... had a dye contrast CT scan done this morning.. A week to find out if I have to have a hernia repair type surgery and or no lifting/exercising for a while.. Spent an hour or so outside in the snow after the CT scan...

I was stopped in my tracts when I read a meme that said "put your positive pants on"... moments later I read the one that I shared on FB last night which said "When someone tells you it can't be done, it's a reflection of their limitations, not yours..." which this resolves the family members hurtful statement.. I instantly realized last night how powerful the mind is.. and I feel much happier today because I think I actually slept well and because some of these stressful things are being resolved... the house loan is a biggie.. getting the CT scan over with was a biggie.. Now just really waiting to see if I have to have surgery...

They weren't kidding when they said stress is a killer..

### nicld — 2015-01-27T22:26:17+0200

I just read Why Quantum Physics Don’t get Fat and he wrote all about the power of the mind. Check it out if you have time. love you ...xoxo ...you already know that!

> \- Being filmed in a few weeks (this isn't stressful, in fact, REALLY happy and excited for it, even though, I have no idea what to expect)..
> \- Went back to my surgeon because I've been having sharp pains on my right hand side, 2" from my belly button.. Blood work yesterday... had a dye contrast CT scan done this morning.. A week to find out if I have to have a hernia repair type surgery and or no lifting/exercising for a while.. Spent an hour or so outside in the snow after the CT scan...
>
> I was stopped in my tracts when I read a meme that said "put your positive pants on"... moments later I read the one that I shared on FB last night which said "When someone tells you it can't be done, it's a reflection of their limitations, not yours..." which this resolves the family members hurtful statement.. I instantly realized last night how powerful the mind is.. and I feel much happier today because I think I actually slept well and because some of these stressful things are being resolved... the house loan is a biggie.. getting the CT scan over with was a biggie.. Now just really waiting to see if I have to have surgery...

> They weren't kidding when they said stress is a killer..

Never let someone who gave up on their dreams talk you out of going after your own......ever!
The highest level than can be reached by a mediocre but experienced mind is a talent for uncovering the weaknesses of those greater than itself. It's much easier it is to be critical than to be correct. Ignore the critics, only mediocrity is safe from ridicule. You should be dare to be different.
When you're different, sometimes you don't see the millions of people who accept you for what you are. All you notice is the person who doesn't.
Never waste your time trying to explain who you are to those committed to misunderstanding you.

### PAL-PIP-WI — 2015-02-16T00:07:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-finally-feel-like-a-success-story.8975/post-155718>

Wow -- I just got my introduction to this forum by reading this ENTIRE thread of MrPinkies success -- what a story!
Very inspiring, and as I have 100 pounds to release myself, very **especially** inspiring for me going forward.
I know & love several children with autism also and have never seen more courageous-strong-inexhaustable & exhausted people as the parents of these darlings....
So it is fabulous to hear about your children's great gains!
I'm a little confused about whether sun & grass or snow is best for us, but either way, we sure have a lot of BOTH in sunny/snowy Colorado where I have made my home since my childhood in Chicago.
In this thread I heard that MrPinkies made so many changes and had great success without understanding ALL of the science behind it, which is helpful because I am almost done reading Dr. Jack's book Epi-Paleo Rx, and I figure my comprehension rate at 40-50%?
N.E.W.B.I.E. here!
Thanks for sharing your story MrPinkies.

Yes, PLEASE start a thread!! I really had wished I started one from the beginning!!! (and let me know where you post it too!)

You can and will lose the weight.. read the book, read the forum and blog posts.. and yes, I am not a biologist or scientist, but, after jumping in with two feet and putting good honest faith effort, I saw results... ### MrPinkies — 2015-03-11T01:39:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-finally-feel-like-a-success-story.8975/post-158145>

Full, extended tummy tuck, with muscle reconstruction in late June of 2014. On the outside, I healed very quickly and nicely.. Inside is taking much longer..

I waited over two months (6 months total post surgery) past when the surgeon told me that I could exercise.. I started with walking.. then running.. in addition to CT...

In the middle of running sprints, I started having very sharp pains on the right side of my stomach, which pain runs vertical, 2 inches from belly button. Before anyone says it.. there's a difference between pain of pushing oneself to new limits and pure insane injury.. 30+ years of ballet dancing.. I get it.. it's on the pure insane injury side of things..

I stopped exercising and lifting.. and the pain died down.. Of course, Ryan melted down in the middle of Target and I had to carry him out like a sack of potatoes which didn't help anything... I can lay down on my left side (which I hate) just fine.. but the second I lay on my right, I feel the need to rearrange my stomach muscles.. like they are all tangled up.. It has come to a point where it's painful to lift anything over 5 pounds.. Thing is.. muscle strength is there, but, there is pain..

The pain started to have a snowball effect.. I finally went back to the plastic surgeon to explain.. He thought that it might be a hernia (but unlikely), but wanted a CT with contrast dye to see if he could get more information..

CT scan showed everything is in it's place and no hernia. So.. he sent me to get a second opinion with a general surgeon. General surgeon determined it was not a hernia and that it's not the actual muscle, but damaged nerves..

So.. one surgeon consulted with the other surgeon and came up with the idea of localizing the pain with a local anesthetic to see what would happen.. 1/15th of a dose.. and it worked... for a few hours...

Next week is full dose of local anesthetic.. and then making plans for a surgery that may or may not solve this issue...

Here's my issues... among many...
1\. I've been CT'ing as much as possible, every day
2\. surgery- not looking forward to it.. and it's not a 100%, 90%, 80%, or even 75% guaranteed success deal.. it's a gamble.. IF I were to have surgery, it would be roughly 6 inches or less incision where my scar is- down the middle- around the belly button.. with 1 drain for a week or so.. and then another 2-3 months in a binder.. and no exercising for a very long time..
3\. I have not gotten back to where I was before the first surgery (in part of why we are buying a house and moving)... I am just now (9 months after surgery) getting my sleep back in order.. This means another year of being knocked down and working my way back up... Not impossible, just sucks..
4\. moving/buying a house, school, kids, Ryan's ABA therapy, you name it, it's on my plate
5\. I need to get back to life.. you know... picking up children clothing and toys off the floor.. be able to carry around a purse with life's precious possessions.. do some dishes and take out trash.. jump, run, sleep on my favorite right side...
6\. Where does wine and sex fit into this deal??? <sighs>

Thoughts?

### cinnamon — 2015-03-12T03:00:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-finally-feel-like-a-success-story.8975/post-158298>

Oh, Kate, I'm sorry to hear that you are struggling with this. I've thought of you many times while healing myself from my emergency appendectomy ("with complications" ) last summer. I look at the scar from the conversion incision - it's about a 2-3 inch long vertical scar, just below the belly button - and I wonder about you and how you are healing from all those scars from your tummy tuck. I still feel weird twinges and things, as if things are not quite all the way healed on the inside side of the scar. The pain you experience sounds both scary and annoyingly debilitating. Such a relief to hear Dr K say it can still heal!

Hey, if there is anything to the quantum entanglement thing (and YES, I think there is!) know that I'm sending positive healing thoughts your way! Think about how great it is going to be once the move is done and you are sitting out in your own backyard in your bikini!

love,
cinnamon

### MrPinkies — 2015-03-12T03:20:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-finally-feel-like-a-success-story.8975/post-158303>

> [cinnamon said:](/goto/post?id=158298)
>
> Oh, Kate, I'm sorry to hear that you are struggling with this. I've thought of you many times while healing myself from my emergency appendectomy ("with complications" ) last summer. I look at the scar from the conversion incision - it's about a 2-3 inch long vertical scar, just below the belly button - and I wonder about you and how you are healing from all those scars from your tummy tuck. I still feel weird twinges and things, as if things are not quite all the way healed on the inside side of the scar. The pain you experience sounds both scary and annoyingly debilitating. Such a relief to hear Dr K say it can still heal!
>
> Hey, if there is anything to the quantum entanglement thing (and YES, I think there is!) know that I'm sending positive healing thoughts your way! Think about how great it is going to be once the move is done and you are sitting out in your own backyard in your bikini!
>
> love,
> cinnamon

I love you, Cinnamon!  This week in Michigan is PERFECT bikini weather~ 45/50 degrees! It is sooooo good to finally see the sun after months of cloudy, grey skies...

Thank you for the well wishes! I had to chuckle when I read what you wrote about feeling weird twinges and things.. For months, my stomach was completely numb... ok ok.. I know I had ice packs on it.. but.. seriously.. I could probably stab my stomach with a fork and not feel it.. and now.. even 9 months later.. there are times were I feel slight twitches or numbness that moves around.. Maybe I'm just a weirdo.. hahahaa.. Anyway.. I tend to be on the over sensitive side of what is going on with my body I guess..

You are right, the pain was really scary at first.. I nearly curled up into a ball for a week or so with an ice pack.. but at this point, its sooo very annoying.. it's hard to walk around in life trying not to use stomach muscles! and since they have tightened up/reconstructed the muscles, I am more aware of them... Not only that, it's hard to put everyone and everything on hold (or at least it seems that way).. My kiddos are very demanding! hahah.. I guess they are somewhat suppose to be! My brain is in a very different place these days.. Controlling energy that I have VS taking it easy to heal.. I've become impatient.. even after reading and rereading the two inch book of warnings of possibilities of what could happen before, during, and years after surgery.. I need to MOVE!!!! lol in the most literal translation.. haha.. less mass does equal more energy!

~Kate

> This past month or so also included:
> \- I had the flu, then strep throat, gained 12 lbs back (back down 3 of the 12 lbs last night alone since this epiphany)
> \- Trying to get a subsidized USDA loan, which pre-qualification came back yesterday as a "good to go" type situation.. So we FINALLY have some direction about finding a place to live...
> \- School work..
> \- Getting Ryan to and from ABA therapy/being everyone and their brothers taxi driver...
> \- Being filmed in a few weeks (this isn't stressful, in fact, REALLY happy and excited for it, even though, I have no idea what to expect)..
> \- Went back to my surgeon because I've been having sharp pains on my right hand side, 2" from my belly button.. Blood work yesterday... had a dye contrast CT scan done this morning.. A week to find out if I have to have a hernia repair type surgery and or no lifting/exercising for a while.. Spent an hour or so outside in the snow after the CT scan...
>
> I was stopped in my tracts when I read a meme that said "put your positive pants on"... moments later I read the one that I shared on FB last night which said "When someone tells you it can't be done, it's a reflection of their limitations, not yours..." which this resolves the family members hurtful statement.. I instantly realized last night how powerful the mind is.. and I feel much happier today because I think I actually slept well and because some of these stressful things are being resolved... the house loan is a biggie.. getting the CT scan over with was a biggie.. Now just really waiting to see if I have to have surgery...
>
> They weren't kidding when they said stress is a killer..

Thank you for sharing that statement, "When someone tells you it can't be done, it is a reflection of their limitations, not yours"
This is so true..My charge hand at work ALWAYS says that to me whenever I come up with any kind of idea that he "poo poos".
Now I have something to say back and confidence in the truth of that statement.

Thank you!
(and yeah..stress is TERRIBLE!!!)

### MrPinkies — 2015-08-22T20:32:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-finally-feel-like-a-success-story.8975/post-175389>

I am SOOOO proud of Ryan!!!!!!!!!!!! He picked up the phone.. dialed Chris's work number... asked to talk with Chris... and then he told Chris about a game he played, the game made him happy because how fun it was, and that he wanted his Dad to come home to play with him... Am I dreaming??!?!?!? because seriously.. that was the COOLEST thing I have ever witnessed in my life!!!

I can't explain the tears running down my face (in happiness) right now... Think about all of the skills needed to do this task... He remembered his Dad's work number.. He had to ask a "stranger" (and actually, Ryan does know the other person on the other end of the line) to speak with his Dad... When Chris got on the phone, Ryan gave a greeting, he explained the game that he played, he gave and expressed a feeling, and that he asked someone to come into his world that is sometimes impossible to come into if not invited... AND properly ended a conversation.... MY SON HAD A BACK AND FORTH CONVERSATION OVER THE PHONE!!!!!

Sometimes... it's the things that seem so small and not a big deal that we take for granted... that are mountains and absolute miracles for others...

### Melanie Procter — 2015-12-27T01:30:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-finally-feel-like-a-success-story.8975/post-184979>

> [MrPinkies said:](/goto/post?id=102418)
>
> Hi friends...
>
> I know, I know.. I went MIA on the forum... Life kinda got busy there for a minute.. but.. I guess... it's time to stop and smell the roses!! (right? well.. not so much in 30 degree winter weather in Michigan..)
>
> So... I've made the decision that my family and I are the ultimate success story..
>
> I am officially down ONE HUNDRED FIFTY FOUR POUNDS!!!!!!!!!! Yep.. 1-5-4... 154.. A little over 110 pounds of that was due to the Leptin Reset. Am I still dreaming?!!? No longer 3XL.. or a 2XL.. or even an XL.. or even a L.. Friends.. I went from a size 26 to a size 10 (and still shrinking!).. I am a MEDIUM!!! It took me a long time to get the hang of things.. but.. I think.. somehow my dear husband finally got through to my thick skull how this all works.. I had a lot of trial and error days.. but.. I never thought I would be under 200 lbs again... My brain still has troubles on occasion with the fact that I am under a size 12 even (which is what I was in High School and when I met my husband I was a size 14).. I've corrected my cholesterol, blood pressure, and the ever dreaded PCOS... I'm on the official count down of 15 lbs... 15lbs and winter in Michigan? No prob, bob, I got this! LOL
>
> My dear husband is down to 135 pounds (from around 180ish).. Still smaller than me, but, I am no longer 100+ pounds heavier than him.. which embarrassed me to no end..
>
> and... of course.. I have to include a section about Autism.. because that is how I operate these days... So.. One of these days, I would LOVE to shake your hand, Dr Kruse.. I hope I don't come off as creepy..
>
> So.. for my husband, the Leptin Reset, did a LOT for his Autism and anxiety issues.. Go read his success story and blog: Autism with a side of bacon..
>
> For me, all of my sensory issues for the most part are 98% GONE!
>
> For my kids.. all I can say is.. pure MIRACLE.. Life has certainly changed in the last year.. My son gained over 2 years worth of language.. His repetitive behavior is 95% of the time- manageable.. and the other 5% is rare and cured with a nap... He is in a general ed classroom with an aide and I can't even begin to explain the tears I had rolling down my face when the teacher was explaining to me that Ryan LOVES to partake in answering questions during class with correct answers.. and that he is starting to ask friends to play with him.. Lizzie is completely caught up, in a preschool class without help, and we are hoping when she turns 6 we can drop the label completely.
>
> Since we have changed every single light bulb in our house.. got rid of our cell phones, iPad, TV, and turned off the wifi.. MY KIDS SLEEP!!!!!!!!!!!!! They get tired and put themselves to bed every single night without a fight at 8/80pm. I am not joking.. I almost wish I was.. Not a single friend believes me!!!
>
> It's our goal to get the magnetico booster pads at tax return time.. I CAN'T WAIT!!!!!! I'm also looking forward to skin removal surgery in late June of 2014.
>
> Last... "impossible" is just an opinion..

a huge, huge congratulations to you. Yes, this forum and the blog will open up miracles. Life becomes more magical by the day. You will be an inspiration to many.

Mel

# Thread: An EMF/EMR treasure trove of information and measurement devices.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-emf-emr-treasure-trove-of-information-and-measurement-devices.8976/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### CindyB — 2013-12-05T19:58:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-emf-emr-treasure-trove-of-information-and-measurement-devices.8976/post-102436>

I've been reading this paper:
Physics Â± Uspekhi 46 (3) 259 Â± 291 (2003)
"Effects of weak magnetic fields on biological systems: physical aspects
V N Binhi, A V Savin"
available here: [http://biomag.narod.ru/pdf/2003.Binhi.ea.PUsp.pdf](http://biomag.narod.ru/pdf/2003.Binhi.ea.PUsp.pdf)

"Unlike biomagnetism studying magnetic fields (MF) of biological systems \[1\], magnetobiology focuses on biological reactions and mechanisms of action of weak (below 1 mT) magnetic fields. There is rapidly growing interest in the biological action of weak magnetic and electromagnetic fields. The American periodical Microwave News has pub- lished a list of hundreds of the Internet hyperlinks to organizations directly involved in electromagnetobiological research (http://www.microwavenews.com/www.html)."

[http://microwavenews.com/about-us](http://microwavenews.com/about-us)

"For more than 30 years, Microwave News has been reporting on the potential health and environmental impacts of electromagnetic fields and radiation. We are widely recognized as a fair and objective source of information on this controversial subject.

Microwave News covers the entire non-ionizing electromagnetic spectrum, with special emphasis on mobile phones and power lines, as well as radar and broadcast towers.

The first print issue was published in January 1981. All our print issues may be downloaded from the News Center at no cost.

In June 2003, Microwave News converted to a Web-based format.

Microwave News is independent and is not aligned with any industry or government agency. Today, we rely on contributions from our readers."​

Check out their tabs for \[EMF/EMR Meters\] and \[EMF/EMR Directory\]. Info on organizations in lots of different countries.

I can see that Dr. J referenced this site back in April this year here:
[http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?6773-My-City&p=84123&highlight=microwavenews#post84123](http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?6773-My-City&p=84123&highlight=microwavenews#post84123)

Good stuff.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-emf-emr-treasure-trove-of-information-and-measurement-devices.8976/post-102448>

# Thread: Hello and excited to finally get out here!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-and-excited-to-finally-get-out-here.8978/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### glightner — 2013-12-05T21:44:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-and-excited-to-finally-get-out-here.8978/post-102440>

I am a newby, I am 49 yrs old 6'5" tall and started out over 342 lbs. I am sure that I am LR, because I had cravings for sugar and carbs all day long, I binged in the evenings. I was very depressed about my weight and my health, I started reading Epi Paleo Rx and Mastering your Leptin and jumping through the many blogs here.

I initially found Dr. Kruse after I found grounding and then the second time he popped up in a search about structured water. I was researching and I made a home structured water device. I bought the book and started reading and then pieceing the blogs together. I lost 7 lbs in 2 weeks with the Paleo Rx and CT Rx. Today I was at a weight program put on by the Veterans Administration and a doctor told me in group after I weighed in, that Coconut oil was a bad oil and that my weight loss was admirable but she needed to consult with a nutritionist because I may be losing weight but the way that I am doing it was not good, I may not be getting the proper nutrition. Yea like I am going to change what I am doing because she said so! She said that she ate a slice of whole wheat toast with peanut butter for breakfast this morning? I told her that this is the first time that I didn't have cravings for food all day long. go figure!

Anyway, I am so excited I can feel the difference in my hunger, I sometimes skip lunch because I am not hungry. I have never skipped a meal for as long as I can remember, so something is working. I have been doing CT for two weeks and I am up to 40 minutes cold bath with 20 lbs of ice on my chest naked, no shirt gloves or boots. I feel that my muscle tone has improved and I think that my weihgt loss has been viseral fat.

I had my gallbladder taken about ten years ago and I alway thought that my weight gain in the midsection was related to that surgery. There are other people that i know with the same profile and we all had the surgery. I have ordered some of the supplements but I am not sure that I ordered the correct ones.

I just want to thank Dr. Kruse for all that he has done and hope to meet him someday to thank him in person!

glightner

### caroline — 2013-12-05T22:48:44+0200

Hi Glightner and welcome! It sounds like you are doing great.

Dr. K. always says that a Steak makes a great supplement ..... and you can't out supplement a bad diet!!

Please start a journal in the journal section - it is great for commitment and it will give Dr. kruse context....

Good job on C/T - keep it up....

Have you listened to JKs podcasts? lots of great, and easy to understand information .... on the home page.

What about your non native EMF exposure? your home? your work? This is extremely important if you are going to get to optimal.

Keep reading and asking questions......

Welcome to the most informative forum on the net

I wish I'd known in my 30s what I know now, I'd have tried to keep my gallbladder. Apparently it's removed more often than any other organ and considered unimportant, that's how much they know I guess. I couldn't go to any sort of slimming club, I would be banned no doubt for telling them what awful misguided advice they are giving out. I'll get my husband's diabetic diet advisor one of these days too!

### caroline — 2013-12-06T03:01:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-function.8979/post-102472>

I am wondering about improving our thyroid function naturally. I haven't given this enough thought ..... until Dr. Jackson talked about it the other day.

I now realise where Dr. K. was wanting me to get to. That sounds simplistic - but I think I connected the dots in the wrong order maybe......

So - what can we do?

do you avoid BPA's? ...... we all know about flouride etc. in our water now. Should we be doing C/T in flouridated water?

I have realized when my testing was done awhile ago - I wasn't drinking bottled water. What difference will that make in my labs?

I really gave it 100% as much as possible\] within a 3 - 4 month period between testing. My rT3 came down and my free T3 went up. How much of a difference could I have made if I had been drinking bottled water?

what other things could we be doing?

We now know to "eat the sun"
Everyday we have so many revelations ..... it is like Christmas everyday around here! But when I have so many presents to open ..... I sometimes forget about one or two ...and have to back track and re evaluate
and re read and re think ..... and sometimes I go ..... so that is what Dr. K. meant!

I often miss the simple things or just overlook them .... in too much of a hurry on this optimal journey.

I think Dr. Kruse has told us numerous times that we sometimes make it too hard..... of course, sometimes, it is excrutiatingly hard ...

### caroline — 2013-12-07T09:27:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-function.8979/post-102691>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=102496)

I am not answering you .... I'm only thinking out loud...

I have had several cups of tea ... and some brandy and given this lots of thought as you suggested .

I really don't get what you said...

My reason for this thread.....my doc wants to put me on thyroid meds - and I don't want to go there just yet. I also didnot understand that a better functioning thyroid would help my progesterone ..... I should have - but I didn't.

So this is why the question about how we could naturally improve our thyroid function. I don't get why this is being worried about nonsense.

You have told us over and over and over that details matter....

I also have to understand my reality .... I can't sleep in the forest, under a tree and in the snow like Peter does. I have to figure out how to do the best I can given my circumstances. I am also never likely to take care of a baby lamb in my apartment like Inger has.

I really am trying to do the best I can. I sometimes miss things because I don't get all the science. That frustrates me because I want to ....

The concepts are easy ... But I don't always connect the dots properly it seems or just plain can't take it all on board.

Dr. Jackson is expanding on all of this for us and I want to really get it....

You said I am clinging to modern life .... I actually thought I was doing the opposite...,

Sometimes you intimidate me and I don't like that feeling ....so I thought I would restate this ....usually I would just let it go .... I didn't want to do that this time....

### Inger — 2013-12-07T10:51:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-function.8979/post-102695>

Audrey I understand you thinking.... but I also see what Jack is saying. I so wish you could see it too! Look, he does not mean you have to do exactly like me and Peter, that would be silly. But rather how you think about things... and the actions that follow.... like the art of letting go. I have so many weak points yet Audrey, that I am working on. I have realized it is about broadening our view.. to see the possibilities instead of believing they are not there, or we cannot. It is pretty amazing how many possibilities there are! And we do not even need to change one thing, but the knowledge we have the choice to choose whatever we want is pretty amazing. That is when confusion is lifted, and it becomes more a choice and that feels 1000 times better.

Maybe... never think we cannot... think... we chose not to instead. then everything becomes... freedom

Maybe start playing with new, revolutionary thoughts in your mind, things you thought you could never do. Just play with the thoughts, imagine what possibilities there are out there for you to explore  you do not need to do anything of it, so no pressure or stress will arise, just make it a play. I do this at times and it is a amazing feeling to just know there are all these possibilities out there! I might just choose to not do some yet because I need to do certain things first. But that makes the "suffering" I have right now way easier because it is a consciously chosen thing.. it is not that I would not understand why. in all this, to keep ones mind open like the mind of a child. Always curious, always learning, willing to try new things. Living in the magic world of not knowing

### Dali Dula — 2013-12-07T17:40:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-function.8979/post-102725>

I think it is obsession that jack is cautioning against. As I much as we all strive for optimal there is great value in balance and letting go.
On the thyroid subject- There is one thing I have learned in two and a half years of bio hacking and that is get your thyroid functioning well by whatever means initially and be very deliberate in your med changes thereafter. You may get better later and change or stop meds. If your thyroid is off everything is off.

### caroline — 2013-12-08T00:25:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-function.8979/post-102751>

As usual..... this thread has taken quite a turn.......it surprises me no end...

This initially wasn't about me - I just simply wondered if we could be doing a better job with our thyroid function naturally.

When mission control asked me to be a moderator - they asked me to help new people with their questions and start threads on things that interested me. This is what I try to do so we stay connected and share ideas/thoughts.

Really - endless was the only one who got what I was saying .....Thanks Krista!

I think any of us who have stuck with Jack do think outside the box - by definition we have to, and want to if we want to be successful on this amazing journey. We all see the possibilities and are excited by every new day. We have often been quite proud of "flying our Freak flag"

We are shunning a lot of things in the modern world ....because we love this journey and are seeing great personal results. I was out very late three times last week and it wrecked me. I don't like that! I don't like all the horrible food and everyone drinking until they are out of their mind. In general, I think, we are feeling that we don't fit in so much anymore ...and I, for one, don't want to. I am making choices that are best for me - end of story. My friends think I am a little weird and I am very okay with that. They worry about me - but they can see amazing results. I think they are pretty confused about how much butter I eat.... and what has happened to my size and shape! LOL - I gotta say I love it!

So - Seahorse ...I know you think I worry too much and sometimes I do - I can't hold it all in my head - so that is why I was asking - are there simple things we have overlooked?

I remember a couple of months ago ... Misty and Gretchen were saying that they had overlooked the importance of hormones in their own n=1 ......That was a big takeaway for me. Gretch has such a fine mind and great deductive reasoning.

Jack said the other day - on FB I think.... learn from other's mistakes - we won't live long enough to make them all ourself!

As I have said before - we had a small restaurant. I learned pretty quickly that you can't afford too many mistakes in a small business or you will be closing the doors pretty quickly. Bankruptcy/failure was not an option! I guess that is my perspective ... I was only ever as good as my last meal. I loved all the learning and evolving and hard, back breaking work and I truly loved all the different, wonderful, quirky people from all walks of life that contribute to a small successful business.

It is the same for Jack .....people have to leave his operating table better than when they laid down - pretty consistently or he would have to find another day job. Interesting thing - over 35 years - our very best staff were children of Doctors - far and away. They are brought up in a particular atmosphere of needing to do the best possible job or there are very serious consequences - there is no do over. Of course in an operating room it is much more serious than 1 meal that isn't of the highest possible standard. I don't mean to suggest otherwise.

Dali - that is exactly what I was trying to say .... I hadn't thought enough about my thyroid. Thanks! and I guess I must come across as obsessive .... I don't think I am at all - I just think an enquiring mind is important and I think this journey is great fun in lots of ways....I love every second of it! I love talking to all you guys and exchanging ideas .... and trying to figure out what Jack really said this time! The learning curve is huge - but I wouldn't want it any other way.... and yes - I am too intense sometimes - but only because I love it and it is really important to me to, on some level, get it!

Sorry this is way, way too long.......I have needed three cups of coffee! If it wasn't 8am \[well when I started!\] I would definitely have gone thru a bottle of wine ....

I really just wanted to say ... I feel great and I feel very lucky indeed - that wasn't the reason for this. There are lots of you that have many confounding problems and I figure the more we talk the better

BTW - I still don't really get what Jack was saying .....

### caroline — 2013-12-08T00:34:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-function.8979/post-102752>

Thanks everyone for your thoughts .... Inger - I will get back to you! I need to go lay in the sand and look at surfers for awhile and gain some proper perspective about the important things in life....

### caroline — 2013-12-08T00:50:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-function.8979/post-102754>

Marta - I am so happy you are doing so well .....thanks for updating us!

### caroline — 2013-12-08T01:03:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-function.8979/post-102756>

Inger - it disturbs me that you think I am a doubter. That couldn't be farther from the truth! Do I ask a lot of questions? maybe .....I love trying to figure things out.......I love seeing everyone working on their masterpiece.

I generally am way, way too trusting and naive ......maybe that is why all the questions. I will have to think about that.

Three years ago I made the decision that I like Jack's ideas and where he was headed.....it made so much sense to me and still does. There are plenty of naysayers .....no one knows that better than him. Has he got it all right? I dunno ..... that is everyones decision to make for themself. No one gets it all right.....I am very okay with that. Is he perfect? - nope - probably not ....but neither am I!

### SeaHorse — 2013-12-08T01:23:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-function.8979/post-102760>

Audrey....some of this make a lot of sense now that I know you were asked to contribute to the forum specifically.....I was getting worried that you were too worried about everyone else and not taking care of yourself. Glad that's cleared up and you do a great job helping newbies.

I'm all for figuring out how to best help your thyroid...I'm going to be trying some T3 this winter, changing my BHRT, lots of CT and figuring out a bit more about vit A and sun eating.....I do have another question for you though.....do you think you are missing a big piece of the puzzle or is it that all the changes you have made just haven't had time to get you where you want to go?

### caroline — 2013-12-08T01:50:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-function.8979/post-102766>

Thankyou Sarah.....finally someone understands!!!! If I have a question about myself I would ask in a particular thread.
I think we all have had some amazing and important discussions ....and it does help me a huge amount. I love all of this ...but some days I just think I should shut up for awhile! ...but then I don't for some reason...

I actually wasn't going to respond at all .....but I thought, rather than be resentful and feel sorry for myself ....I would act like an adult.....

As I have said - I feel great .....my thyroid could be more functional and I have a feeling now that my progesterone is lagging. I will test after Christmas some time. We all know how expensive all of this can be and we have to pick our battles! I am thinking now that I should do some stool testing - so I will look into it. But for me - it has always been a very low pg thing - mostly anyway. I just never thought about my thyroid helping my pg....very dumb!

I am confused about Vitamin A ....that is retinol - right? But I have never been clear ....Is the vitamin A cycle something separate? I kind of think it is - but haven't got around to figuring that out yet! It is hugely important tho.

To explain my situation ...Dr. K. said that if I was in the States - I would be an anti-aging Doc's dream patient. I just needed my tank topped up! ......after years and years of adrenal fatigue from caring for my amazing DH.

I am older than you guys - so now it is about aging gracefully and being as healthy as possible and enjoying every last surfer on the beach!!!  I gotta, somehow, stay out of a nursing home!!!

### cantweight — 2013-12-08T05:26:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-function.8979/post-102792>

I too have not been able to wrap my head around the whole vitamin A cycle. It's been hashed out quite a few times and I just can't seem to grasp it which is a shame because its been linked in threads to issues I am trying to resolve. I try I re read...one of these days someone will write something that puts it together for me

I'm glad you feel great and are enjoying the surfers...that's what it all about anyway...right?

### caroline — 2013-12-08T06:47:01+0200

I will start a new thread and see what turns up! If I get smacked down ....I will just suck it up.....take one for the team!
