### caroline — 2013-12-12T03:44:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hungry-at-night-only.9048/post-103186>

Are you eating enough fat/protein for your BAB? Tell us what you are eating ..........

Basically you need to rise with the sun and set with the sun.....that will help reset your circadian rhythm - and then no fake lites after sunset.

Everything Jack tells you to do is for a very specific reason .....

As Nicole said - the blue blockers are huge!

Have you had your hormones tested? I just realised I don't know for sure if you are male or female?

Can you go to bed a little earlier? I think you aren't eating enough!..Some people have been confused about amount of \[size\] of protein .... but it is about the actual grms of protein in the meat. I don't think that I made that very clear.

### Tms — 2013-12-12T05:02:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hungry-at-night-only.9048/post-103193>

This is a typical day

Breakfast: (I'm totally open to eating more if I need to)
5-6 chicken thighs or 12 oz steak
2 eggs
Some green veggies
1-2 tbl coconut oil
Coffee

Lunch:
8 oz salmon or 3 chicken thighs
Med sized Green salad w veggies. It's about 4-5 cups of lettuce.
1 tbl coconut oil
Some berries

Dinner
8oz approx ground beef or chicken thighs and drumsticks
Broccoli
1 tbl coconut oil
Paleo bread w some almond butter

On fit day my calories are around 1700 a day.

### caroline — 2013-12-12T05:42:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hungry-at-night-only.9048/post-103196>

You are eating a lot! I would definitely try upping your BAB. I needed sometimes 80 gr of protein and I am not very big. The BAB is the critical part. A lot of us had a lot of trouble stuffing so much protein down!

How many carbs are you having?

Paleo bread?

Fruit for me triggers hunger .... It is like I am mainlining sugar...lol. I started to do way better when I ditched the fruit.

Almond butter? Could be a trigger?

In Jack's book - he has a great chapter on labs. Have you had a saliva ASI done? That is important to see where you are as far as hormones and cortisol and DHEA are concerned. You need a diurnal cortisol ....4 times

Cortisol is really important.

Candle lite at night or use red or amber bulbs.

Is your computer wired in? You need to get rid of wifi as much as possible.

Grounding is really important - can you get outside barefooted?

Non native EMF is the huge part of the equation ...90% ..... Food is 10%

Can you turn off the breaker to your bedroom? Do you have a smart meter? Do you live in a big city with massive amounts of EMF surrounding you?

Sorry about so many questions!!!

You will get there ....it really isn't that hard ....but right out of the gate there is a lot to consider ....

C/T is extremely important. -there is an easy start guide.....
.

### caroline — 2013-12-12T22:51:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hungry-at-night-only.9048/post-103245>

About food costs ..... a lot of us struggle with that. Grass fed beef hamburger,liver and heart are inexpensive. I often throw a leg of lamb in the crockpot before I go to bed - and Voila ...I have breakfast and quite a few meals.

If I can make a suggestion? You seem to be trying really hard to get this all right....... but slow down for a minute. Read Jack's book from cover to cover. CT #7 is great......

This journey is going to take a while ....... as far as I am concerned it is for life ... and I am loving every single second.

You will be amazed where you are in 6 months or a year....it is our optimal journey of a life time.

As Randy just said in the comments of Jack's latest blog .... "Life is astounding - embrace the miracles"

Things aren't going to happen overnite - but before you know it - you will notice changes here and there and think WTH - when did that happen???

Read Mr. Pinkies' journal .... she and her family have had amazing successes this past year and a half - as have so many others. Have a look at Hemming's journal - he has been struggling with anorexia ..... but he is doing incredibly well!

None of this is linear - just keep working at it and asking for help if you don't understand something........

### caroline — 2013-12-13T21:27:34+0200

Hi Tms! I don't understand the hunger you are having ..... are you eating enough high quality fat? I sometimes eat at 2:30 or 3:00 if I am very hungry and even then I am not usually hungry the next morning.

Before JK .... I would always be up in the middle of the night raiding the refrigerator and pantry!

Epi-Paleo is really a high fat, medium protein, low carb way of eating. That is why I suggested a BPC or tea.
It is much easier if you are eating a lot of fat.

What is your non native EMF situation? where do you live?

Are you drinking spring water? no flourides etc? are you drinking enough? We all need to be properly hydrated.

I have had problems with constipation too. Lately I am having one half a lemon squeezed into hot water and it really works for me.

What testing have you had done?

If you are around a lot of EMF - wifi etc. it will screw with everything! honestly - don't underestimate this.

Food is only 10% ..... your field of existence is 90%. Don't get me wrong - you can't eat SAD ......but you do need to worry about EMF's big time.

Have you listened to Jack's podcasts?

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-12-14T19:44:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hungry-at-night-only.9048/post-103412>

My mind starts going to food (no particular kind) and I can't really think of much else but that. It's a sensation that even having a piece of gum would solve. But I no longer chew gum.

[http://www.kangen.net/](http://www.kangen.net/) ... makes no mention of flouride; so it is probably safe to guess that any flouride in the water, is still in the water after their process. In a nutshell, filtering flouride out of water is extremely difficult. Best bets are to buy spring water or RO (reverse osmosis) water.

Regarding your work .... is your building constructed w/ metal beams, roof, etc. If so, any EMF inside the building has its effect magnified. The more electronics you are near (wifi or not), the more EMF you are exposed to. Best way to find out is to measure.

In my case, I discovered the motor that raises my adjustable desk was the biggest source of EMF. My solution was to plug the motor into a surge strip and keep the strip turned off except for the times I actually adjust my desk height.

Lunch on days I work will be hard but days off I can add more. And I can buy a jar of oil to keep at work. Dinner I can add more too.
I do live in EMF hell. I always feel so normal when I'm away from here, like on retreat. I'm really sensitive to my environment too. Always have been.
My issues are: weight struggles, although did HCG and have kept it off. (Although it messed with my already compromised system quite a bit).
I have insomnia which vacillates btw not being able to fall asleep to not being able to stay asleep. This has been an issue for me since I was young. I'm an anxious person and always have been. And lastly constipation issues. Also started when I was young.
I can't drink coffee after 2 or 3 because it keeps me up. 1-2 cups in the morning is all I can handle. I do drink caffeinated tea which doesn't seem to impact as much. I switch to decaf later in the day. I've tried no caffeine but I still have sleep issues.
It does impact my anxiety a bit.

hi Tms,

I was just like you a couple of months ago. Never slept through the night, constipation so bad I sometimes took 2 capfuls of miralax a day, did 3 rounds of HCG but my weight did not stay off.

Now, I have slept though the night, still not every night but WAY better than before. Constipation is gone.

For my N=1, my hormones were a mess and have been working on them for the past 5 months.

Dr Jack told me NO coffee if trying to lose weight but still have not figured out why but I have cut way back to maybe once a week. Bullet proof tea works great.

For me, getting the blue blocker glasses made a huge improvement in the sleep, get them ASAP (they're cheap). I also got some magnets from harbor freight and believe those have helped too.

Just take it one day at a time, one piece of the puzzle at a time. Trying to fix everything right away is too over whelming but first, you must find out what your labs look like. There area a bunch of us who just got done having labs done so don't hesitate to ask for help.

Good luck,

Nicole

The one good thing it that by gaining some of the weight back it really made me look into what was going on hormonally and see that I was a freeken mess.

My hubby thinks I am a bit crazy too but since I am sleeping better and the sex drive has increased he's not complaining too much. I just wish I could get him to start listening to some of the webinars.

I hear you about wanting to be "exact", it can be too much sometimes.

I wake up about 4:30 am too but just lay there but usually I have to pee but I need to be up at 5:30 for work. It will take some time to get everything working right again. You did not get sick overnight. One good thing is that if you are in the US it is winter so we have more darkness to work with.

### nicld — 2013-12-18T20:07:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hungry-at-night-only.9048/post-103769>

Why does the partner sleep on the couch? Does he snore or breath heavy and keep you awake (hehehe, mine does)? I have to sleep with one ear plug in and a sound machine on just to drown mine out so I can sleep and even then he gets hit once in a while. Get him back in the bed. If anything, if you can’t stay asleep see if he would not mind if you just cuddled up next to him. There is something very powerful about just touching someone. I tell my hubby I need just some electron sharing, he just laughs at me and thinks I am crazy but NOW I do feel something when we snuggle.

What time do you go to bed? We are usually in bed by 9 pm and then I try to read a little bit on my kindle with my blue blocker glasses on every night until the words get fuzzy. Happily it is taking me forever to read a book so get a book to read. Jack has some good recommended ones and even though the subject matter is very interesting, it may help you sleep. Just use either an amber light of blue blocking glasses when reading not in natural light.

I know I wake up early too but I refuse to look at the clock so I have no idea how early. Do you have to be up at a certain time? If not, get rid of the clocks. That is the worst thing that you can do it to see what time it is. For me, I know that I am waking up earlier but because I do not look at a clock I am not real anxious about how little sleep I got or how much time I have to try and get back to sleep to get some more sleep before I need to wake up.

It will not fix itself over night and if your hormones are out of wack, they will have to start getting better before sleep will improve. Its kinda like a catch 22, you need sleep to fix the hormones but you need the hormones fixed to get sleep.

Oh, and my hubby is not up for getting woke up that early either even for sex.

### Josh — 2013-12-12T05:49:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/transcendental-meditation.9049/post-103197>

Using EE10 as a framework, all meditative practice and energy work: tai qi, qi gong, yoga, etc. increases a being's ability to maintain their physical body in the highest energy state possible based on the resources available. In other words, it reduces and optimizes entropy. In the real world for me this has meant that without a daily dose I simply do not feel well physically or mentally. When I get really run down, I need a few hours per day of silent sitting to regenerate. I have been at this for 23 years and it changes over time. I used to get more "energy" from qi gong and tai qi, but gradually I found that my sitting practice became the most nourishing and energizing. My dad has been at it for over 40 years and he says that nothing regenerates him after hard work, like meditation. We both find that we need a certain amount of sleep that can be reduced to a certain extent if we meditate more, but one does not replace the other for most people. There are some exceptions where meditation pretty much becomes a beings dominant reality with very little sleep. More than one tradition says that being able to sit for 4 hours without moving is a prerequisite for "higher practices" and I have interpreted this as 4 hours must be an average for a being to reach a stable higher quatum state where the physiology functions consistently close to optimum and this opens up other perceptics and abilities, etc..The Goenka people have good programs to teach meditiation all over the world in the Vipassana tradition of Buddha. There are many variations on the theme and some people do better starting with "active" techniques that get one's energy "moving" and then end with a period of silence. THere are also techniques where one simply moves until it stops such as whirling in the Sufi tradition. Nature can be a gate for many people, for others it may be a temple or a train station...

Without meditation, I would never have survived 12 years of whatever has kicked my butt physically. And, without getting my butt kicked physically, I might never have meditated enough to feel blessed? TM was what my father started with when I was 8 I think. Later teachers told me that it was an okay preliminary practice, but not the same as a more pure emphasis on witnessing/watching such as in Zen or Vipassana. The Maharishi was an interesting being and I lived in his home region in India and heard stories of him that expanded my perspective. TM seems to be what he and his tradition felt would be digestible to beings in the world as a starting place. Use what works and leave the rest has been my experience, as Buddha reputedly said: "the truth is what works."

In other words, it reduces and optimizes entropy. In the real world for me this has meant that without a daily dose I simply do not feel well physically or mentally. When I get really run down, I need a few hours per day of silent sitting to regenerate. I have been at this for 23 years and it changes over time. I used to get more "energy" from qi gong and tai qi, but gradually I found that my sitting practice became the most nourishing and energizing. My dad has been at it for over 40 years and he says that nothing regenerates him after hard work, like meditation. We both find that we need a certain amount of sleep that can be reduced to a certain extent if we meditate more, but one does not replace the other for most people. There are some exceptions where meditation pretty much becomes a beings dominant reality with very little sleep. More than one tradition says that being able to sit for 4 hours without moving is a prerequisite for "higher practices" and I have interpreted this as 4 hours must be an average for a being to reach a stable higher quatum state where the physiology functions consistently close to optimum and this opens up other perceptics and abilities, etc..The Goenka people have good programs to teach meditiation all over the world in the Vipassana tradition of Buddha. There are many variations on the theme and some people do better starting with "active" techniques that get one's energy "moving" and then end with a period of silence. THere are also techniques where one simply moves until it stops such as whirling in the Sufi tradition. Nature can be a gate for many people, for others it may be a temple or a train station...
>
> Without meditation, I would never have survived 12 years of whatever has kicked my butt physically. And, without getting my butt kicked physically, I might never have meditated enough to feel blessed? TM was what my father started with when I was 8 I think. Later teachers told me that it was an okay preliminary practice, but not the same as a more pure emphasis on witnessing/watching such as in Zen or Vipassana. The Maharishi was an interesting being and I lived in his home region in India and heard stories of him that expanded my perspective. TM seems to be what he and his tradition felt would be digestible to beings in the world as a starting place. Use what works and leave the rest has been my experience, as Buddha reputedly said: "the truth is what works."

Josh, you've just inspired to take meditation more seriously. I desperately need more mental energy just to get myself through the workday. Whatever is kicking my butt has kicked it so bad that I hardly have the discipline to help myself. I got several poor health habits that I do because they are easy. I need enough energy to overcome these obstacles and to be more resilient to stress.

### vangirl — 2015-12-07T02:46:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/transcendental-meditation.9049/post-183562>

MsYo, I have something called the Biotuner. You should check it out. It's a unique and amazing relaxation device that really works. I would never want to be without it..

### PaulG — 2013-12-12T03:45:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-gilbert-ling-wikipedia-project.9053/post-103187>

Inspired by the following and my sense of injustice at there **NOT being a Wikipedia page for Gilbert Ling**, I thought it would be a great idea for this fantastic community to collaborate on making this happen. (I am not sure why it has not happened already - no conspiracy theories please)

-   16 references to Gilbert Ling in [Energy and Epigenetics 11](http://www.jackkruse.com/energy-epigenetics-11-quantum-puzzle) and Jack's call for everyone to get familiar with Gilbert Ling
-   Dead Poets Society [http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0OyAE7UNIAM](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0OyAE7UNIAM) [http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0wjpRQ\_\_lsI](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0wjpRQ__lsI)
-   The quote "If not you then who, if not now then when"

I know very little about Ling or indeed submitting Wikipedia articles but have provisionally created a [Draft Gilbert Ling Wiki Page](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/User:PaulGWiki/sandbox) that this community can collaborate on.

I suggest that Jack write up the summary of The Association Induction Hypothesis section as he is very knowledgeable about Ling. Also I think he should have final approval on the draft before submission to the Wikipedia review panel as this is his site.

We can use this thread to make suggestions on format and content.

My role is to plant this seed and watch it grow and maybe learn a thing or two in the process.

This is a particularly good discussion and summary of Ling on the [Danny Roddy blog](http://www.dannyroddy.com/main/2013/7/29/generative-energy-podcast-episode-four)

Anyone interested in the Ling back story the most interesting and well written account I found in my research was by [Howard Bloom](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Howard_Bloom) who wrote a chapter on Ling in his book [Global Brain](http://www.amazon.com/Global-Brain-Evolution-Mass-Century/dp/0471419192)
[http://futurepositive.synearth.net/2003/07/front-page-140/](http://futurepositive.synearth.net/2003/07/front-page-140/)

Bloom was diagnoses with CFS since 1988 and worked with musicians such as Michael Jackson and Prince but has since conquered his CFS after 15 years being trapped in a bedroom. His is now able to do between 360 and 520 pushups without stopping, He walks 3.5 miles a day and gives lectures in Moscow, Amsterdam, Paris, Kuala Lumpur, Seoul, and Chengdu

He recorded a number of video interviews about his CFS
[http://www.healtheo360.com/stories/chapter-1-the-origins-of-howard-bloom/#.UxkFWH8aySN](http://www.healtheo360.com/stories/chapter-1-the-origins-of-howard-bloom/#.UxkFWH8aySN)

[ISBN](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/International_Standard_Book_Number) [1932559515](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Special:BookSources/1932559515). I will upload her pdf if I get permission.

### Gagnrad — 2014-04-02T13:53:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-gilbert-ling-wikipedia-project.9053/post-119386>

> [PaulG said:](/goto/post?id=119161)
>
> Hey, good news the article has just been accepted by Wiki after a bit of a dialogue with the editors who thought the original draft was too long and promotional.
>
> [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gilbert\_Ling](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gilbert_Ling)

Must have taken you a long time, a lot of work in there. Well done.

The section that you talk about --

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gilbert\_Ling#Association\_induction\_hypothesis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gilbert_Ling#Association_induction_hypothesis)

I read it. Might it be better broken up into shorter paragraphs? That might make it easier for a reader to assimilate.

It does need proof reading. There are errors of number in verbs -- e.g. "ideas fits". Sometimes plurals are formed like possessives ("identical in both scenario's"), and sometimes the apostrophe is missing from a possessive -- e.g. "worlds smallest particles".

In terms of style there are some oddities.

Slang creeps in: so we have "way ahead of his time" for "far ahead of his time".

We also have:

> Today, biology continues to believe

Biologists might believe things; biology doesn't.

There's a similar construction here:

> Yet, the central dogma of biology today believes that ...

You could have "The central dogma of biology is ...". Or you could have "Today biologists believe ...". But in that sentence you've got a dogma believing something, which it can't.

I don't think this kind of thing really matters -- certainly not in informal contexts (such as on a message board) but it does tend to undermine the authority of information put out for public consumption.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-04-02T16:47:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-gilbert-ling-wikipedia-project.9053/post-119400>

Ling’s ideas, were also considered radical and not parsimonious. His idea were a better fit for quantum mechanics but the laws of quantum mechanics were being worked out at the same time Ling's idea's emerged from his mind and his experiments. His ideas fits the way a cell works in vivo. He was way ahead of his time, so far ahead that no one could fathom what his science implied back in the 1950s The development of the MRI by the Fonar corporation and Ray Damadian are a testimony to his acumen. Experiments in physics and biology have finally caught up to Ling’s work now. QED involves itself with the study of the worlds smallest particles. The elelctromagnetic force is the one physical natural force that controls charge particles with infinite range and power. Yet, the central dogma of biology today believes that all life's is somehow tied to the products of DNA, namely genes. Ling's experiments showed the scientists who read them that biology played on a smaller molecular scale than genes; moreover, that scale used electrons, protons, and the electromagnetic force to move these particles around proteins to alter how biochemistry was observed to perform in vivo in many of his animal experiments. Proteins are normally surrounded by water in vivo. When biochemistry is studied most cells are homogenized to remove water to make it easier to study biochemical reactants and just study the proteins within them. This is an unnatural state of affairs, and has led science to some false assumptions for close to 50 years. Ling realized this early on because of his experimental results in frogs. Piccardi also realize that water chemistry could radically alter and experiment if it was not controlled for in experiments in his study of climates. Gerald Pollack mentioned this linkage in his book, The Fourth Phase of Water. A biologic cell is a dissipative system by its very nature. This implies it has the role or purpose to break symmetry and create a metastable system to react to all environmental possibilities that the cell may face. Ling used the idea of "rigor mortis" to make his point about this state in humans brilliantly in his last two books. While a cell is designed to break symmetries, it must be associated with a molecule capable of breaking symmetry. It turns out all proteins are hydrated in vivo. Liquid water is the perfect chemical to break symmetry with in all life forms. The reason is found in water's molecular 3 D arrangements. Liquid water has perfect symmetry in that no matter from which direction you look at the molecules, the view is the same from a molecular standpoint. But water can and does lose its symmetry in nature naturally. If the water freezes to ice, the perfect symmetry is lost or broken and the property of rigidity emerges in its lattice. The same is true when water becomes a gas or a liquid, a hydronium ion, or an OH group in reactions of charge separating events. Symmetry is broken by any phase transition. This occurs many times in biochemical reaction of cells. And as such, all breaks of symmetry require a transfer of energy by the laws of physics to satisfy the Second Law of Thermodynamics. Symmetry is also broken any time temperature rises or falls or when electrons or protons are moving in any biochemical reaction. Any transfer of energy has the potential to break symmetry and therefore to give rise to emergent properties in the products of these reactions. Ling showed this beautifully, in his experiments with insulin in frog muscles. Insulin reacts differently at high and low temperatures even though its molecular chemical formula is identical in both scenario's. It turns out changes in the atoms that make up insulin rotate and move within their lattice such that insulin can not act to move glucose into cells for metabolic use as one would expect in temperature below 61 degrees. This keeps glucose in the blood plasma where it acts as an antifreeze like chemical in many amphibians and mammals during winter months. One must remember there is a seasonal variation of the electromagnetic force in winter and it is this force that induces the 3D molecular rearrangement that cause insulin to lose its natural function we expects as winter slowly comes on. As such insulin function degrades in cold temperatures without any changes to it molecular formula.

In humans, cancer is a state of excessive symmetry breakage for proteins that generate energy in the cytochromes of mitochondria. Ray Damadian read Ling's work and used many of Ling's ideas to develop MRI scanning technology. Ling's AI hypothesis postulated that the protons from water hydration shells should look differently in an altered electromagnetic field because oncogenesis is an process where symmetry is destroyed and wellness is a "quantum superposition" o possible state where symmetry is kept stable by the atomic size of K+ ions and their interactions with water around proteins.

The cellular matrix is flooded with specific proteins coded for DNA and translated by RNA because of the special abilities they all contain in their 3 D molecular arrangements. These chemicals are all are designed to react in quantized fashion the environment they are in. Their molecular arrangement is such that they can react to a myriad of different environments by small changes in the atoms, their bonds, and even in the bonding angles within their molecular lattice. Ling was early to recognize that most ions and catalysts play a role in withdrawing or donating electrons to and from key proteins in biochemical reactions in cell to cause physiology to perform. He was also the first person to document that insulin was thermoplastic hormone in frog muscle. This means the hormone functions differently above and below certain temperatures. When these changes occurred in frog muscles during his experiments he was the first person to realize insulin was associated with a phase transition in the proteins in muscles. Symmetry is also broken any time temperature rises or falls or when electrons or protons are moving in any biochemical reaction. Any transfer of energy has the potential to break symmetry and therefore to give rise to emergent properties in cells and tissues. All proteins have bends in them. Most have 4 levels of bending called primary, second, tertiary, and quaternary structure. The first two conformational bends are directly controlled by the DNA and RNA code. In other words, the first two bends are givens of the system. The last two, called tertiary and quaternary bending however are 100% subject to the redox potential of the cellular environment. The redox potential is directly proportional to cellular environments electromagnetic field that the proteins find themselves in. In this way, the system is designed to read and react to these electromagnetic signals and proteins are electronically induced to become activated or deactivated by the movements of electrons in proteins of the absorbants or adsorbants within the cells structure. Protons are liberated from the water hydration shells that surround all intracellular proteins. This occurred natural, without any energy input because when water is adjacent to a hydrophilic substance water is broken down to protons and hydoxyl ions. Since the electromagnetic force is the force in nature that controls all charged particles, electrons and protons could be moved around the cells to control signaling on a subatomic scale. Ling showed experimentally that the proposed theory of ATP hydrolysis to catalyze biochemical reactions broke the second law of thermodynamics by a wide margin. Arthur Eddington had a famous quote that said any idea that is not congruent with the second law is not a natural law but a false theory. Unfortunately, for biochemistry they did not pay attention to Eddington, who was the scientist/astronomer that experimentally confirmed Einstein's Theory of Relativity by showing that sun light bent around Mercury in an jungle early in the 20th century.

Today, biology continues to believe that life’s stage occurs on nucleic acids, namely DNA and RNA. Ling's work showed close to 50 years ago that life's stage might happen on a much smaller scale by electronic induction and electrostatic forces of association between water and proteins. These two forms of matter are what eventually bound a cell together using intracellular water as the battery and K+ ions as the glue which held the water in a liquid crystalline structure even when the cell membrane was stripped off the cells by Ling in his lab experiments. His work has gone largely untapped by modern medicine and holds promise for future biophysicists and their experiments.

### PaulG — 2014-04-03T01:47:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-gilbert-ling-wikipedia-project.9053/post-119515>

The original plan was to write one article on Gilbert and publish that and then one on the AIH. To this end I asked Gilbert to write a six line summary of AIH and a longer summary in layperson language and with illustrations. Gilbert has done this but unfortunately the latter is 26 pages long and written in first person and needs substantial rewrite for wiki purposes. I also have not seen the illustrations but what I would love to see is animation that compares membrane pump theory with the AIH theory together with voice over explaining it all. Unfortunately I do not have the skills to do this. I could write a layperson account of AIH but I would need to cite it back to the original books and papers which is relatively tricky. I also have reservations whether wiki would accept the article as they have a policy of [non original research](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:No_original_research) and there are few independent sources that discuss the AIH.

So I would like to expand the section on AIH in the current article but it has to be factual and related to Gilberts ideas not Jack Kruse. The second more detailed AIH article I may be able to sneek in what Jack wrote above as it is excellent. Wiki is an online encyclopaedia not a platform for philosophical, political, scientific, social commentary/speculation and the style has to be quite dry.

# Thread: In Light of EE10, help me scale these please...

### Josh — 2013-12-12T05:11:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/in-light-of-ee10-help-me-scale-these-please.9054/post-103194>

1\. Today a friend who works with high level athletes said he will not use a strong electrical stimulation device on athletes that use ice baths and cold because his work emphasizes eccentric contraction and cold causes concentric contraction. Wouldn't the concentric contractions stop as the tissues warmed. Furthermore, wouldn't the cold counteract and promote healing of traumatized bone and or soft tissue as it would minimize inflammation from intensive work. I am trying to understand how this is different from something like Vasper. Beyong that, I am wondering if cold adaption would/will cause muscles to respond differently to cold period?

2\. My friend also said that only high doses of protein and water prevent clients from experiencing too much inflammation and sympathetic dominance after they work. Would this be evidence of the fundamental need for protein and water to get quatum level biological effects and optimum function in tissue. I asked whether it made a difference if the athlete was keto-adapted and they said they had never researched that. Would keto-adaption and cold-adaption change this to where fat with moderate or even no protein would work as well or better? Perhaps even fasted state in a well keto-adapted individual. How do we determine the critical level for protein consumption to maintain quantum function.

3\. Gerson had great success with cancer treatment centered around the concept of restoring intracellular Potassium levels and he spent years identifying the optimal salts to use in combination with fresh squeezed vegetable juices and supplements to support digestion and upregulate metabolism. Even on his intensive protocols, he said it would take 12-18 months or longer to restore the intracellular calcium levels and physiological function. He used a vegetarian diet with the interesting and notable exception of raw liver juice. Does it make sense to drink high levels of Potassium salts such as the ones he used to restore intracellular potassium levels or will the Epi-Paleo diet provide enough if the food is high enough quality and the person is able to digest it?

A team of physicists has provided some of the clearest evidence yet that our Universe could be just one big projection.

Nevertheless, says Maldacena, the numerical proof that these two seemingly disparate worlds are actually identical gives hope that the gravitational properties of our Universe can one day be explained by a simpler cosmos purely in terms of quantum theory.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/structured-water.9058/post-103282>

They all reduce calcium buildup and decrease hardpan formation. The shower water is a little softer here in N. AZ where the tap water is about 350ppm. I appreciate it for the irrigation, etc. Have R/O and shower filters in the house.

2\. Simple......make sure you have ideal protein low EMF and no water contaminants......like halogens of transfats

Symptoms of excessive abnormal hunger, accompanies with
palpitations. It feels as if there is release of cortisol or adrenaline,
or it could be insulin, which causes angina, dizzines, faintlike symptoms.

Hunger is not for food, and not satiated by various foods.

Blood sugar level is normal. As yet, undiagnosed. Tried various foods, magnesium,
and other minerals.

Suffering from a long list of severe, serious illnesses following two prescriptions
of toxic meds 17 years ago. Refused all meds and invasive diagnostic for over
ten years now. See brief summary of health issues in the thread for new members.

I would like to start the paleo diet, but would like to make sure, it would not
worsen those symptoms.

# Thread: Hi from hopeful and very sick
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-hopeful-and-very-sick.9060/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Daizy — 2013-12-13T00:04:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-hopeful-and-very-sick.9060/post-103257>

From being totally healthy, two prescribed medications 17 years ago,
cascaded in torturous devastating SEVERE symptoms/illness.

A brief summary:

1\. Prescribed fluoroquinolone antibiotic. Fell unwell, palpitations, insomnia
and many more symptoms.
2\. A few months later, prescribed Lariam - caused neurological dysfunction, including ocular toxicity, (inc visual distortion), floaters, halos, and more. Auditory
dysfunction such as hyperacusis, tinnitus, vestibular dysfunction, phototoxicity, severe reduced cognition, agnosia, type of aphasia, and more. Arrythmias, tachycardia daily for many years, up to 200 beats per minute, atrial flutters (not recently), AF, (not recently), bradycardia, very low BP, but can fluctuate to to high, overall too low.

Refused all meds to treat. I was hoping to improve with time, healthy diet and vits and supps. Weak to the point of being spoon fed, worsened to being bedridden/wheelchair bound for many years on/off, with lots of symptoms, including neuropathy.

A year later, unstable angina. Given IV GTN, which caused left ventricular dysfunction (heart failure),
Read a few years back, that IV GTN alters ALDH2 gene which causes LVF. Then, diastolic and systolic heart failure and global hypokinesis. Hypothyroidism, mitochondrial dysfunction, autonomic dysfunction, multiple food allergies (causing malnutrition and suspected scurvy), gallstones, toxic hepatitis. MCS, EMF, vascular (insufficiency, blood clots, right groin valve is dysfunctional, left foot turning blue etc..), and more symptoms.

Had thallium test - refused adenosine over ten years ago. Made me very ill. Angioscan with galdolinium, made me very ill, unstable angina for over two years, extreme headaches, hypo/hyperglycemic attacks, kidneys starting to fail etc.. and low grade on/off activation of CMV, EBV and HHV6 over a period of time, which I believe was activated by antibiotics, in particular amoxycillin, which I took over ten years ago. There is much evidence that antibiotics, in particular, amoxycillin can activate dormant infectious pathogens, in particular, the aforementioned.

There is a long list of symptoms, such as neuropathic pains, muscles pains, joints, bones, etc..

Since, I refused all invasive diagnostic tests, zero medication, no anesthetic (unable to tolerate, even local).Had several attacks of angina, unstable angina, acute coronary syndromes, suspected heart attacks for which I refused all medications. I take Arjuna, Ayurvedic med, no toxicity no reactions, from time to time.

Free radicals so high, that even blending a few vegetables makes me ill. Had oxygen mask once on, and made me iller for about a year. When pollution level is high, makes me very ill, angina, and more.

Many compounds in many foods cause worsening of weird neuro symptoms, and extreme pain in all veins and microvessels. Told some type of inflammatory vasculitis. Inability to metabolize some compounds in foods, makes me feel toxic, as if poisoned, and other symptoms. Found out some of the CYPs enzymes are dysfunctional including GSTS, which explains in part the inability to metabolize so many foods and meds.

Slight improvement since I started bone broths a year ago. Able to add a few more foods to the limited diet.
Unable to eat nuts, seeds, avocados, fish, dairy, I am very low in Omega 3 (as shown in tests), and most nurtrients and minerals.

Since the bone broths has been so helpful, I would like to continue with this. The only meats I can meat at present, are lamb and beef, including liver and kidney. I tried ox heart and could not tolerate. I would love to have been able to eat, it oozes with ubiquinol, as it boils..

Unable to eat all sea foods, except for kombu and dulse seaweeds, unable to tolerate others.

As for vegetables and fruits, very limited, but expanding since starting bone broths.

Tried functional medicine, acupuncture (worsened symptoms), Ayurvedic medicine, (some helpful),
pranayama breathing (made me dangerously ill, specially heart and autonomic), Qi Gong, (made me much
worse), Mudras (type of yoga using finger tips), had the opposite effect, homeopathy, (will try again), antroposophical medicine, (worsened symptoms),

I don't believe detoxing is safe for me, and would like to reduce free radicals using a nutritional protocol, such as the Paleo protocol here. Looking forward to improve, and exchange info.

### Penny — 2014-01-14T18:03:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-hopeful-and-very-sick.9060/post-105636>

Welcome Daizy

I think you might have glucoronidation issues in your liver causing the inability to clear drugs - or it could be methylation/sulfation - and of course your gut is a mess - so, here is my laundry list of things you might try without causing undue detox symptoms - it's quite general and applicable to probably most people but \*go slowly\*:

1) bath/foot soak with Epsom salts - the sulfate and magnesium will help you immeasurably - it will also help with "sulfation" - this clears toxins
2) Phosphatidyl Serine complex with PC/PE - this will help you methylate, but not so much that it will kill you - start slowly - you could also take betaine/TMG - beets
3) Molybdenum - helps sulfite oxidase enzyme to work thus making you feel better -
4) calcium-d-glucarate - helps with glucoronidation - basically, phase one liver detox produces toxic compounds and phase 2 needs to catch up -
5) vitamin d - think of your gut lining as a basketweave - it tightens up the weave so toxins don't leak through causing inflammation - get a vitamin d test too -
6) supports for your heart/mitochondria: d-ribose, magnesium, ubiquinol, acetyl-l-carnitine
7) vitamin c/e - helps reduce (add electrons to) glutathione - your body's main detoxifier - plus it offloads it - there is also a glutathione cream, but it might be too much for you -
8) GAPS diet combined with the epi-paleo diet Jack Kruse came up with - get the Gut and Psychology book by Natasha Campbell Mcbride to help you with your diet -
9) get a genetics test from [www.23andme.com](http://www.23andme.com) and run the results through [www.geneticgenie.com](http://www.geneticgenie.com), mthfrsupport.com, or knowyourgenetics.com so you can figure out which hepatic pathways are blocked and thus have a clue what to do - it's only $99.00
10) digestive enzymes - truly, enzymes, the bone broths and vitamin d are my top 3 suggestions - you are not metabolizing food - enzymes helps a lot -
11) aloe vera, avocados, marshmallow root all soothe the gut -
12) don't try detox until your hepatic pathways are functioning or it won't end well -
13) maybe krill oil and MCT oil for your cell membranes, but I would use an enzyme with them -
14) no wheat/dairy/corn/nightshades/legumes/soy/beans - and nuts are really hard to digest - eat stews/soups/well cooked non-fibrous veggies - after your gut heals, you can start loading up on cruciferous veggies - they have sulfur which binds toxins which you can then excrete - molybdenum helps with that -

I wish you the best and hopefully nothing I've suggested here will make it worse - but it's always possible so do your own research

take care -
Penny
